applications.role: An internship role focused on building NLP-powered solutions and demos using Snowflake's AI/ML ecosystem to support sales engineers in presenting capabilities to potential customers.
applications.jobDescription:
Snowflake is about empowering enterprises to achieve their full potential — and people too. With a culture that’s all in on impact, innovation, and collaboration, Snowflake is the sweet spot for building big, moving fast, and taking technology — and careers — to the next level.

There is only one Data Cloud. Snowflake’s founders started from scratch and designed a data platform built for the cloud that is effective, affordable, and accessible to all data users. But it didn’t stop there. They engineered Snowflake to power the Data Cloud, where thousands of organizations unlock the value of their data with near-unlimited scale, concurrency, and performance. This is our vision: a world with endless insights to tackle the challenges and opportunities of today and reveal the possibilities of tomorrow.

We’re looking for dedicated students who share our passion for ground-breaking technology and want to create a lasting future for you and Snowflake.

WHAT WE OFFER:
Paid, full-time internships in the heart of the software industry

Post-internship career opportunities (full-time and/or additional internships)

Exposure to a fast-paced, fun and inclusive culture

A chance to work with world-class experts on challenging projects

Opportunity to provide meaningful contributions to a real system used by customers

High level of access to supervisors (manager and mentor), detailed direction without micromanagement, feedback throughout your internship, and a final evaluation

WHAT WE EXPECT:
Must be actively enrolled in an accredited college/university program during the time of the internship

Desired Class Level: Pursuing MISM or MBA

Start Date: May 11, May 26th or June 22

End Date: July 31st, Aug 14th, Aug 28th, or Sept 4th.

Familiarity with NLP concepts, like prompt engineering, chaining, and fine-tuning

Familiarity with containers

Ability to build solutions and demos quickly, with a team or independently

Experience in AI conferences/competitions

Able to quickly understand problems and view them in the larger business context

Fast learner

WHAT YOU WILL LEARN/GAIN:
Experience working on a team with world-class sales engineers

Experience creating NLP solutions/demos for a variety of use cases and presenting them to potential customers

Experience with cutting-edge offerings like Snowpark Container Services, Snowflake Cortex, Streamlit, and the Snowflake Native App framework

Every Snowflake employee is expected to follow the company’s confidentiality and security standards for handling sensitive data. Snowflake employees must abide by the company’s data security plan as an essential part of their duties. It is every employee's duty to keep customer information secure and confidential.

Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.

How do you want to make your impact?

For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com

missingSkills.reason: Snowpark Container Services is a core deliverable. Zero container evidence anywhere in this profile. This alone is a disqualifier.

missingSkills.skill: Snowflake Platform (Cortex, Snowpark, Native App Framework)
missingSkills.reason: The entire role is built on Snowflake's ecosystem. Not a single mention of Snowflake in the candidate's experience, projects, or certifications.
Explicitly called out in the role's learning outcomes. Candidate has no documented experience with this demo/app framework.
The JD explicitly lists chaining as a required NLP concept. No evidence of pipeline-level LLM orchestration in any project or role.
Listed as a required NLP familiarity. Candidate's Handshake 'training' is data labeling and voice recording — not model fine-tuning in any engineering sense.
Explicitly desired by the JD. No hackathons, Kaggle, NeurIPS, or any competitive AI exposure documented.
The role requires rapidly building and presenting NLP demos to enterprise customers alongside sales engineers. No evidence of this capability.
The JD explicitly targets MISM or MBA candidates. The profile implies an MIS undergraduate track at George Mason but does not confirm graduate enrollment — which is a hard filter.

experienceGaps: The Handshake 'Generative AI Fellow' role is data labeling and voice recording, not NLP engineering. Calling it AI development on a resume for this role will be seen through immediately by a technical screener.
No end-to-end AI/NLP solution built and deployed — all projects are academic or data analysis focused, not application development.
Zero containerization experience despite containers being a core stated requirement.
No sales engineering or pre-sales demo context — Apple retail is consultative sales, not technical SE work for enterprise software.
No Snowflake, Databricks, or modern cloud data platform exposure of any kind.
All NLP exposure is passive (evaluating outputs, writing prompts for image edits) rather than engineering-driven (building pipelines, APIs, or models).
Graduate enrollment status is ambiguous — this is a hard eligibility gate, not a soft preference.
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is applying for a role that requires NLP engineering, container literacy, Snowflake ecosystem knowledge, and the ability to rapidly build enterprise demos — and they have none of those in any meaningful depth. The Handshake role sounds impressive on the surface but is fundamentally a data labeling gig, and a technical interviewer at Snowflake will see that in 60 seconds. The Apple experience is legitimately good for business acumen, but this is not a sales role — it sits inside a solutions engineering team. This candidate is 12–18 months of focused technical development away from being competitive for this specific posting.
improvementPlan: Get hands-on with Docker immediately — complete a container fundamentals course (Docker's official tutorial + a project deploying a Python app in a container). This is non-negotiable for this role.
Sign up for a free Snowflake trial account and complete Snowflake's official 'Hands-On Essentials' badge series, especially the Data Engineering and Cortex AI tracks.
Build an actual end-to-end LLM demo using LangChain or LlamaIndex with a Streamlit front end and deploy it — then add it to your portfolio. This directly mirrors what this role does.
Enter a Kaggle NLP competition or an AI hackathon (MLH, Lablab.ai) before reapplying. The JD explicitly asks for this and you have none.
Confirm and clearly state graduate enrollment status (MISM or MBA) — if you are not in a graduate program, you are ineligible by the JD's hard requirements.
Reframe the Handshake experience honestly — lead with prompt engineering strategy and quality evaluation frameworks, not 'AI training,' to avoid credibility damage in interviews.
Study LLM chaining patterns (RAG, ReAct, tool-use agents) and build a project demonstrating at least one — this is the core technical skill gap beyond containers.
marketSkillsToLearn: Docker & Kubernetes (container fundamentals)
Snowflake (Snowpark, Cortex, Native Apps)
Streamlit (rapid demo/app development)
LangChain or LlamaIndex (LLM chaining and orchestration)
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architecture
Vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector)
FastAPI (building AI microservices)
dbt (data transformation in modern data stacks)
Hugging Face Transformers (fine-tuning workflows)
analysis.summary: This candidate brings genuine strengths in business strategy, B2B consulting instincts, and surface-level AI familiarity — but is critically under-equipped for the technical depth this Snowflake role demands. The most disqualifying gaps are containers, Snowflake platform knowledge, LLM engineering, and the absence of any AI competition or demo-building experience. Without a focused 12–18 month technical upskilling push specifically targeting the modern AI/cloud data stack, this candidate will not clear even the initial screening filter for this type of role.

applications.role: Supporting project managers and cross-functional teams in the organized coordination and execution of marketing campaigns, client communications, budgets, and project timelines at a remote marketing agency.
applications.jobDescription:
We are looking for an organized project coordinator to support our project manager team in delivering exceptional projects. In this role, you will play a supporting role in ensuring the successful execution of projects. You have exceptional organizational skills, and a relentless commitment to client satisfaction. You will report to a Project Director or Senior Project Manager. 

This role is open to fully remote candidates in the United States or Canada, and must be able to work Eastern time zone hours.



Responsibilities
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure the successful execution of marketing campaigns and projects.

Own routing content through internal processes.

Help monitor project progress, budgets, and timelines to ensure on-time and on-budget delivery.

Work with Sr. PMs and department heads to proactively plan and manage resourcing

Inform stakeholders about project status through regular updates and reports.

Support the management of account budgets, forecasts, and financial performance.

Support the project management and client services team with client communications as needed



Requirements
Bachelor's degree in communications, business, project management or a related field

Experience using project management tools such as Monday, Asana, Smartsheets

Agency or pharma experience a plus

Highly organized with a high attention to detail 

Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation abilities.

Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.



Interested in working with us? Apply online now. 

P10—Not an agency*

*The layer-laden teams. The prehistoric processes. The impersonal, ego-driven dog-and-pony shows. Take what you think you know about agencies and burn it. P10 was born of the idea that if something isn’t working—or could work better—change it. And that’s exactly what we did. We know that what we’re not makes us who we are. And we’re not just another agency. We are people driven by a palpable passion and purpose, felt by our clients from the very first conversation.  

P10 brings together a team of diverse, remote-only seasoned professionals to deliver exceptional value for our clients. Dare to do something different - join us.

presentSkills: Financial tracking and budget awareness (Dispomode, Flashconverter project)

missingSkills.reason: The job explicitly names these tools as requirements. The candidate lists zero project management software experience anywhere in their profile. This is a non-negotiable hard skill for day-one functionality.

This role requires someone who has actually coordinated projects — timelines, deliverables, routing content, managing dependencies. The candidate has zero formal PM experience. Apple retail sales and AI model training do not substitute.
The core of this role is supporting marketing campaign execution. The candidate has no marketing agency, brand, or campaign operations background whatsoever.

The role requires producing regular project status reports for clients and internal stakeholders. There is no demonstrated experience writing structured project reports or managing communication cadences.
The role explicitly requires working with Sr. PMs on resourcing. The candidate has no exposure to resource forecasting or allocation in a professional project context.

Supporting financial performance of client accounts is a core responsibility. The candidate's financial experience is limited to a personal finance app project and retail upselling — not client account P&L management.
Owning content through internal approval and routing processes is a specific operational skill built in agency or marketing environments. The candidate has no exposure to this.
P10 is an agency. Agency culture, client relationship dynamics, and project delivery expectations are a specific professional context. The candidate has no agency-side experience.

experienceGaps: Zero project management experience in any professional context — not even a junior or coordinator role
No marketing, advertising, or agency background — the candidate's trajectory is clearly toward data science and AI engineering, not project operations
Apple experience is retail sales, not transferable to PM coordination — it demonstrates communication skills but not workflow, timeline, or budget management
AI Fellow role at Handshake is gig-based AI model labeling work, not relevant to project coordination
Burger 7 Assistant Manager entry is incomplete and adds no relevant value
Academic projects demonstrate technical and analytical ambition but are engineering and data-heavy — misaligned with the marketing/operations nature of this role
Writing quality in submitted descriptions is informal and unpolished, which is a red flag for a role that requires professional client communications and stakeholder reporting
No demonstrated experience managing competing priorities across multiple client projects simultaneously
No evidence of producing any formal project documentation, status reports, or process maps
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is applying for the wrong job. Their entire skill stack — Python, R, SQL, machine learning, NLP, cloud architecture — is built for a data science or AI engineering career path, not project coordination at a marketing agency. The one transferable asset is their Apple retail communication experience, which shows they can talk to clients, but that alone does not make someone a project coordinator. Without a single hour of experience using Monday, Asana, or Smartsheets, and with no marketing or agency background, this candidate would require six to twelve months of onboarding just to become minimally functional in this role — and that's not what a project coordinator hire is supposed to look like.
improvementPlan: Immediately get hands-on with Monday.com, Asana, and Smartsheets — all three offer free tiers or trials. Complete their official certification programs and build a portfolio of simulated project plans, timelines, and status reports
Pursue a Google Project Management Certificate (Coursera) or PMI CAPM certification to establish foundational PM credentials that hiring managers will actually recognize
Seek an internship, freelance role, or volunteer opportunity at a marketing agency, nonprofit, or startup in a project support or operations coordinator capacity to build direct, relevant experience

Reframe and rewrite your resume to lead with transferable soft skills (client communication, cross-functional coordination from Apple) rather than technical AI and data skills that actively signal misalignment for this role type

Study marketing campaign lifecycle basics — learn how creative briefs, content calendars, approval workflows, and campaign trafficking work in an agency environment through free resources or courses on HubSpot Academy
marketSkillsToLearn: Monday.com (project management platform — industry standard at agencies)
Asana (widely used for marketing project coordination)
Wrike or Smartsheets (common in enterprise and agency settings)
HubSpot (marketing CRM and campaign management — highly valued in agency contexts)
Notion (workflow and documentation management)
Basic financial forecasting and account budget tracking in spreadsheets
Client communication writing — professional status updates, meeting recaps, SOW summaries
Marketing funnel and campaign lifecycle fundamentals
analysis.summary: This candidate is a data science and AI engineering profile applying to a marketing agency project coordination role — a significant category mismatch. While they show genuine communication ability from their Apple tenure and intellectual curiosity through their technical projects, they are missing every core operational requirement: PM tools, agency experience, campaign management, and formal project coordination. Without a deliberate pivot in experience and certifications, this application will not progress past initial screening at any competent agency.

applications.role: Design and launch an AI Champion Network from the ground up to help AAA Auto Club Group employees adopt AI tools effectively and responsibly through program frameworks, toolkits, and onboarding materials.
applications.jobDescription:
It’s a great time to join AAA The Auto Club Group! 

JOIN THE TEAM COMMITTED TO DRIVING YOUR CAREER FORWARD

Job Type:

Full time
 
Exempt/Non Exempt:

 
Hourly
Job Description:



 

Why Intern with the AAA The Auto Club Group (ACG)  

The ACG Internship Program aims to provide a rich, relevant, and rewarding experience for college students. Interns will have weekly touch-bases with the Internship Team and meet virtually with executives from around the organization. The goal of the program is to provide interns with the opportunity to connect, network, and grow as they work on department-specific projects. The experience is designed to enhance the skills and abilities of young professionals and identify future talent for our organization.   

ACG is making a large investment in AI in 2026 and is building the internal infrastructure needed to turn that investment into real workforce impact. A critical component of that effort is the AI Champion Network—a volunteer peer-to-peer network designed to help employees across the organization adopt AI tools effectively, responsibly, and in ways that fit their day-to-day work. 

In this position with the AAA ACG People & Experience Strategy Team, you will:  

Work directly with the People & Experience Strategy team to design, launch, and operationalize the AI Champion Network from the ground up 

Contribute to enterprise transformation and strengthen your program management and change enablement skills 

A DAY IN THE LIFE of an AI Champion Network Intern  

ACG is seeking a motivated student to join our team as an AI Champion Network Intern who will work with a small team to co-create program frameworks, toolkits, onboarding materials, and use-case resources that help employees adopt AI effectively and responsibly. The work requires organizational thinking, strong communication, and the ability to turn ambiguous ideas into structured, usable deliverables. 

In this position, you will have the opportunity to:  

Draft the AI Champion Network program charter, including purpose, operating principles, roles and responsibilities, governance structure, and success measures 

Create Champion onboarding materials: welcome guides, orientation decks, role expectations, and a “first 30 days” roadmap 

Coordinate across HR, IT, and business unit stakeholders to align on program scope, tool boundaries, and approved use cases 

Design a measurement framework for the network, including leading indicators (champion activity, use-case submissions, participation rates) and lagging indicators (adoption trends, employee sentiment) 

WE ARE LOOKING FOR CANDIDATES WHO  

Required Qualifications:   

Must be:  

At least a junior, fully enrolled in a bachelor’s or master’s degree program at an accredited college/university, with a minimum 3.0 GPA  

Studying a field related to Industrial-Organizational Psychology, Organizational Development, Human Resources, Business Administration, Communications, or Behavioral Science 

Legally authorized to work in the U.S  

Must have:   

Skills:  

Strong interpersonal skills, communication skills (oral and written), organizational skills, and strong situational adaptability   

Strong analytical and critical thinking skills  

Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail  

Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment  

Ability to multi-task and appropriately prioritize to ensure timely solutions to problems 

Experience using Microsoft 365 tools (Teams, SharePoint, Word, Excel, PowerPoint)   

Knowledge of:  

Basic math calculations to accurately perform various types of transactions  

Organizational behavior and human factors shaping technology adoption at work 

Basic concepts relating to generative AI tools and their use in workplace settings (hands-on familiarity is a plus; deep technical knowledge is not required) 

Stakeholder analysis and communication planning fundamentals 

Basic program or project management concepts, including how to scope work, set milestones, and manage dependencies 

Basic product/project management topics including stakeholder analysis, adoption frameworks (e.g., HEART, ADKAR), or experience running related workshops 

Ability to:  

Learn the full range ACG products, services and functions to perform the responsibilities of assigned job  

Develop clear, professional written deliverables (guides, decks, frameworks, playbooks) independently with minimal editing required 

Facilitate or support meetings, workshops, or working sessions with cross-functional participants 

Gather qualitative and quantitative data, synthesize findings, and present meaningful insights to leadership 

Work effectively in a remote environment, independently and on teams 

Collaborate across functions and seniority levels 

Preferred Qualifications:   

Ability to learn and adapt to corporate cultures and processes and provide own relevant and effective experience and best practices  

Comfort with working in an environment where responsibilities are broadly defined, resources are limited, and collaboration is critical to success  

Ability to provide good service to internal and/or external customers  

Personal experience experimenting with AI tools (ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot, etc.) in an academic or professional context and an ability to articulate practical use cases 

Experience designing or supporting an employee program, learning initiative, community of practice, or similar volunteer/ambassador network 

Coursework or applied experience in training design, instructional content development, or adult learning principles 

Exposure to people analytics, or survey/listening tools (e.g., Culture Amp, Qualtrics, Glint) 

Familiarity using SharePoint or OneDrive for content organization and knowledge management 

Supervisory Responsibilities:   

None  

Compensation and Time Commitment    

The Auto Club Group will provide compensation of $19.50/hour for 40 hours/week. The interns will be expected to report during core hours Monday through Friday for the duration of the program’s twelve (12) weeks. The program will run from May 18th to August 7th, 2026.  

Work Environment   

This position is currently able to work remotely from a home office location for day-to-day operations unless occasional travel for meetings, collaborative activities, or team building activities is specified by leadership. This is subject to change based on amendments and/or modifications to the ACG Flex Work policy. 

Who We Are

Become a part of something bigger.

The Auto Club Group (ACG) provides membership, travel, insurance, and financial service offerings to approximately 14+ million members and customers across 14 states and 2 U.S. territories through AAA, Meemic, and Fremont brands. ACG belongs to the national AAA federation and is the second largest AAA club in North America.

By continuing to invest in more advanced technology, pursuing innovative products, and hiring a highly skilled workforce, AAA continues to build upon its heritage of providing quality service and helping our members enjoy life’s journey through insurance, travel, financial services, and roadside assistance.

And when you join our team, one of the first things you’ll notice is that same, whole-hearted, enthusiastic advocacy for each other.

We have positions available for every walk of life! AAA prides itself on creating an inclusive and welcoming environment of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and viewpoints, realizing our differences make us stronger.

To learn more about AAA The Auto Club Group visit www.aaa.com

Important Note:

ACG’s Compensation philosophy is to provide a market-competitive structure of fair, equitable and performance-based pay to attract and retain excellent talent that will enable ACG to meet its short and long-term goals. ACG utilizes a geographic pay differential as part of the base salary compensation program. Pay ranges outlined in this posting are based on the various ranges within the geographic areas which ACG operates. Salary at time of offer is determined based on these and other factors as associated with the job and job level.  

The above statements describe the principal and essential functions, but not all functions that may be inherent in the job.  This job requires the ability to perform duties contained in the job description for this position, including, but not limited to, the above requirements.  Reasonable accommodations will be made for otherwise qualified applicants, as needed, to enable them to fulfill these requirements.

The Auto Club Group, and all its affiliated companies, is an equal opportunity employer.  All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability or protected veteran status.

Regular and reliable attendance is essential for the function of this job.

AAA The Auto Club Group is committed to providing a safe workplace. Every applicant offered employment within The Auto Club Group will be required to consent to a background and drug screen based on the requirements of the position.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is a data/tech-oriented business student with genuine AI familiarity and strong interpersonal skills from Apple — but those strengths are largely misaligned with what this role actually demands. The AI Champion Network Intern position is fundamentally an HR program design and change management role, and the candidate has zero background in organizational development, change frameworks, instructional design, or people analytics. The technical AI skills that look impressive on paper (Python, ML, cloud) are completely irrelevant here, and the candidate risks being screened out for being the wrong kind of AI-savvy — technically deep rather than people-and-adoption focused. Without meaningful coursework or experience in OD, IO psychology, change management, or training design, this is a significant mismatch at the core of the role.

Get hands-on with Qualtrics or Google Forms to design and analyze a simple survey — even a self-initiated project measuring peer opinions on anything demonstrates measurement framework capability
Draft a mock AI Champion program charter for a fictional company using publicly available templates — this becomes a portfolio artifact you can reference directly in interviews
Learn SharePoint basics through Microsoft Learn (free) and build at least one content organization structure or team site to demonstrate knowledge management capability
Reframe the Apple experience in application materials to emphasize structured onboarding support, knowledge transfer to business clients, and cross-functional coordination — not sales metrics
Seek out a campus ambassador program, student org leadership role, or volunteer network coordinator position to get direct experience with community-of-practice design before the application deadline
Eliminate or de-emphasize Python, SQL, ML, AWS, and cloud skills from materials targeting this role — they signal the wrong candidate archetype and may actively hurt your positioning

analysis.summary: This candidate has genuine strengths — real AI hands-on experience, proven interpersonal skills, and solid business acumen — but is fundamentally misaligned with the core competency profile this role requires. The AAA AI Champion Intern role is a people and change management position disguised as an AI role, and the candidate's background skews heavily technical and sales-oriented rather than OD or HR-adjacent. With targeted upskilling in change management frameworks, instructional design, and HR program tools over the next several months, this candidate could become competitive for similar roles — but as of today, the gaps are too wide and too central to the position to recommend.

applications.role: An AI and data-focused summer intern role partnering with the Analytics team to analyze customer call data using AI tools to predict client churn and inform Customer Success retention strategy.
applications.jobDescription:
US Remote
AI Analytics (Business Intelligence) Summer Intern

Overview: 

This role is for an AI & data focused intern who will partner with the Analytics team to analyze text data from our customer calls using AI.  The results will be shared with Customer Success leadership and inform CS strategy on client retention. 

Project Scope: 

Using AI, the intern will review existing Gong customer call data and build upon several early-stage models to determine whether existing clients will churn or renew 
Ideally this analysis will inform a larger model to provide an early warning signal to CS about customers threatening to leave 
Key stakeholders include the following teams: Analytics, Data Engineering, Customer Success and Sales 
Deliverables: 

This project changes rapidly as new info is found and implemented.  Some potential tasks include: 

Working with CS and data engineering to find data surrounding key customers we want to evaluate 
Running models through Claude to determine which approaches produce the most predictive outcomes  
Creating PowerPoints to summarize the results of the project for management 
Suggesting changes to the process to improve and strengthen our existing models and data practices 
Learning objectives: 

Tools to gain skills with: SQL, Excel, AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) 
Business exposure: CS, Analytics and Sales leadership 
Professional Development: Role will partially own all development of models through the duration of their internship 
Skills & Qualifications

Background or coursework in data, analytics, math, computer science, business, or a related field
Interest in data analysis and AI applications
Familiarity with tools like Excel; SQL or similar is a plus
Experience or comfort using AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Clear communication skills for presenting insights
Curious, detail-oriented, and eager to learn
Location/Time Zone Preference: United States - Pacific Time Zone preferred

This is an hourly, remote summer internship for college students, offering up to 35 hours per week at a rate of $20 per hour. Pacvue’s internship program runs across two 9-week cohorts, and candidates may choose the option that best fits their schedule: 

Cohort 1 (May 18 – July 17) or Cohort 2 (June 15 – August 14).

 

#LI-Remote

Pacvue is committed to employing a diverse workforce. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability.

missingSkills.skill: Gong or similar call intelligence platform experience
missingSkills.reason: The entire project revolves around Gong call data. Having zero exposure to this platform means a ramp-up period that eats into a 9-week internship.

missingSkills.skill: Text/NLP analytics on unstructured audio or transcript data
missingSkills.reason: The core deliverable is analyzing customer call transcripts for churn signals. NLP is listed as a skill but there is no demonstrated project applying NLP to conversational or call data specifically.

missingSkills.skill: Churn modeling or customer retention analytics
missingSkills.reason: Predicting churn is a domain-specific challenge. The candidate has predictive modeling experience (retail Random Forest) but has never explicitly framed work around customer lifecycle or retention signals.
A stated deliverable is creating PowerPoints for CS and Sales leadership. There is no evidence of this in the profile despite it being listed as a key output.

missingSkills.skill: Claude or ChatGPT used analytically for structured business tasks
missingSkills.reason: The role specifically calls for running models through Claude to test predictive approaches. The candidate's AI tool experience is in training and prompt engineering for generative tasks, not analytical pipelines.
The job explicitly prefers PT. Candidate is East Coast based. Not a dealbreaker but worth flagging given remote collaboration norms.

experienceGaps: Zero experience in customer success, client retention strategy, or churn analytics — understanding why B2B customers leave is domain knowledge that can't be faked with technical skills alone

The Handshake Generative AI Fellow role is essentially crowdsourced gig work (voice recording, image selection), not structured data analytics — it should not be overstated as equivalent to applied AI engineering
No demonstrated experience creating management-facing deliverables like reports, dashboards, or executive presentations for real stakeholders
The Apple role shows strong customer empathy and B2B awareness, but that is 3 years ago and in a retail context — it does not translate directly into SaaS CS strategy

Written communication quality in the portfolio is extremely poor — run-on sentences, informal grammar, inconsistent capitalization — this is a direct liability for a role requiring clear insight communication to leadership
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is technically more capable than what the role strictly requires — SQL, Excel, ML, NLP, and AI tools are all present — which makes them a viable intern prospect. However, the glaring red flag is communication quality: the portfolio descriptions are riddled with grammatical errors, informal phrasing, and disorganized thought, which is a serious concern for a role explicitly requiring executive-facing PowerPoint deliverables and stakeholder communication. The technical foundation is real, but if this candidate cannot present insights clearly in writing, they will struggle to deliver the core outputs Pacvue actually needs. Hire cautiously and assess communication in the interview aggressively.
improvementPlan: Build and publish a churn prediction project using a public SaaS or telecom dataset (IBM Telco is free) — use Python, scikit-learn, and NLP features from text to make it directly relevant to this role

Obtain at least basic familiarity with Gong by watching demos, reading documentation, or exploring their developer resources — show up to interviews able to speak to how conversation intelligence platforms work
Practice executive communication: write a 3-slide mock PowerPoint summarizing a data project finding and include it in your portfolio — this directly addresses the most visible gap
Clean up all written portfolio descriptions immediately — the grammar and sentence structure are unprofessional and will cost you opportunities well beyond this one role

Frame the Apple B2B experience explicitly around customer retention and churn signals — you have real instincts about why enterprise clients stay or leave, but it is buried under retail sales narrative
marketSkillsToLearn: LLM-based text classification for business intelligence (using OpenAI or Anthropic APIs programmatically, not just chat interfaces)
Customer success analytics concepts: NRR, GRR, health scores, early warning systems
dbt or basic data transformation pipelines for analytics engineering context
Looker or Sigma (common BI tools in SaaS analytics stacks alongside Tableau/Power BI)
Basic statistics for churn modeling: survival analysis, logistic regression interpretation, lift curves
analysis.summary: A technically well-rounded candidate whose skill set — SQL, Python, R, ML, NLP, and certified Excel — maps cleanly onto what this internship needs. The retail predictive analytics project and generative AI training experience show real applied initiative beyond the classroom. The critical drag on this profile is written communication quality, which is frankly inconsistent with the executive-facing deliverables this role demands, and the lack of any churn or customer success domain context.

qaHistory.q: What interests you about Pacvue? *
qaHistory.a:
Pacvue sits right at the intersection of things I've been genuinely excited about this past year — AI, data analytics, and how both connect to real business decisions. What pulls me in specifically is that this isn't a research project for its own sake. The output actually goes to Customer Success leadership and shapes how the team responds to clients who might be at risk of leaving. That kind of applied work, where the analysis has a clear decision on the other end of it, is exactly what I've found most interesting in my coursework.

In my MIS 431 project, I built a Random Forest model in R to predict whether customers at a convenience store chain would convert from low-margin fuel buyers to higher-value shoppers. When the variable importance plots pointed to loyalty program enrollment as the top predictor, it wasn't just a statistical finding — it actually changed what we recommended the company do. That experience of watching a model translate into a strategy recommendation stuck with me, and the churn prediction work at Pacvue feels like a more advanced version of that same idea.

The fact that the role involves working with Claude specifically also matters to me. I've built a full platform that uses the Claude API at its core, so I'm not coming in cold to that tool — I've had to think carefully about how to prompt it for structured business outputs, which feels directly relevant to what this project is trying to do with call data.

And honestly, the AdTech and SaaS space is one I want to understand better. Companies in this space are running on data-driven decisions in real time, and getting things like churn prediction right has real revenue implications. That's the kind of environment where the analytical work I want to do actually matters.

applications.role: Providing actionable business intelligence insights across multiple business units by building and maintaining BI pipelines, dashboards, and reports using SQL, Python, and Looker.
applications.jobDescription:
Junior Business Intelligence Analyst
Remote, United States
LetsGetChecked is a global healthcare solutions company that provides the tools to manage health from home through health testing, virtual care, genetic sequencing, and medication delivery for a wide range of health and wellness conditions. LetsGetChecked's end-to-end model includes manufacturing, logistics, lab analysis, physician support, and prescription fulfillment. Founded in 2015 and co-headquartered in Dublin and Atlanta, LetsGetChecked empowers people to take control of their health and live longer, happier lives.

Junior BI Analyst
As a Junior BI Analyst at LetsGetChecked, you will provide multiple business units with actionable insights that improve business functionality, whilst working with the data from an end to end perspective

Responsibilities 
Work with team leads to build, manage and maintain the BI & reporting pipelines
Translate business requirements into reportable KPIs and metrics  
Support configuration and analysis for internal and external client reporting 
Create a variety of scheduled and ad Hoc reports across multiple platforms (dependent on stakeholder requirements)
Conduct quality checks / reports on data to ensure accuracy, consistency and reliability of information
Implement necessary data validation processes and resolve discrepancies or issues identified 
Develop and maintain dashboards leveraging data visualization tools and advanced analytical techniques
Perform statistical analyses to provide insights and recommendations
Liaise directly with the relevant teams, internally or externally to resolve any queries
What we are looking for…..
Degree in IT / STEM / Business 
1+ years experience working as part of a BI & reporting function 
Proven analytical and problem-solving skills 
Strong knowledge of data, information management and data quality standards
Advanced knowledge of SQL, python
Experience with Looker for dashboard development 
Ability to work as part of a team.
 Benefits: 

Alongside a salary $72,400 - $83,000 (Depending on Experience), we offer a range of benefits including: 

Full-time employee benefits include: dental, vision, and multiple group medical plans to choose from, a 401(k) retirement savings plan, group life insurance, (AD&D) insurance, flexible spending account (FSA) and health savings account (HSA), commuter benefits, employer-paid short-term (STD) and long-term disability (LTD) insurance, and additional supplemental insurance plans (spouse life insurance, legal insurance, an employee assistance program, home health testing kits, and a fertility medication discount program). Employees are also provided flexible vacation time, accrued paid sick time, 10 paid holidays, (2 floating holidays for full time non-exempt employees) , and up to 16 weeks of paid parental leave for a birthing parent, and up to 8 weeks of paid bonding leave for non-birthing parents, and up to 12 weeks of family caregiver leave, including 4 paid, to support care for a family member, and a Lifestyle Spending Account allowance each month.

#LI-IF #LI-Remote

Why LetsGetChecked

At LetsGetChecked, we are revolutionizing healthcare by making it more accessible, convenient, and personalized. Our mission is to empower individuals with the knowledge and tools they need to manage their health proactively, so they can live longer, happier lives.

By joining our team, you will be part of a dynamic and innovative company that is dedicated to improving lives through cutting-edge technology and compassionate care. We value our employees and invest in their growth, offering opportunities for professional development and career advancement. Together, we can make a meaningful impact on the future of healthcare and help people take control of their health journey. Join us in our commitment to transforming healthcare for the better.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

At LetsGetChecked, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Please review our Candidate Privacy Policy.

The job requires 1+ years working inside an actual BI function. This candidate has zero professional BI experience. Their BI exposure is entirely academic projects and certifications. That does not meet the requirement.
Translating business requirements into reportable KPIs for real stakeholders is a core responsibility. There is no demonstrated experience doing this in a professional setting.

missingSkills.skill: Data Validation and Quality Assurance Processes
missingSkills.reason: The role explicitly requires implementing data validation processes and resolving discrepancies. No evidence of this in any professional or project context.

missingSkills.skill: Ad Hoc and Scheduled Reporting Across Platforms
missingSkills.reason: Multi-platform reporting for internal and external clients is a daily function of this role. Candidate has no demonstrated experience supporting client-facing or cross-functional reporting.

The job requires advanced SQL. Candidate lists SQL as a skill but has an introductory Python certification and no professional context proving SQL proficiency at an advanced level. Depth is unverified and suspect.
LetsGetChecked operates in a regulated healthcare environment. HIPAA, health metrics, and clinical data context would be valuable. Candidate has zero healthcare exposure.
Modern BI stacks heavily use dbt for pipeline transformations. Not listed on the JD but industry-standard and expected in real BI roles.

experienceGaps: Zero professional BI experience — the single most disqualifying gap for a role that requires 1+ years in a BI function. Academic projects and certifications do not substitute.
Entire professional career (Apple, Burger 7, Handshake) is in sales, retail, and AI model training — none of which involved BI pipelines, dashboards, or data reporting.
No exposure to enterprise data environments, databases, or warehouse tools (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) commonly used in BI stacks.
No demonstrated experience working with cross-functional stakeholders to gather reporting requirements or deliver data insights.
No client-facing reporting experience, which is explicitly required for internal and external client support in this role.
Python depth is introductory — a DataCamp intro certification does not demonstrate the advanced Python required for data manipulation, automation, or analytical workflows in a BI context.
No experience in a healthcare or similarly regulated industry, where data accuracy and compliance standards are elevated.
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is a motivated career switcher with a sales and retail background who is clearly working hard to break into data — the certifications and academic projects show initiative. However, they do not meet the stated requirements for this role: no professional BI experience, no Looker experience, and unverified SQL/Python depth. Sending this profile to a hiring manager for this specific role would be a waste of everyone's time right now. In 12-18 months with the right moves, this candidate could be a legitimate contender.
improvementPlan: Get hands-on with Looker immediately — sign up for Looker's free learning path on Google Cloud Skills Boost and complete the LookML developer certification. This is non-negotiable for this specific role.

Build a BI portfolio with real pipelines: use public datasets (healthcare preferred), build end-to-end projects in SQL + Python + Looker or Power BI that demonstrate KPI dashboards, data validation, and scheduled reporting.
Deepen SQL beyond introductory level — complete Mode Analytics SQL Tutorial, practice on LeetCode SQL and StrataScratch. Be ready to write window functions, CTEs, and complex joins from memory.
Land any BI-adjacent role or internship — even a data analyst intern, reporting analyst, or analytics operations role gives you the 1+ year professional BI experience this and similar roles require.
Complete the Google Business Intelligence Specialization fully and add a BigQuery + Looker Studio capstone project to demonstrate the skill stack.
Learn dbt (data build tool) — it is now a standard expectation in BI pipelines and will differentiate you against other junior candidates.

Reframe the Apple experience on your resume to highlight data-driven decision making, inventory analysis, and metrics tracking (NPS, AppleCare conversion rates) — these are real analytics activities that can support a BI narrative.
marketSkillsToLearn: Looker / LookML
dbt (data build tool)
BigQuery or Snowflake
Advanced SQL (window functions, CTEs, query optimization)
Google Looker Studio
Apache Airflow (pipeline orchestration basics)
Python for BI (pandas, SQLAlchemy, automated reporting)
Stakeholder communication and requirements gathering
Healthcare data and HIPAA compliance basics
A/B testing and experimentation frameworks
analysis.summary: This candidate has a genuine interest in BI and has taken meaningful steps toward the field through certifications and academic projects, but their actual professional background is entirely in sales, retail, and AI model training. The critical gaps — no Looker experience, no professional BI experience, and unproven advanced SQL/Python depth — make them uncompetitive for this specific role today. With 12-18 months of focused skill-building and a junior data role to gain real-world pipeline experience, they could become a solid candidate.

Are you passionate about creating a sense of belonging, inclusive of diverse perspectives and experiences? Do you understand the value of authentic conversations in building trust and understanding? The Inclusive Business Programs (IBP) Internship is an incredible opportunity to put your skills to the test and apply your analytical and theoretical knowledge in real-life business environment.
At VSP Vision, we’re on a mission to help people see every possibility – and we do that through our strategic business pillars that work together to connect nearly 90 million patients and their health data to network doctors in a variety of settings across the globe. 

As the Inclusive Business Programs Intern, you'll have the unique opportunity to partner with business experts and support a dynamic team across the Enterprise. You’ll have the unique opportunity to gain a broad range of experience in the many elements that contribute to the daily operations of a multi-faceted enterprise and develop critical business competencies through various tasks. During this internship, you may assist with the following duties:    

Business Resource Groups (BRG) support including supporting the development, capture, and organization of BRG impact opportunities, and providing support to BRG Program Manager for programming workflows, visual summaries, and event logistics
Provide analytics and metrics support by developing and maintaining structured trackers for inclusion initiatives, BRG activities, and key milestones; building Excel‑based reports that summarize inclusion metrics and trends; supporting the creation of a flexible, scalable business intelligence dashboard to track metrics and results‑based inclusion work; and researching inclusion‑related AI tools, benchmark data, and emerging trends
Assist in developing and maintaining structured trackers for inclusion initiatives, BRG activities, and key milestones
Build Excel-based reports summarizing inclusion metrics and trends as a data source 
Support the development of a flexible, scalable business intelligence dashboard for metrics and results-based inclusion work 
Support research of inclusion‑related AI tools, benchmark data, and emerging trends. 
Build Excel-based reports summarizing inclusion metrics and trends as a data source 
Support the development of a flexible, scalable business intelligence dashboard for metrics and results-based inclusion work

Create accurate, well‑structured PowerPoint visuals used to share inclusion initiatives and team activities with enterprise stakeholders 
Maintain version control and organization of shared files, templates, and metric repositories 
Support tracking of business area partnership follow-ups, weekly team updates and deliverables 
Help with various ad-hoc business management projects as needed  
Participate in process improvements and optimize business operations  
 

Preferred Skills:      

Strong Excel skills, including pivot tables, lookups, filtering, data cleansing, and basic formula development 
Proficiency in PowerPoint for building clear, accurate visual summaries and slides 
Ability to build basic visualizations, with experience in tools like Power BI preferred, or willingness and ability to learn quickly (for dashboards and metrics visualization)

Qualities of the ideal candidate:      

Strong ability to organize information, maintain trackers, and manage multiple data sources 
Skilled in maintaining strong version control and structured file management 
Ability to follow detailed processes and help document repeatable workflows 
Clear written communication skills for drafting internal updates, summaries, and BRG-related notices 
  

Job-Related Requirements:    

  

Facilities to work remotely, including private or semi-private workspace    
Access to high-speed internet   
Technology will be provided

To win this specific role, the candidate must close the inclusion narrative gap urgently — the technical story is already there, the human and cultural story is not.

Your role in our mission
Provide administrative, technical, and operational support to the Technical Service Unit (TSU) that supports the New Jersey Medicaid Management Information System (NJMMIS). This role serves as a central assistant to the TSU team, ensuring accurate document production, dependable office systems support, and clear communications across analytical and technical staff as well as with customers. Healthcare and Medicaid domain experience is a plus.

Serve as the primary contact for the TSU team, triaging incoming requests and managing shared tracking tools.
Create and finalize documents in Word and Excel, and produce secure, well‑organized PDFs using Adobe Acrobat.
Validate documents and reports for accuracy, formatting, and completeness, ensuring all deliverables meet quality and accessibility standards.
Maintain organized shared drives and support improvements in file structure and document workflows.
Draft professional written communications and support meeting coordination and light project support.
Follow established procedures precisely and learn new tools and processes quickly to meet evolving team needs.
What we're looking for
Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel, with familiarity using Adobe Acrobat.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, along with foundational analytical and problem‑solving abilities.
Experience working with shared drives, document management systems, or ticketing tools.
High attention to detail, particularly in document quality reviews and adherence to standards.
Ability to follow documented procedures, learn quickly, and adapt to new tools and processes.
Collaborative team player with basic understanding of SDLC and familiarity with PDF accessibility best practices.
What you should expect in this role
Remote position
The deadline to submit applications for this posting is 3/31/2026.
Video cameras must be used during all interviews, as well as during the initial week of orientation.

As an EBA Technical Business Analyst in Software Engineering at Cincinnati Children’s, you will play a key role in building and enhancing innovative web-based applications that power critical operations across the organization. From supporting end-to-end testing to driving user adoption, you will be at the center of delivering technology solutions that make a real impact. You will gain hands-on exposure to Clinical and Operational software within Information Services, while developing a deep understanding of how technology drives success at every level. 

At Cincinnati Children’s, your curiosity, problem-solving mindset, and passion for continuous improvement won’t just support systems - you’ll help shape the future of how we deliver care, empower our workforce, and transform pediatric healthcare. 

 

JOB RESPONSIBILITIES 

Summary 

Has conceptual knowledge of theories, practices and procedures within their discipline. 

Follows the parameters of defined policies, procedures and practices with guidance. 

Analyze, design, support, maintain, enhance, and implement systems and solutions with low complexity that improves the business operations and employee experience of the medical center. 

Works on projects and tasks with guidance and direction from more senior level team members. 

Observe technical analysts and business systems analysts working with the end user community to learn how to develop requirements for system solutions. 

Addresses production support, application configuration and enhancements with direction from senior level team members as needed. 

Development 

Conceptually understands how to analyze, design, implement, and maintain systems and procedures. 

With guidance, assists in system testing and technical documentation. 

Observes Technical Reviews. 

Utilizes Development lifecycle process and documentation to implement and support system solutions. 

Production Support 

Provides technical support and problem resolution assistance for production and process issues. 

Seeks guidance to troubleshoot and decipher error messages. 

Assists with the appropriate Change Control methods to implement system solutions. 

Supports departmental efforts to improve customer satisfaction. 

Monitors system performance and functionality to avoid potential issues as well as gathering information for future development needs or feasibility studies. 

Customer Service 

Provides end-user support. 

Monitor SLA's and escalates as needed. 

Adhere to and promote continual adoption of change management policies and procedures. 

Model outstanding customer service behavior, including timely and effective follow-up with customers. 

Miscellaneous 

Develop knowledge and professional skills through cross-training, literature and attendance at department meetings and vendor education. 

Develop and maintain positive relationships. 

Works well with others and fosters a positive team environment. 

Participate in instructional seminars. 

Continued development on Cincinnati Children's computer-based systems. 

 

JOB QUALIFICATIONS 

Bachelor's degree in a related field OR equivalent combination of education and experience 

0+ years of experience in a related job discipline

Remote

About Us 

At Cincinnati Children’s, we come to work with one goal: to make children’s health better. We believe in a holistic team approach, both in caring for patients and their families, and in advancing science and discovery. We strive to do better and find energy and inspiration in our shared purpose. If you want to be the best you can be, you can do it at Cincinnati Children’s. 

 Cincinnati Children's is: 

Recognized by U.S. News & World Report as a top 10  best Children's Hospitals in the nation for more than 15 years

missingSkills.reason: This role sits inside IS Digital Health at a children's hospital. Zero clinical or healthcare systems exposure means the candidate will need significant ramp time understanding the environment they are supposed to support and analyze.

The JD explicitly references utilizing the development lifecycle process. The candidate shows no documented experience working within formal SDLC frameworks such as Agile, Scrum, or Waterfall in a BA or analyst capacity.
End-to-end testing is called out directly in the role summary. The candidate has zero documented experience writing test cases, executing UAT, or supporting QA processes for enterprise applications.

A core BA competency. The candidate lists business analysis as a skill but has no demonstrated experience formally eliciting, documenting, or managing business or functional requirements from stakeholders.
The JD explicitly mentions adherence to Change Control methods and change management policies. No evidence the candidate understands ITIL-style change control or formal change management frameworks.
Production support and SLA monitoring are called out responsibilities. No exposure to ITSM tools like ServiceNow, Jira Service Management, or SLA management practices is evident.

The role centers on supporting and enhancing web-based enterprise applications. The candidate built a personal finance app and a Shopify store — that is not equivalent operational enterprise application experience.
Formal technical documentation (system specs, process flows, support runbooks) is a stated responsibility. No evidence of this in the candidate's profile beyond academic project writeups.
Standard tooling for production support environments. No demonstrated familiarity.

experienceGaps: The Apple role is the candidate's most relevant experience for end-user support and customer-facing problem resolution, but it is fundamentally a retail sales position — not a technical analyst or IT support role. Framing it as enterprise B2B consulting overstates its relevance.

The Handshake AI Fellow role, while technically interesting, is AI model training and data labeling work. It has no overlap with business analysis, healthcare systems, application lifecycle support, or stakeholder collaboration in an enterprise context.
The candidate has zero demonstrated experience in a healthcare, clinical, or regulated enterprise IT environment — which is the entire operating context of this role.

No evidence of formal stakeholder management, requirements elicitation, or working within a structured analyst-to-developer workflow. Academic projects and personal ventures do not substitute for this.

The candidate's technical strength skews heavily toward data science, ML, and AI — none of which are relevant to this web app support and BA role. The skill set mismatch means relevant experience is thinner than the volume of listed skills implies.
No documented exposure to production support workflows, escalation procedures, or monitoring enterprise system performance in any professional setting.
analysis.criticalVerdict: This is a low-bar entry-level role and the candidate is qualified in the broadest sense — they have a relevant degree path, some customer service chops from Apple, and a legitimate technical foundation. However, the candidate's profile is skewed toward data science and AI, not business analysis or application support, and there is zero healthcare IT context. The Apple retail experience will resonate for the customer service components, but hiring managers in IS Digital Health will question whether this person has ever touched an enterprise system in a professional capacity. They can compete for this seat, but they should not expect to walk in as anything other than the most junior person in the room.
improvementPlan: Get hands-on with a major EHR platform: pursue Epic's free or low-cost training modules (Epic UserWeb has public content) or pursue a HL7/FHIR fundamentals course to demonstrate healthcare IT awareness.
Complete a formal SDLC or Agile/Scrum certification (CSPO, ECBA from IIBA, or even a Coursera Business Analysis certificate) to credibly claim BA competency beyond just listing it as a skill.

Build or document a real UAT/testing experience: contribute to an open-source healthcare project, volunteer for a nonprofit's system rollout, or create a documented test plan for one of your existing projects to show testing methodology.

Get familiar with a ITSM tool: set up a free ServiceNow developer instance or use Jira to manage a personal or group project and document that experience specifically in terms of ticket management and SLA tracking.

Reframe your Apple resume bullet points to emphasize technical troubleshooting, structured problem resolution, and stakeholder communication — downplay the sales narrative entirely for this application.

Shadow or informational interview with a working healthcare IT business analyst. Cincinnati Children's regularly attends community events. Use that network to understand the actual day-to-day before the interview so you can speak to it authentically.

applications.role: An entry-level technical business analyst role focused on supporting, testing, and enhancing web-based clinical and operational software applications within a pediatric healthcare environment.
applications.jobDescription:
As an EBA Technical Business Analyst in Software Engineering at Cincinnati Children’s, you will play a key role in building and enhancing innovative web-based applications that power critical operations across the organization. From supporting end-to-end testing to driving user adoption, you will be at the center of delivering technology solutions that make a real impact. You will gain hands-on exposure to Clinical and Operational software within Information Services, while developing a deep understanding of how technology drives success at every level. 

At Cincinnati Children’s, your curiosity, problem-solving mindset, and passion for continuous improvement won’t just support systems - you’ll help shape the future of how we deliver care, empower our workforce, and transform pediatric healthcare. 

 

JOB RESPONSIBILITIES 

Summary 

Has conceptual knowledge of theories, practices and procedures within their discipline. 

Follows the parameters of defined policies, procedures and practices with guidance. 

Analyze, design, support, maintain, enhance, and implement systems and solutions with low complexity that improves the business operations and employee experience of the medical center. 

Works on projects and tasks with guidance and direction from more senior level team members. 

Observe technical analysts and business systems analysts working with the end user community to learn how to develop requirements for system solutions. 

Addresses production support, application configuration and enhancements with direction from senior level team members as needed. 

Development 

Conceptually understands how to analyze, design, implement, and maintain systems and procedures. 

With guidance, assists in system testing and technical documentation. 

Observes Technical Reviews. 

Utilizes Development lifecycle process and documentation to implement and support system solutions. 

Production Support 

Provides technical support and problem resolution assistance for production and process issues. 

Seeks guidance to troubleshoot and decipher error messages. 

Assists with the appropriate Change Control methods to implement system solutions. 

Supports departmental efforts to improve customer satisfaction. 

Monitors system performance and functionality to avoid potential issues as well as gathering information for future development needs or feasibility studies. 

Customer Service 

Provides end-user support. 

Monitor SLA's and escalates as needed. 

Adhere to and promote continual adoption of change management policies and procedures. 

Model outstanding customer service behavior, including timely and effective follow-up with customers. 

Miscellaneous 

Develop knowledge and professional skills through cross-training, literature and attendance at department meetings and vendor education. 

Develop and maintain positive relationships. 

Works well with others and fosters a positive team environment. 

Participate in instructional seminars. 

Continued development on Cincinnati Children's computer-based systems. 

 

JOB QUALIFICATIONS 

Bachelor's degree in a related field OR equivalent combination of education and experience 

0+ years of experience in a related job discipline 


 

Primary Location

Remote

 

Schedule

Full time

 

Shift

Day (United States of America)

 

Department

IS Digital Health

 

Employee Status

Regular

 

FTE

1

 

Weekly Hours

40

 

*Expected Starting Pay Range

*Annualized pay may vary based on FTE status

applications.role: An internship role supporting financial systems analysis, implementation, and enhancement initiatives within Accounting and Finance organizations.
applications.jobDescription:
About Intermedia 

Are you looking for a company where YOUR VOICE is heard? Where you can MAKE A DIFFERENCE? Do you THRIVE in a FAST-PACED work environment? Do you wake every morning EXCITED to work with GREAT PEOPLE and create SUCCESS TOGETHER? Then Intermedia is the place for you.

Intermedia has established itself as a leading provider of cloud communications and collaboration tech that allows companies to connect better. We have a strong track record of growth, profitability, and creating an environment where everyone matters. Everyone. While we are fast-paced and admittedly a bit intense, we promise that you won’t be bored. You will find Intermedia is a place where you can indulge your passion for creating and supporting great cloud technology. What’s more, we always look to promote from within and have many employees who have been with us 10, 15, and 20+ years!

Culture at Intermedia is built on teamwork and transparency.  We hold each other accountable and always have each other’s back!

Are you ready to make your mark?

About The Role:

Intermedia is seeking to find our newest Business Systems Analyst Intern to join a high pace & motivated team. This ideal candidate will need to be experienced, dynamic, self-motivated & hands-on individual to help drive the strategy, implementation and enhancement of technology initiatives in support of our financial systems.  Reporting to the Business Systems Manager, this role will support Intermedia’s mission by analyzing and implementing end-to-end technologies utilized by the Accounting and Finance organizations with the goal of delivering business process efficiency and effectiveness. 

In this role you will collaborate across Intermedia functions and support the complex business processes and systems design, configuration, and architecture that revolve around our accounting and finance technology platforms.  This is a highly visible, cross functional role and requires a mix of accounting and finance process knowledge, IT orientation, strong organizational skills and excellent communication and presentation skills.  
What you will be doing:
Support efforts to support projects on financial systems roadmap
Work with Finance and IT/Product teams to review and understand business needs and recommend process improvement and financial systems solutions to address these needs. 
Work with the business users to appropriately justify the need for IT enhancements or new applications that will increase productivity, improve the financial functions and achieve the goals of the Finance department as it relates to system capabilities and cost
Champion financial digital initiatives to improve and enhance effective and efficient systems and processes.
Partner with the Accounting, Finance, and Procurement functions and the IT function to lead key improvements for the financial, procurement, and reporting processes
Planning, process mapping, configuration/development, testing, security, training and support user acceptance testing
Evaluate project complexity, assumptions, constraints and dependencies
Develop a work-plan with BA mentor to manage own and project activities, tasks, deliverables, and schedule
Develop effective communication plan to meet project and stakeholders needs  
Plan/manage requirements approval and change
Identify and communicate risks and issues that may require changes to plans or scope
What you will bring to the role:
Pursuing a Bachelor’s Degree in Administration, Finance or related fields 
Looking to build skills in defining, scoping, and managing technology initiatives from a Finance and Accounting perspective 
Interest in project management and learning more hands-on financial system implementations
Critical thinking skills to make assessments and provide solutions to problems
Deadline driven, self-motivated, and technically savvy
Excellent verbal and written interpersonal communication skills
Superior time management skills
Extremely organized with strong attention to detail
Strong Excel skills; experience using pivot tables and formulas
Netsuite experience is preferred, but not required
Working Knowledge of Microsoft Office
Experience/personal interest with creating advanced Python and Machine Learning Models is plus
Diversity, Inclusion, and Equal Opportunity
We hire, promote, and compensate employees based on their ability to perform their job responsibilities, without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, gender, marital status, national origin, ancestry, age, citizenship, physical or mental disability, sexual orientation, or any other basis protected by applicable law (collectively referred to in our Code of Conduct as “Protected Classes”). We do not tolerate employment discrimination in the workplace, and we are committed to making reasonable accommodations for identified disabilities or other limitations as required by all applicable laws. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.
About Intermedia Intelligent Communications

qaHistory.q: This section is optional. Use it to tell us a little more about yourself.
qaHistory.a:
I'm drawn to this opportunity because it sits right at the intersection of where I've been building my skills and where I want to grow professionally. While my background is heavier on the data science and technical side—Python, machine learning, SQL, building analytics systems—I've spent the past year deliberately working to understand how technology actually solves business problems, not just how to build technically impressive projects.

At Apple, I worked with business owners almost daily, helping them think through technology solutions for their operations. I wasn't configuring NetSuite, but I was having real conversations about device management systems, business financing options, and how to structure their tech infrastructure to support growth. That experience taught me how to translate between technical capabilities and business needs, which is exactly what a Business Systems Analyst does.

I've also been teaching myself the finance systems side. I built a personal financial intelligence platform called Flashconverter that processes bank statements, categorizes transactions using machine learning, and generates Excel reports with financial analysis. It's not enterprise ERP work, but it gave me hands-on experience thinking about financial data structures, reporting requirements, and how automated systems can improve accounting workflows. I understand debits and credits, I've worked with financial data pipelines, and I know how to make complex information digestible for non-technical stakeholders.

What excites me about this role is the chance to learn financial systems implementation from the inside. I know I'm missing NetSuite experience and formal finance domain knowledge—I'm a Business and Information Systems student, not an Accounting major. But I'm someone who learns quickly by doing, asks good questions, and genuinely enjoys the process of mapping business requirements to technical solutions. The job description mentions Python and machine learning as a plus, and while I know that's not the core of the role, I see it as a signal that Intermedia values people who can bring diverse technical perspectives to process optimization.

I'm not trying to fit a square peg into a round hole here. I understand this role is about supporting finance teams, understanding their workflows, and helping implement systems that make their jobs easier—not about building ML models. But I think my combination of technical depth, business exposure, and genuine interest in learning the finance domain makes me someone who could contribute meaningfully while growing into the role. I'm ready to be the person who bridges IT and Finance, and I'm genuinely excited about the opportunity to learn from a team that's been doing this work for years.

applications.role: Building and maintaining BI dashboards and reports to deliver data-driven insights that support operational and strategic decision-making across the organization.
applications.jobDescription:
Job Details
Description
Job Title: Business Intelligence Analyst

Department: Enterprise Business Intelligence

Reports to: Data Visualization Manager

Employment Classification: Regular Full Time

Job Summary:

The Business Intelligence Analyst within the Enterprise Business Intelligence team supports the development of dashboards, tabular reports, and analytical insights that enable operational and strategic thinking across the organization. This role focuses on report and dashboard creation, ad hoc analysis, and assisting with documentation of business requirements and KPIs. The analyst partners with senior team members to translate business questions into data-driven outputs while building foundational skills in SQL, visualization, and data analysis.

Essential Duties:

Build and maintain dashboards and operational reports using enterprise BI tools
Respond to ad hoc reporting requests and assist stakeholders in accessing and interpreting data
Assist in gathering and documenting business requirements, KPIs, and reporting logic
Write SQL queries to retrieve and prepare data for reporting and analysis
Perform exploratory analysis to identify trends, anomalies, and business insights
Apply visualization best practices to create clear, intuitive dashboards that support decision-making
Ensure reports follow established design standards, usability principles, and visualization best practices
Document data sources, calculations, and assumptions supporting reports and dashboards
Partner with senior BI team members to validate metrics and ensure accuracy of outputs
Communicate findings and recommendations in clear, non-technical language
Maintain and update existing reports, dashboards, and reporting datasets
Job Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Engineering, Statistics) preferred or comparable work experience 
0-2 years of experience in a reporting/analytics discipline, preferably with a business intelligence team
Foundational SQL skills including the ability to write queries to retrieve, join, and summarize data
Experience building and maintaining reports and dashboards

Analytical problem solver who has a strong comfort with ambiguity, high attention to detail, efficient time management, technical aptitude, and desire to exceed expectations

Specifically listed as preferred cloud data warehouse. Modern BI teams use Snowflake for data storage and querying. Not having this is a significant gap in their tech stack.

Role requires documenting business requirements, KPIs, and reporting logic. Candidate shows no experience in formal BI documentation practices.

No experience working within a BI team structure, collaborating with senior analysts, or validating metrics with peers.
No demonstrated experience building dashboards for business decision-making in corporate environments.
