analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is overqualified in machine learning and AI but critically underqualified for the actual BI analyst role. The Apple retail experience is irrelevant—it's B2C sales, not corporate analytics. The technical skills lean toward data science (Python, ML, NLP) rather than enterprise BI reporting (SSRS, SSMS, Snowflake, dashboard maintenance). No actual BI analyst experience exists despite the resume padding. They might transition into this role with 3-6 months of intensive SQL Server and enterprise BI tool training, but they're currently a 42/100 match.
improvementPlan: Get hands-on with SQL Server ecosystem immediately: Install SSMS, practice writing complex SQL queries (joins, aggregations, CTEs, window functions) against real databases, not just academic datasets.
Learn SSRS from scratch: Take Microsoft's official SSRS training, build 10+ operational reports (tabular reports with parameters, drill-throughs, subscriptions). This is non-negotiable for this role.
Acquire Snowflake certification or complete Snowflake's hands-on workshop. Learn cloud data warehousing concepts and how to query Snowflake using SQL.

Build a portfolio specifically for BI analyst roles: Create 3-5 enterprise-style dashboards in Tableau that answer real business questions (sales performance, operational metrics, KPI tracking). Document requirements, assumptions, and data sources.
Pivot away from ML/AI emphasis on resume—it hurts you here. Focus entirely on BI tools, SQL, dashboard design, and business reporting. Remove or minimize Python/ML projects.

applications.role: An entry-level Business Intelligence Analyst role focused on independently owning analytical projects, extracting insights from data using SQL and visualization tools, and communicating findings to stakeholders to drive business strategy.
applications.jobDescription:
Analyst, Business Intelligence
United States
Forbes is an iconic global media brand that has symbolized success for over a century. Fueled by journalism that informs and inspires, Forbes spotlights the doers and doings shaping industries, achieving success and making an impact on the world. Forbes connects and convenes the most influential communities ranging from billionaires, business leaders and rising entrepreneurs to creators and innovators. The Forbes brand reaches more than 140 million people monthly worldwide through its trusted journalism, signature ForbesLive events and 49 licensed local editions in 81 countries.

Forbes is seeking a highly motivated Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. This entry-level role is designed for professionals with 1-3 years of experience who are ready to take full ownership of analytical projects, drive strategic insights, and deliver consistently accurate work products. The successful candidate will build a foundation in technical data analysis, structured problem-solving, and proactive stakeholder partnership.

Responsibilities
The Analyst will own and execute analytical tasks, collaborating closely with senior team members and cross-functional stakeholders.

Project Ownership & Execution

Independently own and drive specific components of larger projects, ensuring alignment with project timelines agreed upon with stakeholders.
Manage assigned work end-to-end, demonstrating a solid understanding of project goals and business needs and asking clarifying questions when needed.
Proactively manage stakeholder expectations, communicating key updates and anticipating potential delays.
Analytical Rigor & Accuracy

Utilize SQL and data visualization tools to conduct data analysis and translate findings into clear, actionable business insights.
Apply rigorous and documented data validation techniques to ensure work is accurate and error-free before submission.
Clearly articulate the analysis approach and methodology, justifying decisions with relevant business context (the "why") rather than only focusing on technical implementation.
Strategic Communication & Partnership

Articulate clear and concise takeaways in verbal and written communication.
Simplify complex technical concepts and data narratives for non-technical stakeholders, fostering organizational clarity and alignment.
Provide proactive partnership by identifying opportunities to contribute data expertise or analysis, even when not explicitly assigned.
The ideal candidate
Structured Thinking: Ability to clearly articulate and logically structure thought processes, analysis plans, and conclusions.
Accountability & Reliability: Demonstrated ability to manage assignments, meet agreed-upon deadlines, and deliver complete and accurate work consistently.
Business Acumen: Capacity to quickly understand the core business problem and connect analytical findings directly to business strategy and value.
Team Collaboration: Ability to work effectively within a dynamic team environment and closely collaborate with technical and non-technical stakeholders across different departments.
Proficient in SQL (required for data extraction and manipulation).
Proficient in data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI).
Strong foundational skills in data analysis and interpretation.
Bachelor’s or master’s degree from an accredited university.
1-3 years of professional or internship experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related technical field.
The annual base salary range for this role is $85,000 - $90,000

Forbes has estimated the compensation range set forth above in good faith.  The compensation range is what we believe we will offer, and ultimately pay, a successful candidate.  In determining this range, we consider the experience, level of education (if applicable to the role), knowledge, skills, and abilities required to be had by a successful candidate as well as the budget and the company’s pay rates, generally. This said, we may have to make changes to our compensation estimates and job descriptions from time to time and we expressly reserve the right to do so.  Should we make any such changes, this advertisement will be revised to reflect such revisions.  We encourage you to occasionally re-visit this advertisement to ensure that you are abreast of any changes.  Thank you for your interest in joining Forbes!

#LI-PM1
#LI-Remote

Forbes aims to offer employees the flexibility they need in order to be successful. Some positions may require candidates to be based in a specific location for consideration while some roles may be fully remote (within the U.S.) if it aligns with the needs of the position. This position is only open to candidates residing in California, Colorado, Connecticut, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, & Washington. Due to business operations and compliance requirements, we are unable to consider applicants based outside these states at this time.

Forbes is an equal opportunity employer.

qaHistory.q: Please provide three examples of technical deliverables from your school or work experience that showcase your skills.*
qaHistory.a:
# Technical Deliverable 1: Flashconverter Financial Intelligence Platform

I built a full-stack financial analytics platform called Flashconverter that automatically converts bank statement PDFs into actionable insights using machine learning. The entire system runs client-side for complete privacy, and I coded it from scratch using JavaScript, SheetJS for data parsing, and Chart.js for visualization. The most technically challenging part was developing the ML categorization algorithm—it analyzes merchant names using string similarity matching and learns spending patterns over time through IndexedDB storage, achieving 70-95% confidence scores on transaction categorization. The platform processes data through a multi-stage pipeline: PDF conversion via API, Excel parsing and cleaning with ExcelJS, ML-powered categorization with user-verified pattern storage, and finally generating interactive dashboards with income vs. expense tracking across 19 smart categories. I also built an AI-powered savings optimizer that detects recurring subscriptions and provides personalized financial recommendations. This project directly demonstrates the end-to-end analytical workflow Forbes is looking for—data extraction, transformation, insight generation, and visualization—all while solving a real business problem (my own financial planning for homeownership).

# Technical Deliverable 2: Multi-Cloud Architecture with AWS & Azure

For my MIS 341 final project, I designed and deployed a secure hybrid cloud infrastructure connecting AWS and Azure environments. I architected an AWS VPC with a Site-to-Site VPN using Customer Gateways and Virtual Private Gateways configured with IKEv1 protocols to simulate enterprise connectivity. The security implementation was layered—I used Network ACLs for subnet-level filtering and Security Groups for instance-level access control, specifically configuring TCP protocols for RDP and SSH access. On the storage side, I provisioned AWS S3 buckets with custom JSON-based access policies to manage object visibility and set up Azure Blob Storage with file versioning through snapshots. The most valuable learning came from troubleshooting access denials by adjusting IAM policies and verifying network traffic flows through route table configurations. This project taught me how to work with cloud infrastructure at scale, manage complex permission systems, and validate data pipelines across different platforms—skills that directly translate to managing data warehouses and ETL processes in a BI role.

# Technical Deliverable 3: PrimeStop Retail Strategy Analysis in R

I led a complete predictive analytics project using R and the tidymodels framework to solve a real business problem for PrimeStop Convenience stores—converting low-margin fuel customers into high-margin convenience shoppers. I started with exploratory data analysis to evaluate customer demographics, transactional patterns, and store amenities across their Mid-Atlantic locations. Then I built and compared three classification models (Logistic Regression, Linear Discriminant Analysis, and a hyperparameter-tuned Random Forest) using 5-fold cross-validation to predict customer conversion. I also developed a linear regression model to forecast visit volume for engaged customers. The Random Forest achieved the highest F1-score, and my Variable Importance Plot analysis revealed that loyalty program enrollment and fuel visit frequency were the strongest conversion predictors. I translated these technical findings into an executive-level report with specific business recommendations—restructure the loyalty acquisition funnel and target high-frequency fuel visitors with cross-sell incentives. This project showcases exactly what Forbes needs: SQL-equivalent data manipulation in R, rigorous statistical analysis, and the ability to communicate complex findings to non-technical stakeholders in a way that drives business strategy.

applications.role: Support the Business Intelligence team by conducting research, performing data analysis, creating documentation, and contributing to analytical projects in a healthcare technology environment.

Reveleer is a healthcare data and analytics company that uses Artificial Intelligence to give health plans across all business lines greater control over their Quality Improvement, Risk Adjustment, and Member Management programs. With one transformative solution, the Reveleer platform enables plans to independently execute and manage every aspect of enrollment, provider outreach and data retrieval, coding, abstraction, reporting, and submissions. Leveraging proprietary technology, robust data sets, and subject matter expertise, Reveleer provides complete record retrieval and review services so health plans can confidently plan and execute risk, quality, and member management programs to deliver more value and improved outcomes.

Role Overview: The Business Intelligence (BI) Intern will support the team by conducting research, performing data analysis, creating documentation, and contributing to ongoing projects. This internship is ideal for students interested in analytics, technology, and problem‑solving within a collaborative business environment.

Support business intelligence and cross‑functional projects through research and data analysis
Assist in the development of reports, dashboards, and data visualizations

Reveleer is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status or genetic information, in compliance with applicable federal, state and local law.

qaHistory.q: Experience Summary
qaHistory.a:
I've built hands-on experience with data analysis, BI tools, and documentation through a mix of technical projects and professional work that's taught me how to translate messy data into actionable insights.

At Apple, I spent three years supporting both retail customers and business clients, which required me to constantly analyze inventory data, track NPS scores, and identify patterns in customer purchasing behavior. I'd regularly pull sales metrics to understand which products were moving and why, then document those findings to share with our business team. When I noticed several customers mentioning they owned businesses, I created a tracking system to capture those leads and pass detailed notes to our B2B specialists—basically building a mini CRM process that increased our business referrals. That role taught me how to work cross-functionally and communicate technical information to non-technical stakeholders, which I know is critical in BI.

On the technical side, I've worked extensively with Python, SQL, R, Excel, Tableau, and Power BI across multiple analytics projects. For my MIS 431 Retail Strategy project, I built a complete machine learning pipeline in R using tidymodels to predict customer conversion for a convenience store chain. I handled the full workflow—data cleaning, exploratory analysis, feature engineering with Yeo-Johnson transformations, training three classification models (Logistic Regression, LDA, and Random Forest), and evaluating performance with cross-validation. I documented every step thoroughly and translated the technical results into executive-level recommendations about loyalty programs and marketing spend. That project showed me how to take raw data, apply statistical rigor, and deliver insights that directly impact business decisions.

I also built Flashconverter, a financial intelligence platform that processes bank statement PDFs and converts them into structured Excel reports with automated categorization and data visualizations using Chart.js. The entire data pipeline—PDF parsing with SheetJS, transformation with ExcelJS, and a custom ML algorithm for transaction categorization—runs client-side with no server dependency. I documented the full technical architecture and designed it so the output is immediately usable for financial planning. That project taught me how to think about end-to-end deliverables that remain useful beyond the initial build, which aligns directly with what this internship is asking for.

More recently, I've been working as a Generative AI Fellow at Handshake, where I evaluate AI model outputs, provide detailed quality feedback, and document prompt engineering strategies. It's taught me how to assess technical accuracy systematically and communicate findings clearly to improve model performance—skills that translate directly to validating data and documenting analytical workflows in a BI environment.

I'm genuinely excited about healthcare technology because I see how much impact good data infrastructure can have on patient outcomes and operational efficiency. I know I'll need to learn your specific tools and healthcare domain context, but I'm confident in my ability to pick up new systems quickly, work collaboratively with stakeholders, and deliver high-quality documentation and analysis. I'm looking for an internship where I can contribute real value while learning from experienced BI professionals, and this role feels like the right fit.

applications.role: Track, manage, and maintain highly sensitive security information and processes in support of NSA personnel security clearance operations and counterintelligence missions.
applications.jobDescription:
Responsibilities
Security and Counterintelligence is responsible for the protection of NSA/CSS information, people and facilities worldwide. In support of this mission, Security Information Specialists are integrated into the personnel, physical, and counterintelligence security disciplines to ensure highly sensitive information utilized in all security processes are protected and handled in accordance with all applicable laws and policies. Interfacing with NSA and other agencies, these specialists are the backbone of and the customer service representatives for the Security and Counterintelligence team. Security Information Specialists work in a myriad of offices. The responsibilities of a Security Information Specialist include, but are not limited to:

- Track/manage highly sensitive security processes
- Maintain highly sensitive security information, disclosed only to authorized recipients, in appropriate formats, and within constraints of relevant laws (e.g., the Privacy Act and FOIA).
- Prepare accurate and complete reports that document processes, outcomes, and findings
- Provide basic security analysis and risk recommendations to decision makers and customers
- Create products based on analysis of security and/or counterintelligence data

Job Summary
Security and Counterintelligence is responsible for the protection of NSA/CSS information, people, and facilities worldwide. Security Information Specialists support the Security and Counterintelligence mission primarily through the Office of Personnel Security by tracking, managing and maintaining sensitive security information. Security Information Specialists provide an array of support, to include creating and maintaining security records, updating security systems of record, applying Intelligence Community and NSA/CSS policies for vetting security clearances or physical access, evaluating security clearance requests to ensure affiliates' actions are consistent with adjudicative criteria, and completing other administrative tasks associated with the processing of security clearances. Through this support the Security Information Specialist will learn the intricate details of each process which will enable career growth throughout Security and Counterintelligence and beyond.

Qualifications
The qualifications listed are the minimum acceptable to be considered for the position.

ENTRY/DEVELOPMENTAL
Entry is with a high school diploma or GED and no experience.

Competencies
The ideal candidate has a desire for continual learning with excellent problem-solving and interpersonal skills as well as:

- Strong written and oral communication skills
- Able to work as an individual and in a team environment
- Well-organized and able to handle multiple assignments
- Focused on customer needs
- Knowledge of basic office tools and functions
- Experience using Microsoft Products (Word, Excel, Outlook)
- Able to lift 20 lbs.

Pay, Benefits, & Work Schedule
Pay: Salary offers are based on candidates' education level and years of experience relevant to the position and also take into account information provided by the hiring manager/organization regarding the work level for the position.

Salary Range: $35,749 - $75,059 (Entry/Developmental)
Salary range varies by location, work level, and relevant experience to the position.

Training will be provided based on the selectee's needs and experience.

Benefits: NSA offers a comprehensive benefits package.

Work Schedule: This is a full-time position, Monday - Friday, with basic 8hr/day work requirement between 6:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m. (flexible).

DCIPS Trial Period:
If selected for this position, you will be required to serve a two-year DCIPS trial period, unless you are a veterans' preference-eligible employee, in which case you are required to serve a one-year trial period. This trial period runs concurrently with your commitment to the position, if applicable. Before finalizing your appointment at the conclusion of your trial period, NSA will determine whether your continued employment advances the public interest. This decision will be based on factors such as your performance and conduct; the Agency's needs and interests; whether your continued employment would advance the Agency's organizational goals; and whether your continued employment would advance the efficiency of the Federal service.

Upon completion of your trial period, your employment will be terminated unless you receive certification, in writing, that your continued employment advances the public interest.

If you do not receive certification for continued employment, you should receive written notice prior to the end of your trial period that your employment will be terminated and the effective date of such termination.

How to apply
Apply soonest, as job postings can close earlier than stated end dates due to changes in requirements. It is important to review and note the minimum qualifications, as only those applicants who meet the required qualifications will be contacted to continue the employment process. Please populate the resume tool to showcase any relevant work experience and education related to the position and answer any applicable screening questions. Information collected will be used to determine eligibility, and failure to provide accurate information may result in disqualification for this position.

A confirmation email will be sent after submission of the first application and also after any future updates to submitted applications. **Due to time sensitive communications regarding applications, please ensure your spam filters are configured to accept email from noreply@intelligencecareers.gov. For job vacancies that include stated testing requirements, also include the following: @uwe.nsa.gov, @nsa.gov, and @pearson.com**

U.S. Citizenship is required for all applicants. NSA is an equal opportunity employer and abides by applicable employment laws and regulations. All applicants and employees are subject to random drug testing in accordance with Executive Order 12564. Employment is contingent upon successful completion of a security background investigation and polygraph. Reasonable accommodations may be provided to applicants with disabilities during the application and hiring process where appropriate.


The following four narrative questions provide an opportunity for you to highlight your dedication to public service for the hiring manager and agency leadership (or designee(s)). While your responses are not required and will not be scored, we encourage you to thoughtfully address each question. Please provide a response of 200 words or less to each question. You will be asked to certify that you are using your own words and did not use a consultant or artificial intelligence (AI) such as a large language model (LLM) like ChatGPT or Copilot. (Please attach in application)

1. How has your commitment to the Constitution and the founding principles of the United States inspired you to pursue this role within the Federal government? Provide a concrete example from professional, academic, or personal experience.

2. In this role, how would you use your skills and experience to improve government efficiency and effectiveness? Provide specific examples where you improved processes, reduced costs, or improved outcomes.

3. How would you help advance the President's Executive Orders and policy priorities in this role? Identify one or two relevant Executive Orders or policy initiatives that are significant to you, and explain how you would help implement them if hired.

4. How has a strong work ethic contributed to your professional, academic or personal achievements? Provide one or two specific examples, and explain how those qualities would enable you to serve effectively in this position.

DCIPS Disclaimer
**VETERANS AND TRANSITIONING SERVICE MEMBERS**

Thank you for your service! The National Security Agency (NSA) is part of the Defense Civilian Intelligence Personnel System (DCIPS). All positions at NSA are in the Excepted Service under Title 10, United States Codes (U.S.C.), Section 1601 appointment authority.

Veterans' Preference
In accordance with the procedures provided in DoD Instruction 1400.25, Volume 2005, "DoD Civilian Personnel Management System: Defense Civilian Intelligence Personnel System Employment and Placement," NSA applies veterans' preference, as defined by Section 2108 of Title 5, U.S.C., to eligible candidates. If you are claiming veterans' preference, you are required to provide acceptable documentation of your preference eligibility upon application.

Acceptable documentation includes:
- DD-214: "Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty," which shows dates of service and discharge under honorable conditions (Copy 4); OR

- Certification of Service:
A written document on letterhead from the appropriate branch of the armed forces that certifies the service member is expected to be discharged or released from active duty service in the armed forces under honorable conditions not later than 120 days after the date the certification is signed. The certification should include the military service dates, including the expected discharge or release date; AND

- Standard Form 15 (SF-15)
Application for 10-point Veteran Preference (http://www.opm.gov/forms/pdf_fill/sf15.pdf).

If you are claiming a service-connected disability of 30 percent or more, the documentation you provide must specifically demonstrate this level of disability; AND
- VA Letter of Disability (for 10pt and Sole Survivorship rating)

You may obtain a letter from the Department of Veterans Affairs reflecting your level of disability for preference eligibility by visiting a VA Regional Office, contacting a VA call center, or online (https://www.ebenefits.va.gov/).

**Failure to provide sufficient documentation of veterans' preference eligibility may preclude NSA from identifying you as a preference eligible candidate during the hiring selection process.

applications.role: Supporting mergers and acquisitions activities from pre-deal research through post-merger integration, including due diligence coordination, documentation, and cross-functional project management.
applications.jobDescription:
Tremco Construction Products Group (CPG), part of RPM International Inc., unites industry-leading building envelope solutions manufacturers, service providers and trusted brands—including Tremco, Nudura, Dryvit, Willseal, Weatherproofing Technologies, Inc. and Weatherproofing Technologies Canada—to deliver comprehensive, integrated building systems. With operations spanning North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, India and Latin America, Tremco CPG has a global presence supported by regional businesses and distributors who understand local market needs for commercial, industrial and residential construction and restoration projects.

GENERAL PURPOSE OF THE JOB:

The Business Development/Project Management Intern will support all phases of a deal, from initial research to post-merger integration - balancing the financial, strategic, and operational aspects of mergers and acquisitions to keep the process organized and on track. 

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Other duties may be assigned.

Pre-merger and due diligence:

Conduct market and industry research - compile market, competitor, and industry specific research to help assess potential acquisition targets and identify new opportunities for growth.
Maintain pipeline documentation - help track potential deals by documenting and updating the pipeline of prospective acquisition targets.
Support target evaluation - assist in creating initial investment recommendations by analyzing potential target companies or initiatives.
Process non-disclosure agreements - help with the administrative tasks of new M&A projects.
Coordinate due diligence efforts - manage the process of gathering and reviewing key documents from the target company.
Deal execution and Communication Tasks:

Prepare presentations and reports - summarize research and analysis into presentation materials for internal and external stakeholders, including leadership and management.
Document business process - assist in updating and reorganizing internal documentation related to M&A processes, methods, and templates.
Update project plans and timelines - Track project milestones, manage deliverable timelines, and update project plans to keep the team aligned on progress.
Facilitate team meetings - coordinate meetings with cross-functional teams, prepare agendas and meeting notes, and ensure key takeaways and next steps are communicated to project leads.
Support project execution - collaborate with different departments to help coordinate the deal process.
Post-merger integration (PMI) tasks

Support integration efforts - work closely with the integration team to coordinate the transition of the new business, which involves coordinating with cross-functional teams on operational alignment.
Track integration progress - Assis in monitoring progress against integration milestones, identifying potential delays, and communicating status to relevant team members.
Assist with process improvement - conduct assessments of existing processes to identify areas for improvement following a merger or acquisition.
Analyze integration metrics - evaluate project performance metrics by analyzing data on timelines and results to inform “lessons learned” reviews.
EDUCATION REQUIREMENT:

Currently enrolled or recently graduated with a bachelors degree in business management, marketing, or other related field.

EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENT:

Minimum of one year business experience preferably with project management responsibility.

OTHER SKILLS, ABILITIES, AND QUALIFICATIONS:

Strong analytical and market research skills.
Organized, with the ability to adapt quickly to changing priorities and prioritize tasks.
Excellent listening, communication, and presentation skills.
Project coordination skills.
Business acumen  
PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Incumbent must be able to stand, walk, sit, use hands, reach, talk, and hear.

BENEFITS AND COMPENSATION:

This is a part-time position requiring around 20 hours of work per week, which could increase to 40 hours depending upon project timelines. 

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

applications.role: Generate qualified leads and pipeline opportunities for an electronics supply chain software platform in the North American market through multi-channel outreach and cold calling.
applications.jobDescription:
🚀 Your opportunity
Luminovo is in the middle of a global expansion, bringing our European success story to North America. This is your chance to be one of the pioneers and join our team in North America!

Are you a high-energy business developer driven by curiosity and ambition? Do you thrive on building something new and breaking into fresh markets?

Take ownership of the very top of the funnel for a product poised to transform a trillion-dollar electronics supply chain.

Join our team as Business Development Representative in NYC office (hybrid) and turn cold prospects into warm, qualified opportunities that fuel our growth.

Performance objectives
We don't need you to be an expert in the electronics industry today. But you have to be willing to dive into it to understand our potential customers' unique situations and how our software tools can create value for them.

📣 Lead generation

Drive multichannel outreach. Use a combination of email, phone, LinkedIn, events, and partner networks to connect with technical buyers in the electronics industry.

Leverage modern tooling. Work with state-of-the-art tools like Apollo, Lusha, Clay, and LinkedIn to create and convert lead lists efficiently.

Personalize at scale. Tailor outreach based on buyer signals and account context. Execute proven sequences and improve them through experimentation and feedback.

🤩 Pipeline generation

Qualify ICP Prospects. Lead thoughtful cold call sequences to meet new prospects, and uncover which prospects fit into our ideal customer profile.

Pass with precision. Collaborate closely with Account Executives to ensure smooth handover of high-quality opportunities, complete with crisp notes, relevant context, and suggested next steps.

Nurture long-term plays. Keep lower-priority accounts in motion with thoughtful follow-ups and signal-based reactivation.

📈 Ownership

Track metrics proactively. Stay on top of your metrics and the leading indicators needed to achieve your goals.

Feed the loop. Bring your frontline insights back to product, marketing, and sales leadership to help refine positioning, messaging, and ideal customer profiles.

👤 Character

You are a self-motivated hunter and able to shake off rejection easily.

You are a team player – motivated by the success and drive of your teammates, and eager to see them win!

You want to grow into an Account Executive role, and willing to demonstrate a strong work ethic and develop industry expertise to get there.

🤓 Whom you’ll be working with
Jack, our Account Executive for Canada and Western US

Matt, our Sales Lead for North America

Sam, our Business & Operations Lead for North America

Our Sales and Product Team to fully understand our product roadmap, potential constraints, and share product-related customer feedback

⚡️ Skills, knowledge, and expertise
Just so you know – we purposefully did not create a standard list of minimum qualifications for this role. We care much more about your motivation and ability to help us accelerate technical progress than we care just about your CV. If you're interested to find out more about why we banned requirements from our performance profiles – see here.

⚡️ About Luminovo
Luminovo is the first electronics supply chain platform that unites all data, processes, and stakeholders in the trillion-dollar electronics industry.

We fundamentally believe that technology is the best tool that humankind has developed to tackle the biggest challenges we face as a society today. And we want to do our share in accelerating technological progress.

We bring innovations to life faster and cheaper by creating a more connected and resilient electronics supply chain.

To date, over 300 active customers worldwide are working with us towards this shared goal. Supported by leading investors such as Cherry Ventures, General Catalyst/LaFamiglia, Chalfen Ventures, and others, we have raised more than €20 million.

Our core principles: putting people first & building great things

As a remote-first company headquartered in Munich, our brilliant team is spread across cities including Munich, New York, Austin, Calgary, Berlin, London, Valencia, Nairobi, and Verona, among others. 🌍

Our team includes a mix of product enthusiasts, people advocates, business masterminds, and engineering experts. Among us are graduates from institutions like Stanford University and the University of Cambridge, as well as talented individuals with unconventional CVs. What matters most is their drive to build great things.

We provide an authentic environment based on psychological safety to empower Luminerds from various backgrounds to succeed, grow, and focus on impact. Putting people first means that we deeply care about who our employees are, what makes them unique, and what they excel at.

Behavioral red flags (tardiness, insubordination) compound the skill gaps. This is a 22/100 - not remotely qualified for this position and needs 6-12 months in a structured BDR training program elsewhere before being competitive.

qaHistory.q: Personal Summary
qaHistory.a:
I'm a technical professional who thrives at the intersection of business strategy and hands-on problem-solving, and I see B2B sales as the perfect arena to combine these strengths. My background in AI training and business intelligence has taught me how to quickly master complex technical products—whether I'm evaluating computer vision models or building data pipelines—and translate that knowledge into clear value propositions for different audiences. At Apple, I learned that the best sales conversations start with genuine curiosity about what someone's actually trying to accomplish, not leading with a pitch. I'd spend time understanding a business owner's workflow challenges before introducing MDM solutions or financing options, and that consultative mindset drove my success in converting retail interactions into enterprise partnerships.

What excites me about this BDR role is the chance to build something from scratch in a new market. I'm energized by the idea of being one of the first people to introduce North American manufacturers to Luminovo's platform, knowing that every cold call is helping establish a foothold in a trillion-dollar industry. I'm naturally resilient—working in fast-paced retail taught me to shake off rejection and keep moving forward with the same enthusiasm. I'm also deeply motivated by learning, which is why I'm genuinely interested in diving into the electronics supply chain space even though I'm not an expert today. I want to understand how contract manufacturers think about quoting, sourcing, and inventory so I can have meaningful conversations that uncover real pain points.

I see this role as the foundation for a long-term career in tech sales. I'm willing to put in the work—high call volume, detailed note-taking, continuous learning about the industry—to prove I can generate quality pipeline and eventually step into an Account Executive position. I'm a team player who genuinely wants to see my colleagues win, and I bring a technical learning aptitude that will help me get up to speed on your product faster than most. I'm ready to be a hunter, embrace the grind, and help Luminovo replicate its European success story in North America.

applications.role: Business Analyst Intern to support EverHealth operations through data analysis, process improvement, documentation, and cross-functional collaboration during a 10-week summer internship.
applications.jobDescription:
locations
Remote- US
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted 6 Days Ago
job requisition id
R-105948
At EverCommerce [Nasdaq: EVCM], we are on a mission to digitally transform the service economy with tailored, end-to-end SaaS solutions that simplify and empower the lives of our 725,000+ customers.  As a leading service commerce platform, our modern digital and mobile applications create predictable, informed, and convenient experiences between customers and their service professionals in the areas of Home & Field Services, Health Services, and Wellness industries.

We are building an extraordinary company and looking for talented, energetic, and motivated people to join our team. You can learn more about our Company, Culture and Values here: https://careers.evercommerce.com/us/en 

We are currently seeking rising junior and senior students to apply for our internship program. This is a great opportunity for individuals looking to gain hands-on experience and contribute to meaningful projects.

We are looking for a Business Analyst Intern to join our EverHealth Business Operations team. This role offers an exciting opportunity to collaborate closely with cross-functional partners to support the company-wide intern program, improve reporting and operational processes, create documentation, and participate in ad hoc analytics and process improvement projects.

Responsibilities:

Collaborate with EverHealth cross-functional teams (PMO, Marketing, Sales, Onboarding & Implementation, Customer Support, Customer Success, Product, Data Analytics) to document operational inefficiencies and support process improvement initiatives.
Support the development and upkeep of internal reporting and dashboards (Excel, Salesforce reports, and/or BI tools) to improve data quality and business visibility.
Gather and define requirements for small initiatives by drafting user stories, process flows (BPMs), and simple roadmaps in partnership with functional owners.
Champion AI-enabled analysis and automation by identifying high-impact use cases, piloting lightweight solutions, and scaling what works across teams.
Track key metrics and project status; synthesize updates into clear, data-driven slides and written summaries for stakeholders.
Support the EverHealth operating cadence by coordinating meetings, agendas, notes, and action items, and following up to drive progress.
Conduct structured research and analysis (e.g., workflow mapping, KPI definitions, cost and time drivers) and share recommendations with the team.
Contribute to ad hoc operational projects that improve productivity, customer experience, and revenue outcomes.
Qualifications:

Working knowledge of Excel (pivot tables, basic formulas) and comfort working with data.
Exposure to or interest in SQL and BI/visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) is a plus.
Interest in healthcare operations and B2B SaaS, with a desire to learn how teams use metrics to drive decisions.
High level of attention to detail/accuracy.
Exercise discretion and good judgment and maintain confidentiality when handling sensitive information.
Positive attitude approaching any task no matter how big/small.
Approaches projects with creativity and is willing to think outside of the box.
Effective communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to present complex information in a clear and concise manner.
Proactive, self-motivated, and able to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
Willing to travel for Denver, Colorado for the intern kickoff week and possibly the Intern executive presentations.
What we are looking for:

Rising juniors and seniors pursuing a bachelor’s degree in business, analytics, healthcare management, computer science, or a related field.
Ability to work full-time from June to August 2026 (10-week internship) and authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.
Must be willing to commit to a 10-week internship in the Summer of 2026 (June-August) on a full-time basis.
Enthusiastic about technology and committed to professional development.
Good written and verbal communication skills.
Willing to learn and collaborate with your teammates.
Where:

The EverCommerce team is distributed globally, with teams in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Jordan, New Zealand, and Australia. With a widely distributed team, we are used to working remotely across different time zones. This role can be based anywhere in the United States – if you’re close to one of our offices, we can set you up in-office or you can work 100% remotely. Please note that you must be eligible to work without sponsorship to qualify for this position.

Compensation:

The target base compensation for this position is $20.00 USD per hour. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including location, local market variances, and candidate experience and expertise, and may vary from the amounts listed above.

EverCommerce is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We look forward to reviewing your credentials and getting to know more about your experience!

qaHistory.q: What is your definition of success in this role?*
qaHistory.a:
Success in this role means three things to me: delivering work that genuinely helps the team make better decisions, learning how B2B SaaS operations actually function, and leaving behind documentation that's useful long after the internship ends.

On the deliverable side, I'd measure success by whether the dashboards and reports I build actually get used. At Apple, I learned that the best metrics are the ones people check without being asked — when my business team started referencing device management insights I'd pulled to close deals, that told me the analysis mattered. For this role, I'd want my Excel models or Salesforce reports to be clear and accurate enough that the PMO or Customer Success teams pull them up in their weekly meetings without needing to ask me how to read them.

The learning piece is huge for me. I've worked with data in academic projects like my Retail Strategy analysis using R and tidymodels, but I haven't seen how operational teams use metrics day-to-day in a real SaaS environment. Success means I walk away understanding how EverHealth's cross-functional teams actually coordinate — what KPIs drive decisions, how process improvements get prioritized, and where data quality breaks down. I want to be able to explain to someone how a healthcare SaaS company runs, not just in theory but from having been in the meetings and seen the workflows.

Finally, I'd define success by what I leave behind. My Flashconverter project taught me that documentation either saves people hours or it collects dust — there's no middle ground. If I can create process flows, user stories, or even just clean meeting notes that the next intern or new hire can actually follow, that's a win. I think about the technical documentation I had to write at Apple when setting up business clients on MDM — if it wasn't crystal clear, the client wouldn't be able to maintain their own system. That same standard applies here.

Ultimately, success is showing up every day ready to dig into messy data, ask good questions, and make things a little more efficient than they were before — and doing it in a way that respects everyone's time, including my own.

qaHistory.q: List out at least two of our company values and describe how you would incorporate these into future positions.*
qaHistory.a:
Based on EverCommerce's stated company values, I would focus on two that really align with how I've approached my work: **Empowering Customer Success** and **Collaboration Across Teams**.

At Apple, I saw firsthand how empowering customers drove everything we did. When I worked with business owners, I didn't just hand them devices—I spent time understanding their actual workflow problems. There was one time I helped a small business owner set up device management for his team, and instead of pushing the most expensive solution, I walked him through what would actually solve his specific security concerns. That experience taught me that real empowerment comes from listening first, then building solutions around what people actually need. In a Business Analyst role at EverHealth, I'd bring that same mindset—whether I'm documenting operational processes or building dashboards, I want to make sure the end result actually helps the teams using it, not just checks a box.

The collaboration piece is something I've had to learn through experience. When I built Flashconverter, I was working across different parts of the problem—frontend interface, backend data processing, user needs—and I realized pretty quickly that no single part works well if you're not thinking about how everything connects. At Apple, working with the genius bar and business teams meant I had to translate technical issues into plain language and vice versa. For this internship, I know I'll be working with PMO, Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and Product teams, and I'd approach that the same way: ask questions when I don't understand something, keep communication clear, and make sure whatever analysis or documentation I'm creating actually serves the people who need to use it. I think those values—genuinely helping people succeed and working well across different functions—are what make operations run smoothly, and that's what I want to contribute this summer.

applications.role: Support the Product Management team in researching and analyzing regional requirements for Dining solutions across domestic and international markets, with focus on nutritional standards, language support, and regulatory compliance.
applications.jobDescription:
Job Type
Internship
Description
365 Retail Markets is the most trusted global provider of unattended retail technology, delivering conveniently smart self-service solutions since 2008. The company’s all-in-one platform powers retail spaces across food service, global retail, and hospitality with a comprehensive suite of frictionless smart stores, micro markets, vending, catering, and dining point-of-sale options. These technologies can be found worldwide in corporate offices, manufacturing and distribution facilities, educational campuses, hotels, and beyond.



As a nine-time honoree on the Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing companies in the United States, and with a continually expanding global presence, 365 Retail Markets is committed to growth, innovation, and providing superior, integrated technology that meets the evolving needs of its customers and consumers.

  

We are seeking a Business Analyst Intern to support the Product Management team within our Dining solutions. This role will contribute to a Localization Initiative focused on nutritional standards, language support, and regional regulatory requirements across domestic and international markets.



The intern will assist product managers in researching, analyzing, and documenting regional requirements that impact product functionality. This role is ideal for students interested in product management, market research, regulatory analysis, and global product strategy. The intern will support research and analysis to ensure Dining platform capabilities align with local market requirements across multiple regions. This will include Nutritional, Language, and Regional requirements.



Responsibilities

Assist in reviewing existing product functionality to evaluate support for regional regulations and operational requirements across cities, states, and countries.
Analyze nutritional labeling compliance requirements across domestic and international markets.
Evaluate end-to-end multi-language support within Dining solutions and identify potential gaps.
Conduct research on regional tax regulations, food labeling standards, and dining platform compliance requirements.
Document findings and support the creation of product requirement insights and recommendations.
Collaborate with product managers and cross-functional stakeholders to gather information and validate research findings.
Prepare clear summaries and presentations of research insights for internal product discussions.
  

Learning Outcomes

Through this internship, participants will gain:
Exposure to product development lifecycle and the role research plays in product decisions.
Understanding of the complexities of managing interconnected product requirements across global markets.
Experience working with multiple stakeholders across product, regulatory, and operational teams.
Insight into strategic decision-making informed by data and research.
Skills in communicating research findings clearly and effectively through presentations and documentation.
Requirements
Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Information Systems or Nutrition science or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to evaluate information and identify patterns.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to organize research and present insights clearly.
Demonstrated curiosity and willingness to learn new tools, systems, and concepts.

applications.role: A business-facing analyst role supporting sales, marketing, and go-to-market initiatives by analyzing performance data, identifying trends, and translating insights into strategic recommendations for leadership.
applications.jobDescription:
Business Strategy Analyst
Remote, United States
Want to be a bswifter? 

At bswift we’ve been transforming benefits administration since 1996, making it simpler, smarter, and more human. Our state-of-the-art, cloud-based technology and services empower employees to understand, manage, and love their benefits. From downtown Chicago, and remotely across the country, we serve thousands of companies and millions of people nationwide, reducing administrative burdens and freeing HR teams to focus on creating thriving, people-first workplaces. 

 We’re looking for motivated and goal-driven individuals who share our passion for delivering excellence and creating solutions that make a difference. The reward is a fun, flexible and creative environment with ample opportunity for professional and personal growth. If you love the bswift values of pursue excellence, embrace accountability, deliver superior service, and be a great place to work, we want to hear from you! 

ABOUT US:
bswift is a leading benefits administration company that specializes in providing tailored solutions for our clients. Our mission is to simplify the complex world of employee benefits and deliver exceptional service to our clients. We are looking for a strategic and analytical individual to join our team as a Business Strategy Analyst.

WHAT YOU’LL DO
The Business Strategy Analyst will serve as a business-facing analyst supporting sales, marketing, and go-to-market initiatives. This role focuses on analyzing performance, identifying trends, and translating complex data into clear insights and recommendations for leaders. The ideal candidate brings strong analytical thinking, business acumen, and the ability to independently pull and synthesize data.

WHAT YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR (Essential Functions):
_Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions._
• Analyze sales, marketing, and go-to-market performance to identify trends and opportunities
• Evaluate marketing funnel effectiveness, campaign outcomes, and win/loss results
• Independently source, analyze, and synthesize data from Salesforce, Excel, SQL-based sources, and external market data
• Translate data into clear insights, stories, and recommendations for business leaders
• Support strategic initiatives such as campaign measurement, market research, and growth analysis
• Partner closely with sales, marketing, and sales operations teams to support decision-making
• Deliver ad-hoc analysis to leadership as new business questions arise

WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED (Required Education and Experience):
• Bachelor’s degree in Business, Analytics, Marketing, Economics, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
• Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
• Advanced Excel skills for analysis and modeling
• Experience independently pulling and analyzing data from multiple sources
• Ability to clearly communicate insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

NICE TO HAVE (Preferred Education and Experience):
• Background in marketing analytics, sales analytics, or business analysis
• Experience supporting sales or go-to-market teams
• Familiarity with Salesforce or similar CRM platforms
• Exposure to BI or visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau
• Experience in benefits administration, HR technology, or SaaS environments

OTHER DUTIES
Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

 

 

Why Join bswift?

At bswift, we empower our employees to make a meaningful impact, innovate, and grow. Joining our team means stepping into a collaborative and dynamic environment that values creativity, initiative, and a passion for client success. We are dedicated to fostering an inclusive workplace that celebrates diversity and values each team member’s unique contributions.

Benefits of Working at bswift:

Comprehensive Health Benefits: Access to health, dental, and vision plans to support your wellness and that of your family.
Competitive Compensation: A compensation package that recognizes your skills, experience, and contributions, including performance-based incentives for most roles.
Remote first, Office friendly environment! No time to commute?  No problem!
Retirement Savings Plans: Options to help you plan for a secure financial future with employer-sponsored retirement savings programs.
Professional Development: Opportunities for career growth, including training and access to resources to support your career progression.
Supportive Culture: A work environment that encourages collaboration, open communication, and creative problem-solving, where your voice and ideas are valued.
Employee Wellbeing Initiatives: Programs focused on mental health, financial planning, and wellness resources to help you thrive inside and outside of work.
Make an Impact: At bswift, your work directly contributes to transforming how organizations approach benefits administration and client engagement. Join us to be part of an organization that is making a meaningful difference in the lives of our clients and their employees.

Specific benefit offerings vary by position and may be subject to change.

 

Standard working hours are 8am-5pm Central Time, unless otherwise stated in the Job Description. 

 

 

In the spirit of pay transparency, we are excited to share the base salary range for this position is $85,000-$95,000, exclusive of fringe benefits or potential bonuses.  If you are hired at bswift, your final base salary compensation will be determined based on factors such as geographic location, skills, education, and/or experience. In addition to those factors – we believe in the importance of pay equity and consider internal equity of our current team members as a part of any final offer. Please keep in mind that the range mentioned above is the full base salary range for the role. Hiring at the maximum of the range would not be typical in order to allow for future & continued salary growth. We also offer a generous compensation and benefits package!

At bswift, our mission is clear: Become the preeminent benefits administrator through exceptional people, innovative technology, and delighted customers. And our values are at the heart of everything we do to accomplish that mission. We Pursue Excellence, Embrace Accountability, Deliver Superior Service, and strive to Be A Great Place To Work. We take pride in having an engaged, collaborative team that goes the extra mile to get the job done right.  

 bswift has been regularly named one of Chicago’s Best and Brightest Companies to Work For®, as well as one of the Nation’s Best and Brightest Companies to Work For® year after year. We offer a fun, flexible, and creative environment where you can grow both professionally and personally.

qaHistory.q: Describe a time you used data to answer a business question or make a recommendation. What data did you use, and what was the outcome? This answer will be reviewed prior to moving to the next step.*
qaHistory.a:
During my time at Apple, a business owner approached me about outfitting his team with new devices, and I noticed his company had been making individual purchases rather than working through our business channel. I pulled his purchase history data from our system and realized he'd bought 15+ devices over the past year, all at retail pricing with no financing options or device management. I sat down with him and walked through the numbers—I calculated that by switching to our business program with MDM (Mobile Device Management), he could save about 20% on future purchases through bulk pricing and financing, plus his IT costs would drop because he could remotely manage all the devices instead of handling each one individually.

I created a simple comparison spreadsheet showing his current spend versus what he'd pay through our business team, including the cost savings from our AppleCare for Enterprise plan and the time his staff would save on setup. I also included data from similar-sized clients who'd made the switch and saw their device replacement cycles extend by about 18 months because of better maintenance and tracking. After reviewing the analysis together, he agreed to transition to the business program. Within three months, his company enrolled 25 employees, and I got feedback from our business team that he was saving around $8,000 annually while cutting his IT overhead significantly. That experience taught me how powerful it is to take transaction data, turn it into a clear narrative, and show someone a better path forward—which is exactly what I want to do in a sales and marketing analytics role.

applications.role: Conducting comprehensive market research, competitive analysis, and data intelligence to identify emerging opportunities and inform strategic business development efforts for workforce solutions and government services sectors.
applications.jobDescription:
Business Development Analyst
Louisville, KY, USA
Employees can work remotely
Full-time
Job Title: Market Intelligence Analyst (930936)
State Location: KY
Company Description
We are a purpose-driven organization where support teams directly impact our ability to change the lives of the people and communities we serve. Throughout our US network and global family, our services span workforce and employment, education and training, and community support.

Equus Workforce Solutions is a member of the APM Group, a global network focused on enhancing a person’s health and wellbeing, independence, and employability.

Job Description
The Business Development Analyst is an essential member of APM's business development team, responsible for conducting comprehensive research, data collection, and analysis to identify emerging market opportunities and inform strategic growth efforts. Reporting directly to the Vice President of Business Development, the person in this role conducts research and analysis to generate actionable insights that drive business development and revenue growth. This includes analyzing industry trends, competitor activities, and potential customer needs. The Analyst will play a key role in building the foundational intelligence required to strategically position APM for expansion into new sectors and the successful launch of innovative service models. This role offers an exceptional opportunity for a motivated individual to contribute directly to APM's strategic growth and innovation initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a passion for uncovering insights from data, a strong desire to learn, and an interest in the dynamic intersection of government services, new markets, and innovative solutions.

Market Research & Data Collection: Conduct thorough research on underserved and emerging market sectors, particularly within government agencies and health-related industries. Gather data on market size, growth trends, regulatory landscapes, funding mechanisms, and procurement strategies.
Competitor Analysis: Research and analyze key competitors for specific opportunities and market segments, identifying their strengths, weaknesses, incumbent performance, and strategic approaches.
Customer & Opportunity Intelligence: Collect intelligence on prospective customers, including organizational structures, key decision-makers, strategic priorities, pain points, and existing contract vehicles. Research the feasibility of specific prospects and opportunities.
Trend Monitoring & Analysis: Monitor, evaluate, and report on emerging technologies, policy changes, and industry trends relevant to APM’s strategic interests, providing insights that inform innovation initiatives.
Data Analysis & Synthesis: Analyze collected qualitative and quantitative data to identify patterns, emerging opportunities, risks, and strategic implications for APM. Synthesize complex information into clear, concise findings.
Reporting & Presentation: Prepare compelling and data-driven reports, presentations, and dashboards. Visualize data effectively to communicate key insights and recommendations.
CRM Data Support: Assist with maintaining accurate and up-to-date opportunity data within APM’s CRM system, supporting pipeline management and reporting efforts.
Ad Hoc Research Support: Provide timely research and analytical support for special projects, strategic initiatives, and ad-hoc requests from leadership teams.
Qualifications
Education

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Marketing, Data Analytics, or a related field.
Experience

1-3 years of experience in market research, business intelligence, data analysis, management consulting or a similar analytical role. Internships or academic projects with a strong research component are highly valued.
Familiarity with government contracting, market research, and research analytics is a plus.
Skills and Competencies

Research & Analytical Acumen: Strong ability to conduct thorough qualitative and quantitative research, synthesize information, and identify key insights
Data Visualization & Reporting: Proficiency in creating impactful reports, presentations, and data visualizations using tools like PowerPoint, Excel, and potentially Power BI
Attention to Detail: Meticulous approach to data collection, analysis, and reporting, ensuring accuracy and reliability
Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate findings clearly and concisely to non-technical audiences
Problem-Solving: Curious mindset with a proactive approach to identifying research needs and solving analytical challenges
Tool Proficiency: Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and experience with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) and/or market research databases
Collaboration: Ability to work effectively as part of a team, taking direction and collaborating with senior leaders to achieve shared goals
Initiative & Learning Agility: Eagerness to learn new concepts, tools, and industry specifics, and to take initiative in supporting team objectives
Additional Information
This is an individual contributor role and does not have any direct reports. 

All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Extensive standing, and sitting and continuous computer, phone, and deskwork required. Work will be primarily in a defined office environment with specified hours. Will require one on one and group interaction with individuals from a diverse range of backgrounds. Must be available for occasional out of office meetings, conferences, training, and other events as needed.

Equus Workforce Solutions is a leading provider of workforce development services in North America. With a dedicated and passionate team, Equus puts the industry’s best practices to work by focusing on the development, design, and delivery of demand-driven workforce solutions.

When you join Equus, you can expect extensive learning opportunities and networking programs. But most of all you can expect to make a lasting impact on the lives of others.

At Equus we are strengthened by diversity. We are committed to providing a work environment in which everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. Equus Workforce Solutions is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, gender, age, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, ancestry, religion, national origin, veteran status, physical or mental disability, or reprisal or any other characteristic protected under state, federal, or local law.

applications.role: Entry-level Business Analyst role providing day-to-day production support for technology platforms while assisting with small-scale project coordination and process improvements in the insurance operations environment.
applications.jobDescription:
Associate Business Analyst
Job LocationsUS-NJ-MORRISTOWN | US-NJ-Remote
Job ID 2026-4854 Category IT Type Regular Division Surplus & Specialty
Crum & Forster Company Overview
Crum & Forster (C&F), with a proud history dating to 1822, provides specialty and standard commercial lines insurance products through our admitted and surplus lines insurance companies. C&F enjoys a financial strength rating of "A+" (Superior) by AM Best and is proud of our superior customer service platform. Our claims and risk engineering services are recognized as among the best in the industry.

Our most valuable asset is our people: more than 2500 employees in locations throughout the United States. The company is increasingly winning recognition as a great place to work, earning several workplace and wellness awards for four years running, including the 2025-2026 Great Place to Work® Award for our employee-first focus and our steadfast commitment to diversity, equity and Inclusion.

C&F is part of Fairfax Financial Holdings, a global, billion dollar organization.  For more information about Crum & Forster, please visit our website: www.cfins.com.

 

Job Description
 

 

Job Summary:

 

Seeking an entry-level Business Analyst with a strong interest in production support and project management. This role is ideal for a detail-oriented problem solver who thrives in a dynamic environment and is eager to learn the fundamentals of project management.

The Business Analyst will provide day-to-day support for our technology platforms, ensuring smooth and reliable operations through timely identification and resolution of production issues. In this role, you will collaborate closely with digital, data science, underwriting, and operations teams to support ongoing production activities, coordinate small projects, drive process improvements, and assist with system testing. The successful candidate will be highly motivated, possess an entrepreneurial spirit, have a service mentality, and be comfortable in a fast-paced environment.

The Business Analyst is a member of the Transportation and Sharing Economy Operations’ team, which will support technology platforms across the department.  Transportation and Sharing Economy underwrites admitted and non-admitted Auto, General Liability, and Excess/Umbrella lines of business.

 

What you will do:

 

Production Support:

Serve as the first point of contact for user-reported issues, questions, and requests related to production systems.
Troubleshoot, investigate, and resolve production issues, escalating as needed and ensuring timely communication with stakeholders.
Monitor system health and performance, proactively identifying and addressing potential problems.
Document and track issues, resolutions, and recurring trends to support continuous improvement.
Entry-Level Project Management:

Assist in coordinating small-scale projects and process enhancements, including scheduling meetings, tracking action items, and updating project documentation.
Gather and document business requirements and user stories in a clear and concise manner.
Support backlog management by assisting in organizing, prioritizing, and tracking tasks to maximize business value.
Participate in testing and quality assurance activities to ensure successful deployment of system changes and enhancements.
Communicate project and production status, roadblocks, and solutions to team members and leadership.
Collaboration & Documentation:

Create and maintain process documentation, including step-by-step guides and workflow diagrams.
Contribute to knowledge base articles and user support materials.
What you will bring to C&F:

Bachelor’s degree required.
1-2+ year of experience or relevant internship experience
Strong interest in production support, technology operations, and project coordination.
Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to interact with technical and non-technical audiences.
Willingness to learn, take initiative, and see tasks through to completion.
Attention to detail and commitment to quality.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Visio, Teams).
Experience with Confluence and Jira is a plus.
Experience supporting insurance operations (Auto, General Liability, Excess/Umbrella) is preferred but not required.
#LI-MS

applications.role: Entry-level Business Analyst responsible for production support of technology platforms and coordination of small-scale projects within the Transportation and Sharing Economy Operations team.
applications.jobDescription:
Associate Business Analyst
Job LocationsUS-NJ-MORRISTOWN | US-NJ-Remote
Job ID 2026-4854 Category IT Type Regular Division Surplus & Specialty
Crum & Forster Company Overview
Crum & Forster (C&F), with a proud history dating to 1822, provides specialty and standard commercial lines insurance products through our admitted and surplus lines insurance companies. C&F enjoys a financial strength rating of "A+" (Superior) by AM Best and is proud of our superior customer service platform. Our claims and risk engineering services are recognized as among the best in the industry.

Our most valuable asset is our people: more than 2500 employees in locations throughout the United States. The company is increasingly winning recognition as a great place to work, earning several workplace and wellness awards for four years running, including the 2025-2026 Great Place to Work® Award for our employee-first focus and our steadfast commitment to diversity, equity and Inclusion.

C&F is part of Fairfax Financial Holdings, a global, billion dollar organization.  For more information about Crum & Forster, please visit our website: www.cfins.com.

 

Job Description
 

 

Job Summary:

 

Seeking an entry-level Business Analyst with a strong interest in production support and project management. This role is ideal for a detail-oriented problem solver who thrives in a dynamic environment and is eager to learn the fundamentals of project management.

The Business Analyst will provide day-to-day support for our technology platforms, ensuring smooth and reliable operations through timely identification and resolution of production issues. In this role, you will collaborate closely with digital, data science, underwriting, and operations teams to support ongoing production activities, coordinate small projects, drive process improvements, and assist with system testing. The successful candidate will be highly motivated, possess an entrepreneurial spirit, have a service mentality, and be comfortable in a fast-paced environment.

The Business Analyst is a member of the Transportation and Sharing Economy Operations’ team, which will support technology platforms across the department.  Transportation and Sharing Economy underwrites admitted and non-admitted Auto, General Liability, and Excess/Umbrella lines of business.

 

What you will do:

 

Production Support:

Serve as the first point of contact for user-reported issues, questions, and requests related to production systems.
Troubleshoot, investigate, and resolve production issues, escalating as needed and ensuring timely communication with stakeholders.
Monitor system health and performance, proactively identifying and addressing potential problems.
Document and track issues, resolutions, and recurring trends to support continuous improvement.
Entry-Level Project Management:

Assist in coordinating small-scale projects and process enhancements, including scheduling meetings, tracking action items, and updating project documentation.
Gather and document business requirements and user stories in a clear and concise manner.
Support backlog management by assisting in organizing, prioritizing, and tracking tasks to maximize business value.
Participate in testing and quality assurance activities to ensure successful deployment of system changes and enhancements.
Communicate project and production status, roadblocks, and solutions to team members and leadership.
Collaboration & Documentation:

Create and maintain process documentation, including step-by-step guides and workflow diagrams.
Contribute to knowledge base articles and user support materials.
What you will bring to C&F:

Bachelor’s degree required.
1-2+ year of experience or relevant internship experience
Strong interest in production support, technology operations, and project coordination.
Exceptional analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to interact with technical and non-technical audiences.
Willingness to learn, take initiative, and see tasks through to completion.
Attention to detail and commitment to quality.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Visio, Teams).
Experience with Confluence and Jira is a plus.
Experience supporting insurance operations (Auto, General Liability, Excess/Umbrella) is preferred but not required.
#LI-MS
