applications.role: Support the Business Continuity team in maintaining organizational preparedness for disruptions through documentation, exercises, reporting, and administrative coordination.
applications.jobDescription:
This is a full time, remote Summer Internship that can be based anywhere in the United States or Canada. 

Calix is looking for a Summer intern to join our Business Continuity team. In this role, you will be part of a unique and award-winning internship program within the company. The program provides the opportunity to learn new skills through training and on the job learning. The expected duration is 90 days. 

The Business Continuity Intern will support the Business Continuity (BC) team in maintaining and strengthening the organization’s preparedness for disruptions and incidents. This role provides hands-on exposure to enterprise risk management, crisis preparedness, operational resilience, and compliance activities. Prior knowledge of business continuity is helpful but not required; this role is designed for a motivated individual eager to learn.
Responsibilities and Duties:

Assist with maintaining Business Continuity Plans (BCPs), pre-plans, and supporting documentation.
Help organize and track business continuity exercises, including tabletop exercises and tests.
Support preparation of dashboards, metrics, and leadership reports
Assist with updating Business Continuity SharePoint sites and document repositories.
Track action items and follow-ups from exercises and reviews.
Support basic research on risks, incidents, and continuity best practices.
Assist with training materials, job aids, and awareness content.
Participate in meetings, document notes, and summarize outcomes.
Provide general administrative and coordination support to the Business Continuity team.
Qualifications:

Pursuing an undergraduate degree in Business Continuity, Risk Management, Emergency Management or a similar Major.  Preference will be given to Juniors or Seniors who have previous work or relevant project experience.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills.
Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks.
Proficiency with Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Familiarity with SharePoint and project tracking tools (e.g. Jira) preferred.
Strong analytical or documentation skills preferred.
Willingness to learn new concepts, tools, and processes.
Able to work for the complete summer break (May - August or June - September).
#LI-Remote

The base pay range for this position varies based on the geographic location. More information about the pay range specific to candidate location and other factors will be shared during the recruitment process. Individual pay is determined based on location of residence and multiple factors, including job-related knowledge, skills and experience.

applications.role: Supporting Client Services operations by documenting business processes, gathering system requirements, and assisting with data analysis and reporting to improve operational efficiency.
applications.jobDescription:
Junior Business Analyst
Remote
Keller Postman represents a broad array of clients in class and mass actions, individual arbitrations, and multidistrict litigation matters at the trial and appellate levels in federal and state courts. Serving hundreds of thousands of clients in litigation and arbitration, we have prosecuted high-profile mass tort, antitrust, privacy, product liability, employment, and consumer-rights cases. Our firm also acts as plaintiffs’ counsel in high-stakes public-enforcement actions. Our mission is to achieve exceptional results for our clients, drive innovation in the practice of law, and pursue unparalleled excellence in everything we do. 

 

Purpose:  

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Business Analyst to join our Client Services team. You will work closely with Client Services stakeholders and IT to support day-to-day operations, help document business processes, and assist in analyzing and improving system functionality. 

This is a full-time, remote position.  Compensation for the position is an annual base salary of $50,000 to $55,000 per year, depending on experience, plus the role is eligible for a year-end discretionary bonus and benefits.  

 

Key Responsibilities: 

Collaborate with IT and Client Services teams to gather and document system enhancement requests. 
Assist in writing clear and concise business requirements and documenting them. 
Help analyze current business processes, identify areas for improvement, and translate business needs into action with support from our Strategic Operations Business Analyst. 
Support the creation and maintenance of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). 
Assist in generating and analyzing reports using Excel, Salesforce, and Sigma to support team goals. 
Participate in User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for new system features and enhancements. 
Process administrative work including running skip traces and sending SMS blasts. 
Help develop and update training materials and assist with user training when needed. 
Provide general project support, including maintaining project timelines and tracking progress, and perform other administrative and analytical tasks as assigned. 
 

Skills & Qualifications: 

Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail. 
Clear written and verbal communication skills. 
Willingness to learn and contribute to a team-oriented environment. 
Basic understanding of documenting processes and using flowcharting tools (e.g., Lucidchart or similar). 
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). 
Exposure to Salesforce, Sigma, or similar reporting tools is a plus. 
Ability to stay organized and manage multiple tasks simultaneously. 
 

Education & Experience: 

Bachelor’s degree required. 
1 year or more of experience in a business analysis, operations, or project coordination role is preferred. 
Exposure to tools such as Salesforce, Azure DevOps, or Lucidchart is a plus. 
 

Language Ability:  

Must be able to read, write, and speak fluent English.  
 

Physical Demands:  

This job requires remaining in a stationary position, often standing or sitting for prolonged periods.

applications.role: Research, outreach, and relationship building with government and non-government entities to collect and upload geographic data to enhance Google Maps accuracy using the Geo Data Upload Tool
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description - Business Communications Associate (00067831132)
Job Description
 
Business Communications Associate(Job Number: 00067831132)
 
Description
 
GDO Outreach Operations specialist requires excellent communication, time management and organizational skills to join our Outreach team. The role will focus on researching and outreaching government and non-government entities in various markets to collate geographic data for use on Google Maps through Google’s Geo Data Upload Tool. You will use your business development and relationship building skills to identify and effectively communicate mutually beneficial opportunities to both partners and Google.

RequirementBachelor’s degree in business administration, in GIS, a related field, or equivalent.1 year of experience working in digital/technology companies with tech products.Experience in Business Development and B2B partnerships/sales, customer service.Experience working in digital/technology companies with tech products.Expertise in Salesforce.com; Google Drive.Knowledge in Geographic Information System tools.Excellent communication skills in Portuguese and English.

Detailed Role & Responsibilities● Conduct research in assigned cities across geographies to collate data from approved sources that can be used to enhance the accuracy of Google Maps and benefit end users.● Communicate Google’s geo mission to authoritative entities and engage with potential partners to share basemap data for inclusion on Maps, primarily through promotion of the Geo Data Upload (GDU) Tool, using documentation provided by Google.● Liaise between external partners and Google stakeholder teams to convey requirements, validate data, coordinate and communicate updates about fixes/edits, etc.● Report details of outreach and/or operational issues to GDO Outreach Operations Lead. Report project status and identify insights re: partner expectations, partner user journeys, motivations & challenges, data gaps, outreach best practices, etc.● Manage and track all data submissions (through GDU or outside GDU) with GDO operations teams and various external partners.● Update information in internal planning tools and platforms as required, including logging and tracking all research, outreach, rejections, resulting submissions, etc.● Document on deliverables, such as number of partners contacted/onboarded, Number of open data sources identified or data sets submitted/ ingested, numbers of edits made originating from outreached sources.● Address and resolve external partner queries associated with the project.● Troubleshoot process and operational issues reported by authoritative data sources; ability to develop deep understanding of data formats, structures, etc.● Manage data pipeline from receiving the data captured by Partners to the creation of launch schedules, ensuring highest quality of captured data as outlined in the Operational Guidelines.● Utilize Google and non-Google owned tool to engage with new Partners and track communications

applications.role: Internship supporting senior engineers in prototype production process design of energetic materials and components, involving research, data analysis, computational modeling, and CAD work.
applications.jobDescription:
Job Details
Description
Applied Research Associates, Inc. is seeking an intern (sophomore or junior year of college) to work with senior engineers on prototype production process design of energetic materials and components. Interns may also support office tasks, such as maintaining internal library resources. Interns assist senior staff with data gathering and analysis, computational modelling, computer aided design, along with other tasks. Tasks may include a review of historical documentation and processes to extract and summarize information relevant to assigned research projects; research using internet-based search techniques, informational resources, and other available research tools.

As an intern, you will have the chance to learn through close interactions with senior research scientists that will provide you with opportunities for technical exploration.  You may also be required to occasionally assist with routine office tasks, such as ordering supplies, printing/binding reports, and maintaining internal library resources. This role will take place in the Arlington office and it will be a fully-in person position.

Technical Intern Required Skills:

Due to the nature of the work, qualified candidates must be a U.S. Citizen. The candidate must be an enrolled full-time Sophomore or Junior student and have a GPA of 3.5 or greater (out of a 4.0) towards any B.A. or B.S. degree in Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Chemistry, or Mathematics with 1-2 years of experience. The candidate must be able to work independently and must have good oral and written communication skills.  

Technical Intern Desired Qualifications:

A candidate with a basic understanding of mathematical modeling of physical or engineering systems or processes is desirable.  Basic computer programming and data analysis in any of the following languages are desirable: CAD, Python, Java, JavaScript, C, C++, C#.  Familiarity with hardware design, process controls, robotic automation are a plus. Familiarity with Windows, MS Office, SharePoint, are expected. Demonstrated strong research and analytical skills are a plus.

COMPANY INFORMATION:

Applied Research Associates, Inc. is an employee-owned international research and engineering company recognized for providing technically superior solutions to complex and challenging problems in the physical sciences. The company, founded in Albuquerque, NM, in 1979, currently employs over 2,243 professionals and continues to grow. ARA offices throughout the United States and Canada provide a broad range of technical expertise in defense technologies, civil technologies, computer software and simulation, systems analysis, environmental technologies, and testing and measurement. The corporation also provides sophisticated technical products for environmental site characterization, pavement analysis, and robotics.

At ARA, employees are our greatest assets. The corporation realizes that employee ownership spawns greater creativity and initiative along with higher performance and customer satisfaction levels. ARA gives employees the tools, training, and opportunities to take more active roles as owners. The culture is challenging and innovation and experimentation are the norm. Employees are eligible for contributions which not only add to the company’s success, but also their own through the Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP). The motto, “Engineering and Science for Fun and Profit” sums up the ARA experience. For additional information and an opportunity to join this unique workplace, please apply at careers.ara.com.

applications.role: A 10-week summer internship supporting enterprise data governance, data quality monitoring, and data standards documentation within Hilton's corporate headquarters.
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description - 2026 Corporate Summer Internship - Intern, Enterprise Data Governance Analyst (COR015OX)
2026 Corporate Summer Internship - Intern, Enterprise Data Governance Analyst (Job Number: COR015OX)
Work Locations: Hilton - Global Headquarters - McLean 7930 Jones Branch Drive  McLean 22102
***This role is based at our corporate office in McLean, VA***

The Hilton corporate summer internship program offers the opportunity to gain real-world professional experience in a global company. The program is intended to develop the business leaders of tomorrow. The internship will be in person at our corporate office located in McLean, VA. The internship will begin on June 1, 2026 and end on August 7, 2026. Interns will work full-time (40 hours per week during the 10-week program).

HOW WE WILL SUPPORT YOU

Hilton is proud to support the mental and physical well-being of all Team Members so they can Thrive personally and professionally in a diverse and inclusive environment, thanks to programs and benefits such as:

Go Hilton travel program: Discounted travel with room rates as low as $40/night
Paid Holidays
At Hilton, we believe every Team Member is a leader. We are committed to offering leadership development opportunities and programs through every step of a Team Member's career journey and at every level, both in our hotels and across corporate. 

**Available benefits may vary depending upon terms and conditions of employment and are subject to the terms and conditions of the plans.

HOW YOU WILL MAKE AN IMPACT

Your role is important and below are some of the fundamental job duties that make your work unique.

What projects you will take ownership of: 

Support enterprise data strategy and governance efforts
Document business rules and partner with SMEs and engineers to implement data quality controls
Monitor data quality checks and help investigate and resolve issues
Contribute to defining and maintaining data standards
Analyze datasets to spot trends and data quality gaps
Use tools like SQL or BI platforms as needed
Engage in impactful projects, take part in learning and development workshops, and build connections through networking and social events with Team Members and fellow interns
Support your assigned team through the program, which could include preparing reports, presentations, conducting research, and analyzing data
Encourage a culture of innovation and help develop new ideas and solutions
Utilize organizational, time management, and communication skills to effectively manage tasks and collaborate with your team
Collaborate with your manager and team to deliver a final presentation showcasing your internship experience and learnings
Assist with executing campus events to support internship program engagement and outreach as needed
WHY YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT

You have these minimum qualifications:

Can commit to a full 10-week program (40 hours per week) onsite in our corporate office located in McLean, VA
Sophomore or junior of post-secondary education program; or have less than one (1) year of professional experience
Experience using SQL or BI platforms (Tableau, Power BI, etc.)
It would be useful if you have:

Held a leadership role in your community or engaged with a student run organization
Internship or work experience in the hospitality or customer service (e.g. server, front desk, volunteer, coach)
Experience working with Microsoft Office Suite
Knowledge of programming/coding languages
Curiosity about different technology disciplines
WHAT IT IS LIKE WORKING FOR HILTON

Hilton, the #1 World's Best Workplace, is a leading global hospitality company with a diverse portfolio of world-class brands. Dedicated to filling the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality, we have welcomed more than 3 billion guests in our more than 100-year history. Hilton is proud to have an award-winning workplace culture and we are consistently named among one of the World's Best Workplaces. Check out the Hilton Careers blog and Instagram to learn more about what it's like to be on Team Hilton! 

It is the policy of Hilton to employ qualified persons without regard to color, race, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth and related medication conditions), gender identity or gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, military service, status as a protected veteran, disability, protected medical condition as defined by applicable law, genetic information, or any other protected group status as defined by and subject to applicable federal, state and local laws.  

We provide reasonable accommodations to qualified persons with disabilities to perform the essential functions of the position and provide other benefits and privileges of employment in accordance with applicable law. Please contact us if you require an accommodation during the application process. 

Schedule: Temporary
Brand: Hilton Corporate
Shift: Day Job
Job Level: Apprentice/New Grad/Intern
Job: Universities

applications.role: A 12-month IT internship providing hands-on technical support, system setup, and help desk assistance for individuals with documented disabilities.
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Make an impact by joining ServiceSource, a champion for people with disabilities. Explore new opportunities! ServiceSource is an organization of talented people who drive innovation, embrace change, and strengthen communities.

Pay rate $12.46 -$15.00 per hour.

Job Summary

The Information Technology (IT) Intern will be providing IT support to both the IT department and company staff. This is internship program is for approximately 30 hours/week. This is a 12-month program of learning and job experience for job seekers who have a documented disability.

Primary Duties

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. These duties and responsibilities will be rated on the Annual Performance Review.

Support IT asset Inventory.
Responsible for set up of new systems and rebuild of old systems.
Set up end user profiles.
Install/configure software and firmware updates.
Contact vendors for hardware support.

Additional Responsibilities

Schedule and deploy systems to end users.
Assist end users with logging to systems.
Enter support tickets and answer phones.
Field incoming requests for support via phone, e-mail, and in-person to ensure courteous, timely and effective resolution of end user issues. 
Perform other responsibilities as assigned.

Qualifications: Education, Experience, and Certification(s)

High School Diploma or a General Equivalency Diploma (GED) required.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

Knowledge of basic computer hardware, including installation and troubleshooting. 
Experience with desktop windows 10 and server operating systems.
Proficient written and oral communication skills. 
Proficient interpersonal skills, with a focus on rapport-building, listening, and questioning skills. Strong documentation skills. 
Ability to present ideas in user-friendly language. 
Highly self-motivated and directed. Keen attention to detail. 
Good customer service orientation. Experience working in a team-oriented, collaborative environment.

Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk or hear. The employee frequently is required to stand; use hands and arms; climb or balance; stoop and kneel. The employee is occasionally required to walk and sit. The employee must regularly lift and/or move up to 25 pounds and occasionally lift and/or move up to 75 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.

Work Environment

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. The noise level in the work environment is usually quiet.

What We Offer – for Benefit Eligible Employees May Include:

Because ServiceSource hires the best people, we work hard to provide benefits that make their lives better, so we offer a comprehensive benefit program which features:

Health coverage for you and your family through Medical, Dental, and Vision plans.
Financial protection with 100% company paid Disability, Life, Accidental Death & Dismemberment insurance.
A 403(b)-Retirement plan in which the company matches dollar for dollar on a generous percentage matching up to 3% of your contribution.
Tax advantages through Flexible Spending and Health Savings accounts that allow you to pay for specific healthcare and dependent care expenses with pre-tax dollars.
To help you manage your work and life needs, we offer an Employee Assistance Program, Wellness Program, and Tuition Assistance.
A generous paid time-off program in which the benefits increase based on your tenure with the company.

We are an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer, making employment decisions without regard to a person’s race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and transgender status), national origin, age (40 or older), veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. We are an E-Verify Employer and a drug-free workplace. Pre-employment background checks are required for all employment positions.

PAY TRANSPARENCY POLICY STATEMENT:

The contractor will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant. However, employees who have access to the compensation information of other employees or applicants as a part of their essential job functions cannot disclose the pay of other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise have access to compensation information, unless the disclosure is (a) in response to a formal complaint or charge, (b) in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted by the employer, or (c) consistent with the contractor’s legal duty to furnish information

applications.role: An internship providing financial analysis and data support for Department of Defense and Intelligence Community acquisition programs with exposure to budget activities and senior leadership presentations.
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Overview

Systems Planning and Analysis, Inc. (SPA) delivers high-impact, technical solutions to complex national security issues. With over 50 years of business expertise and consistent growth, we are known for continuous innovation for our government customers, in both the US and abroad. Our exceptionally talented team is highly collaborative in spirit and practice, producing Results that Matter . Come work with the best! We offer opportunity, unique challenges, and clear-sighted commitment to the mission. SPA: Objective. Responsive. Trusted.

The Space and Intelligence Division provides professional services to the US Space Force, Combatant Commands, Intelligence Community, and NASA. Our work includes enterprise architectural assessments, systems engineering and integration, test, planning and execution, cost estimating and analysis, acquisition support, and cybersecurity. We are trusted partners developing approaches and concepts to meet emerging high priority needs, assessing cutting-edge technologies, and supporting capabilities for our National Defense. Come join the fastest growing Division at Systems Planning and Analysis, Inc.!

The Intelligence Programs Group (IPG), part of SPA’s Space and Intelligence Division, is a specialized provider of systems engineering and trusted advisory services to the Space and National Intelligence community. We utilize mission-proven, data-driven advisory tools and capabilities. We provide highly technical support to ensure the government acquires cutting edge technology necessary for our National Defense. Our portfolio includes the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA), National Reconnaissance Office (NRO), and National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA). Come where you will be part of our high-caliber team!

SPA's IPG has a need for a Financial Analyst Intern at our Chantilly, VA location.

Timeline For Intern Selection

 Applications can be submitted and will be reviewed thru 1 April 2026. 
 Interviews will be ongoing and conclude by 15 April 2026. 
 Offers/decisions will be extended by 30 April 2026. 

Responsibilities

Learn and gain experience through providing analytic and financial data analysis support for important DoD and Intelligence Community acquisition activities. This position will provide you with exposure to and expected participation in policy and program research, light data analysis, and development of presentations for senior leadership, focusing on budget and acquisition activities.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

 US Citizenship required with the ability to obtain and maintain a DoD Top Secret Clearance 
 Candidates must be actively enrolled in an accredited, degree-seeking undergraduate or graduate program and have at least one semester remaining following the internship 
 Working toward a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Finance, Business, Managment, Accounting, or similar field 
 Strong written and oral communication skills with ability to synthesize data and communicate clearly via written and verbal means 
 Proficient in using Microsoft Office, including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel to communicate to decision makers 
 Ability to report to designated work location in Chantilly, VA, up to full time, based on the needs of the customer 

Desired Qualifications

 Proactive interest in learning new skills and engaging new challenges 
 Strong customer service skills, with interest in proactively anticipating and addressing client needs 
 Experience with advance analytics such as Tableau

applications.role: Supporting human resource and contract management systems including Workday and Salesforce through system configuration, data validation, testing, and issue resolution as part of the HR IT team.

We’re looking for an Intern with:
Completed coursework in data management, business analysis or information systems 
Exposure to data structures such as XML, XSLT, JSON and query languages such as SQL and WQL

applications.role: This role supports federal field sales teams by managing pipeline activities, customer engagement, post-sale coordination, and account health to ensure consistent customer experiences throughout the account lifecycle.
applications.jobDescription:
Company Overview 
Hexagon US Federal is a proxy-governed subsidiary of Hexagon AB providing an ever expanding portfolio of Hexagon Technologies to U.S. Federal Government organizations, including defense and intelligence agencies to meet a variety of mission requirements. With our unique capabilities and experience we transform state-of-the-art commercial technology into mission-specific solutions for our customers, partnering with them to solve their most challenging problems.
Quick Hits 
Employees: 200
Operating Locations: Chantilly, VA (HQ); Huntsville, AL; Lanham, MD, and other client sites across the US 

Role Overview
The Federal Customer Account Representative supports the Federal field sales organization while serving as a key point of continuity for assigned customer territories. This role focuses on pipeline support, customer engagement, post-sale coordination, and ongoing account health—helping ensure a consistent, high-quality experience for federal customers throughout the account lifecycle.
This position works in close partnership with Federal Account Executives, partners, and internal delivery teams to support growth, retention, and customer satisfaction.
Key Responsibilities: Field Sales & Account Support
Work jointly with the Field Account Executives to drive pipeline and close revenue across assigned territories
Assist with lead qualification, discovery support, and early-stage customer engagement
Coordinate meetings, demos, briefings, and follow-ups with government stakeholders
Assist in the development of sales documentation, quotes, and account materials
Maintain accurate account, opportunity, and activity records in CRM systems
Identify expansion or upsell signals and route opportunities to the appropriate field seller
Key Responsibilities: Inside Sales Activities
Drive revenue opportunities based around targeted sales campaigns such as recapture of lapsed support agreements or targeted product campaigns
Engage inbound inquiries and conduct targeted outreach within assigned Federal accounts
Reinforce solution value aligned to agency mission outcomes
Key Responsibilities: Customer Success & Account Continuity
Serve as a consistent point of contact for assigned customers alongside the field seller
Support customer onboarding and internal handoffs following contract award
Conduct regular check-ins to drive adoption and customer satisfaction
Identify risks to account health and escalate issues proactively
Support renewal preparation by reinforcing value and coordinating with sales and delivery teams
Gather customer feedback and share insights with sales, product, and leadership stakeholders
Qualifications: Required
1–3+ years of experience in inside sales, account support, customer success, or related roles
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Ability to manage multiple Federal accounts and priorities simultaneously
Experience working with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, or similar)
Customer-focused mindset with strong organizational skills
Must be a US Citizen
Must live with in the greater DMV area with reasonable commute to the Chantily office
Qualifications: Preferred
Experience supporting Federal or public sector sales teams
Familiarity with Federal procurement environments and partner-led sales motions
Experience working with primes, system integrators, or channel partners
Exposure to SaaS, cloud, and/or Geospatial technologies
Key Skills & Attributes
Highly organized and detail-oriented
Professional, polished communicator comfortable with Federal stakeholders
Proactive and solutions-oriented
Comfortable working cross-functionally with sales, delivery, and operations teams
Able to balance customer advocacy with revenue-support objectives
At Hexagon US Federal, we prioritize a secure and thorough hiring process. Be aware that we will never extend an employment offer without a careful review and interview process, and all official communications from our representatives will come from a verified Hexagon US Federal email address, never requesting upfront fees or sensitive information. The physical and mental demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these functions.
Ability to remain in a stationary position and operate a computer for extended periods. 
Occasional ability to move or transport items up to 25 pounds. 
Communicate effectively in English (verbal, written) and possess visual and auditory acuity for tasks and safety. 
Manage multiple tasks, prioritize, and maintain focus in dynamic environments. 
Demonstrate strong problem-solving, critical thinking, and analytical skills. 
Maintain consistent attendance, punctuality, and high professional standards. 
Work Environment: Work is performed primarily in a professional office and/or remote home office environment, subject to typical conditions. 
Required Security Clearance: None
Travel: 10%

What we will provide in return:  
- Competitive health care plans with savings accounts 
- Dental and vision plans 
- 401k with 100% company match up to 6%, with immediate vesting on company match 
- Life and disability insurance 
- Learning Management System with robust offerings 
- Tuition Reimbursement Program 
- Flexible working arrangements where possible 
- 13 paid holidays per year 
- Veterans’ focused Employee Resources Group with regular educational sessions and communications 
- Leadership Development Program with multiple learning options 
 
Hexagon US Federal is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. We comply with all applicable federal anti-discrimination laws and provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in its job application procedures. If you have any difficulty using our online system and need an accommodation due to a disability, you may use the following email address, hr@hexagonusfederal.com and/or phone number (703) 264-5600 to contact us about your interest in employment with Hexagon US Federal.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to protected veteran status, disability status, or any other protected class covered by federal, state, or local law. Hexagon US Federal participates in E-Verify.

At Hexagon US Federal, we prioritize a secure and thorough hiring process. Be aware that we will never extend an employment offer without a careful review and interview process, and all official communications from our representatives will come from a verified Hexagon US Federal email address, never requesting upfront fees or sensitive information.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is a poor fit for this federal sales support role. Join a company that sells to federal customers and volunteer for any federal-facing projects, government contract teams, or partner ecosystem work to build relevant domain knowledge
Shadow federal account executives or customer success managers, document 10+ sales calls/customer meetings, and create case studies showing understanding of federal sales motions

Build a portfolio demonstrating: pipeline you've managed (even if small), customer success metrics you've impacted, renewal rates, expansion opportunities identified, and quotes/proposals you've created
Relocate to DMV area (currently unclear if candidate meets this requirement) and network extensively with federal tech sales professionals through LinkedIn, FedTech events, and GovCon community
marketSkillsToLearn: Salesforce Sales Cloud with opportunity/account/contact management
Federal procurement fundamentals (GSA schedules, FAR/DFARS basics, contract vehicles like SEWP, GSA MAS)
Enterprise SaaS sales methodologies (MEDDIC, Command of the Message, Challenger Sale)
Customer Success platforms and practices (Gainsight, ChurnZero, health scoring, QBR execution)
Sales engagement tools (Outreach, SalesLoft, Gong)
Federal partner ecosystem knowledge (primes, system integrators, VARs)
Quote-to-cash processes and CPQ tools
Sales pipeline forecasting and management
Account planning and territory management
Government stakeholder engagement and mission-based selling
Cloud technology fundamentals (AWS, Azure, SaaS architectures) from a sales perspective, not technical implementation
Geospatial technology market understanding (if targeting Hexagon specifically)
analysis.summary: This candidate scores 32/100—a weak match bordering on not qualified. They have transferable soft skills from Apple retail (customer engagement, problem-solving, product knowledge) but lack every critical requirement: B2B sales experience, federal sector exposure, CRM-driven opportunity management, and customer success operations. Their impressive technical skills in data science/ML are orthogonal to this role's needs. They need 1-2 years in actual B2B inside sales or customer success before being competitive for federal sales support positions.

Hexagon US Federal is a proxy-governed subsidiary of Hexagon AB providing an ever expanding portfolio of Hexagon Technologies to U.S. Federal Government organizations, including defense and intelligence agencies to meet a variety of mission requirements. We are seeking a Data Engineering Intern working remote, reporting to an IT manager in
Atlanta, Georgia.

We are seeking an enthusiastic and motivated intern to join our IT Business Applications team at
Hexagon US Federal. As an intern here, you will have the opportunity to gain hands-on experience
in core and secondary business applications, including Deltek Costpoint ERP, Salesforce CRM,

applications.role: Entry-level summer intern position focused on day-to-day cybersecurity operations including monitoring security solutions, investigating incidents, and supporting enterprise network defense.
applications.jobDescription:
Overview
We are looking for an entry level Cybersecurity Operations summer intern to perform the day-to-day operations of the security solutions and use these solutions to identify, investigate and resolve security incidents on the network. This is a great opportunity for someone who likes to dig in and understand the intricacies of cyber defense, has a technical hands-on mindset, and exhibits good work ethic and a positive attitude to learning the ins and outs of information security operations for an enterprise environment.

Responsibilities
Maintain in-place security tools and processes
Evaluate, test and implement new security tools & technologies
Respond to computer security incidents
Research system and network logs and alerts as they relate to incidents
Identify gaps in cyber operations capabilities and assist in developing those capabilities
Develop technical summary information for presentation to management
Qualifications
High School diploma and 1-2 years of college or related work experience
Understanding of incident response process in an enterprise operations environment
Understanding of cyber threats and defenses against those threats
Knowledge of Windows, Linux, and networking
Knowledge of enterprise security technologies such as web filtering, endpoint protection and forensics, sandbox, threat intel integration, firewalls, SIEM, patch and vulnerability management etc.
Familiarity with using scripting languages for security automation
Ability to juggle multiple tasks and projects with varying priorities
Ability to document findings for audiences with various levels of technical expertise
MUST BE A US CITIZEN
May need to obtain and maintain Secret security clearance
Cyber Defense Club membership a plus!

applications.role: An intern position to research and implement digital currency payment systems for international grantees working in democracy and freedom advancement.
applications.jobDescription:
About NED

The National Endowment for Democracy (NED) seeks to advance democracy and freedom. NED seeks to empower its partners to inform, organize, and mobilize constituencies for political reforms as a means to build and sustain democracy.

At NED, you will join a team of committed individuals who are mission- and values-driven. We are looking for individuals who will deliver their talents in support of our work.

Our values include:

Respect

Empathy

Accountability

Adaptability

Excellence

Position Summary

NED is creating student and employee partnerships to facilitate institutional growth and development. We seek a motivated, energetic, organized, and resourceful intern specializing in digital currency systems who would be excited to join a fast-paced Financial Freedom team. The intern will use their expertise in digital payments to work closely with Finance, Grants, and Program staff in support of NED’s innovation and implementation of digital currency systems for grantee payments. The intern will work with the Financial Freedom team lead and project manager to conduct research on best practices for digital payments, contribute to the development of internal NED systems and processes, and develop digital payments solutions for NED grantmaking staff and grantees.

This position will start on May 18, 2026, for no more than twelve (12) weeks and may not be extended. This is a paid position, at $17.95 per hour. Intern working hours will be minimum 20 hours per week.

This internship is for an in-person opportunity position based in Washington, DC.

Primary Responsibilities

Research

Conduct research into legal, technical, and security environments for digital currency use in countries where NED grantees are considering digital currency payments and monitor developments impacting existing or potential grantee digital currency payment practices.

Maintain up-to-date reference resources explaining available digital currency system options in each relevant country context.

Identify and assess available external training opportunities for NED staff and grantees, including programs offering training and consultations specific to regional or country contexts.

Digital Currency Solutions

Monitor developments in digital payment technology to identify new tools or systems with the potential to improve security or efficiency for grantees receiving digital currency payments.

Provide input on optimizing digital currency payments system for maximum efficiency, security, and accountability.

Provide technically-informed assessments of potential digital currency products or services.

Qualifications

Current enrollment in or recent graduation from an undergraduate or graduate degree program in international relations, comparative political science, international development, computer science, information technology, finance, or other relevant field.

Demonstrated practical skills in research, analysis, and digital payment system design.

Deep knowledge of diverse digital currency tools and security.

Passionate about digital currency as a tool for supporting civil society and democratic values worldwide.

Discretion to collaborate with politically vulnerable individuals and groups prioritizing duty of care.

Preferred professional experience in technical support for digital currency systems users or working with international organizations and with cross-cultural communication.

Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States for any employer. NED does not provide visa sponsorship and will not consider candidates who now or in the future require employment visa sponsorship.

Talent in Action

Consistent with NED’s talent statement, and grounded in our values, the successful individual in this role will demonstrate the following competencies in the delivery of their work while supporting NED’s efforts to advance democratic change:

Purpose-Driven

Reliable

Expertise

Respectful Collaboration

Curiosity

Results-Oriented

An Optimistic Mindset

improvementPlan: Gain foundational cryptocurrency knowledge: Complete Coursera 'Bitcoin and Cryptocurrency Technologies' (Princeton) and 'Blockchain Basics' (University at Buffalo). Set up wallets, conduct transactions, understand different chains (Bitcoin, Ethereum, stablecoins).

Develop democracy/civil society context: Volunteer with organizations like Freedom House, Human Rights Watch, or local refugee resettlement agencies. Read NED's annual reports and grantee case studies. Understand authoritarian digital surveillance and financial restrictions.

Create relevant projects: Build a cryptocurrency payment solution prototype for a hypothetical NGO scenario. Research and document digital payment options for activists in 3-5 repressive regimes. Write analysis of crypto adoption in civil society contexts.

Get international development exposure: Take courses in international relations, comparative politics, or development studies. Attend events at think tanks (Carnegie, Brookings) focused on democracy and technology.

Target appropriate roles first: Apply for general international development internships, democracy promotion organizations, or fintech companies to build relevant domain knowledge before pursuing this specialized intersection.

applications.role: Maximize recovery of bankrupt accounts through negotiation, litigation, fraud detection, and specialized servicing of charged-off mortgage loans while serving as liaison with legal counsel on bankruptcy matters.
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description
To maximize recovery of bankrupt accounts through negotiation, reaffirmation, legal proceedings, fraud detection and collection efforts. Serve as liaison for OGC and outside legal counsel on bankruptcy litigation or adversary complaints. Provide specialized servicing of charged-off mortgage loans. Serve as subject matter expert regarding section functions, systems, policies and/or procedures.

Responsibilities
Analyze accounts included in the bankruptcy for potential abuse and/or fraud to determine if litigation is warranted to protect NFCU's interest and reduce financial loss
Monitor attorneys assigned to accounts to determine status of litigation; advise attorneys of any legal issues and their impact on NFCU
Process and distribute time sensitive legal documents, notification of suits against NFCU, Cease and Desist for both filer and non-filer, and third party communications to ensure compliance with Bankruptcy Laws to minimize the risk of litigation
Review bankruptcy docket report and analyze accompanying schedules with emphasis on Statement of Intent for Chapter 7, and Plan information for Chapter13 to determine the impact on NFCU
Communicate with attorneys and trustees, members, agents, title companies and various third-party and internal/external groups regarding bankruptcy
Negotiate attorney fees and recommend approval to Supervisor
Analyze Chapter 7, 11 and 13 bankruptcy filings to determine the potential for asset recovery
Initiate the appropriate course of action for asset recovery based on collateral, cross-collateral potential, and non-filing parties in accordance with State repossession laws
Represent or retain outside legal counsel to represent NFCU at court proceedings
Manage legal notifications (e.g., Mechanic's Liens, Adversary Complaints, Lift of Stay, Motions to Avoid Lien, Motions to Value, Judgment Liens, and Plan Confirmation, etc.), and determine appropriate course of action
Negotiate Short Sale request, settlement, and reaffirmation based on the bankruptcy disposition Process charged-off bankruptcy impaired equity loans; facilitate Proof of Claims and review motions
Monitor bankruptcy progression for adherence of repayment and update NFCU systems to ensure accurate reporting
Pursue fraud cases through the court system for Non-Discharge and Objections to Discharge
Evaluate bankruptcy cases and determine collateral market values; file Proof of Claim with the appropriate court on all asset cases
Maintain knowledge of and ensure compliance with applicable federal and state laws, rules, regulations and NFCU policies and procedures
Assist with reviewing escalated member issues and with determining the appropriate course of action to resolve issues
Perform other duties as assigned
Qualifications
Working knowledge of applicable federal and state laws, rules and regulations
Experience in financial counseling, negotiating, and explaining decisions to members
Experience in financial transaction/processing related responsibilities
Ability to work independently and in a team environment
Experience working with all levels of staff, management, stakeholders, vendors
Experience interacting with attorneys, courts, or trustees
Ability to comprehend, analyze, interpret, communicate and apply government and financial industry regulations, related principles and practices, and company instructions, procedures, and policies
Ability to maintain confidentiality and demonstrate integrity
Effective skill exercising initiative and using good judgment to make sound decisions
Effective skill following, interpreting and applying relevant data/instructions to guidelines, procedures, practices and regulations
Effective member/customer service skills
Effective skill interacting tactfully and effectively in difficult situations
Effective research, analytical, and problem solving skills
Effective skill building effective relationships through rapport, trust, diplomacy and tact
Effective skill assimilating information, analyzing facts, and developing logical conclusions
Effective organizational, planning and time management skills
Effective skill to influence, negotiate and persuade to reach agreeable exchange and positive outcomes
Effective verbal and written communication skills
Effective database, word processing, and spreadsheet software skills
Desired Qualifications

Familiarity with the systems and software used by the Lending Collection's Division
Familiarity with NFCU functions, philosophy, operations and organizational objectives
Familiarity with NFCU products, services, processes, policies and procedures
College level courses with concentration in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field
Hours: Monday - Friday, 8:00AM - 4:30PM

Location: 820 Follin Lane, Vienna, VA 22180

applications.role: This is a hands-on technical operations role providing IT support and troubleshooting across multiple healthcare clinic locations while learning internal systems with growth potential into advanced technical positions.
applications.jobDescription:
Job description
If you’re the person your friends and family go to when something tech-related breaks, this role is for you.

Company Overview

RxWellness is a fast-growing healthcare platform operating multiple chiropractic and wellness clinics across Northern Virginia. Our mission is to bring natural healthcare to the forefront while building a scalable, technology-driven clinic network.

As we grow from 12 to 20+ locations, we are building an internal team to support our technology systems, clinic infrastructure, and data operations.
 

Job Description

We are hiring a Technical Operations Associate to support our clinics and internal systems.

This is a hands-on, entry-level role where you will help troubleshoot real-world technology issues across our clinics while learning how our systems, data, and software work together behind the scenes.

This role is ideal for someone early in their career who is:

-good with technology

-enjoys solving problems

-willing to learn quickly

-comfortable working both in-office and in real environments

You will work directly with leadership and our technical team and will have the opportunity to grow into a larger systems or technical role over time.
 

What You’ll Do

Clinic IT & Technical Support

Troubleshoot WiFi, internet connectivity, and networking issues

Set up and maintain computers, workstations, and peripherals

Assist with basic server and system troubleshooting

Travel to local clinic locations to resolve technical issues

Systems & Technology Support

Help maintain and support internal systems (EHR, reporting tools, etc.)

Assist with resolving day-to-day technical issues across the company

Work with team members to identify and fix recurring system problems

Learning & Growth Projects (Over Time)

As you grow in the role, you may also:

Assist with data/reporting systems (SQL, Tableau, etc.)

Help support integrations between systems (EHR, CRM, etc.)

Participate in larger technology projects (cloud migration, system upgrades)
 

What We’re Looking For

We are NOT looking for someone who knows everything already.

We are looking for someone who:

-is naturally good with technology

-enjoys figuring things out

-is comfortable using Google/ChatGPT to solve problems

-takes initiative instead of waiting for instructions

-is reliable and follows through
 

Preferred Background (Flexible)

Computer Science, Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Engineering, or related field

OR strong hands-on experience with technology (building PCs, fixing systems, etc.)

0–2 years experience (internships or part-time experience count)

 

Nice to Have (Not Required)

Basic understanding of networking (routers, WiFi, etc.)

Exposure to SQL, data, or reporting tools

Experience troubleshooting technical issues

 

Location

Based in Northern Virginia (Herndon area)

Some travel to local clinic locations required

Compensation

$60,000 – $90,000 depending on experience

Strong growth opportunity as the company scales
Additional Bonus opportunities

 

Why This Role is Different

This is not a typical entry-level IT job.

You will:

-work on real systems in a growing company

-gain exposure to multiple technologies

-solve real problems across multiple locations

-have a clear path to grow into more advanced technical roles

 

How to Apply

Apply through Handshake and include a short answer to the following:

“Tell us about a time you had to figure out a technical problem you didn’t already know how to solve.”

missingSkills.skill: Physical IT infrastructure support (WiFi, routers, networking hardware)
missingSkills.reason: This is a hands-on field technician role requiring you to physically travel to clinics, troubleshoot network equipment, set up workstations, and fix connectivity issues. Your experience is entirely software/cloud-based with zero evidence of handling physical IT infrastructure.

Healthcare clinics run on Windows PCs, not Apple devices. Your Apple Store experience with iOS troubleshooting doesn't translate to Windows enterprise environments, printer setup, or PC hardware repair.
You'll be supporting clinic staff using Electronic Health Records systems daily. Zero healthcare IT experience means steep learning curve in a regulated, high-stakes environment.
This role requires driving to multiple clinic locations to resolve real-time issues. Your work has been retail floor-based or fully remote—no evidence of independent field service work.
They need someone who can assist with server troubleshooting and basic sysadmin tasks. Your cloud knowledge is theoretical/project-based, not hands-on operational IT infrastructure.
Clinics use printers, scanners, medical devices—you have no demonstrated experience supporting office peripherals beyond Apple accessories.

experienceGaps: No hands-on IT support experience outside Apple's closed ecosystem—this role needs Windows enterprise environment expertise
Zero healthcare industry exposure—clinic environments have unique compliance, urgency, and workflow requirements unlike retail
No field service or independent on-site troubleshooting experience—your roles were either in-store with immediate team support or remote
Lack of SMB/enterprise IT infrastructure experience—setting up workstations, managing network hardware, configuring VPNs in real production environments
No evidence of 'fix-it' mentality with physical hardware—your technical work is heavily data/software/cloud-focused, not hardware repair
Missing practical networking skills—your AWS VPC project was academic; they need someone who can physically troubleshoot router configs and WiFi dead zones
analysis.criticalVerdict: You're a data analyst/ML engineer trying to pivot into hands-on IT support, and it shows. Your skills are impressive but completely misaligned—you can build machine learning models and cloud architectures, but there's zero evidence you can physically set up a router, troubleshoot a printer, or support Windows workstations in a clinic. This role needs someone who built their own PC, runs a home lab, or has help desk experience. Your Apple Store background is retail customer service, not enterprise IT infrastructure. You'd be learning the entire job from scratch.
improvementPlan: Get hands-on IT support experience immediately—volunteer for a nonprofit's IT needs, work help desk part-time, or get a desktop support internship to demonstrate you can handle physical infrastructure

Build a home lab: set up a Windows domain controller, configure enterprise WiFi with multiple access points, practice printer troubleshooting, and document everything on GitHub to prove hands-on capability
Get CompTIA A+ certification within 60 days—this proves foundational IT support skills and shows you're serious about pivoting from data/ML to infrastructure
Shadow or freelance as an IT field technician for small businesses—you need proof you can independently drive to sites, diagnose network issues, and fix problems without hand-holding
Learn basic healthcare IT concepts—research EHR systems (Epic, Cerner), HIPAA compliance basics, and clinic workflow to show industry interest
Reframe your Apple Store experience honestly—focus on the hands-on device troubleshooting and diagnostics, not the sales/customer service fluff. Right now your resume reads as 80% sales, 20% tech.
marketSkillsToLearn: CompTIA A+ certification
Windows Server administration basics
Active Directory fundamentals
Network troubleshooting (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP)
Cisco networking fundamentals (CCNA level)
VPN configuration and management
MDM platforms (Intune, Jamf, but for Windows environments)
Basic Linux server administration
Ticketing systems (ServiceNow, Jira Service Desk)
Remote support tools (TeamViewer, AnyDesk, RDP)
Healthcare IT basics (EHR systems, HIPAA compliance)
Printer and peripheral troubleshooting
analysis.summary: You're overqualified in data science/ML and underqualified in practical IT support. This isn't a data analytics role disguised as IT—it's genuine hands-on field technician work requiring Windows infrastructure expertise you don't have. Your technical skills are real but irrelevant here; they need someone who troubleshoots routers and printers, not someone who builds machine learning models. Either pivot hard into infrastructure or find roles that leverage your actual data/analytics strengths.

As we grow from 12 to 20+ locations, we are building an internal team to support our technology systems, clinic infrastructure, and data operations.
 

Job Description

We are hiring a Technical Operations Associate to support our clinics and internal systems.

This is a hands-on, entry-level role where you will help troubleshoot real-world technology issues across our clinics while learning how our systems, data, and software work together behind the scenes.

This role is ideal for someone early in their career who is:

-good with technology

-enjoys solving problems

-willing to learn quickly

-comfortable working both in-office and in real environments

You will work directly with leadership and our technical team and will have the opportunity to grow into a larger systems or technical role over time.
 

What You’ll Do

Clinic IT & Technical Support

Troubleshoot WiFi, internet connectivity, and networking issues

Set up and maintain computers, workstations, and peripherals

Assist with basic server and system troubleshooting

Travel to local clinic locations to resolve technical issues

Systems & Technology Support

Help maintain and support internal systems (EHR, reporting tools, etc.)

Assist with resolving day-to-day technical issues across the company

Work with team members to identify and fix recurring system problems

Learning & Growth Projects (Over Time)

As you grow in the role, you may also:

Assist with data/reporting systems (SQL, Tableau, etc.)

Help support integrations between systems (EHR, CRM, etc.)

Participate in larger technology projects (cloud migration, system upgrades)
 

What We’re Looking For

We are NOT looking for someone who knows everything already.

We are looking for someone who:

-is naturally good with technology

-enjoys figuring things out

-is comfortable using Google/ChatGPT to solve problems

-takes initiative instead of waiting for instructions

-is reliable and follows through
 

Preferred Background (Flexible)

Computer Science, Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Engineering, or related field

OR strong hands-on experience with technology (building PCs, fixing systems, etc.)

0–2 years experience (internships or part-time experience count)

 

Nice to Have (Not Required)

Basic understanding of networking (routers, WiFi, etc.)

Exposure to SQL, data, or reporting tools

Experience troubleshooting technical issues

 

Location

Based in Northern Virginia (Herndon area)

Some travel to local clinic locations required

Compensation

$60,000 – $90,000 depending on experience

Strong growth opportunity as the company scales
Additional Bonus opportunities

 

Why This Role is Different

This is not a typical entry-level IT job.

You will:

-work on real systems in a growing company

-gain exposure to multiple technologies

-solve real problems across multiple locations

-have a clear path to grow into more advanced technical roles

 

How to Apply

Apply through Handshake and include a short answer to the following:

“Tell us about a time you had to figure out a technical problem you didn’t already know how to solve.”

applications.role: Hands-on technical support for multi-location healthcare clinics, including troubleshooting IT issues, maintaining systems and workstations, traveling to clinic locations for on-site support, and supporting internal technology systems like EHR and reporting tools with opportunity to grow into advanced technical roles
applications.jobDescription:
Job description
If you’re the person your friends and family go to when something tech-related breaks, this role is for you.

Company Overview

RxWellness is a fast-growing healthcare platform operating multiple chiropractic and wellness clinics across Northern Virginia. Our mission is to bring natural healthcare to the forefront while building a scalable, technology-driven clinic network.

As we grow from 12 to 20+ locations, we are building an internal team to support our technology systems, clinic infrastructure, and data operations.
 

Job Description

We are hiring a Technical Operations Associate to support our clinics and internal systems.

This is a hands-on, entry-level role where you will help troubleshoot real-world technology issues across our clinics while learning how our systems, data, and software work together behind the scenes.

This role is ideal for someone early in their career who is:

-good with technology

-enjoys solving problems

-willing to learn quickly

-comfortable working both in-office and in real environments

You will work directly with leadership and our technical team and will have the opportunity to grow into a larger systems or technical role over time.
 

What You’ll Do

Clinic IT & Technical Support

Troubleshoot WiFi, internet connectivity, and networking issues

Set up and maintain computers, workstations, and peripherals

Assist with basic server and system troubleshooting

Travel to local clinic locations to resolve technical issues

Systems & Technology Support

Help maintain and support internal systems (EHR, reporting tools, etc.)

Assist with resolving day-to-day technical issues across the company

Work with team members to identify and fix recurring system problems

Learning & Growth Projects (Over Time)

As you grow in the role, you may also:

Assist with data/reporting systems (SQL, Tableau, etc.)

Help support integrations between systems (EHR, CRM, etc.)

Participate in larger technology projects (cloud migration, system upgrades)
 

What We’re Looking For

We are NOT looking for someone who knows everything already.

We are looking for someone who:

-is naturally good with technology

-enjoys figuring things out

-is comfortable using Google/ChatGPT to solve problems

-takes initiative instead of waiting for instructions

-is reliable and follows through
 

Preferred Background (Flexible)

Computer Science, Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Engineering, or related field

OR strong hands-on experience with technology (building PCs, fixing systems, etc.)

0–2 years experience (internships or part-time experience count)

 

Nice to Have (Not Required)

Basic understanding of networking (routers, WiFi, etc.)

Exposure to SQL, data, or reporting tools

Experience troubleshooting technical issues

 

Location

Based in Northern Virginia (Herndon area)

Some travel to local clinic locations required

Compensation

$60,000 – $90,000 depending on experience

Strong growth opportunity as the company scales
Additional Bonus opportunities

 

Why This Role is Different

This is not a typical entry-level IT job.

You will:

-work on real systems in a growing company

-gain exposure to multiple technologies

-solve real problems across multiple locations

-have a clear path to grow into more advanced technical roles

 

How to Apply

Apply through Handshake and include a short answer to the following:

“Tell us about a time you had to figure out a technical problem you didn’t already know how to solve.”

Would need 6-12 months of traditional IT support experience before being viable for this role.

applications.role: Provide administrative and operational support to VP of Growth for Program Integration in the Defense sector, assisting with business development activities, meeting coordination, note-taking, and various business development operations tasks in a remote internship capacity
applications.jobDescription:
Here is a great opportunity to learn and work for a leading Fortune 500 company.  This fast-paced environment will have you working in their Defense sector working with many types of Business Development opportunities.  You will work mostly remote with potential travel to certain events and able to complete internal Leidos training to help further your upcoming career.

Program Integration, Business Development Team is looking for interns interested in a career in Business Development.  This position is an excellent opportunity for a college student with a Business background to support the Defense Growth team.

This position will require an individual with excellent adaptability, organization, communication skills, and is instinctively proactive in nature.  The ideal candidate would understand many business-related fundamentals as well as sales concepts and terms.

In this role, the candidate will need to thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, understand prioritization, have attention to detail, collaborate with internal stakeholders, and handle multiple simultaneous tasks.

This position primarily supports the VP of Growth for Program Integration, Defense sector.

Primary Responsibilities

Provide daily support to the VP of Growth for Program Integration

Assist Business Development Operations Analyst with Business Development action items as requested

Coordinate and schedule meetings

Accurate, organized, and punctual note taking abilities with after action report delivery

Self-Starter
 

Basic Qualifications

Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in business development, Business Administration, Marketing, or related field of study

US Citizenship Required

In good academic standing (GPA 2.5 or higher)

Proficient in MS Office 365 applications (Excel, MS PowerPoint, MS Word, Outlook, Teams)

Complete tasks individually and as a team participant

Have a basic understanding of business management terms

Strong desire and ability to keep organized

Candidate should be available 20-40 hours per week.  Hours can vary based on workload and candidate's school schedule
 

Preferred Qualifications

Ability to learn new software applications quickly

Experience with multiple software applications

Prefer candidates local to Huntsville, AL or remote.  90% telework. 10% local office work.

Strong written and verbal communication skills

If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares.

Original Posting:
March 20, 2026
For U.S. Positions: While subject to change based on business needs, Leidos reasonably anticipates that this job requisition will remain open for at least 3 days with an anticipated close date of no earlier than 3 days after the original posting date as listed above.

Pay Range:
Pay Range $41,600.00 - $75,200.00
The Leidos pay range for this job level is a general guideline only and not a guarantee of compensation or salary. Additional factors considered in extending an offer include (but are not limited to) responsibilities of the job, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity, alignment with market data, applicable bargaining agreement (if any), or other law.

#Remote

applications.role: Supporting the Business Intelligence and Data Analytics team by building metrics, dashboards, and data visualizations to help leadership make informed decisions. The role focuses on learning BI tools, data modeling, visualization techniques, and transforming complex data into clear insights while collaborating with technical and functional experts.
applications.jobDescription:
Job ID
322892
Job Title: Data Analyst / BI Developer Intern - Summer 2026
Job Category: Intern/Co-op
Time Type: Part time
Minimum Clearance Required to Start: None
Employee Type: Part-Time On-Call
Percentage of Travel Required: None
Type of Travel: None
* * *

The Opportunity:
As a Data Analytics / BI Developer Intern, you will support our Business Intelligence and Data Analytics team in building metrics, dashboards, and data visualizations that help leadership make informed decisions. You will gain hands-on experience working with BI tools, data modeling, and visualization techniques, while collaborating with technical and functional experts. This role offers an opportunity to learn how to transform complex data into clear, compelling insights that drive business outcomes. This position offers a hybrid work schedule and you will be required to report to the Arlington, VA office.

Responsibilities:
Assist in gathering analytical requirements from subject matter experts. Support the design and development of data models that optimize analytical reporting. Help build dashboards, reports, and visualizations to support end-user reporting needs. Contribute to data preparation tasks such as extraction, transformation, and cleansing. Learn to interpret data trends and present findngs in a clear and accessible way. Collaborate with team members to document processes and reporting standards.

Qualifications:

Required: 

Currently pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Applied Math, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related field.
Must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for employer sponsorship.
Must be eligible to obtain and maintain applicable security clearance.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).

Desired:

Familiarity with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.
Knowledge of SQL or other data query languages.
Previous academic, project, or internship experience with data visualization or analysis.
Minimum GPA of 3.0.
Experience with SharePoint and/or basic UI/UX principles.
-


What You Can Expect:

 A culture of integrity.

At CACI, we place character and innovation at the center of everything we do. As a valued team member, you’ll be part of a high-performing group dedicated to our customer’s missions and driven by a higher purpose – to ensure the safety of our nation.

An environment of trust.

CACI values the unique contributions that every employee brings to our company and our customers - every day. You’ll have the autonomy to take the time you need through a unique flexible time off benefit and have access to robust learning resources to make your ambitions a reality.

A focus on continuous growth.

Together, we will advance our nation's most critical missions, build on our lengthy track record of business success, and find opportunities to break new ground — in your career and in our legacy.



Pay Range:

There are a host of factors that can influence final salary including, but not limited to, geographic location, Federal Government contract labor categories and contract wage rates, relevant prior work experience, specific skills and competencies, education, and certifications. Our employees value the flexibility at CACI that allows them to balance quality work and their personal lives. We offer competitive compensation, benefits and learning and development opportunities. Our broad and competitive mix of benefits options is designed to support and protect employees and their families. At CACI, you will receive comprehensive benefits such as; healthcare, wellness, financial, retirement, family support, continuing education, and time off benefits.

Since this position can be worked in more than one location, the range shown is the national average for the position.

The proposed salary range for this position is:

$39,900-$79,900
CACI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.

The Apple role proves soft skills and learning ability, but the gap between 'built personal finance tool' and 'delivered BI dashboards to leadership' is significant. For an internship at CACI, the technical skills check boxes, but they need to understand this isn't just about knowing Tableau—it's about working in a clearance-required, documentation-heavy, mission-critical environment where 'good enough' doesn't cut it.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has solid technical foundations with the right tools (SQL, Python, R, Power BI, Tableau) and strong academic project work, making them technically qualified for an intern role. However, there's a critical red flag: zero professional BI/analytics experience. The Apple role was retail sales (not analytics despite the data mining mentioned), and the Handshake role is AI model training, not BI development. For an internship, the technical skills compensate somewhat, but the lack of U.S. work authorization clarity and security clearance eligibility is a potential dealbreaker for a defense contractor. If citizenship/clearance boxes check out, they're hirable but will need significant onboarding.
improvementPlan: Immediately clarify U.S. citizenship status and security clearance eligibility - this is non-negotiable for CACI roles and must be front-and-center on applications

Reframe work experience to emphasize analytical aspects: at Apple, highlight any data analysis you did (inventory forecasting, sales reporting, customer behavior analysis) rather than focusing purely on sales metrics

Complete 1-2 end-to-end BI projects that mirror real job responsibilities: gather mock requirements, design a data model, build ETL pipeline, create dashboard in Power BI/Tableau, and document the process
Get hands-on with SharePoint and learn basics of collaboration tools common in government contracting environments (Microsoft ecosystem focus)

Build a portfolio specifically showcasing BI work: create 3-4 dashboards solving real business problems with clear documentation of requirements gathering, data modeling decisions, and insights generated
Network with current CACI employees or attend their recruitment events to understand the culture and expectations around cleared positions
Explicitly add GPA to resume/profile if 3.0+ (they desire this) - if below 3.0, emphasize project work and certifications even more heavily
marketSkillsToLearn: Advanced DAX (Power BI) and calculated fields (Tableau) for complex analytical calculations
Dimensional modeling techniques (Kimball methodology, star schemas, slowly changing dimensions)
Data warehousing concepts and ETL/ELT best practices
SharePoint for collaborative BI environments (especially for government contractors)
Git/version control for BI assets and code collaboration
Alteryx or similar data preparation/blending tools popular in enterprise BI
Basic understanding of government security frameworks (NIST, FedRAMP) if targeting cleared positions
Documentation and technical writing skills for BI processes and standards
Agile/Scrum methodologies as applied to analytics projects
Advanced Excel (Power Query, Power Pivot) which remains critical in government contracting
analysis.summary: Technically competent candidate with strong foundational skills in SQL, Python, R, Power BI, and Tableau, plus solid academic project work. Major concern is complete absence of professional BI/analytics experience - work history is retail sales and AI training, not business intelligence development. For an internship, technical skills barely carry this across the threshold, but clearance eligibility uncertainty and lack of practical BI context are significant risks. If citizenship checks out and they can articulate analytical thinking from past roles, they're worth interviewing but will require substantial mentorship.

Boeing has shaped the course of human history through aerospace innovations.
