applications.role: Supporting DevOps and Data Engineering teams by contributing to CI/CD workflows, learning deployment environments, working with cloud technologies (AWS and Azure), and assisting with Data & AI initiatives including LLM pipeline projects while gaining hands-on experience with modern DevOps tools and practices.
applications.jobDescription:
Intern (Data Engineering)
Job Category: Data Engineering
Requisition Number: INTER003029
 

Posting Details
Posted: February 25, 2026
Full-Time
LocationsShowing 1 location
Reston, VA 20191, USA
Job Details
Description
The American College of Radiology (ACR) is home to nearly 40,000 medical imaging specialists who are shaping the future of patient care. As an intern here, you won’t just observe—you’ll contribute to work that drives national policy, advances clinical research, and strengthens quality and safety in healthcare.

You’ll be part of a mission‑driven nonprofit with more than 500 employees working together in a culture that values curiosity, integrity, and bold ideas. It’s a place where early‑career talent is encouraged to grow, take initiative, and make a real impact.

If these values resonate with you— • Integrity • Visionary thinking • Excellence • Leadership • Transparency • Member‑Driven purpose
~then you’ll fit right in. We’d love to have you bring your energy and perspective to our team.

 

We’re looking for a motivated Summer Intern (Data Engineering) interested in DevOps, Data Engineering, and cloud technologies. In this role, you’ll support both our DevOps and Data teams by helping gather technical requirements, learning how different deployment environments operate, and contributing to CI/CD workflows. You’ll gain hands-on experience with tools such as Azure DevOps, GitHub, Nexus Repository, AWS S3, AWS Glue, AWS Lambda, and AWS CloudFormation, while also assisting with Data & AI initiatives, including LLM pipeline projects.

This internship offers the opportunity to work on meaningful technical projects and build practical skills across modern DevOps and data engineering practices.  If that's your future, we'd love to review your resume! 

Responsibilities: 

Gathering requirements
Understanding various deployment environments
Working with our existing DevOps tools
Understand our current Data & AI projects including LLM pipelines.
Work on continuous integration and continuous delivery of these projects.
This role would require working with the following tools and services: AWS S3, AWS Glue, AWS Lambda, Azure pipeline, GitHub, Nexus repository, Nexus IQ scan, and AWS CloudFormation. 

 Additional Requirements: 

Must be pursuing a degree in Computer Science or an equivalent major.
Must be good at programming, ideally Python & SQL.
Hands-on expertise with Versioning tools preferably GitHub, and Nexus Repository.
Have a clear understanding and some hands-on experience with CI/CD tools available in AWS and Azure.
Strong attention to detail, good work ethic, ability to work on multiple projects simultaneously, and good communication skills
Solve novel and complex business problems with high quality maintainable code.
Communicate technical information clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
What to Expect

We will be conducting interviews beginning in March.
Our intention is to extend an offer for this opportunity by mid-April.
Interns will join the team on May 18th and conclude their experience on August 7th.
******************************************************************

Work Location: This position will be Remote.  Viable candidates will reside in the Eastern Standard Time zone so as to maintain a routine schedule of our core office hours (8:30A - 5:00P). Qualified candidates must have reliable internet service, professional discipline and have excellent time management skills.

*******************************************************************

ACR is committed to a total rewards compensation philosophy.  ACR’s goal is to pay competitively and equitably.  Interns work a 32.5 hour work week remotely, from 5/18/26 – 8/7/26, with every Friday afternoon off!  Depending on class level, a reasonable estimate of the hourly compensation range for this position is $19.00 – $23.00.  

*******************************************************************

If you would like to put your experience to great use in a professional team-oriented environment, please apply online. To learn more about ACR’s rewarding employee experience, culture, and benefits (for regular, full-time employees), visit: https://www.acr.org/Jobs


Equal Opportunity Employer/Protected Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities
This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights notice from the Department of Labor.

applications.role: Conducting policy research and data analysis to support transportation sector decarbonization efforts, including tracking environmental policies, analyzing vehicle and emissions datasets, developing transportation model inputs, and co-authoring research publications on the impact of policies and technologies on emissions, air quality, and public health
applications.jobDescription:
Policy and Data Analysis Intern (Washington DC)
Washington, District of Columbia (Hybrid)
Employment Type

Intern

Minimum Experience

Entry-level

Compensation

$23.87 hourly

The International Council on Clean Transportation (ICCT) is an award-winning, independent global non-profit research organization dedicated to designing and implementing policies that address the impacts of the global transportation sector on climate change and public health. We provide high-quality, data-driven research to public officials, as well as to civil society and industry. Our mission-driven and diverse workplace values intelligence, flexibility, transparency, and good humor.  



The ICCT is looking for a Policy and Data Analysis Intern based in either our Washington, D.C. or San Francisco, CA office.  This fellowship provides an opportunity to work at the intersection of policy and data analysis to support applied research conducted by internal ICCT programs as well as external partners committed to accelerating transitions to zero-emission electric vehicles. You will report to a Senior Researcher in ICCT’s Modeling Center. 



You Will: 

Track and compare environmental transportation policies adopted or proposed by national governments, states, and cities. 
Analyze and incorporate datasets relating to fleet/vehicle characteristics and energy/emissions performance into our models. 
Help develop inputs and scenarios for transportation models to quantify the impact of policies and technologies on emissions, air quality, public health, and charging infrastructure needs 
Co-author reports, journal articles, and other publications 
 

You Have: 

A strong academic record in engineering, economics, policy, transportation, environment, or energy studies as a rising senior or a graduate student. 
Demonstrated academic or professional experience in vehicle technology, climate change, air quality, energy, or environmental issues; familiarity with environmental and energy challenges in the transportation sector is preferred. 
Superior proficiency in data analysis with Python (pandas, scipy, numpy, matplotlib) or R (tidyverse) alongside Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, lookups, complex conditional statements). 
Demonstrated skills in policy or related research, analysis, and presentation. 
Excellent interpersonal and English communication skills, both written and verbal. 
Ability to work independently and engage effectively with team members. 
 

Our Benefits:      

This is a full-time (40 hours per week) internship opportunity that will be based in our Washington, D.C., or San Francisco office for 12 weeks and have an hourly rate starting at $23.87(DC) or $26.25(SF). Interns are eligible for a limited benefits package, which includes paid time off, internet subsidy, and other benefits. Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.  

 

Application:      

To apply, please submit a resume and,  

a cover letter indicating your interest.
one writing sample primarily written by the applicant (in English) that demonstrates applied research and data analysis capabilities.
one Python or R code sample demonstrating the requisite analysis skills. This should not be a boilerplate code from a course assignment. 
the names and contact information of two professional or academic references.  
 

Incomplete applications will be given less priority. A start date between May 18th ,2026 and June 8th ,2026, would be preferred. Applications will be accepted through March 1st, 2026.

applications.role: Entry-level Software Implementation Consultant role focused on supporting federal and government contractor clients through SaaS solution implementation, AI-powered contract management system configuration, client workshops, and project documentation while developing technical consulting skills in a cohort-based onboarding program
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description
At Icertis, we are transforming how United States federal agencies and government organizations manage their most important contracts—spanning defense, healthcare, public safety, and beyond. 

Our AI-driven solutions are designed specifically to improve efficiency and compliance for US government contractors and federal agencies. Our dedication to innovation and excellence in Microsoft technology was recognized with the 2024 Microsoft Defense and Intelligence Partner of the Year award for outstanding achievements supporting federal missions. 

Join our public sector professional services team as part of a cohort-based hiring model. Cohort members start on the same date and participate in a collaborative onboarding and development journey. You will help drive technological transformation for the US government contractors and federal agencies where your work will make a direct impact on national operations. Discover more about Icertis Public Sector Solutions. 

About the Opportunity 

You’ll join an experienced consulting team in a collaborative, mission-driven environment that encourages curiosity and hands on learning. As a Software Implementation Consultant, you’ll work on meaningful projects that help public sector organizations modernize essential systems across healthcare, aerospace, defense, and public safety. 

At Icertis, you’ll learn from certified experts and mentors while practical experience with enterprise SaaS delivery early in your career. Through training, platform certifications, and real project work, you’ll build skills in technology, AI, business processes, and client consulting. You’ll earn Icertis certifications that strengthen your long-term career in SaaS and grow into roles such as Functional Consultant, Senior Consultant, or Solution Architect as your expertise develops. 

This is a full-time, onsite position located in our Northern Virginia Reston Town Center office (5 days per week). #LI-RS1

Responsibilities
Work with an experienced consulting team to support client onboarding at Icertis ICI.  
Support pre-implementation workshops to better understand their product requirements, support system demos, and user training sessions with guidance from senior consultants. 
Contribute to project documentation, data reporting, and client communications. 
Assist with solution configuration, testing, design activities, to align Icertis solutions with client needs. 
Learn about client AI requirements and Icertis AI capabilities.  
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, QA, CloudOps) to support quality delivery.  
Learn how government processes and security requirements translate into SaaS solutions. 
Key Expectations 

Show confidence and professionalism in client-facing settings. 
Communicate clearly and collaborate effectively at all levels. 
Demonstrate learning agility and adaptability with new tools and processes. 
Show interest in SaaS consulting and a desire to build trust with public sector clients. 
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science Business, Information Management, Information Technology, Business Analyst or related technical field. 
Prior experience with government contractors or federal agencies in our industry during internships or through work is highly desirable. Prior exposure to software delivery, process improvement through projects or coursework is a plus. 
Demonstrated interest in the practical application of AI tools, with hands-on experience gained through coursework, internships, projects, research, or self-directed learning (e.g., using AI tools to automate workflows, analyze data, build prototypes, or enhance software implementations beyond basic ChatGPT usage). 
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to work with curiosity to learn. 
Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate in a global team environment. 
Interest in technology consulting, SaaS platforms, or enterprise business applications. 
Motivation to work in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment supporting U.S. public sector transformation. 
Requirements: 

Must have earned a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree between 2023 and Spring 2026 
Ability to attend onsite final interview if invited 
Be available to start work along with other cohort hires on Monday, June 22nd, 2026
 

This position will be working on US government contracts which require either US Citizens or US Persons (Permanent Residents) depending on the project.  Additionally, this role may require the ability to obtain and maintain a US government Top Secret security clearance.  

Salary range and description

Min $75,000 - Max $90,000 USD per year 

Pay offered will vary based on job-related factors such as location, experience, training, skills, and abilities. In addition to the base salary, an equity component is included in the compensation package. 

Our Commitments: Icertis is committed to:   

Icertians (employees) and their family's physical, mental and financial health and wellbeing

applications.role: Providing research support to SHRM market research teams through survey design, data analysis, AI-assisted text analytics, and data visualization to translate workforce and organizational research into actionable insights
applications.jobDescription:
Intern, Market Research
Req #1230
1800 Duke St, Alexandria, VA 22314, USA
Job Description
Posted Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 12:00 AM
SHRM is a member-driven catalyst for creating better workplaces where people and businesses thrive together. As the trusted authority on all things work, SHRM is the foremost expert, researcher, advocate, and thought leader on issues and innovations impacting today’s evolving workplaces.  With nearly 340,000 members in 180 countries, SHRM touches the lives of more than 362 million workers and their families globally.
 

SUMMARY

SHRM’s paid internship program enables students to gain valuable real-world experience through tailored assignments in their areas of interest and fields of study. Students will be tasked with team-specific projects and tasks and will participate in professional development projects and events to prepare them for their future careers. This role provides research support to SHRM market research teams in survey research, data analysis, research communication, and data visualization. The intern will provide support for ongoing research projects. Specific responsibilities may include survey design, survey programming, data analysis, and literature review. 

This role is well-suited for students interested in applying social science research methods and analytics to real-world organizational and workforce challenges.

This is a paid internship starting at $20.00/Hour.

This is 10-weeks summer internship from June 2, 2026 - August 6, 2026

Key Responsibilities

• Support the design, programming, and analysis of survey-based research using platforms such as Qualtrics.

• Assist with data analysis and interpretation, including cross-tabulations, trend analysis, and summarizing key findings from survey and customer feedback data.

• Apply AI-assisted text analytics and natural language processing (NLP) techniques to analyze open-ended responses, identify themes, and detect patterns in unstructured data.

• Conduct secondary research and environmental scans to support SHRM’s research initiatives and thought leadership.

• Support Voice of the Customer (VoC) research programs, including analysis of sentiment, customer feedback, and emerging themes across multiple feedback channels.

• Collaborate with the research team to prepare presentations, reports, and executive summaries, translating data into actionable insights.

Key Qualifications / Skills

• Quantitative and analytical skills – Ability to work with survey data and statistical outputs using tools such as Excel, SPSS, R, or similar tools to identify patterns, trends, and insights.

• Data visualization and storytelling – Experience or interest in building dashboards and communicating insights using tools such as Power BI, Excel, or other analytics platforms.

• Research and critical thinking – Ability to conduct secondary research, literature reviews, and competitive intelligence to support organizational and market research initiatives.

• AI and text analytics familiarity – Experience or interest in using AI, natural language processing (NLP), or machine learning tools to analyze open-ended text, identify themes, or detect patterns in qualitative data.

• Strong written and verbal communication skills – Ability to synthesize complex data and research findings into clear, concise summaries and actionable insights for internal stakeholders.

Ability to effectively leverage artificial intelligence (AI) tools and technologies to streamline workflows, enhance productivity, and improve overall work quality

Preferred Area of Study

Students currently pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree in a social science, business, or analytics-related field, including:

• Industrial / Organizational Psychology
• Organizational Sciences
• Sociology
• Political Science / Public Policy
• Market Research or Marketing
• Data Analytics / Data Science
• Business Analytics
• Human Resources or Organizational Studies

Physical Requirements

This position operates in a typical office environment and requires the ability to perform essential job functions with or without reasonable accommodation. Physical requirements may include:

Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.
Frequent use of hands and fingers for typing, handling documents, and using office equipment.
Occasional standing, walking, bending, and reaching.
Ability to lift and carry up to 30 pounds as needed.
Clear verbal and written communication skills for effective interaction with colleagues and stakeholders.
Work environment

Hybrid Schedule (3 Days In-Office / 2 Days Remote):
This position follows a hybrid work schedule, with Tuesday through Thursday in office and Monday and Friday remote.  Employees must be available during standard business hours, with core hours beginning between 8:00–9:00 a.m. and concluding between 5:00–6:00 p.m. local time.

Our employment practices are in accordance with the laws that prohibit discrimination against qualified individuals on the basis of race, religion, color, gender, age, national origin, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran’s status, marital status, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, or any other status protected by applicable law.
 
SHRM is an equal opportunity employer (Minority/Female/Disabled/Veteran).
 
We do not sponsor applicants for work visas.

Specific research methodology mentioned. No evidence of experience with survey-specific analytical techniques.
Specialized market research methodology. While candidate has customer experience, lacks formal VoC research framework knowledge.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has strong technical chops with R, Python, ML, and NLP that check important boxes, but they're coming from a tech/retail background with zero survey research experience. The Qualtrics gap is killer—that's the primary tool for this role. Their AI and data skills are legitimately strong, but they've never designed a survey, run cross-tabs, or worked with actual market research methodologies. For an internship where they'd need to hit the ground running on Qualtrics and survey design, this is risky.
improvementPlan: Immediately take free Qualtrics training course (Qualtrics XM Basecamp certification) to demonstrate platform familiarity before interview
Complete 1-2 online courses in survey research methodology and questionnaire design (Coursera or MOOC platforms)

Learn SPSS basics through free IBM training or university resources—at minimum understand syntax and output interpretation
Study Voice of Customer frameworks and apply sentiment analysis to real customer feedback dataset (Reddit, reviews) to bridge NLP skills to VoC
Reframe Apple experience to emphasize data collection, customer insights analysis, and pattern identification from customer interactions
Review academic literature on organizational behavior or workforce trends to demonstrate ability to conduct literature reviews

MaxDiff and conjoint analysis (advanced survey techniques)
analysis.summary: Strong technical foundation with R, Python, ML, and NLP that aligns well with analytical requirements, but glaring gap in survey research experience and Qualtrics platform knowledge. Candidate has proven learning agility and solid data analysis skills, but lacks the core market research methodologies central to this internship. Would need rapid onboarding on survey design and research frameworks. Acceptable risk for an internship if candidate demonstrates quick learning and genuine interest in market research methodologies.

qaHistory.q: Reason For Leaving?
qaHistory.a:
I'm graduating in May 2026 and looking for full-time opportunities where I can apply what I've learned in a professional environment. My time at Apple was valuable—I learned a lot about customer service, technical troubleshooting, and how to communicate complex ideas clearly to different audiences. But I realized through my coursework and projects, especially working with Python, R, and machine learning, that I'm really drawn to roles where I can work directly with data to solve business problems and uncover insights.

I've been building those skills through my classes and side projects—like the retail analytics project where I used R and tidymodels to predict customer behavior, or the financial intelligence platform I built that processes transaction data and generates insights. I want to be in a role where that kind of analytical thinking is the core of the work, not just a side piece. This SHRM internship is exactly that—it's focused on survey research, data analysis, and using AI to extract meaning from text, which aligns directly with what I've been studying and what I'm genuinely excited about. I'm ready to transition from retail into a research and analytics-focused role where I can contribute to real organizational insights.

applications.role: Supporting the Digital team with data analysis, software development, and AI/automation implementation to drive business insights and process improvements in the clean energy infrastructure sector
applications.jobDescription:
Digital Intern – Summer 2026
Tysons, VA
Overview 

Madison Energy Infrastructure (“MEI”) believes in the power of clean energy infrastructure and has quickly emerged as a preeminent developer, investor, asset owner, and operator of distributed generation. This role represents a critical next step in MEI’s evolution toward offering direct, end-to-end services to Fortune 500 corporates, public entities, nonprofits, and other private customers alike. 

As a Summer 2026 Intern, you will gain hands-on experience supporting high-impact initiatives within the Digital team. This role is designed to provide meaningful exposure to the clean energy industry, real project ownership, and the opportunity to contribute to a fast-growing organization. 

What you’ll do 

Assist with data analysis, reporting, and visualization to support business insights.
Support development of software tools, dashboards, and internal applications.
Help prototype and implement AI and automation use cases to streamline processes.
Collaborate with Digital team members to translate business needs into technical solutions.
Document solutions, workflows, and learnings for reuse and scalability. 
What we’re looking for 

Currently pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field.
Interest in data, software development, and emerging technologies (AI, automation, analytics).
Familiarity with at least one programming or scripting language (e.g., Python, SQL, JavaScript).
Strong curiosity, problem-solving mindset, and willingness to learn.
Clear communicator and collaborative team player. 
Location & Schedule: 

This is a full-time (40 hours per week), hybrid internship based in our Tysons, VA office. Interns will work four days per week in-office and one day remotely to maximize in-person collaboration and exposure to the business. 

The 10-week program runs from June 1 through August 7, 2026. Interns will participate in our all-company offsite on June 24, 2026 as part of their immersive experience. The office will be closed in observance of Juneteenth (June 19, 2026) and Independence Day (July 3, 2026). 

Commitment & Compensation:  

This is a paid internship. The hourly pay range for this role is $20–$40. Compensation within this range is determined based on factors including academic level (undergraduate vs. graduate), year in program, and relevant experience. 

To Apply:  

To apply, please submit your resume through our careers page. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Selected candidates will be contacted regarding next steps in the interview process. 

 
How We Work
At Madison Energy Infrastructure, we believe in the power of clean energy infrastructure to drive real impact—for our customers, our partners, and the communities we serve. We’ve quickly emerged as a preeminent developer, investor, and operator of distributed generation, helping organizations across sectors meet their energy goals with certainty, speed, and trust. Our robust portfolio consists of over half a gigawatt of clean energy infrastructure projects across more than 25 states.
 
We’re building a team that thrives on ownership and ambition. As we expand into direct, end-to-end services for Fortune 500 companies, public entities, and nonprofits alike, we rely on collaborative thinkers who bring structure to complexity, energy to execution, and curiosity to every challenge.
 
Our environment is fast-paced and outcome-oriented—but grounded in the belief that how we work together matters as much as what we deliver. If you’re excited to shape the future of clean energy and be part of a high-performance, high-integrity team, we’d love to meet you.
 
Madison Energy Infrastructure is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status.

missingSkills.reason: MEI operates specifically in distributed generation and clean energy infrastructure. Zero demonstrated interest or knowledge in this vertical. For a mission-driven company, this shows lack of research and genuine interest.

Job explicitly mentions 'dashboards' and 'visualization to support business insights.' Candidate lists these in skills but has zero project evidence of actually building dashboards in these platforms. Chart.js is not enterprise-grade BI tooling.

Role requires 'development of software tools, dashboards, and internal applications' for Fortune 500 clients. All projects shown are consumer-facing or academic. No B2B software development experience.
Job explicitly seeks 'AI and automation use cases to streamline processes.' Candidate shows ML/AI model training but no workflow automation, RPA, or business process automation experience.
Role requires 'Document solutions, workflows, and learnings for reuse and scalability.' No evidence of creating technical documentation, API docs, or knowledge bases.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has solid technical fundamentals and legitimate hands-on experience with the core technologies MEI needs (Python, SQL, JavaScript, ML, cloud infrastructure). The AI training work at Handshake and diverse project portfolio demonstrate adaptability and technical curiosity. However, there's a glaring disconnect: zero demonstrated interest in clean energy, sustainability, or infrastructure - concerning for a mission-driven company. The experience skews heavily consumer/retail rather than B2B enterprise tools. For an intern role, the technical bar is met, but the lack of domain alignment and enterprise software experience are notable gaps.
improvementPlan: Immediately research MEI's portfolio and clean energy sector - be prepared to articulate WHY you want to work in this industry beyond 'it's a tech role.' Read about distributed generation, solar/battery storage, and corporate renewable energy procurement.

Build one dashboard project using Tableau or Power BI (free student licenses available) with energy-related data (solar generation, EV adoption, grid data). This addresses both the tool gap and domain gap in one project.

Create a small automation project (Python script that automates a business workflow - data pipeline, report generation, email automation) and document it thoroughly with README, workflow diagrams, and business value proposition.

Reframe your Apple B2B experience more strategically in interviews - emphasize the enterprise MDM consulting, translating technical requirements for business owners, and Fortune 500-style solution architecture.
Prepare specific examples of 'translating business needs into technical solutions' from your projects using the STAR method - this is explicitly what they're looking for.
During interviews, ask intelligent questions about their tech stack, data infrastructure, and how the Digital team supports renewable energy deployment - show genuine curiosity about their mission.

analysis.summary: Candidate presents strong technical capabilities across Python, SQL, JavaScript, ML, and cloud platforms with legitimate hands-on experience. The technical foundation meets the internship bar. However, significant red flags include zero demonstrated interest in clean energy/sustainability (concerning for mission-driven MEI) and limited enterprise B2B software development experience. For an intern role competing against candidates who've specifically targeted clean tech, this is a solid maybe - hirable if they can articulate genuine interest in the mission during interviews, but not a slam dunk.

qaHistory.q: What interests you about Madison and this internship? *
qaHistory.a: I'm drawn to Madison Energy Infrastructure because you're doing exactly what I want to spend my career working on — using technical skills to solve real infrastructure problems that matter. The clean energy space is one of the few industries where I can legitimately say the work has tangible environmental impact, and that's important to me. What specifically caught my attention about this internship is the combination of data analysis, software development, and AI implementation all in one role. At Handshake, I've been training multimodal AI systems by evaluating computer vision outputs and writing detailed prompts to optimize model behavior, which taught me how to think about AI as a practical tool rather than just a buzzword. I built a full-stack financial platform called Flashconverter that processes bank statements through a custom machine learning algorithm and generates Excel analytics dashboards, so I understand how to take raw data and turn it into something people can actually use to make decisions. The fact that you're looking for someone to prototype AI and automation use cases is exciting because I've seen firsthand how much faster you can move when you automate repetitive processes — at Apple, I identified business clients by analyzing purchase patterns and connecting them to our enterprise team, which was basically process optimization through better data use. I'm also genuinely curious about how distributed generation infrastructure works and what the technical challenges are in scaling clean energy projects across 25+ states. The hybrid structure in Tysons works well for me since I'm based in Northern Virginia, and the idea of working four days in-office actually appeals to me because I learn faster when I can ask questions in person and see how experienced people approach problems. This feels like the right combination of technical depth, mission-driven work, and real exposure to how a fast-growing company operates.

applications.role: Supporting data-driven decision making by developing analytical reports, dashboards, and insights using financial and medical operational data to inform business performance and identify improvement opportunities within a healthcare organization
applications.jobDescription:
Data Analytics Intern
Job Category: Intern
Requisition Number: DATAA006494
 

Posting Details
Posted: March 16, 2026
Full-Time
Hybrid
LocationsShowing 1 location
Washington, DC 20002, USA
Job Details
Description
Mid-Atlantic Permanente Medical Group (MAPMG) invites applications for a Data Analytics Intern to join our Analytics / Business Intelligence department. This position is a full-time, 40 hours per week, internship opportunity based out of our regional office located in Washington, D.C. with a hybrid work model. Please note, this position is ineligible for benefits. The summer internship program runs for about 12 weeks with a start date of May 29th and an end date of August 14th. Click here to learn more about MAPMG’s Internship Program.

Mid-Atlantic Permanente Medical Group is comprised of more than 1,700 Permanente physicians and nearly 300 staff professionals who come together to make a positive impact on the health and lives of more than 800,000 members in Virginia, Maryland, and the District of Columbia.

Reporting to a Senior Data Analyst / Analytics Supervisor, the Data Analytics Intern supports MAPMG in making strategic, data-driven decisions by assisting with the analysis, manipulation, and reporting of financial and medical operational data. In this role, you will work with MAPMG data systems and tools to develop analytical reports and insights that inform business performance and identify opportunities for improvement.

You can expect to:

Develop data queries, database tables, and analytical infrastructure to support reporting and business insights.
Prepare reports, dashboards, and visualizations using tools such as SQL, Excel, Tableau, or Power BI.
Partner with analysts and internal stakeholders to understand business questions and translate them into analytical solutions.
Provide analytical support for monthly reporting, business cases, and operational performance monitoring.
Analyze financial and medical operational datasets to identify trends, performance drivers, and opportunities for improvement.
Respond to stakeholder requests for detailed data analysis to support business decisions.
Support the development of ETL processes and assist with maintaining data structures used for reporting and analytics.
What is required:

Must be a current student enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program in Health Informatics, Statistics, Epidemiology, Business Analytics, Data Science, or a related field. Recent graduates are not eligible for the internship program.
Must be available to work full-time (40 hours per week) for a minimum of 12 weeks.
Previous experience or coursework related to data analytics, healthcare data, or business intelligence is a plus.
Proficiency in SQL and experience with Tableau or Power BI required. Familiarity with Excel and relational databases strongly preferred. Exposure to Python, SAS, or Databricks is a plus.
Strong ability to analyze complex datasets, structure ambiguous problems, and communicate insights clearly.
Strong communication, organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to present analytical findings to stakeholders.
Must be able to commute to the Washington, D.C. regional office as required for a hybrid work schedule.
Candidates must either currently live in or be willing to relocate to the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area.
Must provide documentation of required COVID-19 and influenza vaccinations as a condition of employment.
The starting hourly rate for this position ranges from $20.00 to $25.00, which considers an applicant’s skills and qualifications, certain degrees and prior job experience, training, market data, and other relevant factors. Internships are non-exempt positions and are not eligible for employee benefits.

Equity, Inclusion, and Diversity:

MAPMG continuously works to identify and mitigate healthcare inequities, and that starts with providing an inclusive, supportive environment for our physicians and staff. We encourage applicants of any race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or national origin who value diversity and will commit to practicing culturally competent healthcare.

External hires must pass a background check and drug screening.
We are proud to be an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer.
We value our diversity and E/O/E M/F/D/V.


Equal Opportunity Employer
This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights notice from the Department of Labor.

Primary concern is complete lack of healthcare domain experience - this medical group role requires understanding clinical and financial operational metrics from day one, which will require significant onboarding. Communication skills from Apple retail experience are valuable but don't substitute for analytical stakeholder management. Solid candidate worth interviewing, but expect learning curve on healthcare fundamentals. Score of 72 reflects technical readiness but domain inexperience - hire if willing to invest in healthcare training during the 12-week internship.

qaHistory.q: What interests you about Madison and this internship? *
qaHistory.a:
I'm drawn to MAPMG because of its direct impact on healthcare delivery for over 800,000 members across the Mid-Atlantic region. As someone who has built financial intelligence platforms and worked extensively with data visualization tools like Tableau and Power BI, I'm excited about the opportunity to apply these skills in a mission-driven healthcare setting where the insights I generate could directly improve patient care and operational efficiency. What particularly interests me is the hybrid of financial and medical operational data—I've worked with retail analytics and business intelligence at Apple, where I identified business opportunities through probing customer needs and passing leads to our enterprise team, but I've never had the chance to work with healthcare data where the stakes are so much higher and the potential for meaningful impact is clear.

The 12-week structure is ideal for diving deep into one domain. I'm specifically interested in learning how healthcare organizations balance financial performance with clinical outcomes, and how analytics can identify improvement opportunities that serve both. I've built ETL pipelines and developed analytical infrastructure in my personal projects—like Flashconverter, where I engineered a full data processing pipeline from PDF bank statements to categorized insights with 70-95% ML confidence scores—and I'm eager to apply that same end-to-end thinking to support MAPMG's strategic decisions. The chance to partner with senior analysts and translate business questions into analytical solutions matches exactly how I approached my Retail Strategy project at Mason, where I used R and tidymodels to identify which customer behaviors actually drove revenue conversion, then presented those findings as actionable recommendations.

I'm also motivated by the challenge of working in a regulated environment where data accuracy and compliance matter from day one. I know I'll have a learning curve on healthcare-specific metrics and terminology, but my technical foundation in SQL, Python, and dashboarding tools is solid, and I'm ready to immerse myself in understanding how medical groups operate. This role would let me grow as an analyst while contributing to an organization whose mission—making a positive impact on health and lives—aligns with why I want to build a career in data-driven decision making.

applications.role: The role focuses on streamlining marketing content operations through AI enablement and workflow automation, including WordPress content management optimization, PowerPoint production workflow improvements, and developing reusable AI-powered solutions to improve marketing team efficiency and scalability.
applications.jobDescription:
Marketing Automation & AI Enablement Intern (Summer 2026)
McLean, Virginia, United States; Richmond, Virginia, United States
Who We Are:

Exiger transforms supply chains into a strategic advantage—advancing our mission to make the world a safer and more transparent place to succeed. Our AI platform, 1Exiger, delivers instant visibility into complex supplier ecosystems, leveraging proprietary data and advanced AI to surface risk, automate compliance, and unlock efficiencies and cost savings to strengthen long-term resilience. Trusted by 550+ global customers—including Fortune 500 companies and U.S. government agencies—Exiger is a recognized, award-winning leader in supply chain AI and a FedRAMP® authorized provider to the federal government.

Marketing Automation & AI Enablement Intern (Summer 2026)
Location: McLean, VA or Richmond, VA (Hybrid)
Duration: June 15, 2026 – August 21, 2026
Pay Rate: $25/hour

Role Summary
Exiger is seeking a highly motivated and intellectually curious Marketing Automation & AI Enablement Intern to join our Marketing team for Summer 2026. Reporting to marketing technology and campaign leaders, this intern will work on high-impact initiatives focused on streamlining content operations, improving workflow efficiency, and scaling the practical use of AI across the marketing organization.

This is a full-time, paid internship designed for students who enjoy combining marketing, technology, and problem-solving. The role offers hands-on exposure to real operational challenges and the opportunity to build solutions that are adopted by cross-functional teams. The position follows a hybrid schedule and may be based in McLean, VA or Richmond, VA, depending on location.

What You'll Do:
As a Marketing Automation & AI Enablement Intern, you will work on a defined set of projects with clear success criteria, focused on improving how the marketing team operates at scale:

WordPress Content Automation & Image Library Optimization

Upload, organize, tag, and structure Exiger’s image library within WordPress

Identify patterns in metadata and propose improved tagging standards

Recommend or prototype automation approaches to streamline content publishing workflows

PowerPoint Production Workflow Optimization

Audit current slide creation workflows, including common requests and bottlenecks

Research tools such as AI slide generators, template automation, or formatting aids

Propose updated PowerPoint templates and workflows that reduce manual effort and improve consistency

AI Enablement & Internal Workflow Support

Test, document, and propose workflows using ChatGPT Enterprise and other AI tools

Build reusable prompt libraries or lightweight automations (e.g., summaries, templates, tagging suggestions)

Document workflows in a way that non-technical team members can easily adopt

Additional Operational Support

Contribute to ad hoc process improvement initiatives across the marketing organization

Assist with evaluating new tools, documenting processes, and supporting emerging high-impact projects aligned with operational efficiency goals

 
What You Need:
Rising junior, senior, or grad student currently pursuing a degree in Marketing, Digital Media, Design, Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related field

Strong interest in AI tools, automation, and workflow optimization

Familiarity with WordPress or other CMS tools

Basic understanding of design principles and PowerPoint

Comfortable researching technology solutions and documenting processes clearly

Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with a cross-functional team

Interest in applied, business-facing problem solving (not purely theoretical work)

Bonus Skills (Not Required):

Experience experimenting with tools such as ChatGPT, automation platforms (e.g., Zapier, n8n), Canva, Adobe tools, or REST APIs

Curiosity and willingness to test and iterate with emerging AI technologies

 

Why Intern with Us:
Valuable hands-on experience in a dynamic and supportive team

Mentorship from industry experts
Hybrid work environment with meaningful in-office collaboration

Hands-on experience working with an award winning Marketing team

Dedicated mentorship and professional development opportunities

Exposure to real-world SaaS security, compliance, and risk management challenges

Inclusive, mission-driven culture focused on impact, learning, and growth

Networking Opportunities within the organization
 

#Li-hybrid #Li-DNI

This is a hybrid position based in either McLean, VA or Richmond, VA. The pay rate is $25.00 /hour.

Exiger is named a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Supplier Risk Management, twice selected as one of Fast Company's 'Brands That Matter,' and recipient of the Third Party Risk Association's Innovator Award, Exiger's technology has been recognized by leading analyst evaluations and 50+ awards. Learn more at Exiger.com and follow Exiger on LinkedIn.

At Exiger, our values define how we work and why we lead. We are mission-inspired, imagination-driven, trust-anchored, and compassion-focused—committed to building technology that makes the world safer, more transparent, and more resilient.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability or protected veteran status, or any other legally protected basis, in accordance with applicable law.

Exiger’s hybrid work policy is periodically reviewed and adjusted to align with evolving business needs.

applications.role: Entry-level fellowship focused on applying data science and statistical analysis to support political, non-profit, and corporate clients through data cleaning, visualization, polling operations, and developing innovative analytics solutions for social impact organizations
applications.jobDescription:
Data Science Fellow
Washington, DC
About BlueLabs

BlueLabs is a leading provider of analytics services and technology dedicated to helping our partners do the most good with their data. Our team of analysts, scientists, engineers, and strategists hail from diverse backgrounds yet share a passion for using data to solve the world’s greatest social and analytical challenges. Since our inception we’ve worked with more than 400 organizations ranging from advocacy groups, unions, political campaigns, and international groups. In addition, we service an ever-expanding portfolio of commercial clients in the automotive, travel, CPG, entertainment, healthcare, media, and telecom industries. Along the way, we’ve developed some of the most innovative tools available in analytics, media optimization, reporting, and influencer outreach.

About The Team:

The BlueLabs Data Science Team develops deep expertise and uses data to inform strategic advice to our clients. Our polling work designs, implements, and fields tools that power our analysis and messaging. We take a deep dive into research that looks at what motivates people to vote, buy a product, hold an opinion, and whether campaigns are successful. Our statistical research identifies areas where we can better assess the impact of various factors in the campaign and polling areas. Our fast-paced, impact-driven team conducts innovative analysis to support our clients’ work. We bring definition to challenging problems, creatively identify data sources, then design analytics that accurately measure impact and move the needle every day. We work in changing, ambiguous environments, so we embrace nuance, inclusivity, and complexity to deliver programs that work for the diverse groups of individuals who rely on us.

About The Role:

Fellows will be provided a chance to work directly with data scientists, strategists, analysts, and client leads on meaningful, hands-on work, while being guided and supported by their manager. Fellows will also be expected to complete a capstone project as part of their program with BlueLabs.

The Data Science Fellow will work with our team to help our clients harness their data to improve and test their outcomes. 

The Data Science Fellow will work with BlueLabs for 12 weeks from June 3, 2026 - August 23, 2026.

In this position you will:

Find new and innovative ways to apply new and existing data to our work
Help expand our Influencers product including identity resolution and network analysis 
Assist in the creation, maintenance and improvement of our internal tools that power things like our polling operations
Perform rigorous data cleaning and analysis to inform our work across our political, non-profit and corporate projects
What We Are Seeking:

Genuine passion for working with a progressive social impact organization
Familiarity with a coding language such as R or Python - SQL is a plus!
Familiarity with general statistical concepts and best practices
Interest or experience in creating informative and engaging data visualizations
Interest or experience in applying foundations of statistics in a work or project setting
Ability to communicate technical concepts to a non-technical audience both in writing and verbally
What We Offer

BlueLabs offers a friendly work environment and competitive benefits package including:

premier health insurance plan
paid time off
professional development & learning stipend
flexible working hours
remote/telecommuting options
and more! 
BlueLabs is willing to complete paperwork for successful candidates who are students and are interested in earning academic credit for the fellowship experience with us.

The compensation for this role is $25 per hour, at 40 hours per week.

At BlueLabs, we celebrate, support and thrive on differences. Not only do they benefit our services, products, and community, but most importantly, they are to the benefit of our team. Qualified people of all races, ethnicities, ages, sex, genders, sexual orientations, national origins, gender identities, marital status, religions, veterans statuses, disabilities and any other protected classes are strongly encouraged to apply. As an equal opportunity workplace and an affirmative action employer, BlueLabs is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. BlueLabs endeavors to make reasonable accommodations to the known physical or mental limitations of qualified applicants with a disability unless the accommodation would impose an undue hardship on the operation of our business. If an applicant believes they require such assistance to complete the application or to participate in an interview, or has any questions or concerns, they should contact the Director, People Operations.  BlueLabs participates in E-verify. EEO is the Law (Link to external DOL site)

Collection of Personal Information Notice:

As you are likely aware, by submitting your job application, you are submitting personal information to our company. We collect various categories of personal information, including identifiers, protected classifications, professional or employment related information and sensitive personal information. We may retain and use this information for up to three years, in order to come to a decision on whether or not you are a good fit for our company. We may also retain or use some of this information to comply with any requirements under law, or for purposes of defending ourselves in any litigation. We do not use this information for any other purpose, or share it with third parties, unless you become an employee. To learn more, or to see our full Notice to Job Applicants, please click here.

missingSkills.reason: BlueLabs explicitly mentions polling work is core to the team - designs, implements, and fields tools that power analysis and messaging. Candidate has zero demonstrated experience with survey research, questionnaire design, or polling operations.

The job explicitly calls out helping expand their Influencers product including identity resolution and network analysis. Candidate shows no graph analytics, social network analysis, or entity resolution work.

BlueLabs serves political campaigns, unions, and advocacy groups. Understanding voter behavior, campaign metrics, and political data is fundamental. Candidate's experience is entirely retail/commercial with zero political/advocacy exposure.

The role emphasizes 'accurately measure impact' and 'assess the impact of various factors in campaign areas.' Candidate shows predictive modeling but no A/B testing, experimental design, or causal inference methodology.

Job mentions 'what motivates people to vote, buy a product, hold an opinion' - requires understanding of behavioral economics, psychology-informed analytics. Candidate's work is primarily technical/transactional.

First requirement is 'Genuine passion for working with a progressive social impact organization.' Candidate profile shows zero mention of political engagement, volunteer work, or social impact initiatives. This is a dealbreaker for culture fit.

experienceGaps: No demonstrated experience with political campaigns, advocacy organizations, or social impact projects
No polling, survey research, or opinion research experience
Limited experience with ambiguous, fast-paced consulting environments - retail work is structured and transactional
No evidence of working on projects where data directly informed strategic decision-making for external clients
Missing context for progressive causes, voter behavior, campaign operations, or nonprofit sector
No capstone or academic projects related to political science, public policy, or social good applications
Lack of experience presenting technical findings to strategic decision-makers or campaign leadership
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has solid technical fundamentals in Python, R, SQL, and general data science - the coding skills are there. However, they're completely missing the domain expertise that makes BlueLabs unique: polling methodology, political analytics, progressive advocacy context, and campaign impact measurement. The retail background shows customer service skills but not the strategic consulting mindset for fast-paced campaign work. Most critically, there's zero demonstrated passion for progressive social impact, which BlueLabs lists as their first requirement. This reads like someone applying broadly to data science roles without understanding what makes political analytics different.
improvementPlan: Immediately demonstrate progressive political engagement: volunteer for local Democratic campaigns, progressive advocacy orgs (ACLU, Planned Parenthood, climate groups), or get-out-the-vote efforts. Document this prominently in your profile.

Take online courses in survey methodology and polling: Look for courses on Coursera/edX covering questionnaire design, sampling methodology, and survey weighting. MIT's Political Analytics course or similar would be valuable.

Build a political data science project: Analyze FEC campaign finance data, predict election outcomes using demographic data, or study voter turnout patterns. Use real political datasets (OpenSecrets, MIT Election Lab) and showcase understanding of political context.

Learn network analysis and identity resolution: Complete DataCamp or Coursera courses on graph analytics, social network analysis using Python NetworkX or R igraph. Build a project tracking political donor networks or social media influence patterns.

Study causal inference methods: Learn A/B testing, difference-in-differences, propensity score matching through courses like 'Causal Inference' or experimentation design. Apply to analyzing campaign intervention effectiveness.

Reframe existing projects with social impact lens: Your financial platform and retail strategy work could emphasize 'democratizing financial literacy' or 'improving access for underserved communities' - show you think about social good.
Connect with political data professionals: Join groups like Data for Progress, Blue Shift Labs alumni networks, or attend Progressive Data Conference. Network to understand the industry better.
Add messaging/communication analytics project: Analyze political speech patterns, message testing, or social media sentiment around policy issues - closer to BlueLabs' messaging work.
marketSkillsToLearn: Survey methodology and polling operations
Causal inference (A/B testing, experimental design, difference-in-differences)
Network analysis and graph analytics (NetworkX, igraph)
Political data sources (VAN/NGP, PDI, voter files, FEC data)
Message testing and communications analytics
Voter targeting and microtargeting models
Geospatial analysis for redistricting and campaign strategy
Time series forecasting for election prediction
Text mining for political sentiment analysis
Advanced statistical modeling (multilevel models, Bayesian methods)
Media mix modeling and attribution
Propensity scoring and matching methods
analysis.summary: Candidate possesses solid foundational technical skills in Python, R, SQL, and basic machine learning that meet the coding requirements. However, they completely lack the political analytics domain expertise, polling methodology knowledge, and demonstrated passion for progressive causes that are central to BlueLabs' mission. The retail background doesn't translate to the fast-paced, strategic consulting environment of campaign work. Without immediate, concrete steps to demonstrate political engagement and build relevant domain projects, this is a weak fit despite technical competence.

qaHistory.q: What excites you about a position with BlueLabs?*
qaHistory.a:
What excites me most about BlueLabs is the chance to do data science work that actually matters. I've spent time building technical projects and working with data, but I've always felt like something was missing when the end result was just optimizing a sales funnel or improving click-through rates. The idea of using Python, R, and SQL to help campaigns understand voter behavior, or helping nonprofits figure out where to focus their resources — that's the kind of work I want to be doing.

I'm genuinely drawn to the progressive social impact mission. I grew up watching policy debates affect real people's lives, and I've always believed that good data can lead to better decisions that help more people. The fellowship structure is perfect for where I am right now — I have solid fundamentals in Python, R, and data visualization through projects like my financial intelligence platform and retail analytics work, but I know I'm missing domain-specific experience in polling methodology and political analytics. Working directly with data scientists and strategists while contributing to real client projects sounds like the ideal way to build that expertise.

The hands-on nature of the role is what really stands out. I learned the most at Apple when I was thrown into real customer problems and had to figure out solutions on the spot, and I see that same kind of learning environment here — cleaning messy data, building visualizations that inform strategy, helping power polling operations. I want to be in the weeds solving actual problems, not just sitting through lectures. The capstone project requirement tells me BlueLabs takes the fellowship seriously as a real development opportunity, not just free labor.

I'm also excited about the fast-paced, impact-driven environment. My best work has always come when the stakes are real and the timeline is tight — whether that was tailoring AI resume systems or managing retail operations during product launches. The idea of working in "changing, ambiguous environments" where you have to embrace complexity and deliver results every day is exactly the kind of challenge I'm looking for right now.

applications.role: Analyzing day-to-day operational issues in financial center operations, providing resolutions, supporting product and service implementations, monitoring processes, conducting data analysis, and ensuring compliance with procedures and guidelines
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description:

At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. We do this by driving Responsible Growth and delivering for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day.

Being a Great Place to Work is core to how we drive Responsible Growth. This includes our commitment to being an inclusive workplace, attracting and developing exceptional talent, supporting our teammates’ physical, emotional, and financial wellness, recognizing and rewarding performance, and how we make an impact in the communities we serve.

Bank of America is committed to an in-office culture with specific requirements for office-based attendance and which allows for an appropriate level of flexibility for our teammates and businesses based on role-specific considerations.

At Bank of America, you can build a successful career with opportunities to learn, grow, and make an impact. Join us!
 

Job Description:
This job is responsible for analyzing the day-to-day issues, researching, and providing resolution for various issues identified within a business unit. Key responsibilities include supporting defined procedures, routine analytics, and creating reports and completing exception processing.

Responsibilities:

Assists with coordination of implementation for products, services, and operational solutions
Supports the design, documentation, implementation, and monitoring of new products and services
Provides guidance regarding procedural, technical, and operational changes
Expands business products knowledge within operations environment
Builds network by developing relationships with partners and teammates
Inspects data to identify issues and trends
Collects and interprets data to validate operational processes
Required Qualifications:

A minimum of two years recent experience in a BAC financial center, with particular emphasis on operations management
Must be able to fulfill role requirements without daily managerial oversight
Has the ability to learn and adapt to new information and technology platforms
Will follow established processes and guidelines in daily self-directed activities, adhering to all applicable laws and regulations
Must be able to travel to any financial center within defined market(s); may need to travel to two different financial centers in the course of a single day
Enthusiastic, highly motivated self-starter with strong work ethic, intense focus and attention to detail
Must possess strong verbal and written communication skills; including the ability to communicate effectively with partners regarding areas of opportunity identified
Must be flexible to work weekends and/or extended hours, as needed
Desired Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree or business relevant associate degree such as business management, business administration, or finance
Experience in the following industries: Consumer banking/financial services, mortgage, or retail
Skills:

Customer and Client Focus
Monitoring, Surveillance, and Testing
Process Performance Management
Research
Adaptability
Business Analytics
Critical Thinking
Reporting
Written Communications
Data Management
Policies, Procedures, and Guidelines Management
Process Design
Process Effectiveness
Strategic Thinking
Shift:

1st shift (United States of America)

40

applications.role: Finance process analysis and improvement through Oracle ERP/EPM system documentation, stakeholder collaboration, and operational efficiency initiatives within a nonprofit healthcare organization
applications.jobDescription:
Are you interested in making a world of difference in cancer care?

Cancer strikes more than 10 million people worldwide each year. As the leading medical society representing doctors who care for people with cancer, the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) is committed to conquering cancer through research, education, and promotion of the highest quality care.

Internship Overview:
ASCO is a flexible, high-performance membership organization where employees collaborate to support our mission through evidence, care, and impact. Together with Conquer Cancer, the ASCO Foundation, we foster a culture that prioritizes customer-centricity, emphasizes teamwork, and commits to quality. Our culture, ASCO Works – Our Way of Working, has long enabled workplace flexibility and embraced technology to help us achieve balance. 

This is an Alexandria, VA based internship. To learn what it’s like to work at ASCO, click here.

Who we are looking for:

ASCO is seeking a Business Analyst Intern that is interested in finance, systems, and process improvement. The Business Analyst Intern will have an immersive, project-based internship providing hands-on experience in finance process analysis, system documentation and stakeholder collaboration while contributing to real operational improvements that support ASCO’s mission.

If you are looking to gain real-world experience in finance operations, process improvement, and enterprise systems while making an impact through operational efficiency, we’d love to hear from you!
Internship activities include:

Assist in analyzing existing finance processes including identifying bottlenecks, inefficiencies and/or how the process may be improved by leveraging Oracle or other in-house tools. 
Gather and organize data related to process flows. 
Assist in developing and delivering integrations and report data sources for Oracle ERP and EPM. 
Create detailed process documentation and flowcharts for finance processes. 
Assist in the creation of standard tests to be used for Oracle release testing. 
Collaborate with various stakeholders in the finance department to gather insight and feedback on the current processes and/or issues with financial systems. 
Assist in developing and delivering training materials for new processes centered on Oracle ERP and EPM. 
Other reporting and administrative activities as needed. 
Required qualifications:

Currently enrolled in an undergraduate BA/BS degree program, or graduate program, or recently graduated from a 4-year degree program. Candidates must be at least a junior or entering their junior year of college at the start of the internship.
Pursuing a degree in finance, accounting, information technology or related field 
Analytical and problem-solving skills 
Strong communication and interpersonal skills 
Attention to detail and commitment to accuracy 
Ability to work collaboratively in a team as well as independently 
Experience with the Microsoft Office Suite (365 preferred); proficient in Microsoft Excel 
Ability to work in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment with flexibility to adapt to changing needs 
Solid organizational and time management skills and attention to detail  
Excellent customer service skills and professionalism  
Willingness to enhance skills  
Duration: 10 weeks during the summer – June 8, 2026 to August 14, 2026. This internship is 37.5 hours per week.  

Compensation: This summer internship position is paid at a rate of $20/hour for actual hours worked. Additionally, ASCO will provide parking or a metro subsidy.

Location: This internship is based in Alexandria, VA and will require working onsite the majority of the time with some offsite work flexibility. Candidates should reside in the D.C. metro area or be able to relocate at their own expense. If you are not able to work at ASCO Headquarters, please do not apply. 

ADA/Physical Requirements

qaHistory.q: Why are you interested in this internship?*
qaHistory.a:
I'm interested in this internship because it sits right at the intersection of three things I've been actively building toward: systems thinking, process improvement, and using data to make operations more efficient. At Apple, I spent three years learning how customer-facing operations actually work—not just the front-end experience, but the backend systems, workflows, and handoffs that either make things run smoothly or cause bottlenecks. I became the go-to person for troubleshooting operational issues, whether that was diagnosing why a device management setup wasn't working or figuring out how to streamline the way we handled business client onboarding. That experience taught me how to look at a process, spot inefficiencies, and work with different teams to fix them.

What draws me to this role specifically is the chance to apply that same mindset to finance operations—an area I'm genuinely interested in but haven't had the chance to work in professionally yet. I built a financial intelligence platform called Flashconverter to teach myself how money flows, how to track expenses, and how to optimize personal finances, and that project showed me how much I enjoy working with structured data and building systems that make financial processes clearer and more actionable. The idea of doing similar work in a real organization, with stakeholders, real data, and enterprise systems like Oracle, is exactly the kind of hands-on learning I'm looking for.

I'm also drawn to ASCO's mission. I've seen cancer affect people close to me, and the idea of contributing—even indirectly—to an organization that's working to improve cancer care feels meaningful. The fact that this internship isn't just about supporting existing processes but actively improving them through documentation, testing, and stakeholder collaboration makes it feel like real work, not busy work. I want to spend my summer learning how enterprise systems actually function, how finance teams operate, and how to make those operations better—and this internship seems like the perfect place to do that.

qaHistory.q: Are you currently enrolled in an undergraduate BA/BS degree program, or graduate program, or recently graduated from a 4-year degree program?*
qaHistory.a: Yes, I am currently enrolled at George Mason University pursuing a Bachelor of Science degree in Business with a concentration in Business Information Systems and Analytics, and I am on track to graduate in May 2026. I am entering my senior year and will be available for the full 10-week duration of the internship from June 8 to August 14, 2026. My program has given me a strong foundation in both the business operations side and the technical systems side—I've completed coursework in data analytics, business process analysis, cloud computing, and enterprise systems architecture. I'm also based in Northern Virginia, so I can easily commute to the Alexandria office and work onsite as required. This timing works perfectly for me since the internship falls right between my final semester and graduation, and I'm genuinely excited about the opportunity to apply what I've been learning in a real-world healthcare nonprofit setting before I transition into my full-time career.

applications.role: Supporting investor relations and capital development through market research, investor intelligence gathering, CRM data management in Salesforce, and reporting on the institutional credit investor landscape while tracking capital pipeline and investor engagement activities
applications.jobDescription:
The Investor Relations Analyst will support Invictus Capital Partners’ capital development and investor engagement efforts through market research, investor intelligence, CRM management, and reporting. This role sits at the intersection of investor relations, market research, and data analytics, providing critical insights into the institutional credit investor landscape while supporting day-to-day investor reporting and pipeline tracking. 

This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early-career professional interested in private credit, structured finance, and capital markets. The ideal candidate is intellectually curious and interested in learning how institutional investors allocate capital across credit and alternative investment strategies. They are detail-oriented, take pride in producing accurate work, and are developing a polished, professional communication style that allows them to work effectively with senior internal stakeholders. This individual has a strong analytical foundation, the ability to organize and interpret data, and is eager to learn, grow, and build a career within investor relations and capital markets.
Key Responsibilities
Market & Investor Research
Conduct ongoing research on institutional investors, allocators, and consultants across the credit and alternative investment landscape
Utilize platforms such as eVestment, Preqin, PitchBook, and similar databases to identify prospective investors, mandates, and capital-raising opportunities
Maintain and analyze market intelligence related to investor preferences, portfolio allocations, fund strategies, and competitive positioning
Support capital development initiatives by preparing investor profiles, market summaries, and opportunity analyses
 
Investor Relations & Capital Pipeline Support
Support the VP of Investor Relations with capital-raising activities and investor engagement efforts
Track and report on the capital pipeline, investor outreach, and active opportunities
Assist in managing outstanding investor deliverables, follow-ups, and reporting timelines
Support placement agent reporting and data requests as needed
 
CRM & Reporting (Salesforce)
Maintain accurate and up-to-date investor and prospect data within Salesforce
Generate regular and ad hoc reports related to investor activity, pipeline status, and engagement metrics
Support ongoing improvements to CRM data quality, reporting processes, and analytics
Assist with data preparation for internal reporting, leadership updates, and investor materials
 
Ad Hoc Analysis & Support
Provide analytical support for investor presentations, fundraising materials, and internal strategy discussions
Respond to data and analysis requests from Investor Relations leadership and senior management
Assist with special projects related to investor communications, reporting automation, and market analysis
Skills, Knowledge and Expertise
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related field
Strong proficiency in Excel (data analysis, pivot tables, basic modeling)
Strong working knowledge of Outlook and general business technology tools
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; high attention to detail
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities
 
Preferred
0–2 years of relevant experience in investor relations, capital markets, private equity, asset management, consulting, or financial services (internships count)
Familiarity with Salesforce or other CRM systems
Exposure to institutional investors, private credit, structured finance, or alternative investments
Experience using market intelligence platforms such as eVestment or Preqin
 
 Key Competencies: 
Analytical & Research Capability: Gathers, organizes, and interprets market and investor data to generate meaningful insights on institutional capital allocation trends.
Attention to Detail: Ensures accuracy and consistency in CRM data, investor reporting, market research, and internal deliverables.
Communication Skills: Produces clear, professional written materials and communicates effectively with senior internal stakeholders.
Data & Systems Proficiency: Leverages Excel, Salesforce, and market intelligence platforms to support reporting, pipeline tracking, and investor engagement.
Intellectual Curiosity & Learning Agility: Demonstrates strong interest in private credit, capital markets, and investor behavior, with a willingness to learn and grow.
 
How This Role Demonstrates Our Values: 
Integrity: Maintains high standards of accuracy, data quality, and professionalism in investor information, reporting, and communications.
Collaboration: Supports the VP of Investor Relations and partners with internal teams to advance capital development and investor engagement efforts.
Excellence: Delivers thoughtful analysis, reliable reporting, and well-organized research that contributes to informed decision-making.
Critical Curiosity: Actively seeks to understand investor strategies, market dynamics, and competitive positioning to enhance capital-raising effectiveness.
Benefits
Great compensation package, including competitive base pay
Discretionary annual bonus opportunity
Invictus medical, dental and vision is covered at 100% for all tiers.  
We offer FSA’s for healthcare, dependent care, transit and commuter costs. 
Safe harbor 401(k) match of 4%; employees need 3-months of service to be eligible to participate. No vesting after that. 
$80 per month cell phone reimbursement.  
20 Days of PTO accrued at the beginning of the year, 10 company holidays plus sick time.
Tuition Reimbursement
About Invictus Capital Partners
Invictus Capital Partners, LP (“Invictus”), a leading investment firm, and its subsidiary Verus Residential Loanco, LLC (“Verus,” and together with Invictus, the “Companies”), manage and oversee investments in Non-Agency single family residential mortgage loans.  Invictus is located in Washington, D.C. and has approximately 50 employees, and Verus is located in Minneapolis and has approximately 200 employees.  The Companies have overseen the acquisition of over $38 billion of mortgage loans since their inception.  In addition, Invictus manages over $19 billion in gross assets and has completed over 70 rated securitizations.  Interested candidates can learn more about the Companies by visiting www.invictuscp.com and www.verusmc.com.
