analysis.summary: Candidate demonstrates strong technical aptitude with Python, SQL, and client communication skills but has a critical domain mismatch for ERP consulting. Zero exposure to PeopleSoft/Oracle Cloud, no Java, missing enterprise business process knowledge, and lacking implementation project experience creates insurmountable gaps for immediate hiring. Would need 6-12 months of intensive ERP training and hands-on implementation experience before becoming viable for this role. Better suited for data analyst or business intelligence positions where ML/Python skills are valued.

qaHistory.q: Reason for Leaving apple
qaHistory.a:
I left Apple because I realized I'd grown as far as I could in that environment and wanted to take my career in a more technical direction. When I started, I was a seasonal hire who got converted to permanent because of strong performance—I was hitting 65% attach rates on AppleCare when the store average was 40%, and I maintained a 95%+ NPS score. But after three years, I found myself repeating the same conversations and sales processes every day. The role was focused on customer service and retail transactions, and while I valued building those client relationships, I wasn't growing my technical skills in the ways I wanted.

What I did enjoy at Apple was the technical troubleshooting side—running diagnostics, helping customers with backups, working with the business team on MDM implementations for enterprise clients. Those moments where I could solve a real technical problem or talk through how our systems could integrate into a business workflow were the most fulfilling parts of the job. I realized I wanted to move into a role where that kind of work was the core focus, not just an occasional part of my day.

Around the same time, I'd been building technical skills outside of work—learning Python and SQL, working on data projects in my coursework, and developing a financial intelligence platform that involved PDF parsing, machine learning categorization, and database management. I knew I wanted to transition into a role where I could combine technical problem-solving with business impact, which is exactly what drew me to consulting and implementation work. Leaving Apple was about choosing growth and aligning my day-to-day work with where I want to build my career long-term.

qaHistory.q: We seek to inspire each employee to be their best! Please list at least 3 factors that you have found best motivate you professionally. (You can list more than 3 if you'd like!)
qaHistory.a:
Three factors motivate me most professionally: solving problems that create real impact, continuously learning technical skills, and working with people who push me to grow.

I'm most energized when I can take something complex and broken and turn it into something that actually works for people. At Apple, I found myself staying late to help a business owner figure out device management for his team—not because I had to, but because I could see how much smoother his operations would be once we got it right. That same drive showed up when I built Flashconverter. I wasn't just converting PDFs—I was solving my own financial planning problem, which made every coding challenge feel worthwhile. When I can see the direct connection between my work and someone's ability to do their job better, that's what keeps me going.

Second, I genuinely love learning new technical tools and systems. I get excited about understanding how things work under the hood. At Mason, I taught myself cloud architecture through trial and error, setting up VPNs and configuring security groups until I actually understood what each piece did. When I started the Handshake AI role, I jumped into prompt engineering and computer vision evaluation without formal training because I wanted to understand how these models think. The idea of diving into PeopleSoft and Oracle Cloud, learning how enterprise systems connect, and mastering a completely new technical domain honestly sounds really engaging to me. I know there's a steep learning curve, but that's exactly what I'm looking for.

The third factor is being around people I can learn from. At Apple, I learned more from watching senior team members handle difficult client situations than from any training video. When my SEO partner taught me keyword analysis for Dispomode, he didn't just tell me what to do—he explained the logic, and that changed how I approached the whole project. I work best in environments where I can ask questions without feeling dumb and where people expect me to contribute ideas even when I'm still learning. That mentorship structure you mentioned—working with senior consultants and solution architects—is actually a huge draw for me.

A fourth factor I'd add is ownership. I like being responsible for something end-to-end, even if it's a small piece of a larger project. Whether it was managing my section of the Apple Vision Pro demo or building the entire technical pipeline for Flashconverter, I do my best work when I know the outcome depends on me following through. That kind of accountability makes the work feel more meaningful.

qaHistory.q:
Q
we know that you have choices and we want to udnerstand your goals. Can you list yout top five factors that you consider when exploring a new job opportunity

just list it and give me a 2 sentence reason fro each
qaHistory.a:
1. **Technical Learning & Skill Development**
Coming from a data science and AI background, I want a role where I can build depth in enterprise systems and learn technologies I haven't worked with yet—like ERP platforms, system integrations, and business process automation. This position offers training on PeopleSoft and Oracle Cloud, which would fill a real gap in my technical toolkit while letting me apply my existing SQL, Python, and cloud experience in a new domain.

2. **Clear Path from Technical to Consulting**
At Apple, I learned I genuinely enjoy the consultative side of technology—understanding what clients actually need and translating that into solutions—but I was limited to retail product recommendations. I'm specifically looking for a role that teaches me how to do that work at the enterprise level, with structured mentorship and a roadmap toward technical lead or solution architect positions, which this role explicitly offers.

3. **Hands-On Problem Solving Over Theory**
My best work has always come from building real things—whether that was coding Flashconverter to solve my own financial tracking problem or troubleshooting customer issues on the Apple genius bar. I need a job where I'm configuring systems, writing code for integrations, testing implementations, and fixing issues, not just sitting in meetings or doing abstract analysis.

4. **Working Directly with Clients**
The part of my Apple role I enjoyed most was talking to business owners about their technology needs and figuring out solutions like MDM setups or workflow improvements. I want to continue developing that client-facing skill in a professional services environment where I can learn how consultants manage stakeholder expectations, communicate technical concepts, and deliver measurable business outcomes.

5. **Company Investment in Entry-Level Development**
I'm realistic that I'm coming in without ERP experience, so I need an employer that's committed to training rather than expecting me to already know everything. The fact that Metaformers highlights mentorship, certifications, continuing education, and leadership development tells me they're willing to invest in someone who has strong fundamentals and work ethic but needs to learn the domain.

qaHistory.q: we know that you have choices and we want to udnerstand your goals. Can you list yout top five factors that you consider when exploring a new job opportunity
qaHistory.a:
When I'm exploring a new job opportunity, learning and mentorship is my top priority. I've been most successful when I have experienced people around me who can guide my development—like when I worked at Apple and senior team members taught me how to approach business conversations or troubleshoot technical problems. I want to join a team where senior consultants are invested in helping me grow my technical and consulting skills, especially since I'm new to ERP systems.

Second, I look for technical challenges that will expand my skill set. I already have a solid foundation in SQL, Python, and JavaScript, but I'm really interested in learning how enterprise systems actually work—how integrations connect systems together, how business processes like finance and supply chain translate into software configuration, and how to write clean code that solves real operational problems. I want to work on projects where I'm building things that matter, not just doing repetitive tasks.

Third, exposure to real client work is essential to me. My experience at Apple taught me how valuable it is to understand what a client actually needs versus what they say they need. I spent a lot of time probing business owners about their operations and translating their problems into technology solutions, even if it was just setting up device management or optimizing their workflow. I want to continue developing that consultative skill set by working directly with clients on implementations.

Fourth, I need a clear path for career growth. I'm specifically interested in roles that can lead to technical lead, solution architect, or project management positions. I don't want to get stuck in a purely coding role—I want to understand the full implementation lifecycle and eventually be able to own parts of a project. I'm drawn to this role because it explicitly mentions developing leadership capabilities and growing into those kinds of positions.

Finally, I care about the company's willingness to invest in me as someone without direct ERP experience. I'm fully aware I don't have PeopleSoft or Oracle Cloud experience, and I haven't worked on enterprise implementation projects before. But I learn fast, I'm not afraid to ask questions, and I'm willing to put in the work to get up to speed. I'm looking for a company that values strong fundamentals and potential over checking every box on day one—somewhere that provides real training and gives people room to grow into the role.

qaHistory.q: Breifly tell us why you are a match for this opportunity
qaHistory.a:
I'm genuinely excited about this role because it sits right at the intersection of technical problem-solving and business impact—exactly what I've been drawn to throughout college and my work experience. While I haven't worked directly with PeopleSoft or Oracle Cloud yet, I have a solid foundation in the core technical skills this position requires: SQL, Python, JavaScript, database querying, and cloud platforms like AWS and Azure from my coursework. More importantly, I've already experienced what it's like to translate technical work into real business value.

At Apple, I spent nearly three years working directly with business clients—not just selling them products, but actually consulting with them on their operational needs. I'd sit down with business owners, understand their workflows, and recommend technology solutions like Mobile Device Management setups that would genuinely improve how they ran their companies. That experience taught me how to ask the right questions, communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders, and take ownership of delivering results that mattered to the client. I also built my own e-commerce business where I handled everything from coding custom website features in JavaScript and HTML to managing inventory systems and financial tracking—so I understand how business processes like supply chain and operations actually work on the ground.

What really draws me to consulting is that same dynamic I loved at Apple: being the person who bridges the gap between complex systems and the people who need them to work. I'm ready to learn ERP systems from the ground up, and I know I can pick them up quickly because I've consistently done that with new technologies—whether it was training AI models at Handshake or building financial analytics tools from scratch. I want to grow into someone who can lead implementations and architect solutions, and I genuinely believe this role is the right place to start building that career.

applications.role: Entry-level digital consulting role focused on helping clients with digital transformation initiatives across four specialized areas: Application Development (custom solutions and system integrations), Cloud Infrastructure (cloud strategy and migration), Data Solutions (data analytics and machine learning), or Transformation Services (business process improvement and change management). The role involves collaborating with experienced teams to deliver enterprise technology solutions while developing technical and consulting skills.
applications.jobDescription:
Job description
Baker Tilly is a leading advisory, tax and assurance firm, providing clients with a genuine coast-to-coast and global advantage in major regions of the U.S. and in many of the world’s leading financial centers – New York, London, San Francisco, Los Angeles, Chicago and Boston. Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP and Baker Tilly US, LLP (Baker Tilly) provide professional services through an alternative practice structure in accordance with the AICPA Code of Professional Conduct and applicable laws, regulations and professional standards. Baker Tilly US, LLP is a licensed independent CPA firm that provides attest services to its clients. Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP and its subsidiary entities provide tax and business advisory services to their clients. Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP and its subsidiary entities are not licensed CPA firms.

Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP and Baker Tilly US, LLP, trading as Baker Tilly, are independent members of Baker Tilly International, a worldwide network of independent accounting and business advisory firms in 141 territories, with 43,000 professionals and a combined worldwide revenue of $5.2 billion. Visit bakertilly.com or join the conversation on LinkedIn, Facebook and Instagram.  

 

Please discuss the work location status with your Baker Tilly talent acquisition professional to understand the requirements for an opportunity you are exploring.

 

Baker Tilly is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability or protected veteran status, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected basis, in accordance with applicable federal, state or local law.

 

Any unsolicited resumes submitted through our website or to Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP, employee e-mail accounts are considered property of Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP, and are not subject to payment of agency fees. In order to be an authorized recruitment agency ("search firm") for Baker Tilly Advisory Group, LP, there must be a formal written agreement in place and the agency must be invited, by Baker Tilly's Talent Attraction team, to submit candidates for review via our applicant tracking system.

 

 

Job Description:

 

Responsibilities:
Baker Tilly’s Digital Consulting Practice combines deep functional, industry, and technical capabilities to help clients solve their toughest enterprise digital challenges. Baker Tilly Digital is focused on the integration of advanced technologies with core business transformational services to support companies in successfully navigating the complexities of digital transformations.

As a member of the 2026 Baker Tilly Digital Consultant Class, you will have the opportunity to:

Learn and execute meaningful work engagements that have an impact on the business

Collaborate with experienced Staff, Seniors, Managers, and Partners with specialized experience and technical knowledge across numerous industries and services

Engage directly with a variety of clients ranging from privately held businesses to publicly traded multi-national corporations

Learn directly from mentors and additional Baker Tilly employees through a series of networking activities, formal training, and an open work environment

Impact the surrounding community through volunteering events, such as Junior Achievement and United Way


A Baker Tilly Digital Consultant is a direct placement in one of the following focus areas, determined based on a combination of skillset, experience, and interest.

Application Development:
Description: This area of Baker Tilly Digital introduces innovative applications and services into uncharted territory to create new opportunities for our clients. We help clients successfully blaze the trail to transform and enhance their business.
Role: A Consultant on this team collaborates with our experienced Baker Tilly team members to envision and create custom solutions that enhance customer engagement and refine their operational efficiency. Services provided include application strategy, application implementation (development of system integrations, data conversions, reports and dashboard development using cloud-based tools), application modernization, and custom software development.

Cloud Infrastructure:
Description: Business environments across industries look different than they did just a few years ago. Fortunately, cloud is revolutionizing business in ways that can help you keep up, giving life to new products and services.
Role: A Consultant on this team collaborates with our experienced Baker Tilly team members to scale and secure cloud technology to help leverage it for innovation and growth. Services provided include cloud strategy, cloud infrastructure development, cloud migration, and cloud optimization (modernization).

 

Data Solutions:
Description: Capturing the data that matters most and bringing it into focus is no trivial matter and is critical to driving business results. Clients rely on our experience in data solutions to drive their business forward.
Role: A Consultant on this team collaborates with experienced Baker Tilly team members to help our clients derive value from data, whether it’s through advanced machine learning, data visualization or working to implement new data processes for a “single source of truth.” Services provided included data strategy and program capabilities, data visualization, enterprise data analytics modernization, data governance and data management, advanced analytics and machine learning, and strategic planning.

Transformation Services:
Description: Effectively developed and implemented digital transformation strategies are game changing for businesses. From improving client operational efficiency and workplace productivity, to bettering customer experiences— this Baker Tilly team focuses on accelerating our client’s sustainable growth for the long run.
Role: A Consultant on this team collaborates with experienced Baker Tilly team members to understand our clients’ business processes, identify opportunities for transformation and define the overarching strategy, implement transformative solutions, and enable a smooth transition for stakeholders. Services provided include adoption and organizational readiness, business strategy assessments and roadmap development, business application technology evaluation, and project and program management.

 

Qualifications

General Qualifications (for all focus areas listed above):

Be enrolled as a full-time student during the 2025 - 2026 school year in Business, Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain, Accounting, Operations Management, Information Systems/Technology, Computer Science, Analytics, Information/Data Management, Management Information Systems/Technology, or a related field

Availability to begin full-time employment in the summer of 2026

Outstanding academic performance with a minimum overall GPA of a 3.0 required with at least a 3.2 GPA preferred

Relevant internship, work experience and/or involvement with a professional organization

Ability to provide exceptional client service, demonstrate commitment to continuous learning, display passion and commitment towards finding creative and efficient solutions to analytical problems, achieving results and exhibit a sense of urgency and commitment to quality and the timely completion of duties

Demonstrates analytical, problem-solving, critical thinking, decision-making, organizational, and interpersonal skills

Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite, including but not limited to Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Visio

The ability to work effectively in a team environment with all levels of client personnel in various industries

Excellent written/verbal communication and collaboration skills

There is currently no immigration sponsorship available for this position

Be available to travel as needed for client projects


Technical Qualifications (for the following focus areas: Application Development, Cloud Infrastructure, and Data Solutions):

Interest in new and emerging technologies and enterprise technology architecture

Experience with programming languages, such as Java, Python, and SQL

Knowledge of cloud-based services and associated infrastructure – (experience in design and implementation of cloud-based services in Azure/AWS is highly desirable)

Data and Infrastructure candidates will need to have the ability to design relational data models and query databases using SQL

AI focused candidates will need to have a solid understanding of machine learning and a general understanding of robotic process automation (experience with SVMs and NLP/text mining especially desirable)

 

The compensation range for this role is $59,640 to $126,440. Actual compensation is influenced by a variety of factors including but not limited to skills, experience, qualifications, and geographic location.

experienceGaps: No formal consulting or advisory services experience with structured project deliverables
Lacks enterprise technology implementation experience (ERP, CRM, cloud migrations, data warehouse projects)
Missing multi-client project experience typical of consulting environments
No demonstrated experience with billable hours, client presentations, or formal business case development

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has solid technical fundamentals and impressive self-learning capability, but they're stretched thin across too many surface-level skills without depth in any consulting-specific domain. The Apple experience shows strong client communication and business acumen, which is valuable, but retail sales is fundamentally different from multi-month consulting engagements with deliverables, documentation, and stakeholder management. They'd likely struggle in their first 6 months without significant hand-holding. That said, for an entry-level 2026 graduate role, the raw intelligence, technical curiosity, and client-facing confidence make them trainable—just barely over the hiring threshold with a 3.0+ GPA assumption.
improvementPlan: Immediately pursue a summer 2025 consulting or technology implementation internship at a Big 4 firm, boutique consultancy, or corporate IT department to gain structured project experience before graduation

Complete at least one end-to-end cloud infrastructure project: design VPC architecture, implement multi-tier application with RDS/database, document architecture decisions, and demonstrate cost optimization
Learn Java and build 2-3 projects involving Spring Boot REST APIs with database integration to demonstrate enterprise application development capability
Take formal courses or certifications in business process management (BPM), change management, or project management (CAPM) to understand consulting delivery frameworks
Redesign existing projects to simulate client deliverables: create executive summaries, technical documentation, ROI analysis, and implementation roadmaps for each project
Gain hands-on experience with data modeling: design normalized database schemas for complex business scenarios and document with ERDs
Participate in case competitions (consulting or technology strategy) to develop structured problem-solving and presentation skills under pressure

Network aggressively with Baker Tilly employees on LinkedIn, attend their campus events, and explicitly ask about the consulting delivery lifecycle to demonstrate genuine interest beyond technical skills
marketSkillsToLearn: Enterprise cloud platforms: AWS Solutions Architect Associate or Azure Fundamentals certification with hands-on labs
Modern data stack: dbt, Snowflake, Databricks for enterprise data analytics
Business intelligence tools: Advanced Tableau/Power BI with DAX and complex data modeling
DevOps fundamentals: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions)
Enterprise integration patterns: REST/SOAP APIs, message queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ), ETL tools (Informatica, Talend)
Agile/Scrum methodology with hands-on sprint experience
SQL advanced topics: query optimization, stored procedures, indexing strategies
Business analysis frameworks: SWOT, Porter's Five Forces applied to technology decisions
Data governance and compliance: GDPR, SOX, data lineage concepts
Communication artifacts: creating executive dashboards, writing business requirements documents, stakeholder analysis
analysis.summary: This candidate sits right at the threshold with a 72/100—they have the technical aptitude and impressive self-starter mentality, but lack the consulting DNA and production-grade implementation experience Baker Tilly actually needs. The Apple role demonstrates client empathy and business thinking, but translating iPhone features is not the same as delivering a cloud migration roadmap to a CFO. They're trainable and have upside, but would require significant mentorship investment and might struggle with the ambiguity of real consulting work. For a 2026 grad program, they're a calculated risk: strong technical foundation but needs rapid professional maturity development.

applications.role: An executive-sponsored, six-month talent acceleration program for early-career professionals focused on hands-on AI, analytics, and customer experience projects. Participants work on innovation-driven initiatives, engage with senior leaders, and deliver outcome-driven results while building capabilities in AI solutions, data analysis, and strategic problem-solving within Zensar's Customer Experience & Success organization.
applications.jobDescription:
Job description
About Zensar Technologies

Zensar is a leading digital solutions and technology services company partnering with global organizations on their digital transformation journey. A technology partner of choice, with strong track-record of innovation, credible investment in digital solutions and assertion of commitment to client’s success, Zensar’s comprehensive range of services and solutions enable clients achieve new thresholds of performance. Part of the $40 billion APAX Partners’ portfolio of companies, Zensar is uniquely positioned to help existing businesses run efficiently, manage legacy transformation and plan business growth through innovative digital platform. https://www.zensar.com

Working at Zensar

Working at Zensar is an enriching experience. While work is driven by innovation and passion, fun is taken seriously too. An open environment is encouraged making it easy to brainstorm with colleagues. Creative thinking is encouraged through time out activities. Moreover, the offices have been designed to foster creativity and communication, bringing a little bit of home into work every day. Zensar provides and a comprehensive benefit package for all fulltime employees. 

Customer Experience & Success 

Seeking Early-in-Career (EIC) Talent – Students, Fresh Graduates & Emerging Professionals

About the Role

This position is a high impact, executive sponsored talent acceleration program designed to rapidly onboard, enable, and activate early career professionals. This six month immersive experience places participants in meaningful, innovation driven work that creates real business value for Client’s Customer Experience & Success (CEnS) organization.

Participants work on hands-on technical and strategic projects, engage with senior leaders, and deliver outcome driven project results. The program is structured to help emerging talent build capabilities quickly, convert potential into performance, and grow into contributors who can drive impact at scale. Seeking Early-in-Career (EIC) Talent – Students, Fresh Graduates & Emerging Professionals

Ideal Candidates

Students (Junior/Senior year – part time)
Fresh Graduates (within 6–12 months – full time)
Emerging Professionals (1–2 years of experience – full time)
Education Requirements

Currently pursuing or recently completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in:

Artificial Intelligence 
Machine Learning / Applied Machine Learning 
AI Engineering / Intelligent Systems 
Data Science
Information Systems 
Statistics or Applied Mathematics 
Computer Science (with a focus on AI, analytics, cloud, or strategy) 
Software Engineering (with exposure to AI, automation, or prototyping) 
Computer Interaction / UX (for AI agent interactions and workflow design) 
Core Competencies

Candidates should bring a combination of intellectual curiosity, analytical ability, and communication strength:

Analytical Thinking: Ability to work with data to identify patterns, trends, and 
Communication: Ability to synthesize information and present insights clearly to stakeholders.
Project Management Fundamentals: Understanding timelines, deliverables, and stakeholder coordination.
Tech Fluency: Basic awareness of Microsoft Azure and cloud concepts is a plus. Exposure to Microsoft ecosystem tools (Teams, SharePoint, Azure)
Preferred Skills

Experience from coursework, projects, or internships in:

Agentic AI solutions, automation concepts, or AI enabled workflows
Data analysis using Excel, Power BI, or similar tools
Applying structured thinking to break down complex problems and propose solution paths
Demonstrated ability to move from problem identification → hypothesis → prototype/initial solution
Ways of Working

Candidates should be able to:

Operate independently with a solution oriented mindset, proactively identifying what needs to be done rather than waiting for direction
Navigate a matrixed organization with guidance, engaging the right stakeholders and seeking information efficiently
Take ownership of deliverables from initiation through completion 
Demonstrate resilience and adaptability when working through ambiguous or evolving project contexts
Soft Skills

Highly proactive, self-driven, and energized by tackling ambiguous problems 
Comfortable seeking clarity, asking thoughtful questions, and engaging confidently with senior stakeholders 
Strong collaborators with attention to detail and follow-through
Open to feedback, coachable, and committed to continuous learning and improvement 
Naturally inclined to think in terms of outcomes and find a way forward, even when challenges arise
Who Should Apply?

This program is ideal for individuals who want to:

Build real-world, high impact experience at Zensar, contributing directly to priority business and innovation initiatives.
Work in a fast paced, innovation focused environment where they can apply analytical, strategic, and AI driven problem-solving skills.
Accelerate their career in analytics, AI, business architecture, or technical solutioning, gaining exposure to cross functional teams and senior leaders.
Tackle enterprise level challenges by proactively identifying problems, proposing solutions, and driving meaningful outcomes.
Thrive in ambiguity - finding a way forward even when requirements are evolving or undefined.
Develop a strong solution mindset, taking ownership of deliverables end-to-end and demonstrating resilience in complex or matrixed environments.
Residency Eligibility:

Candidates must be a Green Card Holder or a U.S. Citizens. No exception!!!!!

analysis.roleType: Early Career - Customer Experience & Success (AI/Analytics Focus)
presentSkills: Data Analytics & Visualization (Power BI, Tableau, Excel)
Stakeholder Engagement (senior leader interaction at Apple)

missingSkills.reason: Job explicitly calls for exposure to Microsoft ecosystem tools. While candidate has cloud experience, no evidence of Teams/SharePoint usage which are daily collaboration tools in enterprise CX environments.

This is a preferred skill explicitly mentioned. Candidate has AI model training experience but no documented experience building or implementing agentic AI solutions or automated workflows - critical for the CX transformation focus.

While candidate shows project execution, there's no evidence of formal PM methodologies (Agile, Scrum, waterfall), timeline management tools, or structured deliverable tracking - essential for 6-month accelerated program.

Apple retail is hierarchical; this role requires navigating complex enterprise matrix structures. No evidence of working across multiple stakeholder groups simultaneously in ambiguous enterprise settings.

Job explicitly requires 'problem identification → hypothesis → prototype/initial solution' structured thinking. Academic projects show analysis but lack formal hypothesis-driven consulting frameworks critical for CX transformation work.

experienceGaps: No demonstrated experience working in a formal corporate innovation program or transformation initiative - academic and retail experience don't translate directly to enterprise CX consulting environments
Limited evidence of working through highly ambiguous, evolving project requirements in a professional setting - Apple retail had defined processes; this role requires comfort with undefined scope
No documented experience presenting analytical insights or strategic recommendations to C-level or VP-level stakeholders - critical for this executive-sponsored program
Lack of enterprise software/SaaS exposure - all experience is either consumer-facing (Apple retail) or academic; missing understanding of B2B software delivery and customer success models
No evidence of end-to-end ownership of a business deliverable with measurable outcomes in a corporate setting - projects show technical capability but not business impact quantification
Missing structured consulting methodology experience (problem structuring, issue trees, MECE frameworks) that would be expected in a strategy-focused CX role
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is a 72/100 - a solid match with real potential but notable gaps. The AI/ML technical foundation is legitimately strong, and the Apple B2B experience shows customer-facing maturity rare for early career talent. However, the resume screams 'technically capable student with retail sales experience' rather than 'emerging professional ready for enterprise transformation work.' The Handshake AI fellowship is interesting but too recent and narrow to demonstrate the strategic, consultative problem-solving this role demands. The candidate has the raw ingredients but needs to prove they can operate in ambiguous, matrixed corporate environments beyond academic projects and retail customer service.
improvementPlan: IMMEDIATELY: Reframe all experience using business outcome language - quantify impact from Apple role (revenue generated, efficiency improvements, process changes implemented) and translate academic projects into business case studies with measurable results

CRITICAL GAP: Get hands-on with Microsoft ecosystem ASAP - complete Microsoft Learn paths for Teams, SharePoint, and Azure AI services; obtain free Azure AI certifications to demonstrate platform familiarity beyond AWS

BUILD MISSING NARRATIVE: Create 1-2 portfolio projects specifically demonstrating 'agentic AI workflow automation' - use LangChain or AutoGen to build a simple multi-agent system solving a business problem; document the problem→hypothesis→solution framework explicitly

DEMONSTRATE ENTERPRISE READINESS: Seek out any opportunity (even volunteer/unpaid) to work on a real business transformation project, innovation initiative, or cross-functional team in next 30-60 days - startup consulting, university innovation lab, pro-bono strategy work - anything showing matrixed stakeholder management

CLOSE COMMUNICATION GAP: Develop 2-3 case study presentations showing strategic thinking - take existing projects and present them as consulting deliverables with executive summaries, hypothesis frameworks, and business recommendations; practice presenting to professionals for feedback

STRENGTHEN PM CREDENTIALS: Complete a lightweight PM certification (Google Project Management Certificate or similar) and explicitly map past work to project management fundamentals - create retrospective project plans for academic work showing timeline/deliverable/stakeholder management
marketSkillsToLearn: Microsoft Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Apps) - becoming standard for business process automation
Customer Success Metrics & Analytics (NPS, CSAT, churn analysis, customer journey mapping)
Prompt Engineering & LLM Application Development (LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector databases)
Business Process Modeling (BPMN, process mining tools like Celonis)
Agile/Scrum Methodologies with certifications (CSM or PSM-I)
Data Storytelling & Executive Communication (frameworks like Pyramid Principle, McKinsey-style slide design)
Azure AI Services (Azure OpenAI, Cognitive Services, AI Studio)
Change Management Fundamentals (Prosci or similar frameworks)
API Integration & Workflow Automation (Zapier, Make, n8n for no-code/low-code solutions)
SQL Advanced Analytics (window functions, CTEs, query optimization for business intelligence)
Customer Data Platforms & CRM Analytics (Salesforce Service Cloud, HubSpot)
Design Thinking & Innovation Frameworks (IDEO methodology, Stanford d.school approaches)
analysis.summary: This candidate presents a technically competent early-career profile with legitimate AI/ML foundations and surprising B2B customer-facing maturity from Apple. The core technical skills align well with job requirements, but the experience portfolio reveals someone who hasn't yet operated in the ambiguous, matrixed, enterprise transformation environment this role demands. With focused effort to close the Microsoft ecosystem gap, demonstrate agentic AI capabilities, and reframe existing experience through a business outcome lens, this candidate could be competitive. The raw talent is evident, but the professional readiness for a high-stakes, executive-sponsored innovation program is questionable without additional proof points of strategic consulting capability and enterprise software domain knowledge.

applications.role: Design, develop, and maintain Power BI dashboards and data models to support MSP and sales strategy teams through data transformation, visualization, and business intelligence reporting
applications.jobDescription:
Recruitment Fraud Alert

We’ve learned that scammers are impersonating Commvault team members—including HR and leadership—via email or text. These bad actors may conduct fake interviews and ask for personal information, such as your social security number.  

What to know:

Commvault does not conduct interviews by email or text.
We will never ask you to submit sensitive documents (including banking information, SSN, etc) before your first day.
If you suspect a recruiting scam, please contact us at wwrecruitingteam@commvault.com 

 

About Commvault 

Commvault (NASDAQ: CVLT) is the gold standard in cyber resilience. The company empowers customers to uncover, take action, and rapidly recover from cyberattacks – keeping data safe and businesses resilient. The company’s unique AI-powered platform combines best-in-class data protection, exceptional data security, advanced data intelligence, and lightning-fast recovery across any workload or cloud at the lowest TCO. For over 25 years, more than 100,000 organizations and a vast partner ecosystem have relied on Commvault to reduce risks, improve governance, and do more with data. 

The MSP Partner Business Intelligence Intern will design and maintain Power BI dashboards for MSP and sales teams by transforming data, building data models and translating business needs into clear visualizations. Support ad hoc analysis, document reporting logic, and improve data quality and automation.

 

Responsiblities: 

Support the design, development, and maintenance of Power BI dashboards used by the MSP and sales strategy teams.
Gather, clean, and transform data from multiple internal sources to ensure accurate and consistent reporting.
Partner with business stakeholders to understand reporting requirements and translate them into effective visualizations.
Build and enhance data models, measures, and calculations (e.g., DAX) to support analytics use cases.
Document dashboard logic, data sources, and assumptions to support handoff and long‑term maintainability.
Assist with ad hoc analysis and reporting requests to support ongoing sales and partner initiatives.
Identify opportunities to improve data quality, automation, and reporting efficiency.
Qualifications: 
Pursuing a degree in Business Analytics, Data Science, Finance, or related field
Proficiency in Power BI (including DAX), Salesforce reporting, and Excel
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Interest in enterprise GTM strategy, co-sell motions, and partner ecosystems
 

Must be available to work from Tuesday May 26th until Friday August 7th. 

Eligibility Requirements

Be at least 17 years of age prior to scheduled start date
Be currently enrolled at an accredited institution
Be enrolled at an accredited institution the semester immediately following the summer internship program (Fall 2026)
You’ll love working here because:
We care. Our Vaulters aren’t just colleagues; they’re a community that supports and inspires each other every day
Real work. Real impact. Forget busywork—our interns tackle meaningful projects that make a difference. You’ll leave with skills, confidence, and experience that set you apart
Connections that count. Our program opens doors to different business functions, giving you the chance to collaborate, network, and build relationships that last well beyond your internship
 

#LI-Remote 

Thank you for your interest in Commvault. Reflected below is the minimum and maximum base salary range for this role. At Commvault we use broad salary ranges in our job postings to reflect the diverse levels of expertise and experience among our candidates and is not reflective of the total compensation and benefits package. The specific salary offered will be determined based on your unique qualifications, including your relevant experience, skills, and the value you bring to the role. While the range provides a general idea of the compensation, it is important to note that placements within the range are not automatic and will be carefully considered to ensure a fair and competitive offer. We are committed to rewarding talent and experience.

Pay Range

$39,520 - $118,560 USD

Commvault is an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are always committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status and we will not discriminate against on the basis of such characteristics or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we work.

Commvault’s goal is to make interviewing inclusive and accessible to all candidates and employees. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to participate in the interview process or apply for a position at Commvault, please email accommodations@commvault.com For any inquiries not related to an accommodation please reach out to wwrecruitingteam@commvault.com.

missingSkills.reason: Explicitly required in the job description for building measures and calculations in Power BI. While candidate has Power BI listed, there's no evidence of actual DAX proficiency or projects demonstrating calculated columns, measures, or time intelligence functions.

Job requires proficiency in Salesforce reporting specifically. Candidate lists 'CRM & Salesforce' as a skill but has zero demonstrated experience with Salesforce reports, dashboards, or data extraction. Apple retail experience doesn't count as Salesforce platform knowledge.

Job explicitly requires building and enhancing data models. Candidate lists Power BI as a tool but provides no evidence of creating relationships, star schemas, or optimizing data models for performance.

Role specifically supports MSP partner business. Candidate has zero exposure to channel sales, partner ecosystems, co-sell motions, or MSP business models, which are central to understanding stakeholder needs.

Job description explicitly mentions interest in enterprise GTM strategy and co-sell motions. Candidate's retail B2B experience at Apple doesn't translate to understanding complex enterprise sales cycles or partner-driven GTM approaches.

experienceGaps: No demonstrated experience building or maintaining actual Power BI dashboards in a professional setting
Zero experience gathering requirements from business stakeholders and translating them into reporting solutions
No evidence of working with sales teams or understanding sales operations metrics, pipeline reporting, or revenue analytics
Lacks documentation experience for technical deliverables (data models, dashboard logic, assumptions)
No experience with data quality initiatives, automation workflows, or improving reporting efficiency at scale
Missing exposure to partner ecosystem dynamics, channel sales, or B2B2B business models that are core to MSP strategy
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has strong foundational technical skills and legitimate BI tool exposure, but there's a concerning gap between listing 'Power BI' as a skill and actually having done the work this role requires. The Salesforce claim is particularly problematic—retail CRM usage is not Salesforce reporting proficiency. However, for an internship, the analytical foundation is solid, and the candidate demonstrates learning agility through diverse technical projects. With 2-3 weeks of focused DAX and Salesforce self-study, they could be interview-ready.
improvementPlan: Complete Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst certification with specific focus on DAX functions, time intelligence, and calculated measures—build at least 2 portfolio projects demonstrating these skills

Get hands-on Salesforce experience: create a free Developer Edition account, complete Trailhead modules on Reports & Dashboards, and build 3-5 sample reports showing filtering, grouping, and cross-object reporting

Rebuild one existing project (preferably Flashconverter or Retail Strategy) as a complete Power BI solution with proper data modeling, DAX measures, and documentation to demonstrate end-to-end BI development
Study MSP business models and partner ecosystem fundamentals—read white papers on channel sales, co-sell motions, and how MSPs operate to speak intelligently about the business context in interviews
Create a GitHub repository showcasing Power BI projects with documented data sources, model diagrams, DAX formulas, and business requirements to demonstrate professional-level BI work
marketSkillsToLearn: Advanced DAX patterns (iterator functions, context transition, calculation groups)
Power BI Service administration and deployment pipelines
Power Query M language for complex ETL transformations
Salesforce Einstein Analytics / Tableau CRM
Sales operations metrics (ARR, MRR, pipeline coverage, win rates)
Data warehouse concepts and dimensional modeling
Azure Data Factory or similar ETL orchestration tools
Version control for BI assets (Power BI + Git integration)
Performance tuning for large-scale BI solutions
analysis.summary: Strong technical foundation with legitimate BI tools exposure, but critical skills gaps in DAX and Salesforce reporting that are non-negotiable for this role. The candidate has the analytical horsepower and learning capacity to succeed, but needs 2-4 weeks of focused skill-building to be truly competitive. For an internship, the raw material is there—but don't mistake tool familiarity for job-ready proficiency.

applications.role: First line technical support for internal employees and contractors, providing remote technology support, troubleshooting issues, managing tickets, and escalating complex problems to higher-level support teams
applications.jobDescription:
Position Overview
Job Description Summary
At PNC, our people are our greatest differentiator and competitive advantage in the markets we serve. We are all united in delivering the best experience for our customers. We work together each day to foster an inclusive workplace culture where all of our employees feel respected, valued and have an opportunity to contribute to the company's success.

As a Technology Solution Center Analyst within PNC's TSC SERVICE DESK organization, you will be based in Pittsburgh, PA, Strongsville/Cleveland, OH.

This is a remote position. Work may be performed from a quiet, confidential space in a home location, approved by PNC. This position may not be available in all geographic locations.

SHIFT: Monday - Friday 6:00 am - 3:00 pm EST

*PNC will not provide sponsorship for employment visas or participate in STEM OPT for this position.*

Service Desk Experience

Ticket Experience

Phone Experience

IT Background
 

This position may be eligible for remote work in select geographic locations, subject to approval by PNC. If approved, work must be conducted from a quiet, secure, and confidential home-based workspace. Occasional in-office participation may be required based on business needs.
PNC will not provide sponsorship for employment visas or participate in STEM OPT for this position.
Job Description
Provides the first line of support and problem resolution for technology products and applications.
Utilizes utilities and tools to provide remote technology support services for internal employee/contactor technology.
Escalates issues to second and third level support teams through pre-determined directions or procedures.
Resolves employee/contractor technology issues during point of contact.
Partners with end-user and other analysts to resolve normal and unusual information system problems.
PNC Employees take pride in our reputation and to continue building upon that we expect our employees to be:

Customer Focused - Knowledgeable of the values and practices that align customer needs and satisfaction as primary considerations in all business decisions and able to leverage that information in creating customized customer solutions.
Managing Risk - Assessing and effectively managing all of the risks associated with their business objectives and activities to ensure they adhere to and support PNC's Enterprise Risk Management Framework.
Qualifications
Successful candidates must demonstrate appropriate knowledge, skills, and abilities for a role. Listed below are skills, competencies, work experience, education, and required certifications/licensures needed to be successful in this position.

Preferred Skills
Customer Solutions, Data Architecture, End Users, Problem Resolution, Technical Support
Competencies
Call Center Technologies, Customer Support Operations, Hardware Infrastructure, Help Desk, IT Standards, Procedures & Policies, Problem Solving, Technical Troubleshooting
Work Experience
Roles at this level typically require an Associates or equivalent degree as well as related experience or product knowledge to accomplish primary duties. Typically requires 3+ years experience. In lieu of a degree, a comparable combination of education, job specific certification(s), and experience (including military service) may be considered.
Education
Associates
Certifications
No Required Certification(s)
Licenses
No Required License(s)
Pay Transparency
Base Salary: $31,000.00 – $59,800.00
Salaries may vary based on geographic location, market data and on individual skills, experience, and education.
Application Window
Generally, this opening is expected to be posted for two business days from 03/12/2026, although it may be longer with business discretion.
Benefits
PNC offers a comprehensive range of benefits to help meet your needs now and in the future. Depending on your eligibility, options for full-time employees include: medical/prescription drug coverage (with a Health Savings Account feature), dental and vision options; employee and spouse/child life insurance; short and long-term disability protection; 401(k) with PNC match, pension and stock purchase plans; dependent care reimbursement account; back-up child/elder care; adoption, surrogacy, and doula reimbursement; educational assistance, including select programs fully paid; a robust wellness program with financial incentives.
In addition, PNC generally provides the following paid time off, depending on your eligibility: maternity and/or parental leave; up to 11 paid holidays each year; 9 occasional absence days each year, unless otherwise required by law; between 15 to 25 vacation days each year, depending on career level; and years of service.
To learn more about these and other programs, including benefits for full time and part-time employees, visit pncthrive.com.

Disability Accommodations Statement
If an accommodation is required to participate in the application process, please contact us via email at AccommodationRequest@pnc.com. Please include “accommodation request” in the subject line title and be sure to include your name, the job ID, and your preferred method of contact in the body of the email. Emails not related to accommodation requests will not receive responses.  Applicants may also call 877-968-7762 and say "Workday" for accommodation assistance. All information provided will be kept confidential and will be used only to the extent required to provide needed reasonable accommodations.


At PNC we foster an inclusive and accessible workplace.  We provide reasonable accommodations to employment applicants and qualified individuals with a disability who need an accommodation to perform the essential functions of their positions.

Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO)

PNC provides equal employment opportunity to qualified persons regardless of race, color, sex, religion, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, veteran status, or other categories protected by law.

This position is subject to the requirements of Section 19 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) and, for any registered role, the Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 (SAFE Act) and/or the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA), which prohibit the hiring of individuals with certain criminal history.

California Residents
Refer to the California Consumer Privacy Act Privacy Notice to gain understanding of how PNC may use or disclose your personal information in our hiring practices.

analysis.roleType: IT Service Desk / Help Desk Support
analysis.seniorityLevel: Entry to Mid-Level (3+ years service desk experience required)

missingSkills.reason: This is explicitly listed as a core requirement. The role requires 3+ years of traditional IT service desk experience managing enterprise tickets, escalations, and corporate technology support. Retail tech support is fundamentally different.

missingSkills.skill: Ticketing System Experience (ServiceNow, Remedy, JIRA Service Desk)
missingSkills.reason: Service desk analysts live in ticketing systems daily. No evidence of using enterprise ITSM platforms for incident management, SLA tracking, or queue management.
Must support Windows domain environments, Active Directory, VPN, enterprise email systems, network connectivity issues - none of this exists in retail Apple support.

missingSkills.skill: Remote Support Tools (TeamViewer, LogMeIn, Remote Desktop)
missingSkills.reason: Position requires providing remote technology support to internal employees. No experience with enterprise remote access/support tools.
Banking environments are predominantly Windows-based. Candidate has only Apple ecosystem experience (iOS, macOS) with no Windows troubleshooting background.
Service desk supports business applications (CRM, ERP, banking software). Experience is limited to consumer apps and Apple-specific software.
Enterprise service desks operate on ITIL principles for incident, problem, and change management. No evidence of understanding these frameworks.
Must diagnose connectivity issues, VPN problems, printer configurations. While multi-cloud project shows some networking knowledge, no practical hands-on troubleshooting experience.
Common service desk responsibility includes resetting passwords, managing mailboxes, and troubleshooting Office apps. No demonstrated experience.
Listed as a key competency. Service desks use ACD systems, call routing, and phone queue management different from retail floor interactions.

experienceGaps: Zero enterprise IT service desk experience - all experience is consumer retail technology
No exposure to corporate IT environments, employee support, or internal customer service
Lacks understanding of escalation procedures in structured IT support hierarchies
No experience with SLA-driven support (response times, resolution targets)

applications.role: Rotational summer internship focused on learning multifamily real estate investment management and capital markets through hands-on financial modeling, underwriting analysis, investor presentation preparation, and market research across multiple business divisions
applications.jobDescription:
Description


Middleburg Communities is a fully integrated multifamily investment, development, construction, and management company operating in the Southeastern and Mid-Atlantic United States. We are currently searching for a Summer Analyst Intern to join our Investment Management and Capital Markets Teams at our Corporate Headquarters in Vienna, VA.

 

At Middleburg Communities, we share a vision for greater value creation through community impact. Our success is rooted in a genuine desire to serve our local communities in thoughtful and holistic ways. By enhancing the lives of others, we contribute positively to neighborhoods and maximize real returns for our partners. Each Team Member’s participation in our Mission, living by our Values, and promoting the Vision of Excellence makes Middleburg Communities a great place to work.



Our Analyst Internship Program focuses on providing our participants the opportunities to collaborate with and learn directly from the Middleburg senior and executive leadership teams through our 8-10 week paid rotational program. The program is positioned within Investment Management and will be working directly with all of Middleburg’s business divisions including investments, capital markets, acquisitions, property management, and development.

 

This exceptional program provides broad hands-on exposure to several aspects of the Multifamily real estate industry at one of the region’s top-performing firms. You can expect that no two days will be the same and each day will provide a new opportunity to truly learn about all facets of our business and provide critical support.  

 

Role Responsibilities Overview:

Build financial models using Excel to evaluate historical & future data projections on financial performance for investment management team; 
Provide underwriting support for prospective acquisition & development ventures using excel models for investment strategy review;
Create investment offering memorandums & assist in preparing investor presentations & reports for the capital markets team;
Perform property due diligence & market research for the asset management team;
Conduct comprehensive analysis on performance for critical operating activities like leasing & renewals to assist the property management teams.  
Requirements


Knowledge & Experience

Current Junior or Senior pursuing a Bachelor's program in Real Estate, Business, Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related program required at an accredited college or university
Desire to learn all aspects of real estate industry to accelerate your development & learning to build your career upon.
Advanced proficiency in MS Excel, including building models & data analysis.
Excellent written & oral communication & presentation skills using MS Word & PowerPoint
Strong time management skills, including the ability to multitask & meet deadlines in a timely manner.
Ability to be a self-starter; build knowledge of transactions & industry terms; contribute beyond direct scope of role
Summary

While they demonstrate analytical ability and work ethic, they lack every critical skill this role demands: real estate financial modeling, underwriting, property due diligence, investment analysis, and industry knowledge. The hiring manager will reject this application in 30 seconds because there's no evidence the candidate understands what real estate analysts actually do day-to-day. This internship is designed for finance/real estate majors who already know DCF models and cap rates - not someone who needs to learn the entire industry from scratch.

Get industry exposure: Join Real Estate Club at university, attend NAIOP or ULI young professional events, and network with real estate professionals to understand deal flow and investment processes

Target entry-level real estate roles first: Apply to property management analyst roles or real estate brokerage analyst positions where your data skills are valuable but real estate expertise isn't assumed
Reposition your narrative: Stop leading with Python/ML and emphasize transferable skills like Excel analytics, business problem-solving, and client-facing experience from Apple

Consider alternative path: Apply to real estate tech companies (Zillow, CoStar, Yardi) where your data science skills are the primary requirement, then transition to traditional real estate investing after 2-3 years

applications.role: Real estate investment underwriting and analysis for new acquisition opportunities, including financial modeling, due diligence, asset management support, market research, and investor/lender reporting for a multifamily rental housing development firm
applications.jobDescription:
Description


Middleburg is a vertically integrated real estate investment, development, and construction firm specializing in attainable rental housing across the southeastern United States since 2004.



Middleburg is seeking to add a diligent and resourceful team member to the Investments team who will be involved in underwriting and conducting due diligence for new investment opportunities, investment sales, asset management, research, and reporting.  This position will require daily interaction and collaboration with other teams across the organization including but not limited to Land Acquisitions, Development, Asset Management, Research, and Property Management.



This position will be full-time, in person, located in the Vienna, VA headquarters and will report directly to the Managing Director of Investments.



Essential Duties and Responsibilities:

Responsibilities for the position include, but are not limited to the following:

Underwriting
Collaborate with the Land Acquisitions, Development, and Research teams to assess investment opportunities to meet company goals regarding the development pipeline
Conduct thorough diligence on all matters relating to revenue and expense projections
Prepare complete and accurate financial models incorporating varying capital structures as advised by the Managing Direct of Investments
Routinely update and maintain Middleburg’s various underwriting tools
Investment Sales
Prepare offering memorandums and supplemental marketing materials for investor/lender presentations and meetings
Adequately address investor/lender diligence questions related to underwriting
Conduct analyses related to Asset Management including but not limited to:
Internal valuations
Hold/Sell analyses
Portfolio analyses including summarizing current and historical performance
Conduct analyses related to Research including but not limited to:
Comparable sales analyses
Comparable operating expense analyses
Comparable tax analyses and tax incentives
Demographic and employment trends
As applicable, legislative changes impacting underwriting
Produce reporting including but not limited to:
Project-specific lender/investor reporting
Portfolio reporting to fund investors
Internal reporting
Requirements


Knowledge and Experience: 

Bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, mathematics, economics, or related field. 
Some prior acquisitions or real estate experience and/or training is a plus (0-2 years).
Eagerness to learn sourcing and acquisitions process in real estate.
Some Knowledge of the market and industry trends, cap rates, the price per unit, rents, etc.
Prior experience with evaluating, underwriting, and modeling real estate is a plus.
Must be comfortable with reviewing and understanding site selection criteria’s
Comfortable with calling and contacting Brokers in order to source potential investment opportunities.
Have experience creating complex Excel spreadsheets and models.
Experience with GIS/Mapping software.
Perform other ad hoc duties as assigned.

applications.role: Providing technical expertise and support for EDI B2B communications and systems integration, including partner onboarding, requirements gathering, solution delivery, testing, deployment, and ongoing support for data exchange between the organization and partner systems
applications.jobDescription:
Job Summary

The Associate Analyst Technology role will play a crucial role in ensuring the smooth exchange of data between the organization and partner systems. This position is responsible for providing technical expertise and support for business-to-business EDI (B2B) communications and systems integration. Primary responsibilities include onboarding business partners, requirements gathering, gap analysis, technical specification review, testing, deployment, ongoing support, monitoring, and serving as a key point of contact for internal and external stakeholders.
Job Description

MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES

Provide technical expertise for EDI B2B communications, systems integration, and interface development, including low to medium complexity solutions and quality assurance activities.
Collaborate with business users to gather requirements, evaluate solution options, and translate needs into system specifications with minimal oversight.
Independently manage EDI solution delivery and support tasks, seeking guidance from senior team members for complex issues and navigating organizational resources as needed.
Serve as the primary contact for related issues and enhancements, maintaining strong relationships with internal stakeholders, customers, and vendors.
Coordinate and maintain partner data transactions, including setup, testing, troubleshooting, and compliance across networks and systems.
Utilize technical tools to analyze data, resolve mapping gaps, and support existing processes, ensuring smooth production operations and issue resolution.
Perform testing, implementation, and maintenance of partner definitions and translation objects, using appropriate software tools and adhering to quality standards.
Create and maintain documentation, including business process/system designs, user guides, procedures, and training materials, following departmental standards.
Support production environments through issue analysis and resolution, including on-call support for critical incidents outside regular hours.
Communicate effectively across teams and levels, sharing expertise, providing status updates, and maintaining a professional and collaborative demeanor.
MINIMUM JOB REQUIREMENTS

Education

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, information technology, related field, or equivalent work experience with desired systems and technologies.
Certification / Licensure

Work Experience

0–2 years of experience in building, maintaining, and supporting systems integrations on relevant IT platforms.
Experience in B2B integration; familiar with standard concepts, practices, and procedures within the field.
Knowledge / Skills / Abilities

Knowledge of mainstream integration platforms and data standards.
Good working knowledge of order-to-cash or procure-to-pay processes.
Strong analytical skills to understand business goals and design appropriate solutions.
Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
Strong oral and written communication skills to effectively interact with integration partners and internal customers.
Demonstrate collaborative skills and ability to work well within a team.
Ability to work under pressure to meet deadlines and multitask/prioritize as needed.
Ability to act and learn on own initiative and execute objectives without day-to-day supervision.
Medline Industries, LP, and its subsidiaries, offer a competitive total rewards package, continuing education & training, and tremendous potential with a growing worldwide organization.

The anticipated salary range for this position:

$62,000.00 - $93,000.00 Annual
The actual salary will vary based on applicant’s location, education, experience, skills, and abilities. This role is bonus and/or incentive eligible. Medline will not pay less than the applicable minimum wage or salary threshold.

Our benefit package includes health insurance, life and disability, 401(k) contributions, paid time off, etc., for employees working 30 or more hours per week on average. For a more comprehensive list of our benefits please click here. For roles where employees work less than 30 hours per week, benefits include 401(k) contributions as well as access to the Employee Assistance Program, Employee Resource Groups and the Employee Service Corp.

We’re dedicated to creating a Medline where everyone feels they belong and can grow their career. We strive to do this by seeking diversity in all forms, acting inclusively, and ensuring that people have tools and resources to perform at their best. Explore our Belonging page here.

Medline Industries, LP is an equal opportunity employer. Medline evaluates qualified individuals without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, neurodivergence, protected veteran status, marital or family status, caregiver responsibilities, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws.

applications.role: Non-IT Business Analyst role focused on business performance analysis, process improvement, and data-driven decision support within the Business Development department. Primary responsibilities include conducting organizational research, documenting and optimizing workflows, delivering accurate reporting and analysis, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to enhance productivity and efficiency.
applications.jobDescription:
📢 We’re Hiring: Business Analyst
📌 Department: Business Development (Non‑IT Role)

Step into a high‑impact role where your insights shape key business decisions. This position is not an IT role—it sits within our Business Development Department, focusing on strategy, process improvement, and meaningful organizational growth. If you love solving problems and turning data into smart actions, this opportunity is for you. 🚀

Why Join Crawford & Company:
💻 Offers Work From Home Position | Candidates can be anywhere in the US!
🧘 Excellent Crawford Benefits Supporting Financial, Physical & Mental Wellness
🤝 Generous Employee Referral Bonus Program
🛍️ Access to Multiple Employee Discounts

Role Overview:
📊 Analyze business performance & productivity—conduct research, organizational studies, and data-driven evaluations to uncover insights and opportunities.
📝 Document and improve processes—simplify existing workflows and recommend enhancements to boost overall efficiency and effectiveness.
📈 Deliver accurate reporting & analysis—provide clear, relevant insights that support informed decision-making for key initiatives.
🤝 Collaborate & communicate effectively—partner closely with internal teams, support internal reporting needs, and continuously learn to stay ahead in a dynamic environment.
Responsibilities
Conduct research studies to enhance productivity by optimizing the use of application systems.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure project execution aligns with defined objectives, timelines, and quality standards. Support vendor management and knowledge management initiatives.
Provide data and information to business/corporate units, aiding in appropriate decision-making. Utilize complex financial models that incorporate all relevant metrics and business drivers to assist in developing priorities within business units and across the company.
Participate in developing business automation request requirements for new or existing system designs.
Evaluate and critique software packages and emerging technologies for business applicability. Analyze and incorporate new technologies into existing or future systems in collaboration with business/corporate units and IT.
Prepare project budgets and conduct cost/benefit analyses, as requested.
Coordinate with cross-functional teams to integrate work processes and standard operating procedures effectively, maximizing overall benefits.
Develop, write, publish, and maintain standard operating procedures/process flows and documentation.
Participate in planning and conducting pilots and contribute to the development of training programs.
Take ownership of progress tracking, internal reporting processes, and preparation of presentations and documentation for assigned projects.
Represent Crawford at meetings with customers, vendors, and outside consultants as needed.
Keep the Manager informed verbally and in writing about activities and problems within the assigned area of responsibility. Refer matters beyond limits of authority and expertise to the Manager for direction.
Perform other related duties as required or requested.
Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Accounting/Finance, Industrial Engineering, or a related field.
Equivalent combination of education and experience is also considered.
One to three years of professional experience providing a working knowledge of applied business analysis concepts.
Experience with reporting methods, general business practices, and procedures.
Exposure to supporting project execution and data-driven solutions.
Excellent skills with Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio, etc.
Demonstrates ability to manage projects involving planning, coordinating, and controlling.
Working knowledge of project techniques and software is desirable.
Proficiency in business process engineering, data management, and analytics.
Demonstrates sound skills in fact gathering, defining objectives, and determining alternatives.
Ability to analyze problems, develop hypotheses, and make sound recommendations.
Demonstrates effective and diplomatic oral and written communication skills, including the ability to develop and convey complex information clearly and persuasively (including formal presentations) and to respond to questions from all levels of the organization.
Demonstrates a sound knowledge of technology and how to relate business needs in data processing terms.
Proficiency in developing financial models.
Proactive and detail-oriented approach, with a focus on delivering high-quality outcomes.
Effective time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.
A strong team player with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.
Commitment to continuous learning and professional development.
Must be able to travel as required. Travel is variable both in frequency and duration.
