qaHistory.q: Can you describe your experience in Market Research and Data Analysis? Please elaborate.
qaHistory.a:
My market research and data analysis experience comes from a mix of hands-on projects and practical applications where I really had to dig into data to understand what was happening and make recommendations.

At Apple, I did a lot of informal but strategic market research through direct customer conversations. I'd approach business owners who came into the store and ask detailed questions about how they used technology in their operations—things like how many devices they managed, what their pain points were with setup and security, and what their actual workflow looked like. I'd take that information and analyze patterns across multiple business customers to identify which Apple solutions would actually solve their problems. For example, I noticed that a lot of small business owners were struggling with device management across their teams, so I'd compile that information and pass it to our business team with specific insights about what features mattered most. This helped me understand how to connect customer needs to available solutions, which is really the core of market research.

My most rigorous data analysis experience came through my Retail Strategy project at Mason, where I worked with a real dataset from a convenience store chain trying to convert fuel-only customers into in-store shoppers. I used R and the tidymodels framework to build and compare three different machine learning models—logistic regression, linear discriminant analysis, and a random forest model that I tuned using cross-validation. I started with exploratory data analysis to understand the distributions and relationships in the data, then engineered features and ran the models to find out what actually predicted customer conversion. The random forest model performed best, and when I dug into the variable importance plots, I found that loyalty program enrollment and fuel visit frequency were the strongest predictors. I translated those technical findings into business recommendations, like restructuring how they acquired loyalty members and targeting high-frequency fuel customers with specific promotions.

I also applied market research principles when I built Dispomode, the e-commerce site. I used Ahrefs to conduct keyword gap analysis and identify search terms with high traffic but low competition—basically finding inefficiencies in how competitors were approaching SEO. I'd analyze search volume data, keyword difficulty scores, and user intent to figure out which product pages to prioritize building. That research directly informed our content strategy and helped us scale to about 100,000 monthly visitors.

Right now at my AI training role with Handshake, I'm analyzing model outputs to determine which results best match the original intent—it's a different type of analysis, but it's still about evaluating multiple options against specific criteria and making data-informed decisions about quality. I'm comfortable working with Excel, SQL, Python, R, Tableau, and Power BI depending on what the task needs, and I'm used to turning raw data into insights that actually help make decisions.

qaHistory.q: How many years of professional experience do you have as a Business Analyst?
qaHistory.a:
I have about 6 months of professional experience that directly relates to business analysis work. While my job title at Apple was Product Specialist, a significant part of my role evolved into what you'd call business analysis—especially after I started identifying business clients in the store and connecting them with our enterprise team.

I'd spend time analyzing customer purchase patterns and business needs, documenting their technology infrastructure challenges, and recommending process improvements through Apple's ecosystem. For example, I'd meet with business owners, ask detailed questions about their workflows, document their pain points around device management or employee productivity, and then work with our Business team to propose solutions like MDM implementations or financing structures. I also tracked my own performance metrics constantly—analyzing my conversion rates, NPS scores, and identifying which approaches worked best with different customer segments.

More recently, in my AI training role at Handshake, I've been conducting quality assessments and providing detailed feedback on model performance, which involves a lot of analysis and documentation work. But I want to be transparent—I don't have the traditional 1-3 years of dedicated business analyst experience in a corporate office setting that your job description mentions. Most of my analytical work has been self-directed through academic projects, my e-commerce business where I did market research and tracked financial performance, and the client-facing problem-solving at Apple. I'm genuinely excited about this role because it would let me apply the analytical skills I've been building in a more structured business development environment.

applications.role: Supporting information security risk management through conducting risk assessments, vendor security reviews, GRC platform administration, and documentation of security processes while collaborating with cross-functional teams to track and mitigate identified risks
applications.jobDescription:
Sony Corporation of America, located in New York, NY, is the U.S. headquarters of Sony Group Corporation, based in Tokyo, Japan. Sony's principal U.S. businesses include Sony Electronics Inc., Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC, Sony Music Entertainment, Sony Music Publishing and Sony Pictures Entertainment Inc. With some 900 million Sony devices in hands and homes worldwide today, a vast array of Sony movies, television shows and music, and the PlayStation Network, Sony creates and delivers more entertainment experiences to more people than anyone else on earth. To learn more: www.sony.com/en.
 

Position Summary 

Sony Corporation of America (SCA) is seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Intern Risk Management to support the Corporate Information Security Department (CISD). This internship provides hands-on exposure to information security risk management, governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) processes, and third-party security assessments. The intern will work closely with the Risk Management team to help protect Sony’s information assets, systems, and services. 

The CISD summer internship program is designed to provide skills, knowledge, and hands-on work experience in the information security and privacy industry, supporting the personal growth of our interns as well as Sony business results.  

This 12‑week summer internship runs from June through August and offers a hybrid work option. 

 

Responsibilities: 

Assist with information security risk assessments for systems, applications, projects, and third-party vendors. 

Support the preparation of risk assessment documentation and reports for internal stakeholders. 

Help with vendor security reviews, including gathering evidence and reviewing security questionnaires. 

Collaborate with security, technology, and business teams to track identified risks and mitigation actions. 

Support activities related to the global GRC platform, including data entry, intake tracking, and documentation. 

Assist in documenting business requirements, process flows, and enhancement requests for GRC initiatives. 

Participate in identifying gaps in processes or controls and proposing improvement ideas. 

Support testing activities, including test case execution and validation of results. 

Assist with training materials, presentations, and stakeholder communication. 

Provide general administrative and project support for risk management and GRC initiatives as needed. 

Honesty, trustworthiness, and ethical conduct are material requirements for the responsibilities outlined above. 

 

 

Qualifications: 

Current student pursuing a bachelor's or master's degree in business, Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Engineering, or a related field. 

Interest in information security, risk management, or GRC. 

Basic understanding of information security concepts (e.g., access management, networking, cloud, or data protection) is a plus. 

Strong analytical, research, and documentation skills with attention to detail. 

Ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical audiences. 

Comfortable working with data, spreadsheets, and documentation. 

Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). 

Self-motivated, eager to learn, and able to work both independently and in a team environment. 

Ability to manage multiple tasks and adapt in a dynamic environment. 

All candidates must be authorized to work in the USA.

applications.role: Providing administrative, operational, and customer service support for multiple trust and benefit plans, serving as the primary contact between members, trustees, insurers, and staff while managing billing, claims verification, payment workflows, and secure recordkeeping
applications.jobDescription:
Trust Services Account Specialist

 

 

The Air Line Pilots Association, International (ALPA), the largest airline pilot union in the world and the largest non-governmental aviation safety organization in the world (representing over 80,000 pilots at 42 U.S. and Canadian airlines) seeks an experienced Trust Services Account Specialist for our office in Tysons (McLean), Virginia.  Under the guidance of senior staff and management and as an integral part of the Member Insurance Team, the Trust Services Account Specialist will assist the Member Insurance Department by providing administrative, operational, and customer-service assistance for the: 

 

ALPA Pilot Welfare Benefit Plan (PWBP);
Voluntary Employees’ Beneficiary Association (VEBA);
ALPA Canada Insurance Trust (ACIT); and,
Air Canada Pilots Benefits Trust (ACPBT).
 

Serving as the primary operational contact between members, trustees, insurers, and ALPA staff, they ensure accurate processing of member inquiries, billing adjustments, Extended Mutual Aid (EMA) intake and documentation, claim verification, monthly payment workflows, and secure recordkeeping. They safeguard trust assets and protect member benefits while maintaining strict confidentiality and adherence to departmental procedures.

 

Travel:  5 - 10%. 

 

ALPA is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity and inclusion in a safe workplace.  We prohibit discrimination, harassment and harmful behavior of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, gender identity, caste, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or other protected characteristics as outlined in federal or provincial laws.  We highly value everyone and all are encouraged to apply, including minorities, veterans, and people with disabilities.

 

This position is covered by a collective bargaining agreement.

ALPA is a member-driven, staff supported, union with two internal professional unions.

 

Qualifications:

 

Bachelor’s degree in relevant area, e.g. Insurance, Business, Accounting, or Finance, from an accredited college or university required; or, equivalent combination of education and practical experience.
Three (3) years administrative or customer/member service experience; five (5) or more preferred.
Voluntary benefits insurance experience is a definite plus.
Solid organizational skills.
Demonstrated performance as a self-starter with the ability to work independently, without close supervision, and provide administrative solutions to various issues as they arise.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, oral and written, for effective interaction with all levels of contacts, internal and external.
Strong attention to detail and follow-through skills.
Software: Microsoft Excel, Word, Power Point, and Outlook required; experience with Aptify, SharePoint, and Tableau preferred.
 

 

Physical Demands:

 

Note: The physical demands described herein are characteristic of those that must be met to successfully perform the essential functions of this position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals to perform the essential physical activities of this position described below.

 

Constantly operates a computer/smartphone/tablet.   Regularly required to maintain a stationary position, move about the office, determine what others have said or written, and converse with others and exchange accurate information.

 

Regularly required to sit, stand, bend, reach, and move about the office and travel locally and nationally.   Also includes occasional bending, stooping, squatting, and/or pushing and pulling or moving, e.g., to pack, unpack, and/or move cases.

 

Occasionally required to move, raise, reach, and/or retrieve binders, books, boxes, and files up to ten (10) pounds (lbs.).  While on travel, may be responsible to move, raise, reach, and/or retrieve binders, boxes, cases, and/or files weighing as much as 50 lbs.   (Assistance may not always be available.)

 

ALPA offers competitive salaries with terrific benefits, including:

 

401k Plan with Non-Elective Employer Contribution of 12% plus 2% contribution into a Market-Based Cash Balance Plan (MBCBP) after 180 days of employment.  No employee contribution required!  Employees can choose to make pre-tax, Roth, and after-tax contributions to the 401(k). The plan permits in-plan Roth conversions and has a 4-year vesting schedule. The MBCBP has a 3-year cliff vesting period; 
Generous health care benefits on day one –  Aetna PPO Plan, Aetna High Deductible Health Plan, and a Kaiser HMO Plan (where available), which includes coverage for medical, dental, and vision benefits for the employee, their spouse, and/or dependent children. Employees do not have to pay premiums if they enroll in the Aetna High Deductible Health Plan or the Kaiser HMO Plan;
15 days paid vacation and 13 holidays per year plus 2 volunteer days per year; 
Generous sick and bereavement leave;
Competitive parental leave;
Company-paid coverage for long term disability, life insurance, and accidental death & dismemberment;
Flexible Spending and Health Savings accounts;
Retiree health plan and a retiree health account (retiree HRA). Funds in the retiree health account can be used to pay for eligible medical expenses in retirement;
Education Assistance Program that reimburses 100% of eligible expenses;
Optional benefits include a legal plan, supplemental life insurance, college 529 plan, pet insurance, long term care insurance, and qualified transportation fringe benefits (where available); and,
Partial remote work opportunities are available after six months of service.
 

PROJECTED ANNUAL SALARY RANGE:  $ 67,981.00  –  $ 95,276.0

qaHistory.q: If applicable, are you willing to relocate to the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area at your own expense? If so, please provide the maximum amount of time that you would need to complete your relocation.*
qaHistory.a: Yes, I'm willing to relocate to the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area at my own expense. I'm actually already based in Northern Virginia, so this position in Tysons is extremely convenient for me. Since I'm local to the area, I wouldn't need any relocation time and could start immediately once given an offer. I currently live close enough that commuting to the McLean office would be straightforward, and I'm very familiar with the area from my previous work at the Apple Store in Tysons. Being local also means I don't have any logistical barriers like finding housing or adjusting to a new city, so I can focus entirely on getting up to speed with ALPA's trust services operations from day one.

qaHistory.q: Our industry occasionally experiences extenuating circumstances which might require you to work extended hours. Are you able to work nights and weekends, as needed; and, are you able to travel up to 10% of the year?*
qaHistory.a:
Yes, I'm absolutely able to work extended hours, nights, and weekends when needed, and I can travel up to 10% of the time. During my three years at Apple, I regularly worked outside standard hours, especially during product launches and high-traffic periods. When the Apple Vision Pro launched, I came in early and stayed late to support the rollout, and I volunteered to work weekends during the holiday season when customer service demands were highest. I learned that operational roles sometimes require flexibility, and I've always been the person who stays until the work is done right—whether that meant helping a customer finish a complex setup after closing time or coming in on my day off to cover for a colleague.

I also understand that trust administration work has critical deadlines around billing cycles, claims processing, and member inquiries that can't always wait until Monday morning. My experience managing time-sensitive customer issues at Apple taught me how to prioritize urgent matters and stay organized under pressure. As for travel, I'm actually excited about that aspect. I don't have constraints that would prevent me from traveling, and I see it as an opportunity to build stronger relationships with trustees, insurers, and members across different locations. The 5-10% travel requirement is completely manageable for me, and I'm ready to be flexible with my schedule to support the Member Insurance Department's needs.

qaHistory.q: In this position, you must be a self-starter with the ability to work independently, without close supervision, and provide administrative solutions to various issues as they arise. Are you comfortable in this role?*
qaHistory.a:
I'm very comfortable working independently and taking initiative without needing constant supervision. At Apple, I had to manage multiple customer interactions at once during busy periods, and no one was looking over my shoulder telling me what to do next. I learned to read situations quickly and figure out the best path forward on my own. For example, when customers came in with unique technical issues or complicated trade-in scenarios, I couldn't always run to a manager—I had to troubleshoot the problem myself, use the diagnostic tools I had access to, and come up with solutions that worked for both the customer and the store's policies.

The same was true when I built Flashconverter. I didn't have a team or a boss giving me direction—I had to identify the problem I was trying to solve, research the best technical approach, teach myself the tools I needed, and then actually build the platform from start to finish. When something broke or didn't work the way I expected, I had to debug it on my own and find creative workarounds. That project taught me how to be resourceful and figure things out independently, which I think translates really well to an administrative role where problems come up throughout the day and you need to handle them without waiting for someone to tell you what to do.

qaHistory.q: In this position, you will process member inquiries, billing adjustments, Extended Mutual Aid intake and documentation, claim verification, payment workflows, secure recordkeeping, and safeguard trust assets and protect member benefits while maintaining strict confidentiality and adherence to departmental procedures. Are you comfortable in these roles?*
qaHistory.a:
Yes, I'm absolutely comfortable taking on these responsibilities. Throughout my experience at Apple, I became very familiar with managing sensitive customer information and working through detailed documentation processes. I regularly handled customer account details, payment processing, and billing adjustments while ensuring everything stayed secure and confidential. When customers had issues with their accounts or needed changes to their billing information, I had to be really precise with the documentation and make sure I followed Apple's strict protocols every time.

I also gained solid experience working across multiple systems to verify information and process requests accurately. At Apple, I frequently coordinated between different teams—technical support, business specialists, and management—to resolve customer issues, which taught me how important it is to keep clear records and communicate effectively across different groups. I'm naturally very detail-oriented, which came through in my work with the Apple Vision Pro program where I was trusted to handle high-value transactions and maintain accurate records of customer interactions.

The confidentiality aspect is something I take seriously. At Apple, I dealt with business clients' sensitive information, including their purchase history and account details, and I understood that maintaining that trust was non-negotiable. I'm comfortable following established procedures carefully and asking questions when I need clarification to make sure everything is done correctly. While I'll definitely need to learn the specific systems and procedures for trust administration, the core skills of accurate recordkeeping, following protocols, and handling sensitive information with care are things I've already developed and am ready to apply in this role.

qaHistory.q: Do you have experience with voluntary benefits insurance?*
qaHistory.a:
I don't have direct experience working with voluntary benefits insurance products. My professional background has been focused on technical customer support and data analytics rather than insurance administration.

That said, during my time at Apple, I did work extensively with clients to understand their specific needs and explain complex product offerings, which required me to quickly learn technical details about various programs and communicate them clearly to customers. I also had to navigate different coverage options like AppleCare protection plans, help customers evaluate what made sense for their situation, and process transactions while ensuring accuracy in documentation. While this isn't insurance experience, I developed strong skills in explaining benefits, answering detailed questions about coverage, and managing customer information with attention to detail.

I recognize that voluntary benefits insurance has its own specific terminology, regulations, and processes that I'll need to learn. I'm genuinely interested in this field and confident I can get up to speed quickly, especially given that the role emphasizes administrative support and customer service, which align well with my background. I'm a fast learner who's comfortable diving into new systems and picking up industry-specific knowledge as I go.

qaHistory.q: Do you have administrative and/or customer service experience? If so, how many years?*
qaHistory.a:
Yes, I have approximately three years of administrative and customer service experience from my time at Apple. I started as a seasonal Product Specialist in September 2021 and was promoted to a permanent position within three months because of my performance with customers and attention to operational details.

My role involved a lot more than just sales—I spent significant time managing customer information, processing transactions, and maintaining detailed records in Apple's systems. I regularly handled sensitive customer data during device setups, troubleshooting sessions, and trade-in processes, which taught me the importance of confidentiality and accuracy. I also worked extensively with billing adjustments, payment processing, and resolving discrepancies when customers had questions about charges or financing options.

One aspect that really prepared me for administrative work was coordinating between different teams. I served as a connection point between customers, the Genius Bar technical team, and our business specialists. When customers had complex issues—like needing both technical support and business account setup—I made sure all the right information got to the right people and followed up to ensure nothing fell through the cracks. I also documented customer interactions thoroughly and maintained a 95%+ customer satisfaction score, which required strong attention to detail and clear communication.

The role taught me how to manage multiple priorities at once, work independently without constant supervision, and solve problems as they came up. While I don't have specific insurance or trust fund experience yet, I'm confident that my foundation in customer service, administrative accuracy, and managing sensitive information transfers well to supporting members with their benefit plans and processing their requests correctly.

applications.role: Supporting AI innovation initiatives for the Chief Transformation Officer by conducting research, developing prototypes, experimenting with AI tools and models, and contributing to practical AI use cases in federal government contracting environments
applications.jobDescription:
Edgewater Federal Solutions is seeking a detail-oriented, resourceful, and intellectually curious individual to serve as an Artificial Intelligence (AI) Applied. Projects Intern supporting our Chief Transformation Officer (CTO) and the CTO Shop’s various innovation initiatives. This role is designed for an individual who can contribute immediately to ongoing AI projects, research efforts, and prototype development supporting federal mission environments.

 

This position is intended for a highly motivated individual who is comfortable working in a fast-paced, project-driven environment. The intern will contribute directly to real AI initiatives currently under development. The role offers hands-on exposure to emerging AI tools, model capabilities, and real-world implementation challenges in federal contracting.

 

This internship will require a full-time commitment of 8 hours a day, 40 hours per week, Monday-Friday. The internship will be held on a hybrid model, with a preference for attending in-person working sessions in the Reston, Virginia office

 

Applicants should be prepared to provide an Unofficial Transcript of their collegiate courses as well as a cover letter. 

Responsibilities
Work in a team environment with regular team meetings and status updates 
Support development and experimentation with AI use cases (e.g., automation, analysis, data enrichment, retrieval-augmented generation concepts) 
Analyze emerging AI tools, model capabilities, and federal adoption trends 
Research business development with consideration for AI-enabled solutions 
Support consulting efforts related to operational processes for federal contractors 
Learn about various platforms to include Microsoft Azure and the use the AI tools within
Assist in rapid prototyping and testing of AI solutions supporting current CTO innovation initiatives
Support evaluation and experimentation with emerging AI tools, models, and platforms
Help identify practical AI use cases that can support operational efficiency and mission delivery 
Qualifications
U.S. Citizenship is required by contract.
Demonstrated curiosity and strong willingness to learn 
Recent graduate or current senior in a College/University
Enrolled in a field of study or career track related to the internship experience (i.e. in machine learning, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, data science, or relevant field)
Demonstrated interest in applied AI experimentation or building prototypes
Basic proficiency in Python
Foundational understanding of AI concepts (e.g. model API calls, prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
Student must be in good academic and personal standing
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and ability to work effectively in a team environment
Motivated individual demonstrating engagement in extracurricular or professional activities
Strong interpersonal, training, and organizational skills
Ability to work independently and contribute quickly within a project-driven environment
Must be able to attend in person
Preferred Qualifications:

Basic familiarity with cloud platforms such as Microsoft Azure
Experience in data analysis or scripting
Proficiency in AI concepts
Interest in federal contracting or mission-driven technology environments
About Us:  

Edgewater Federal Solutions is a privately held government contracting firm located in Frederick, MD. The company was founded in 2002 with the vision of being highly recognized and admired for supporting customer missions through employee empowerment, exceptional services and timely delivery. Edgewater Federal Solutions is ISO 9001, 20000-1, 270001 certified, appraised at CMMI Level 3 Maturity for Development and Services, and has been named in the Top Workplaces in the Greater Washington Area Small Companies for 2018 through 2025. 

  

It has been and continues to be the policy of Edgewater Federal Solutions to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, marital status, veteran status, and/or other statuses protected by applicable law.

applications.role: End-to-end customer support ownership with strong emphasis on using AI tools to scale support operations, translating customer feedback into product improvements, and building foundational support infrastructure for an early-stage B2B company
applications.jobDescription:
Customer Support Specialist
Austin, TX or Remote (US) | Full-time | Salary + Equity

About the Role
This is an early, high-leverage hire at Suppli. As one of our first customer-facing team members, you won't just close tickets — you'll be the person who understands our customers better than anyone else in the company and translates that understanding into real product improvements. You'll work directly with our founders, engineers, and customers to make sure every interaction with Suppli feels fast, human, and genuinely helpful.

We're looking for someone who takes full ownership, moves without being asked, and uses AI not as a novelty but as a daily tool — to draft faster, research smarter, and spend their time on the things that actually matter.

Who Thrives Here (read this first — it saves everyone time)
You own the outcome, not just the ticket. You don't close a support request and move on — you ask why it happened, whether it will happen again, and what we should do about it. You connect the dots between individual issues and systemic problems.

You're AI-native, not AI-curious. You use tools like Claude, ChatGPT, or similar every day — to draft responses, summarize call transcripts, research edge cases, and build internal documentation faster than anyone expects. You don't wait to be taught; you find the workflow yourself.

You context-switch well. One hour you're triaging a complex onboarding issue. The next you're writing a help article, logging a bug in Linear, and jumping on a quick call with a supplier. You don't lose the thread. You thrive in the fluidity.

You communicate with precision and warmth. You write responses that are clear and human — not robotic, not over-apologetic. On a call, you put customers at ease and come away with useful information. You can translate a technical issue into plain language and a customer complaint into a clear product requirement.

You have a bias for action. When something is broken, you fix what you can and escalate what you can't. When there's no playbook, you write one. You take ownership of gaps without being asked.

This isn't a 9–5 role. Building a great company means going the extra mile. We're not a face-time culture, but when it matters — and it will — you're there.

What You'll Own
Be the voice of the customer internally. Synthesize patterns from inbound issues, calls, and conversations into clear product feedback. Log bugs and feature requests in Linear with enough context that engineering can act on them. You're the best signal we have on what's broken and what's missing.

Resolve support requests end-to-end. Own inbound support across email, chat, and calls — from triage to resolution. Use AI tools to draft, research, and respond faster than feels possible manually. Escalate intelligently when needed; close the loop always.

Build the support infrastructure. Write and maintain our help center, FAQs, onboarding guides, and internal playbooks. Design the systems that let us scale knowledge rather than headcount. When you see a recurring question, your instinct is to eliminate it.

Run onboarding with confidence. Help new merchants and suppliers get up and running. Understand their workflows well enough to anticipate where they'll get stuck and guide them past it before they ask.

Work cross-functionally. You're the connective tissue between what customers are experiencing and what the team is building. Partner with sales on deal blockers, with product on prioritization, and with engineering on reproducing and resolving bugs. You'll often know the answer before anyone else does.

Improve how we support over time. Propose and implement workflow improvements — using AI tools, better documentation, or smarter processes — so the support function gets faster and more effective as we grow, not just bigger.

Must-Have
3+ years of customer support or customer success experience in a B2B environment

You're an early riser - you don't have a problem starting your day at 8AM CT

Demonstrable AI-native workflow — you can walk us through how you use AI tools to move faster and do better work today

Strong written communication — your responses are clear, warm, and precise. You can write a help article and a bug report with equal care

Comfort with ambiguity — you've worked in an environment where the playbook didn't exist yet and you helped build it

Technical curiosity — you're not an engineer, but you can read a basic error message, understand a workflow, and communicate clearly with a technical team

High ownership mindset — you notice gaps, you close them, and you don't need to be asked twice

Bonus Points
Experience at an early-stage startup where you wore multiple hats

Familiarity with tools like Intercom, Linear, Notion, or similar

Background in payments, accounting, fintech, or adjacent industries — you'll understand our customers faster

Comfort reading basic data — support metrics, ticket trends, customer health signals

presentSkills: AI tool familiarity (Handshake AI training work)

missingSkills.reason: Role explicitly requires 3+ years in B2B customer support or success. Candidate has 3 years in retail sales/product specialist role, not support. This is a fundamental disqualifier.
No evidence of managing support queues, triaging tickets, or end-to-end issue resolution in a professional support context. Apple role was sales-focused, not support-focused.
Role requires building support infrastructure, writing FAQs, and maintaining help centers. No demonstrated experience creating customer-facing documentation at scale.

missingSkills.skill: Bug logging and technical escalation workflows
missingSkills.reason: Must log bugs in Linear with engineering-ready context. No experience working with product/engineering teams in this capacity or using tools like Linear, Intercom, or similar.

While candidate has AI training experience, there's no evidence of using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) to draft support responses, research edge cases, or build documentation faster in a support context.
No experience with SaaS onboarding, merchant/supplier workflows, or understanding B2B software adoption patterns. Apple experience is hardware/retail focused.
Role mentions reading support metrics, ticket trends, and customer health signals. Candidate has analytics skills but no demonstrated application in support operations.
Role emphasizes building playbooks where none exist. Candidate's experience is in large, structured organizations (Apple, Handshake) with established processes.
Bonus requirement. No demonstrated experience in financial services, payments infrastructure, or adjacent industries.

experienceGaps: Zero professional B2B customer support or customer success experience - this is the primary disqualifier
No experience managing support channels (email, chat, calls) in a professional capacity
No demonstrated experience building support infrastructure, knowledge bases, or scalable documentation
Missing cross-functional collaboration with engineering and product teams on bug resolution and feature requests
No startup experience where they've operated without established playbooks or processes
Lack of exposure to B2B SaaS business models, merchant/supplier relationships, or technical support workflows
No evidence of using support-specific tools (Intercom, Zendesk, Linear, etc.) or managing ticket queues
Apple experience heavily weighted toward retail sales metrics (Apple Care attachment, NPS, upselling) rather than support resolution and customer enablement
Most recent role (Handshake AI Fellow) is a gig/training role, not customer-facing support work
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is fundamentally unqualified for this role. They have retail sales experience masquerading as support experience - the Apple role was primarily about selling devices and Apple Care, not resolving customer issues or building support infrastructure. They lack the 3+ years of B2B support experience that's non-negotiable, have never managed support tickets, built help documentation, or worked with engineering to log bugs. While they show technical curiosity and AI familiarity, they've never applied these skills in a support context. This is a 20-point candidate being evaluated for an 85-point role.
improvementPlan: Get 2-3 years of actual B2B customer support or customer success experience at a SaaS company - this is non-negotiable and cannot be skipped
Start using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) daily in a professional context to draft responses, create documentation, and solve customer problems - document specific workflows and time savings
Learn support-specific tools: get hands-on with Intercom, Zendesk, Linear, Notion by building personal projects or taking courses that simulate support workflows
Build a portfolio of customer-facing documentation: create help articles, FAQs, troubleshooting guides, and onboarding materials that demonstrate clear, warm, precise writing

Transition current technical skills into a support context: take an entry-level support role at a startup where you can learn ticket management, escalation workflows, and cross-functional collaboration
Study B2B SaaS business models, particularly in fintech/payments - understand merchant onboarding, supplier workflows, and common pain points in these domains
Reframe your Apple experience honestly: it was retail sales, not support. Focus narrative on problem-solving and customer communication, but acknowledge the experience gap
Join a smaller company or startup (50-200 people) where you can build support processes from scratch and demonstrate ownership in ambiguous environments
marketSkillsToLearn: Support ticketing systems (Zendesk, Intercom, Freshdesk)
Bug tracking and project management tools (Linear, Jira, Asana)
Knowledge base platforms (Notion, Confluence, Help Scout)
Customer success methodologies and frameworks
Technical troubleshooting and root cause analysis
SaaS metrics (CSAT, NPS in support context, Time to Resolution, First Response Time)
API basics and reading error logs
CRM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot) from a support perspective
SQL for basic data querying and support analytics
Prompt engineering for support automation and AI-assisted responses
analysis.summary: This candidate does not meet the minimum requirements for this role. Their 3 years at Apple was in retail product sales, not B2B customer support, and they lack fundamental support experience: ticket management, documentation authoring, bug logging workflows, and engineering collaboration. While they demonstrate technical curiosity and some AI exposure, they've never applied these skills in a professional support context. They need 2-3 years of actual B2B SaaS support experience before being competitive for a role like this.

qaHistory.q: What method of support are you most familiar with? ie. email, phone, chat. *
qaHistory.a:
I'm most familiar with in-person and phone support from my time at Apple, where I spent three years helping customers face-to-face on the retail floor and over the phone with Apple corporate for account recovery issues. Most of my day involved walking customers through technical problems in real-time — troubleshooting their devices, explaining new features, or helping them recover forgotten Apple IDs by calling support lines together. I got comfortable reading people's body language and adapting my communication style on the spot, which taught me how to explain technical concepts in ways that actually made sense to them.

That said, I know this role is heavily email and chat-based, which is different from what I've done professionally. I haven't managed a support inbox or used tools like Intercom before. But I do write a lot — I built all the help documentation for my e-commerce project and handled customer questions through Shopify's messaging system, so I'm used to writing clearly and making sure people understand without being able to ask follow-up questions right away. I'm also someone who picks up new tools quickly, and I've been using AI daily (Claude and ChatGPT) to draft responses and troubleshoot problems faster, so I'm confident I can adapt to a ticket-based workflow and get up to speed quickly on whatever system you use.

qaHistory.q: What are common tickets you’ve resolved in your previous positions? *
qaHistory.a:
I don't have traditional customer support ticket experience, so I need to be honest about that gap — but I've resolved similar types of issues in customer-facing contexts that gave me relevant problem-solving experience.

At Apple, I regularly handled technical troubleshooting situations that felt a lot like support tickets, even though they weren't logged in a formal system. Common issues I resolved included customers who couldn't access their Apple ID (which meant walking them through account recovery with Apple corporate), iCloud backup failures (diagnosing whether it was storage space, network connectivity, or settings), and device performance problems that required running diagnostics through my laptop to identify hardware versus software issues. I also frequently dealt with customers confused about how features worked — things like setting up Apple Pay, configuring Face ID, or understanding why their photos weren't syncing across devices.

The pattern I noticed was that most issues fell into a few categories: password/access problems, data sync confusion, feature education gaps, and occasional hardware diagnostics. I got pretty good at probing quickly to figure out which bucket someone's issue fell into, then either resolving it on the spot or escalating to the Genius Bar with clear context.

What I haven't done is manage these interactions through a ticketing system like Intercom or Linear, write help articles to prevent recurring questions, or log bugs for an engineering team. I understand that's a core part of this role, and I'm genuinely excited to learn those workflows — especially the part about using AI tools to draft responses faster and building documentation that scales knowledge instead of just answering the same question repeatedly.

The Apple role was consultative retail sales, and the AI experience is model training rather than using AI tools for support productivity. Run onboarding with confidence. Help new merchants and suppliers get up and running. Understand their workflows well enough to anticipate where they'll get stuck and guide them past it before they ask.

Work cross-functionally. You're the connective tissue between what customers are experiencing and what the team is building. Partner with sales on deal blockers, with product on prioritization, and with engineering on reproducing and resolving bugs. You'll often know the answer before anyone else does.

Improve how we support over time. Propose and implement workflow improvements — using AI tools, better documentation, or smarter processes — so the support function gets faster and more effective as we grow, not just bigger.

Must-Have
3+ years of customer support or customer success experience in a B2B environment

You're an early riser - you don't have a problem starting your day at 8AM CT

Demonstrable AI-native workflow — you can walk us through how you use AI tools to move faster and do better work today

Strong written communication — your responses are clear, warm, and precise. You can write a help article and a bug report with equal care

Comfort with ambiguity — you've worked in an environment where the playbook didn't exist yet and you helped build it

Technical curiosity — you're not an engineer, but you can read a basic error message, understand a workflow, and communicate clearly with a technical team

High ownership mindset — you notice gaps, you close them, and you don't need to be asked twice

Bonus Points
Experience at an early-stage startup where you wore multiple hats

Familiarity with tools like Intercom, Linear, Notion, or similar

Background in payments, accounting, fintech, or adjacent industries — you'll understand our customers faster

Comfort reading basic data — support metrics, ticket trends, customer health signals

applications.role: Supporting SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) implementation by driving deployment activities across design, testing, training, cutover, and hypercare phases while bridging the gap between program teams and end users through requirements gathering, user story creation, and role mapping for trade compliance operations
applications.jobDescription:
Post Date

Mar 12, 2026
Location

New Brunswick,
New Jersey
ZIP/Postal Code

08901
Job Type

Contract
Category

Business Analysis
Req #

NNJ-12840fed-16f7-4603-9b7e-9308efcfd967
Pay Rate

$46 - $57 (hourly estimate)
Job Description
We are seeking a Business Analyst to support a SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) implementation as part of the GTS/Transcend program. This role will drive function‑specific deployment activities across design, testing, training, cutover, and hypercare, while ensuring seamless integration across Innovative Medicine and Johnson & Johnson business functions and maintaining ERP template integrity and reliability. The Business Analyst will bridge the gap between the program team and end users by translating trade operations needs into clear requirements and executable user stories, supporting role mapping, and enabling standardization of core business capabilities and functional integrations within the end‑to‑end (E2E) process framework.
This position partners closely with functional leads, super users, and program teams to ensure readiness, compliance (GxP), data integrity, and business continuity through go‑live and stabilization.

We are a company committed to creating diverse and inclusive environments where people can bring their full, authentic selves to work every day. We are an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that believes everyone matters. Qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment regardless of their race, color, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, marital status, national origin, ancestry, genetic factors, age, disability, protected veteran status, military or uniformed service member status, or any other status or characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please send a request to HR@insightglobal.com.To learn more about how we collect, keep, and process your private information, please review Insight Global's Workforce Privacy Policy: https://insightglobal.com/workforce-privacy-policy/.
Required Skills & Experience
3–5+ years in trade operations/compliance and/or enterprise system implementation support
Trade Compliance / Trade Operations background, with demonstrated understanding of import/export compliance and operational trade processes
Experience with an SAP GTS Implementation
Proven ability to create requirements, write/validate user stories, and perform role mapping (including documenting roles/authorizations impacts to business process execution)
Excellent communication and written skills
Benefit packages for this role will start on the 1st day of employment and include medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as HSA, FSA, and DCFSA account options, and 401k retirement account access with employer matching. Employees in this role are also entitled to paid sick leave and/or other paid time off as provided by applicable law.

applications.role: Supporting consulting project implementation through data analysis, business requirements gathering, SharePoint management, and process optimization while managing multiple projects and providing recommendations to improve business outcomes
applications.jobDescription:
At Allstate, great things happen when our people work together to protect families and their belongings from life’s uncertainties. And for more than 90 years, our innovative drive has kept us a step ahead of our customers’ evolving needs. From advocating for seat belts, air bags and graduated driving laws, to being an industry leader in pricing sophistication, telematics, and, more recently, device and identity protection. 



Job Description

This position is responsible for being able to understand and can apply more complex leveled consulting methodology. Responsible for supporting the implementation of consulting projects through in-depth research, data analysis and recommends potential solutions. May lead simple projects or process design efforts.



Key Responsibilities

Support team operations by completing a variety of tasks and projects across diverse responsibilities, adapting to shifting priorities and needs
Manage multiple projects simultaneously by prioritizing tasks, balancing deadlines, and maintaining strong time‑management practices
Manage, edit, and optimize SharePoint sites, ensuring content is current, user‑friendly, and aligned with organizational standards
Presents facts and information regarding project details, alternative suggestions to negotiate mutually acceptable solutions where there are opposing ideas
Demonstrates understanding of business unit and/or industry systems, processes and requirements.
Presents facts and information regarding project details, alternative suggestions to negotiate mutually acceptable solutions where there are opposing ideas
Gathers and analyzes business and stakeholder requirements. Collects and analyzes process data to initiate, develop and recommend business practices and procedures.
Gives and receives constructive formal/informal coaching and feedback based upon observed and/or recorded performance
Make recommendations about business practices regarding current projects that will simplify, automate, streamline business processes that will improve business outcomes
Experience

• 2 or more years of experience (Preferred)

Business Processes, Consulting, Project Management Consulting

applications.role: Supporting business development activities within the Federal Civilian IT sector, including capture strategy, proposal operations, market planning, and business operations to optimize market presence and drive business growth in the federal government contracting space
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description
The Federal Civilian IT (FCIT) Business Development team is looking for interns who have an interest in a career in Business Development. The program is designed to prepare high potential interns for future positions in Leidos BD by exposing them to a variety of challenging work environments and pairing them with BD leaders who will provide mentoring throughout their internship.

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES:


The FCIT Business Development team is a dynamic group of professionals responsible optimizing our market presence and business growth within the Federal Civilian government sector. Our teams are fast-paced, strategic and highly motivated to win and capture new business. Successful candidates typically have a high-energy level, strong enthusiasm, passion for innovative thinking, a competitive edge, and strong communication skills.
We offer intern assignments in the following BD functional areas:


-Capture Strategy and Support
-Business Operations
-Market Planning & Support
-Proposal Operations
-Capture Excellence

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:


-Actively pursuing a Bachelor’s Degree
-Academic excellence
-Demonstrated leadership abilities and skills
-Energy, enthusiasm and competitive edge
-Flexibility
-Outstanding written and verbal communication skills
-Willingness to continually learn and grow
-Ability to work in a team
-Ability to work under pressure
-U.S. Citizenship is required

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:


-3.0 GPA
-Knowledge and experience in the US Government's procurement process
-Major in Business, Marketing or related discipline
-Knowledge of Government Contractor’s proposal process and development

If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares.

March 12, 2026
For U.S. Positions: While subject to change based on business needs, Leidos reasonably anticipates that this job requisition will remain open for at least 3 days with an anticipated close date of no earlier than 3 days after the original posting date as listed above.

Pay Range:
Pay Range $41,600.00 - $75,200.00
The Leidos pay range for this job level is a general guideline only and not a guarantee of compensation or salary. Additional factors considered in extending an offer include (but are not limited to) responsibilities of the job, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity, alignment with market data, applicable bargaining agreement (if any), or other law.

applications.role: Supporting R&D organization by developing and maintaining performance metrics, creating dashboards, analyzing data to track program health and milestones, and generating reports that enable data-driven decision-making for leadership within the Digital Modernization Sector
applications.jobDescription:
The Business Intelligence Intern will support the R&D organization within the Digital Modernization Sector by developing, tracking, analyzing, and reporting key performance metrics that enable data-driven decision-making. This role partners with cross-functional technical and program teams to ensure accurate measurement of program health, milestone progress, resource utilization, and strategic outcomes. The Intern will help strengthen performance visibility, improve reporting processes, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across the R&D portfolio.

Primary Responsibilities:

Develop, maintain, and continuously improve performance metrics and KPIs for the R&D program.

Create standardized dashboards and reporting tools to track program health, milestones, and outcomes.

Collect, validate, and consolidate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.

Generate recurring weekly, monthly, and/or quarterly performance reports for leadership and stakeholders.

Support metric definition workshops with technical and program teams to ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

Conduct benchmarking analyses against internal goals and external industry standards where applicable.

Automate data collection and reporting processes to improve efficiency and reliability.

Support continuous improvement initiatives by translating data insights into actionable recommendations and after-action reports.

Prepare executive-ready presentations summarizing performance insights and key takeaways.

Basic Qualifications:

Rising junior or senior pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Business Management.

Have worked in a diverse team environment and been successful in high-stress environments.

Ability to perform successfully in a highly complex business environment.

Be highly customer-oriented and demonstrate the ability to collaborate with various stakeholders.

Experience in operations to include defining metrics, data collection, data analysis, and reporting.

Experience in management to include leading a team, logistics planning, and financial management.

Proven track record of critical thinking and developing creative problem and hard problem solutions with a focus on outcome-based delivery of meaningful innovations.

Self-motivated and focused on delivering outcomes with the ability to work independently and in teams.

If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares.

applications.role: Supporting R&D organization through development and maintenance of performance metrics, dashboards, and data-driven reporting to enable strategic decision-making and continuous improvement initiatives
applications.jobDescription:
The Business Intelligence Intern will support the R&D organization within the Digital Modernization Sector by developing, tracking, analyzing, and reporting key performance metrics that enable data-driven decision-making. This role partners with cross-functional technical and program teams to ensure accurate measurement of program health, milestone progress, resource utilization, and strategic outcomes. The Intern will help strengthen performance visibility, improve reporting processes, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives across the R&D portfolio.

Primary Responsibilities:

Develop, maintain, and continuously improve performance metrics and KPIs for the R&D program.

Create standardized dashboards and reporting tools to track program health, milestones, and outcomes.

Collect, validate, and consolidate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.

Generate recurring weekly, monthly, and/or quarterly performance reports for leadership and stakeholders.

Support metric definition workshops with technical and program teams to ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

Conduct benchmarking analyses against internal goals and external industry standards where applicable.

Automate data collection and reporting processes to improve efficiency and reliability.

Support continuous improvement initiatives by translating data insights into actionable recommendations and after-action reports.

Prepare executive-ready presentations summarizing performance insights and key takeaways.

Basic Qualifications:

Rising junior or senior pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Business Management.

Have worked in a diverse team environment and been successful in high-stress environments.

Ability to perform successfully in a highly complex business environment.

Be highly customer-oriented and demonstrate the ability to collaborate with various stakeholders.

Experience in operations to include defining metrics, data collection, data analysis, and reporting.

Experience in management to include leading a team, logistics planning, and financial management.

Proven track record of critical thinking and developing creative problem and hard problem solutions with a focus on outcome-based delivery of meaningful innovations.

Self-motivated and focused on delivering outcomes with the ability to work independently and in teams.

If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares.

applications.role: Maintaining data integrity and accuracy across supply chain systems, developing reports and dashboards to support business decisions, and identifying process improvement opportunities through data-driven analytics to enhance operational efficiency
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
We are seeking a Supply Chain Data Analyst to support operations and supply chain through accurate data management, reporting, and process improvement. This role is responsible for maintaining clean, reliable data within operational systems, developing reporting that supports business decisions, and identifying opportunities to improve processes through data-driven insights.
The ideal candidate is highly detail-oriented, comfortable working with large datasets, and motivated to improve operational efficiency through better data and analytics.
Key Responsibilities:Data Management & System Integrity
Input, validate, and maintain accurate data across supply chain and operational systems.
Ensure data consistency and integrity across multiple platforms and reporting environments.
Identify data quality issues and implement processes to prevent errors and improve reliability.
Maintain master data including SKUs, product attributes, vendor information, and operational parameters.
 
Reporting & Analytics
Develop and maintain reports and dashboards supporting operations, product development, and marketing teams.
Analyze supply chain and sales data to identify trends, opportunities, and risks.
Support inventory planning, product performance analysis, and operational KPIs.
Automate recurring reporting processes where possible to improve efficiency.
 
Process Improvement
Identify opportunities to streamline workflows and improve operational processes through better data management and reporting.
Partner with cross-functional teams to design and implement improved data processes.
Document data workflows, reporting logic, and system processes.
Qualifications:
Required
Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Business Analytics, Data Analytics, Economics, or related field.
2+ years of experience in data analysis, supply chain analytics, or operations analytics.
Strong proficiency in Excel
Experience with SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
 
Preferred
Experience with ERP, inventory management, or supply chain planning systems.
Familiarity with e-commerce or apparel supply chains.
Experience automating workflows using scripts, APIs, or data tools.
Basic knowledge of forecasting, inventory management, or S&OP processes.
 
Key Skills:
Data management and data quality control
Reporting and dashboard development
Supply chain analytics
Process improvement
Cross-functional collaboration
Data visualization
Why Join Comfrt?
Be part of a purpose-driven company that’s shaping the future of comfort and mental wellness.
Work in a supportive, creative environment where your ideas and growth are valued.
Enjoy Comfrt benefits such as generous paid time off, company-covered health insurance, 5% 401k match, and discounts on all Comfrt products! 
Join a passionate team making an impact on exciting projects, all while enjoying the flexibility and collaborative support of a fully remote environment.
 
Comfrt is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. This role is open to remote candidates in multiple U.S. states. The salary range for this position is up to $75,000 annually. Actual compensation will be determined based on factors such as years of experience, skills, certifications, and geographic location.

applications.role: Gathering and documenting business requirements from stakeholders, translating business needs into functional specifications, and facilitating communication between business and technical teams to implement innovative solutions that improve client business operations
applications.jobDescription:
Business Solutions Analyst
Category: Business Analysis (functional and technical)
Main location: United States, Virginia, Arlington
Position ID: J0326-0758
Employment Type: Full Time
Position Description:
CGI Federal is looking for a Business Solutions Analyst to assist in identifying and implementing innovative solutions that improve client business operations. You will gather requirements, define functional needs, and develop solutions.

This position is located in Arlington, VA.

Your future duties and responsibilities:
- Gather and document business requirements from stakeholders.
- Assist in translating business needs into functional specifications.
- Work with technical teams to ensure the development of feasible solutions.
- Conduct cost-benefit analysis for potential solutions.
- Facilitate communication between business and technical teams.

Required qualifications to be successful in this role:
- Bachelor's degree in Business, IT, or related field.
- 0–4 years of experience in business analysis.
- Knowledge of requirements gathering techniques and tools.
- Experience working with cross-functional teams.
- Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Desired qualifications/non-essential skills required:
- Experience with Agile methodologies and Scrum framework.
- Familiarity with project management tools (e.g., Jira, Trello).

CGI is required by law in some jurisdictions to include a reasonable estimate of the compensation range for this role. No experience working with software development teams to ensure feasibility of solutions - technical background is data science/ML, not SDLC collaboration
No demonstrated experience facilitating workshops, stakeholder interviews, or requirements elicitation sessions with diverse business users
Missing exposure to enterprise software implementation projects or system integration initiatives
No federal government sector experience - different culture, processes, and constraints than commercial sector
Lack of experience translating between business stakeholders and technical teams in a formal capacity - Apple work was product consultation, not solution design
No evidence of managing requirements across multiple projects or handling competing stakeholder priorities
Missing practical application of BA methodologies, frameworks, or formal requirement gathering techniques (JAD sessions, use cases, etc.)
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is overqualified technically but underqualified in core business analysis practices. They're a data scientist trying to pivot into BA work without understanding that BA is a distinct discipline requiring specific documentation, facilitation, and requirements management skills - not just analytics. The Apple experience shows client-facing ability but it's sales consultation, not requirements gathering for software development. For a 0-4 year role at CGI Federal, they could grow into it, but they'd be starting from scratch on fundamental BA skills despite having 3+ years of work experience.
improvementPlan: Immediately learn formal BA documentation: Take IIBA-endorsed courses on writing Business Requirements Documents (BRDs), Functional Requirement Specifications (FRS), and User Stories. Practice creating requirements traceability matrices.

Get hands-on with Jira: Create a free Jira account, set up a mock project, practice writing user stories with acceptance criteria, manage a backlog, and understand how BAs use it for requirement tracking.
Complete Agile/Scrum certification: Get Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO) or Professional Scrum Master (PSM I) to understand how BAs function in Agile environments and participate in ceremonies.

Reframe current experience: Rewrite resume to emphasize requirement gathering aspects of Apple role (client needs assessment, solution specification, stakeholder communication) using BA terminology, not sales language.
Learn cost-benefit analysis frameworks: Study TCO analysis, ROI calculations for IT projects, and business case development - apply this retrospectively to projects to demonstrate capability.
Study federal contracting basics: Understand FAR regulations, security clearance levels, and how federal IT projects differ from commercial work - this shows awareness of CGI Federal's environment.
Create a BA portfolio piece: Take one of your existing projects and reverse-engineer formal BA documentation (BRD, functional specs, use cases, process flows) to demonstrate you can think like a BA.
Network with BAs at consulting firms: Conduct 3-5 informational interviews with BAs at firms like CGI, Deloitte, or Accenture to understand the day-to-day reality and language of the role.
marketSkillsToLearn: Requirements elicitation techniques (interviews, workshops, observation, surveys)
Business process modeling (BPMN 2.0, swimlane diagrams, process flows)
Use case and user story writing with acceptance criteria
Requirements traceability and change management
Agile/Scrum BA practices (backlog refinement, sprint planning participation)
Stakeholder analysis and management techniques
Gap analysis and as-is/to-be process documentation
UML diagrams (activity, sequence, class diagrams)
Wireframing and mockup tools (Balsamiq, Axure, Figma for BA purposes)
SDLC knowledge across methodologies (Waterfall, Agile, hybrid)
Microsoft Visio or Lucidchart for process documentation
Confluence for requirements documentation and collaboration
SQL for data analysis to support requirements validation
API and integration requirements specification
Testing support (UAT planning, test case review, defect triage)
analysis.summary: Score of 58 reflects a candidate with strong technical chops and client-facing skills but lacking formal BA experience and methodology. They meet basic qualifications (degree, communication skills, cross-functional work) but are missing critical documentation, Agile, and requirements management skills that define the BA role. With focused upskilling in BA fundamentals and reframing of existing experience, could become competitive for entry-level BA positions within 2-3 months.

﻿
SAIC Business Management Summer 2026 Internship

The SAIC Business Management team is excited to offer summer internship opportunities for college students in 2026. This program is ideal for those seeking real-world professional experience in an organization dedicated to using technology and innovation to serve and protect the world.

Flexible Work Options: Positions are available in-person at Reston, VA headquarters, hybrid, or fully remote nationwide.

Why Join Us?

Hands-on Experience: Collaborate with senior leaders and contribute to impactful projects.

Skill Development: Participate in training sessions, lunch-and-learns, and a collaborative intern project presented to senior leaders.

Networking and Growth: Build connections through social events and gain exposure to potential full-time opportunities for post-graduation.

Accelerated Skills: Development in a dynamic environment.

Internships are available across diverse business disciplines:

Finance: Accounting, Treasury, Tax, and Financial Planning
Contracts & Procurement
Corporate Development
Corporate Strategy

Qualifications

﻿
Candidate Qualifications:

We’re looking for self-starters with strong analytical, organizational, and collaboration skills.
Current college student with two or more years of coursework in a business-related major.
Proficient in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint (analytics, formulas, data presentation).
Strong adaptability to changing responsibilities and deadlines.
Proven teamwork or leadership experience; prior related work experience is a plus.
U.S. Citizenship is required.
What You’ll Gain:

Take the next step to build your career with SAIC!
 

Target salary range: Up to $40,000. The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position based on experience and other factors.


Overview

SAIC accepts applications on an ongoing basis and there is no deadline.

SAIC is a premier Fortune 500 mission integrator focused on advancing the power of technology and innovation to serve and protect our world. Our robust portfolio of offerings across the defense, space, civilian and intelligence markets includes secure high-end solutions in mission IT, enterprise IT, engineering services and professional services. We integrate emerging technology, rapidly and securely, into mission critical operations that modernize and enable critical national imperatives.

We are approximately 24,000 strong; driven by mission, united by purpose, and inspired by opportunities. SAIC is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Headquartered in Reston, Virginia, SAIC has annual revenues of approximately $7.5 billion. For more information, visit saic.com. For ongoing news, please visit our newsroom.
