applications.role: Supporting aftermarket services through data visualization, business analytics, and CRM optimization. The role involves identifying business opportunities, analyzing operations and market data, implementing new tools for customer account management and repair center optimization, and refining data capture and modeling processes for the aerospace and defense industry.
applications.jobDescription:
ob Description
You don’t see it, but it’s there. Our employees work on the world’s most advanced electronics – from saving emissions in the City of Lights to powering the Mars Rover to protecting the F35 fighter jet. At Electronic Systems, you’ll be among the brightest minds, working on the aerospace and defense industry’s most difficult problems. Drawing strength from our differences, we’re innovating for the future. And you can, too. Our flexible work environment provides you a chance to change the world without giving up your personal life. We put our customers first – exemplified by our missions: “We Protect Those Who Protect Us®” and “We Innovate For Those Who Move The World TM.” Sound like a team you want to be a part of? Come build your career with BAE Systems.

In Controls & Avionics Solutions, you will have the opportunity to help define and develop the next generation of avionics – whether it is fly-by-wire flight controls, full authority digital engine controls, or power management.

Because this role involves a combination of independent and collaborative work, this position can be conducted 100% remotely.

The Controls and Avionics Solutions business is part of the Electronic Systems sector within BAE Systems Inc. We are a leader in high integrity controls for both the military and commercial aviation markets, and are focused on investing and developing for future growth. In Controls & Avionics Solutions, you will have the opportunity to help define and develop the next generation of avionics – whether it is fly-by-wire flight controls, full authority digital engine controls, or power management. Our core capabilities and future technologies lie in: active controls, aftermarket services, aircraft electrification, autonomy, cabin systems, engine systems, and next-gen controls. This internship will be in support of our aftermarket services where we provide world class support throughout the lifecycle of our products, up to 600+ airlines and MROs globally.

BAE Systems is looking for an Intern with experience using data visualization tools that has an interest in the business lifecycle, customer relationship management (CRM), and operations optimization through data analysis, data modeling, and data management. The qualified candidate will have the opportunity to assist in the design and development of these capabilities and opportunity identification to help support all phases of the business lifecycle.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
Identifying, quantifying, capturing and/or modeling new business opportunities
Gathering, analyzing, and evaluating information collected on internal operations, customers, competitors and other market factors that can contribute to a business's competitive advantage and its operations
Advancing and implementing new tools to support various functions (customer account management, winning business, repair center optimization, etc)
Refining how we utilize data (capture, cleaning, organization, and modeling)


Required Education, Experience, & Skills
Currently enrolled in, or recently a post grad (with good academic standing) for a Bachelor or Master degree in Analytics, Business, Mathematics (or other related fields), or a degree with an Analytics or Business minor/concentration
Experience using data visualization software such as PowerBI, Tableau or Looker, data preparation and cleaning tools such as Alterx or Tableau Prep, SQL, MS Office suite of programs (MS Word, PowerPoint, and Excel)


Preferred Education, Experience, & Skills
Experience using Python
Comfortable with complex data relationships (ERD)
Knowledge/Experience with machine learning, time series analysis, and deep learning frameworks
Effective prompt engineering skills to leverage AI when necessary
Prior experience in the aerospace industry


Pay Information
Full-Time Salary Range: $39861 - $53766

Please note: This range is based on our market pay structures. However, individual salaries are determined by a variety of factors including, but not limited to: business considerations, local market conditions, and internal equity, as well as candidate qualifications, such as skills, education, and experience.

Temporary employees generally are not eligible for BAE Systems benefits, but can elect to participate in the 401(k) savings plan. Temporary employees working 20+ hours per week are eligible for medical benefits, the employee assistance program, and business travel accident insurance.


About BAE Systems Electronic Systems
BAE Systems, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of BAE Systems plc, an international defense, aerospace and security company which delivers a full range of products and services for air, land and naval forces, as well as advanced electronics, security, information technology solutions and customer support services. Improving the future and protecting lives is an ambitious mission, but it’s what we do at BAE Systems. Working here means using your passion and ingenuity where it counts – defending national security with breakthrough technology, superior products, and intelligence solutions. As you develop the latest technology and defend national security, you will continually hone your skills on a team—making a big impact on a global scale. At BAE Systems, you’ll find a rewarding career that truly makes a difference. Electronic Systems (ES) is the global innovator behind BAE Systems’ game-changing defense and commercial electronics. Exploiting every electron, we push the limits of what is possible, giving our customers the edge and our employees opportunities to change the world. Our products and capabilities can be found everywhere – from the depths of the ocean to the far reaches of space. At our core are more than 14,000 highly talented Electronic Systems employees with the brightest minds in the industry, we make an impact – for our customers and the communities we serve.

This position will be posted for at least 5 calendar days. The posting will remain active until the position is filled, or a qualified pool of candidates is identified.

SAIC is a premier Fortune 500 mission integrator focused on advancing the power of technology and innovation to serve and protect our world. applications.jobDescription:
Revenue Ops Analytics & Automation Intern
United States
Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.

Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

This position is 100% remote anywhere in the US.

Overview:

Precisely’s Summer Internship Program is a paid, part-time opportunity for undergraduate students actively enrolled in university. This is an 10-week program that begins Monday June 8, 2026 and ends Friday August 14, 2026. This opportunity is 100% remote and you must be located in the United States during the summer internship. The Revenue Ops Analytics & Automation Intern will work closely with the Revenue Operations team.

The Revenue Operations Analytics & Automation Intern will support the design, analysis, and automation of the systems and processes that drive our gotomarket motion. This role blends analytics, AIdriven automation, web development, and business process optimization to solve real operational challenges across Sales, Operations, and Customer teams. 

This is a hands on, project based internship where success is measured by delivered outcomes, not observation. The intern will leave with tangible artifacts—dashboards, automations, an internal site, process documentation, and analyses—that directly improve revenue operations and can be showcased in future roles. 

What you will do:

Designing and building an internal intranet site to serve as a centralized repository for Revenue Operations knowledge, reporting, and processes 
Documenting and improving endtoend revenue workflows to increase clarity and scalability 
Turning systems data into actionable insights and dashboards 
Participate in projects focused on automating manual workflows using modern tools and AI agents 
What we are looking for:

Comfortable working with data and analyzing data sets to draw meaningful business insights, even when data is imperfect, incomplete, or evolving
Experience using website builders or collaboration tools such as Notion, Webflow, SharePoint, Confluence, or Google Sites
Understanding of how internal websites and tools support information sharing, enablement, and operational workflows
Ability to understand, document, and clearly communicate step‑by‑step processes
Prior experience mapping workflows through coursework, internships, student organizations, or part‑time roles
Strong intellectual curiosity and a genuine desire to learn
Effective time‑management skills and the ability to deliver projects with guidance in a dynamic, fast‑paced environment
Program benefits:

Valuable experience related to the degree you are pursuing.
Hands on experience at an established tech company. 
Networking opportunities with company leaders around the globe. 
#LI-REMOTE

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has exceptional technical fundamentals and analytical horsepower that exceed typical intern expectations, but faces a domain mismatch problem. The strong data science, cloud, and programming skills are impressive, yet there's zero demonstrated experience with the collaboration platforms (Notion/Confluence/SharePoint) that are literally the primary deliverable of this internship. The Apple B2B sales role shows business acumen, but RevOps is a specialized function requiring familiarity with sales systems, pipeline analytics, and operational workflows that aren't evidenced here. This is a smart, technically capable candidate who would need significant onboarding into RevOps tools and domain context.
improvementPlan: Immediately create a personal knowledge base in Notion or build a mock internal site in Webflow to demonstrate proficiency with these specific platforms - add this as a portfolio project within 1-2 weeks
Take a free RevOps fundamentals course (Pavilion, RevOps Co-op, or Sales Impact Academy) to understand sales pipeline metrics, lead-to-cash workflows, and CRM architecture
Build a small automation project using Zapier or Make that connects Salesforce/HubSpot to Notion or creates a dashboard - document the business process you're automating
Reframe the Apple Business Specialist experience to emphasize any CRM usage, sales process documentation, or operational reporting you did - quantify business metrics tracked
Add a weekend project mapping a sample revenue workflow (lead gen → qualification → close → renewal) with process documentation to show you understand RevOps thinking
Get hands-on with Google Sites or Confluence free tier to build a sample 'Sales Playbook' site demonstrating information architecture skills the role requires
marketSkillsToLearn: Notion and Confluence for enterprise knowledge management
Webflow or other no-code website builders
Salesforce administration and reporting fundamentals
Revenue Operations frameworks (SaaS metrics, pipeline management, funnel analysis)
Modern automation platforms (Zapier, Make/Integromat, Workato)
HubSpot or similar marketing/sales automation CRM
SQL for CRM/sales data analysis (PostgreSQL, Snowflake)
Looker or Mode Analytics for revenue dashboards
Process mining and workflow documentation methodologies
AI agent frameworks for business automation (LangChain, n8n)
Airtable or similar low-code database tools for operations
Data pipeline tools for RevOps (Fivetran, Census, dbt)
analysis.summary: Strong technical candidate with excellent data skills and learning agility, but faces a tools and domain gap for this specific RevOps internship. The Python/SQL/ML stack is impressive, but the role needs hands-on proficiency with Notion/Confluence/Webflow and understanding of revenue operations workflows - neither of which are clearly demonstrated. With 2-3 weeks of focused upskilling on collaboration platforms and RevOps fundamentals, this candidate could be competitive; their analytical foundation and business consulting experience from Apple provide a solid base to build upon.

qaHistory.q:
Precisely is empowering every employee with AI to unlock new ways of working, learning, and innovating. This question is to help us understand the extent of your knowledge and use with AI tools. Please select one of the below that best describes you.*
Extensively – I use AI tools daily


If you answered extensively or moderately to the above question, please list the AI tools you use and briefly describe how you use them.
qaHistory.a:
I use AI tools daily across multiple aspects of my work and personal projects, so "extensively" definitely describes my current relationship with these technologies.

The main tools I work with are ChatGPT, Claude, and GitHub Copilot. In my current role as a Generative AI Fellow at Handshake, I'm literally training AI models every day—evaluating computer vision outputs for image editing tasks and recording voice samples for natural language processing development. This work has given me a really granular understanding of how these models behave, where they break down, and what makes a good prompt versus one that produces unusable results.

For my personal projects, I rely heavily on ChatGPT and Claude for problem-solving when I'm building out features. When I developed Flashconverter, my financial intelligence platform, I used AI to help debug JavaScript issues, optimize my machine learning categorization algorithm, and even brainstorm better approaches to handling IndexedDB storage patterns. I don't just copy-paste code—I use it more like a rubber duck that talks back, helping me think through architectural decisions or catch edge cases I might have missed.

I also use Cursor AI as my code editor, which has Copilot-like functionality built in. It's been incredibly helpful for speeding up repetitive coding tasks and suggesting implementations when I'm working with APIs or unfamiliar libraries. During my cloud architecture project for MIS 341, I used AI to help me understand the nuances between AWS and Azure's security group configurations when I was troubleshooting access issues.

Beyond coding, I use ChatGPT for research synthesis, turning complex technical documentation into more digestible explanations, and even for drafting initial versions of process documentation—which I know will be relevant for this role. I've found AI particularly useful for translating between technical and business language, which was super helpful during my time at Apple when I needed to explain enterprise MDM solutions to business clients who didn't have IT backgrounds.

applications.role: Analyzing, optimizing, and implementing business processes within Default Management to ensure regulatory compliance and operational efficiency. The role involves translating complex regulatory requirements into actionable processes, collaborating across departments to drive system and process enhancements, and managing change initiatives from design through deployment.
applications.jobDescription:
Summary:

The Analyst, Business Process is responsible for reviewing, researching, and deciphering regulatory and compliance material to translate for Default Management. They work closely with change management, legal, IT and various lines of businesses to implement changes necessary to ensure state, federal, investor and insurer guidelines are met. The Business Process Analyst works collaboratively with various business groups in designing and implementing planning solutions for Default Administration. They deliver data, own processes, audits automation, and assists business partners in defining and implementing system and process enhancements.

Essential Job Functions:

Analyze the process and procedures that Default has in place. Collect data and perform research to gain a complete understanding of the Default department business needs in order to measure the effectiveness of the current practices.

Works cross-functionally to drive optimized service business design within the Default department through opportunity/issue identification, systemization, testing, training and deployment.

Understand the Default department’s use of systems, reports & tools and function as a subject matter expert.

Ensure that system and tools are adequate and properly utilized. Propose and develop enhancements as necessary.

Develops recommendations for improvement based on observation, data analysis, audit recommendation, and evaluation of business cross departmental workflow.

Coordinate with external and internal business contacts for responding to system interface and data issues.

May deliver presentations and training courses including measurement, analysis, improvement and control.

Translate agency and regulatory technical material and concepts into verbal and written communications for Default Management. 

Assess the change, determine the impacts, and based on impact assist Default Management to design a strategy to support the department through the change, execute the strategy, and measure its effectiveness based on a change adoption model.

Design and facilitate meetings or focus groups as appropriate.

Maintain documentation with respect to all Default projects.

Ability to organize and manage multiple projects and timelines.

Evaluates, designs, executes, measures, monitors and control business processes.

Ensure that business process outcomes are in harmony with an organization’s strategic goals.

Work collaboratively across all departments of Default to help improve the management of a business process.

Focus on the entire process from beginning to end, introducing innovation into the process that can impact results, enhance profitability and assist the organization to meet its business objectives goals.

Maintain regular and punctual attendance.

Other Related Duties:

Performs other related duties as assigned.

Supervisory Responsibilities:

This position is an individual contributor with no direct reports and has no supervisory responsibilities.

Qualifications:

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential function satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required.

Education and/or Experience:

Bachelor’s Degree from a Four (4) year College or University.

3-5 years related experience and/or training.

Language Skills:

Ability to read, analyze, and interpret general business periodicals, professional journals, technical procedures, or government regulations. Ability to write reports, business correspondence, and procedure manuals. Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from groups of managers, clients, customers, and the general public.


Mathematical Skills:

Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as discounts, interest, commissions, and percentages.


Reasoning Ability:

Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of concrete variables in situations where only limited standardization exists. Ability to interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram, or schedule form.  The duties performed are essential to the administration of the department.

Customer Service Relationships:

Assesses and diffuses problem situations and requires influencing others to reach consensus. Requires tact and diplomacy to handle difficult customer relationships. Explores alternatives and creative solutions to meeting the needs of customers.

Work Complexity:

Tasks are multiple and diverse with some interrelationship across processes. Work requires the direct application of a variety of procedures, policies and/or precedents.

Physical Demands:

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.  While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to sit and talk or hear.  The employee is occasionally required to stand; walk; use hands to finger, handle, or feel; and reach with hands and arms. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds.

Work Environment:

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Work is normally performed in a typical interior office work environment which does not subject the employee to any hazardous or unpleasant elements. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.

Equal Employment Opportunity:

Freedom Mortgage is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants without regard to race, ethnicity, color, sex, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, religion, national origin, age (40 and over), disability, military status, genetic information or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws.

Americans with Disabilities Act:

Applicants as well as employees who are or become disabled must be able to satisfactorily perform the essential job functions of the position either with or without reasonable accommodation. Applicants as well as employees are encouraged to meet with Human Resources as the organization shall review reasonable accommodations on a case-by-case basis in accordance with applicable law.

Job Responsibilities:

The statements reflect the general duties and responsibilities considered necessary to perform the essential functions of the job and should not be considered as an all-inclusive list of all the work requirements of the position. Freedom Mortgage Corporation may change the specific job duties with or without prior notice based on the needs of the organization.

analysis.roleType: Business Process Analyst - Regulatory Compliance & Default Management

missingSkills.reason: This role is 100% focused on Default Administration processes. Zero mortgage servicing, foreclosure, or loss mitigation experience. Candidate would have no context for the business domain.
Core responsibility is translating regulatory material for Default Management. No experience with mortgage regulations, CFPB, HUD, GSE guidelines, or compliance frameworks.
Must be SME on Default department systems. No experience with loan servicing platforms, MSP, Encompass, or mortgage-specific tools.
Explicit requirement to 'assess change, determine impacts, design strategy based on change adoption model.' No formal change management experience shown.
Role owns processes and audits. No background in audit frameworks, SOX, internal controls, or compliance auditing.
Cannot analyze Default processes without understanding delinquency stages, loss mitigation options, foreclosure timelines, and servicing workflows.
Role requires evaluating, designing, executing, monitoring business processes end-to-end. No evidence of formal BPM methodology (Six Sigma, Lean, BPMN).
Must 'decipher regulatory and compliance material' and translate for management. No experience reading legal documents, agency guidelines, or regulatory filings.

experienceGaps: Zero mortgage industry experience - no context for default management, servicing, or loan lifecycle
No regulatory compliance background - never worked with federal/state financial regulations
No financial services domain knowledge - retail tech sales at Apple is completely different vertical

No experience working with legal teams on compliance implementation

3 years of relevant experience required - candidate has ~2.5 years total work experience, none in target domain
analysis.criticalVerdict: This is a hard mismatch. The candidate has strong data analytics and technical skills but zero mortgage industry experience and no regulatory compliance background. This isn't a Business Analyst role using Python and Tableau—it's a mortgage default operations role requiring deep domain expertise in foreclosure processes, loss mitigation, and regulatory compliance. The candidate would be completely lost trying to 'translate agency regulatory material for Default Management' when they've never seen a servicing guideline, GSE requirement, or state foreclosure law. The technical skills are impressive but irrelevant without the mortgage servicing context.
improvementPlan: Get mortgage industry experience immediately - pursue entry-level roles in loan servicing, loss mitigation, or mortgage operations at a servicer or lender to understand the business
Learn mortgage regulations - study RESPA, TILA, CFPB servicing rules, Fannie/Freddie guidelines, state foreclosure laws, and investor requirements
Obtain compliance certifications - pursue CRCM (Certified Regulatory Compliance Manager) or similar financial services compliance credentials
Gain formal change management training - get certified in PROSCI, ADKAR, or Lean Six Sigma to build process improvement methodology
Target hybrid roles first - look for 'Business Analyst' roles at mortgage companies that value your analytics skills but provide default/servicing exposure
Build audit experience - seek roles involving process audits, controls testing, or compliance monitoring in any financial services context
Network with mortgage professionals - join MBA (Mortgage Bankers Association) and attend default servicing conferences to understand the domain
Take mortgage servicing courses - pursue education through MBA or STESSO to learn default management fundamentals

JIRA or similar process management tools
Requirements traceability and UAT coordination
analysis.summary: Strong technical candidate with impressive data analytics and programming skills, but completely wrong background for this mortgage default compliance role. The 3-5 years of mortgage servicing and regulatory experience required is entirely absent. This role needs someone who can read GSE servicing guides and state foreclosure laws, not someone who builds financial tracking apps. The candidate needs 2-3 years in mortgage operations before being competitive for this specific position.

applications.role: Supporting sales teams with day-to-day deal execution including quote building, contract management, redline review, enforcing compliance guardrails, and optimizing deal desk processes to increase deal velocity in a SaaS environment
applications.jobDescription:
Analyst, Deal Desk
Remote - United States
About AlphaSense: 
The world’s most sophisticated companies rely on AlphaSense to remove uncertainty from decision-making. With market intelligence and search built on proven AI, AlphaSense delivers insights that matter from content you can trust. Our universe of public and private content includes equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, trade journals, and clients’ own research content.

The acquisition of Tegus by AlphaSense in 2024 advances our shared mission to empower professionals to make smarter decisions through AI-driven market intelligence. Together, AlphaSense and Tegus will accelerate growth, innovation, and content expansion, with complementary product and content capabilities that enable users to unearth even more comprehensive insights from thousands of content sets. Our platform is trusted by over 6,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with more than 2,000 employees across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland. Come join us!

Location:  New York/US-Remote

Reports to: Director, Sales Program Management


About the Team:

The Revenue Operations team at AlphaSense drives productivity and improves efficiency for our account executives, account managers and sales development reps. We create and implement operational reporting & analytics, improve processes, and enable strategic initiatives that impact the revenue organization.  At our core, we are a dynamic team of highly driven self-starters who are passionate about operations and thrive in a fast paced, challenging environment.

About the Role:

We are seeking a detail-oriented, strategic, and collaborative Deal Desk Analyst to support our sales teams in day to day execution of key deals. This role will serve as a partner to Account Executives and Account Managers; working with them to build quotes, execute contracts, and review redlines. The ideal candidate has experience working in a deal desk role for a SAAS company.

Who you are:

2+ years of experience in deal desk or contract management in a SaaS or technology environment. 
Understanding of B2B commercial contracts and deal structures.
Experience working with CRM (Salesforce), CPQ tools, and contract management systems (e.g., Ironclad, DocuSign CLM).
Excellent negotiation, analytical, and communication skills.
Detail-oriented with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to pushback in challenging situations.  
What You’ll Do

Deal Structuring & Review
Help maintain team SLAs for timely changes 
Assist sales teams in designing quotes and contracts, including product bundling, and multi-year "ramp" deals.
Serve as the primary gatekeeper for non-standard terms, ensuring all necessary stakeholders (legal, accounting, finance) sign off on exceptions.
Support policy and compliance by enforcing "guardrails" regarding discounting levels, revenue recognition standards, and legal liability.
Work with sales enablement to train sales representatives on how to use Salesforce CPQ and Ironclad. 
Process Optimization: Identify "friction points" in the sales cycle and implement automation or workflow improvements to increase deal velocity.
For base compensation, we set standard ranges for all roles based on function and level benchmarked against similar stage growth companies and internal comparables. In order to be compliant with local legislation, as well as to provide greater transparency to candidates, we share salary ranges on all job postings regardless of desired hiring location. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including candidate experience/expertise and may vary from the amounts listed below.

You may also be offered a performance-based bonus, equity, and a generous benefits program.

Base Compensation Range

$67,000 - $84,000 USD

AlphaSense is an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to a work environment that supports, inspires, and respects all individuals. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling AlphaSense’s commitment to equal employment opportunity. AlphaSense does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of race, color, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, religion, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, military or veteran status, disability, or any other non-merit factor. This policy applies to every aspect of employment at AlphaSense, including recruitment, hiring, training, advancement, and termination.

In addition, it is the policy of AlphaSense to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances where a particular employee works.

Recruiting Scams and Fraud

We at AlphaSense have been made aware of fraudulent job postings and individuals impersonating AlphaSense recruiters. These scams may involve fake job offers, requests for sensitive personal information, or demands for payment. Please note:

AlphaSense never asks candidates to pay for job applications, equipment, or training.
All official communications will come from an @alpha-sense.com email address.
If you’re unsure about a job posting or recruiter, verify it on our Careers page.
If you believe you’ve been targeted by a scam or have any doubts regarding the authenticity of any job listing purportedly from or on behalf of AlphaSense please contact us. Your security and trust matter to us.

No evidence of reviewing, negotiating, or managing B2B SaaS contracts. This is a primary responsibility including reviewing redlines and non-standard terms.
Role requires hands-on experience with Salesforce CPQ or similar tools to build quotes and manage complex deal structures. Candidate has no CPQ experience.
Daily tool usage required for contract execution and workflow. No evidence of experience with these specific systems.
Understanding of multi-year ramps, product bundling, subscription pricing models, and revenue recognition standards is fundamental. Candidate has no SaaS sales operations background.
Must understand B2B commercial terms, discount policies, legal liability clauses, and work with legal/finance teams on exceptions. No evidence of this expertise.
Deal Desk must enforce compliance with revenue recognition policies (ASC 606). No finance or accounting operations experience evident.
Role requires training sales reps on CPQ and contract tools. While candidate has client-facing skills, no experience training internal sales teams.
Must negotiate terms with both customers and internal stakeholders. Apple retail experience doesn't translate to complex B2B SaaS deal negotiation.

experienceGaps: Zero experience in SaaS or technology sales operations environment
No exposure to subscription-based business models or recurring revenue structures
Missing Revenue Operations or Sales Operations experience entirely
No evidence of working cross-functionally with Legal, Finance, or Accounting teams on deal approvals
Lack of understanding of B2B enterprise sales cycles and deal velocity optimization
No experience with SLA management or operational metrics in a sales support function
Missing exposure to policy enforcement, discount approval workflows, or pricing governance
No background in contract lifecycle management from quote to signature to renewal
Apple B2B experience was focused on hardware/device sales, not software licensing or SaaS agreements
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is fundamentally mismatched for this Deal Desk role. They're a technical person with data science/analytics skills and some retail B2B experience, but lack every critical competency this role requires: deal desk experience, contract management, CPQ tools, SaaS deal structuring, and revenue operations background. The 2+ years deal desk requirement isn't met at all—they have zero relevant experience. This would be setting both the candidate and the company up for failure.
improvementPlan: Gain actual deal desk experience by targeting entry-level Sales Operations or Revenue Operations Coordinator roles at SaaS companies (accept that this role is too senior)
Take courses on SaaS business models, subscription pricing, and revenue recognition (ASC 606 fundamentals)
Get hands-on with Salesforce CPQ through Trailhead certifications and build a demo org to practice quote configuration
Learn contract management basics: NDA review, MSA structures, SOW creation, and legal risk assessment
Shadow or intern with a Deal Desk team to understand the quote-to-cash process in a real SaaS environment
Study common SaaS contract terms: annual vs multi-year deals, ramp deals, co-termination, true-ups, renewal structures
Pursue roles like Sales Operations Analyst, Salesforce Administrator, or Pricing Analyst to build adjacent skills
Network with Revenue Operations professionals to understand the career path and skill requirements
marketSkillsToLearn: Salesforce CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote)
Contract management platforms (Ironclad, DocuSign CLM, Conga)
SaaS metrics and revenue operations fundamentals (ARR, ACV, bookings, revenue recognition)
Quote-to-cash process expertise
Deal structuring and pricing strategy for B2B SaaS
Revenue recognition standards (ASC 606/IFRS 15)
Approval workflow design and policy enforcement
Cross-functional stakeholder management (Legal, Finance, Sales)
Sales enablement and internal training delivery
Advanced Salesforce administration and reporting
Discount and pricing governance
Contract redline review and negotiation tactics
analysis.summary: This candidate has strong technical and analytical capabilities but is completely unqualified for this specific Deal Desk role. They lack the foundational 2+ years of deal desk/contract management experience in SaaS that is explicitly required, and have no exposure to CPQ tools, contract management systems, or revenue operations processes. The Apple B2B experience involves device sales, not software licensing or complex SaaS deal structuring. This is a 22/100 match—they would need 2-3 years of direct experience in SaaS sales operations before being competitive for this position.

qaHistory.q: Do you have experience working with any of the following?: Salesforce, Ironclad, Juno, Dealhub, Conga, Zuora, Docusign. If so, which ones? *
qaHistory.a:
I have experience with Salesforce and DocuSign, though I want to be transparent that my exposure has been more on the CRM and analytics side rather than specifically in a deal desk context.

During my time as a Business Specialist at Apple, I used Salesforce daily to track B2B client interactions, manage account information, and monitor our enterprise sales pipeline. I got comfortable navigating the system to pull client histories, update opportunity stages, and coordinate with our business team on larger accounts. While I wasn't building quotes in Salesforce CPQ specifically, I did work extensively with Apple's internal systems to structure business financing options and custom device configurations for enterprise clients, which gave me a good foundation in how deal data flows through a CRM.

I've also worked with DocuSign in a basic capacity for contract execution and document workflows, though I haven't used it as part of a formal CLM system. What I don't have yet is hands-on experience with the specialized deal desk tools like Ironclad, Juno, DealHub, Conga, or Zuora. I know these are critical for this role, and I'm genuinely excited to learn them—I tend to pick up new platforms quickly, and my technical background with data systems and APIs gives me confidence I can get up to speed fast. In my Flashconverter project, I taught myself how to integrate multiple financial APIs and build workflows from scratch, so learning enterprise software is something I'm comfortable tackling head-on.

Join a SaaS startup in a Sales Operations or Business Operations role where you can wear multiple hats and gain exposure to deal processes, even if not formally Deal Desk
Take a contract management fundamentals course to understand commercial terms, redlines, MSAs, Order Forms, and SOWs
Network with Revenue Operations professionals and request informational interviews to understand the career path from Sales Ops Coordinator → Deal Desk Analyst → Senior Deal Desk
Consider roles like Sales Operations Analyst, Salesforce Administrator, or Quote-to-Cash Analyst that provide adjacent experience and pathways into Deal Desk
Gain understanding of SaaS business models, ARR/MRR calculations, and subscription economics through online courses or entry-level roles in SaaS companies

applications.role: Provide dedicated data and analytical support to the casino vertical by tracking performance metrics, building BI dashboards, analyzing player behavior, supporting A/B testing initiatives, and delivering actionable insights to optimize player engagement, content performance, and casino product experiences
applications.jobDescription:
Rush Street Interactive (NYSE: RSI) is a market leader in online casino and sports betting, currently operating real-money gaming with our brands: BetRivers.com, PlaySugarHouse.com, and RushBet.co. We’re building bridges between online, social and land-based gaming businesses to create amazing, integrated experiences that keep players in the game.

The Casino Data Analyst provides dedicated data and analytical support to the casino vertical. This includes tracking performance metrics and delivering actionable insights to optimize player engagement and content performance. This role works closely with Product, Engineering, and Operations to monitor casino-specific user behavior, and support A/B testing initiatives. This role helps improve company operational processes, identify growth opportunities, and make sense of customer behavior. 

What You'll Do:

Track and analyze KPIs related to casino games, player engagement, and content performance.
Build and maintain BI dashboarding for internal stakeholders.
Scope, plan, and execute business intelligence projects supporting the casino vertical.
Deliver actionable insights to support casino product optimization and strategic planning.
Monitor player behavior unique to the casino vertical to identify trends, risks, and opportunities.
Support product teams with A/B test design, execution, and analysis.
Evaluate the adoption of new features and content to inform product roadmap decisions.
Partner with Product and Engineering teams to optimize game experiences based on player data.
What You'll Bring:

Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field preferred or equivalent relevant experience.
2+ in analytics, business intelligence, or data science roles, ideally in gaming, tech, or consumer platforms.
Proficiency in SQL and data platforms like Snowflake.
Experience building dashboards using Tableau or similar BI tools.
Strong problem-solving skills and strategic mindset; Intellectually curious.
Ability to translate data insights into compelling narratives for business and product stakeholders.
Effective in cross-functional environments with Product, Engineering, and Casino Operations.
Experience with or understanding of statistics, probabilities, games, and/or casino products, preferred.
Familiarity with experimental design and interpreting test results, preferred.
Able to travel occasionally both domestically and internationally, up to 5% of the time. #LI-REMOTE
This role spans a wide breadth of experience at Rush Street Interactive, welcoming candidates with diverse experiences, backgrounds, and locations. The pay range reflects potential variations in experience, skills, and location. The pay range does not reflect total compensation, which includes base pay, benefits, and/or bonuses. For candidates outside the US (Canada, Europe, Latin America), we take a locally informed approach to compensation and will share specific ranges based on your country of residence.
Location Eligibility (for nationwide roles): Open to candidates across the U.S.

Location Eligibility (for other roles): Location requirements may vary and will be specified in the job posting.

Benefits:

Discretionary annual company bonus (Eligibility Varies by Role) 
401(k) plan with 100% company match on the first 4%
Comprehensive medical coverage (HSA & FSA plans), dental, and vision insurance
Paid Time Off (Eligibility Varies by Role)
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Pay Range

$75,000 - $95,000 USD

What Makes Us Great:
Comprehensive compensation
Work-life balance initiatives
Autonomy – we embrace personal freedom and responsibility
Creativity – we are open to new ideas of how we can be better
Growth – we want you to develop personally as well as professionally
Top-notch professionals who are passionate about what they do
People-oriented environment and supportive atmosphere
As a rapidly growing company in an emerging industry, you’ll have a huge impact on our product and our company. We like proactive team members and strive to have a company of self-disciplined professionals who enjoy collaboration, having fun, and of course, achieving together what others believe to be improbable. We are dedicated to treating everyone with respect and to support your professional and personal growth.

Rush Street Interactive is an equal opportunity employer and committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to race, national origin, ancestry, sex, sexual orientation, sexual identity and expression, marital status, family status, lifestyle, age, culture, religion, military and veteran status, citizenship, or disability.

Be at the forefront of this ground-breaking industry! Apply now!

missingSkills.reason: Explicitly required data platform for the role. The company uses Snowflake as their data warehouse, and this is non-negotiable for day-one productivity.
Core responsibility of the role. No demonstrated experience designing, running, or analyzing A/B tests in a product environment. This is a daily task.
This is a casino-specific vertical role. Understanding player behavior, casino game mechanics, RTP, volatility, and gaming KPIs is essential. Zero evidence of gaming domain knowledge.
While candidate has Tableau/Power BI skills, there's no evidence of building and maintaining production dashboards for business stakeholders in a corporate environment.
No experience working embedded with Product and Engineering teams to optimize digital product experiences based on user behavior data.
No demonstrated experience defining, tracking, or owning business KPIs in a professional analytics capacity. Academic projects don't translate to business metric ownership.
The role requires daily partnership with technical teams. Candidate's experience is primarily retail B2B and solo projects, not tech product development.

experienceGaps: Zero professional data analyst experience - Most recent role is AI training/evaluation (contractor work) and prior is retail sales, not analytics
No experience in gaming, gambling, casino, or highly regulated consumer digital products - This is a specialized vertical requiring domain expertise
No track record of supporting product teams with data-driven decisions - Academic projects and personal builds don't demonstrate stakeholder management or business impact
Missing 2+ years professional analytics experience requirement - The Handshake AI Fellow role (Dec 2025-Present, ~3 months) is not analytics and Apple role was enterprise sales, not data analytics
No evidence of experimental design or statistical testing in production environments - A/B testing is a core function, but candidate only shows academic modeling projects
No experience with real-time player behavior monitoring or engagement analytics - Casino analytics requires understanding churn, LTV, session duration, game performance metrics

Lacks experience translating technical insights into business narratives for non-technical stakeholders in a corporate setting - Client-facing retail experience is different from internal product stakeholder management
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has strong foundational technical skills and obvious aptitude for analytics, but they fundamentally lack the professional experience this role requires. They don't have 2+ years in analytics roles - their background is retail sales and very recent AI training work. More critically, they have zero gaming industry exposure and no demonstrated experience with core job functions like A/B testing, product analytics, or KPI ownership in a corporate environment. This is not a junior role where someone learns analytics on the job - it requires immediate contribution to a specialized vertical.
improvementPlan: Get actual analytics experience first - Apply for junior/entry-level Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Analyst roles at tech companies, even if not gaming. You need 1-2 years of professional experience before targeting this mid-level specialized role
Learn Snowflake immediately - Complete Snowflake certification and hands-on practice. This is the #1 technical blocker since it's explicitly required

Build A/B testing portfolio - Take courses on experimental design (Udacity A/B Testing course, or similar), then create 2-3 detailed case studies analyzing real A/B test scenarios with statistical rigor (sample size calculations, significance testing, confidence intervals)

Develop gaming industry knowledge - Study casino game mechanics, player behavior metrics (RTP, volatility, house edge), and iGaming KPIs. Follow industry publications, take iGaming courses, or consider entry-level roles at gaming companies

Create production-grade dashboards - Build 2-3 fully developed Tableau dashboards that simulate business scenarios (not academic). Focus on stakeholder-ready visualizations with filtering, drill-downs, and clear business narratives
Transition your current AI role toward analytics - If possible, pivot your Handshake work to include data analysis projects. Document any quantitative evaluation work as analytics experience

Target gaming-adjacent roles - Look for analytics positions at entertainment, consumer apps, or fantasy sports companies to build relevant digital product experience before jumping to casino-specific verticals

Advanced SQL for analytics (window functions, CTEs, query optimization)
Python for data analysis (pandas proficiency beyond basics)
A/B testing & experimental design (power analysis, significance testing)
Product analytics frameworks (funnels, cohorts, retention analysis)
Google Analytics or Amplitude (product analytics platforms)
Statistical inference & hypothesis testing
Data modeling & dimensional modeling concepts
dbt (data build tool) for analytics engineering
Advanced Tableau (calculated fields, parameters, dashboard performance)
KPI development & business metric frameworks
User behavior analysis & segmentation
Looker or Mode Analytics
Git for analytics code collaboration
Stakeholder communication & data storytelling in corporate settings
analysis.summary: Candidate has solid technical foundations (SQL, Python, R, BI tools) and clear analytical aptitude, but lacks the professional experience this role demands. With primarily retail sales background and only 3 months in an AI training role, they don't meet the 2+ years analytics experience requirement. Critical gaps include zero gaming industry exposure, no Snowflake experience, no professional A/B testing work, and missing product analytics experience. This is a specialized mid-level role requiring immediate impact - candidate needs 1-2 years of foundational analytics experience before being competitive.

applications.role: Supporting AI research and machine learning infrastructure by creating and reviewing procurement and buying-related deliverables to train and enhance AI systems, working as an independent contractor on a flexible, remote basis.
applications.jobDescription:
About the Role
Mercor is seeking experienced Buying and Purchasing Agents to support a leading AI lab in advancing research and infrastructure for next-generation machine learning systems. This engagement focuses on diagnosing and solving real issues in your domain. It's an opportunity to contribute your expertise to cutting-edge AI research while working independently and remotely on your own schedule.

Key Responsibilities
You’ll be asked to create deliverables regarding common requests within your professional domain 

You’ll be asked to review peer developed deliverables to improve AI research

Ideal Qualifications
4+ years professional experience in your respective field

Excellent written communication with strong grammar and spelling skills

More About the Opportunity
Expected workload: ~30 hours per week, with flexibility to scale up to 40 hours

Project start date: immediately, lasting for around 3-4 weeks

We consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics and provide reasonable accommodations upon request.

Contract and Payment Terms
You will be engaged as an independent contractor.
This is a fully remote role that can be completed on your own schedule.
Projects can be extended, shortened, or concluded early depending on needs and performance.
Your work at Mercor will not involve access to confidential or proprietary information from any employer, client, or institution.
Payments are weekly on Stripe or Wise based on services rendered.
Please note: We are unable to support H1-B or STEM OPT candidates at this time.
About Mercor
Mercor partners with leading AI labs and enterprises to train frontier models using human expertise. You will work on projects that focus on training and enhancing AI systems. You will be paid competitively, collaborate with leading researchers, and help shape the next generation of AI systems in your area of expertise.

applications.role: The role focuses on business analysis activities including requirements gathering, documentation, and stakeholder collaboration. Primary responsibilities involve working with business users and IT teams to elicit and document functional and non-functional requirements, create user stories in JIRA, manage project documentation, and work with Salesforce reporting and workflows, primarily within a financial services or regulated environment.
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
W2 Only(No C2C/1099)



Job Title- Junior IT Business Analyst

Work Location: TYSONS, Virginia - Local

Visa: USC, Green Card Only

Mode of Interview: In-Person Interview

Compensation: $40-45/hr W2 --- looking for junior - with min 3 years of experience



Strong Communication skills to work with key stakeholders

 Strong Project Documentation experience/ ability to define future requirements 

 Ability to collaborate with business users and IT teams of diverse backgrounds to deliver solutions that meet business requirements while aligning with strategic direction.



Qualifications

Equivalent combination of education and experience is considered.
• Bachelor's degree in business, Computer Science or a related field is required.

 • Minimum of three (3) years of experience performing business analysis, working with large amounts of complex data, and developing workflows.

 • Experience with Microsoft Teams and SharePoint.

 • Experience working for a financial organization or other highly regulated sector.

 • Agile/Scrum background preferred.

• Experience with JIRA, Confluence, any project management tool: MS Project, Smartsheet, etc.

 • Experience with eliciting and documenting both functional and non-functional technical requirements in financial services setting is preferred.

 • Experience gathering technical and business requirements and documenting User Stories in Jira.

 • Proficient in Salesforce functions to include reporting, dashboards, and workflows.



Qualifications

• Excellent verbal and written communication skills - comfortable and self-assured communicating to all levels of the organization, both business and IT.
• Effective time management, problem solving, and multi-tasking skills.
• Effective presentation skills.
• Ability to manage priorities effectively and move between projects as needed.
Supervisory Responsibility

This position will not supervise employees.
Licenses and Certifications
No additional licenses or certification required.
Work Environment
While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly exposed to an indoor officesetting with moderate noise.
*Most roles require working in an office setting with moderate noise and the ability to lift 25pounds.


Best Regards,

Jaideep Shastri

Sr. Technical Recruiter || FUSTIS LLC 

916-365-9533 (D) | jaideep.shastri@fustis.com

applications.role: Data-driven business analysis focused on customer acquisition, retention, and engagement through analytics, marketing campaign management, and operational support in a fast-growing gig economy startup
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Company Overview:

Empower is a fast-growing, profitable startup that is on a mission to transform the gig economy, so that hard-working drivers can earn a sustainable living. We are seeking a highly motivated, responsible, and detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our team. If you want to be an integral member of a team that is disrupting Uber and cares about making a difference for gig economy workers, then we’d like to hear from you. 



Role Overview:

The Business Analyst will report to, and work closely with, our central operations team lead. In this role you will need to identify and implement data-driven scalable strategies to grow our business. Successful team members have an “extreme ownership” mentality, understand the importance of data and attention to detail, and are high-energy solution oriented problem solvers. We’re looking for team players who are looking to make an immediate impact at a high-growth disruptive startup. This role can be remote (U.S. based only), or in-person in McLean, Virginia.



Opportunities:

Develop and use analytical tools to measure and generate ways to improve customer acquisition/retention/engagement and grow overall platform usage
Hands-on assistance managing dynamic and growing marketplaces using custom dashboards
Serve in both project management and operator roles on various short-term and long-term ops, comms, and marketing initiatives
Help manage and improve our automated drip campaigns. This includes ensuring timely and consistent deployment, as well as conducting in-depth analyses of open rates and campaign effectiveness
Draft copy for and implement email, text, and other digital communication and marketing campaigns
Run and expand Empower’s vehicle-exterior advertising program
Run and expand Empower’s in-vehicle marketing efforts
Help create content for Empower’s unpaid, organic social media campaigns
Help manage paid media campaigns to maximize ROI


Candidate Requirements:

Bachelor’s or other 4-year degree with high academic achievement
0-2 years of post college professional experience, ideally in a fast paced environment
Experience in data analytics (proficiency with Excel/Google Sheets required; experience with SQL/R/Python is a plus). 
An innate understanding of ROI
Ambitious and dependable


What Do We Offer?

Competitive compensation: $60,000 - $80,000 base salary (based on experience) + equity + performance based bonus + competitive benefits
Being part of a great team to better the lives of gig workers 
Exposure to C-Suite and other senior team members across all departments
No dull or unchallenging days!

missingSkills.reason: Role explicitly requires drafting copy for email, text, and digital campaigns. No evidence of marketing content creation experience in candidate's background.

missingSkills.skill: Email/drip campaign management (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Braze, etc.)
missingSkills.reason: Key responsibility is managing automated drip campaigns, analyzing open rates, and ensuring deployment. Candidate has zero marketing automation platform experience.

missingSkills.skill: Paid media campaign management (Facebook Ads, Google Ads)
missingSkills.reason: Role requires managing paid media campaigns to maximize ROI. Candidate's technical SEO and e-commerce experience doesn't translate to performance marketing.

missingSkills.skill: Social media content creation and management
missingSkills.reason: Responsible for creating content for organic social media campaigns. No demonstrated experience in social media marketing or content strategy.
Core responsibility is measuring and improving customer acquisition/retention. Candidate has analytics skills but no experience in growth marketing metrics.
Role serves in both PM and operator roles. Candidate has technical project experience but not in marketing operations or communications initiatives.
Role involves managing dynamic marketplaces. Candidate lacks two-sided marketplace or gig economy platform experience.

experienceGaps: Zero marketing or growth operations experience - all background is technical/analytics or B2B sales
No demonstrated copywriting, content creation, or communications experience
No hands-on experience with marketing automation tools, CRM platforms (beyond Salesforce mention), or campaign management
No experience in customer lifecycle management, retention strategies, or growth marketing
Never worked in a startup environment - Apple retail is vastly different from fast-paced startup operations
No experience managing advertising programs (vehicle-exterior, in-vehicle marketing mentioned in JD)
Technical skills are overqualified (ML, NLP, cloud architecture) while marketing operations skills are completely absent
E-commerce founder experience shows entrepreneurial mindset but in a different vertical (vaping products vs gig economy) and doesn't demonstrate the marketing execution skills needed
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is technically overqualified but operationally underqualified for this specific role. They have strong data analytics fundamentals and technical chops that exceed requirements, but they're missing 60% of what this job actually entails: marketing operations, campaign management, copywriting, and growth execution. This isn't a pure analytics role - it's a hybrid growth operator position that requires hands-on marketing skills the candidate simply doesn't have. The Apple B2B experience shows client-facing ability but doesn't translate to digital marketing campaign execution.
improvementPlan: Immediately gain hands-on experience with email marketing platforms - take HubSpot Academy certifications, set up and run actual drip campaigns (even for personal projects)
Build a portfolio of marketing copywriting - write 20-30 sample email campaigns, SMS campaigns, and social media posts to demonstrate ability to craft compelling marketing content
Take Google Ads and Facebook Blueprint certifications, then run $500-1000 in actual paid campaigns to understand performance marketing and ROI optimization
Volunteer for a startup or small business to manage their marketing operations for 3-6 months - get real experience with campaign deployment, A/B testing, and growth metrics
Learn marketing automation tools (Mailchimp, Braze, Customer.io) through hands-on projects - set up automated workflows and analyze campaign performance
Study growth marketing fundamentals - read 'Traction' by Gabriel Weinberg, take Reforge courses, understand acquisition funnels, retention cohorts, and growth levers
Reframe resume to emphasize any content creation, communication, or customer engagement work from Apple role rather than leading with technical ML/cloud projects that aren't relevant here
marketSkillsToLearn: Marketing automation platforms (HubSpot, Mailchimp, Braze, Iterable)
Performance marketing (Google Ads, Facebook Ads Manager, campaign optimization)
Copywriting for conversion (email, SMS, push notifications)
A/B testing and experimentation frameworks
Customer lifecycle analytics (cohort analysis, retention curves, LTV)
Growth marketing frameworks (acquisition funnels, viral loops, referral programs)
CRM management and segmentation strategies
Social media content strategy and organic growth tactics
Marketing analytics (Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Amplitude)
Basic graphic design for marketing (Canva, Figma for marketing assets)
analysis.summary: Candidate has strong technical analytics capabilities that exceed job requirements but is missing critical marketing operations skills that comprise the majority of this role. The 0-2 years experience range fits, but their experience is in the wrong domains - they need practical marketing execution skills, not more data science. With 6-12 months focused on building marketing operations experience, they could become competitive, but right now they're a 58/100 match at best.

Develop and use analytical tools to measure and generate ways to improve customer acquisition/retention/engagement and grow overall platform usage
Hands-on assistance managing dynamic and growing marketplaces using custom dashboards
Serve in both project management and operator roles on various short-term and long-term ops, comms, and marketing initiatives
Help manage and improve our automated drip campaigns. This includes ensuring timely and consistent deployment, as well as conducting in-depth analyses of open rates and campaign effectiveness
Draft copy for and implement email, text, and other digital communication and marketing campaigns
Run and expand Empower’s vehicle-exterior advertising program
Run and expand Empower’s in-vehicle marketing efforts
Help create content for Empower’s unpaid, organic social media campaigns
Help manage paid media campaigns to maximize ROI


Candidate Requirements:

Bachelor’s or other 4-year degree with high academic achievement
0-2 years of post college professional experience, ideally in a fast paced environment
Experience in data analytics (proficiency with Excel/Google Sheets required; experience with SQL/R/Python is a plus). 
An innate understanding of ROI
Ambitious and dependable


What Do We Offer?

Competitive compensation: $60,000 - $80,000 base salary (based on experience) + equity + performance based bonus + competitive benefits
Being part of a great team to better the lives of gig workers 
Exposure to C-Suite and other senior team members across all departments
No dull or unchallenging days!

applications.role: Applying data science and advanced analytics to solve complex business problems across industries, owning the full analytics value chain from problem framing through algorithm design to deployment of scalable AI solutions, while working in client-facing consulting engagements
applications.jobDescription:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


We Are BCG X

 

We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions.


What You'll Do

Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions.
 
Our Data Scientists are part of our rapidly growing team to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions.
 
Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, creative, and communicative.


What You'll Bring

We are looking for dedicated individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and redefining organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following:
Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams
Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations
Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner
Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience)
Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data
Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients
Have strong project management skills
Please note: any degree programs (including part-time) must be completed before starting at BCG.

 

TECHNOLOGIES:
Programming Languages: Python


Additional info

You must live within a reasonable commuting distance of your home office. As a member of that office, it is expected you will be in the office as directed. This role puts you on an accelerated path of personal and professional growth and development and so, at times, requires extended working hours. Our work often requires travel to client sites.

 
FOR U.S. APPLICANTS: BCG is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and is committed to a policy of administering all employment decisions and actions without regard to race, national origin, religion, age, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, or protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by local, state, or federal laws, rules, or regulations.
 
The first-year base compensation for this role is:
Data Scientist I: $110,000 USD 
Data Scientist II: $145,000 USD
Data Scientist III: $160,000 USD
Senior Data Scientist: $190,000 USD
 
At BCG, we are committed to offering a comprehensive benefit program that includes everything our employees and their families need to be well and live life to the fullest. We pay the full cost of medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees – and their eligible family members. * That’s zero dollars in premiums taken from employee paychecks. All our plans provide best in class coverage: 

Zero-dollar ($0) health insurance premiums for BCG employees, spouses, and children 

Low $10 (USD) copays for trips to the doctor, urgent care visits and prescriptions for generic drugs 

Dental coverage, including up to $5,000 in orthodontia benefits 

Vision insurance with coverage for both glasses and contact lenses annually 

Reimbursement for gym memberships and other fitness activities 

Fully vested Profit-Sharing Retirement Fund contributions made annually, whether you contribute or not, plus the option for employees to make personal contributions to a 401(k) plan 

Paid Parental Leave and other family benefits such as elective egg freezing, surrogacy, and adoption reimbursement 

Generous paid time off including 12 holidays per year, an annual office closure between Christmas and New Years, and 15 vacation days per year (earned at 1.25 days per month) 

Paid sick time on an as needed basis 

 

*Employees, spouses, and children are covered at no cost. Employees share in the cost of domestic partner coverage.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is a recent graduate with strong academic fundamentals and impressive retail sales experience, but lacks the core professional data science experience BCG X demands. The 3 years at Apple demonstrates excellent client communication skills, but it's B2C retail, not enterprise consulting. The AI Fellow role is too recent and junior (evaluation work, not model development or deployment). For a firm that requires candidates who 'own the full analytics value-chain end to end' and have 'significant experience applying advanced analytics,' this candidate needs 2-3 years in a dedicated data science role before being competitive for BCG X.
improvementPlan: Immediately pursue Data Scientist I role at a tech company, startup, or analytics consultancy (not BCG tier yet) to gain 2-3 years of hands-on production ML experience
Build and deploy 2-3 end-to-end ML systems with real users - focus on MLOps: Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, model monitoring, A/B testing frameworks
Master deep learning: Complete fast.ai courses, build PyTorch/TensorFlow projects with CNNs, RNNs, Transformers - deploy at least one deep learning model to production
Develop operations research skills: Take courses in optimization (linear programming, mixed integer programming), simulation, and advanced statistical inference (causal inference, Bayesian methods)
Gain consulting exposure: Join a boutique analytics consultancy or take client-facing data science role where you present to executives and translate technical work to business impact
Build thought leadership: Publish technical blog posts on Medium/Towards Data Science, present at local data science meetups, contribute to open-source ML projects
Learn distributed computing: Master Apache Spark, Dask, or Ray for big data processing - work with datasets >1TB
Target intermediate roles: Apply to Data Scientist II positions at firms like Capital One, Deloitte Analytics, or mid-tier consultancies to build consulting + DS hybrid experience
Quantify business impact: In current AI Fellow role, measure and document how your work improves model accuracy, reduces training time, or impacts business KPIs - learn to speak in ROI terms
Network strategically: Attend BCG X recruiting events, connect with BCG data scientists on LinkedIn, understand their project portfolios to see the experience gap firsthand
