SQL optimization for large-scale databases
Graph analytics and network analysis
Consulting frameworks (MECE, hypothesis-driven problem solving)
Business case development and ROI quantification
Executive communication and data storytelling
Domain expertise in strategic areas (healthcare analytics, climate tech, financial services)
analysis.summary: Candidate shows strong foundational skills, excellent communication abilities from retail experience, and promising academic projects, but falls significantly short of BCG X's requirements for professional data science consulting experience. The lack of production ML deployment, consulting delivery, team leadership, and advanced technical depth (deep learning, operations research, big data) makes this a weak match. With 2-3 years in a dedicated data science role building real-world systems and business impact, this candidate could become competitive for BCG X's entry-level positions.

applications.role: Managing and facilitating federal change control processes for Oracle Health Government Services clients, coordinating between internal teams, external vendors, and federal clients to ensure quality software delivery while maintaining compliance with change management procedures and documentation requirements
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Job Description

As a member of the Oracle Health Government Services change control team, you will work with federal internal and external teams who submit change requests for Federal clients. You are responsible for ensuring that a quality, integrated software solution is delivered in a timely manner, at budget, and to our client's satisfaction. This involves working closely with the client to understand and manage their expectations, as well as working closely with Oracle and third-party vendors to ensure delivery.

Career Level - IC2

Responsibilities

As a member of the Oracle Health Government Services change control team, you responsible for the following.

 Facilitate and drive all Federal change request 
 Provide clear guidance to requestors on documentation and requirements 
 Coordinate, organize, and transcribe submitted change requests into Federal Change control ITSM tool: SDLC 
 Guide client on change review before and during change control board meetings 
 Coordinate Vendor Partner Change Control Board (VPCCB) voting 
 Assist in Change control continual process improvement 
 Organize and tracking documentation as change requests move through the approval process 
 Assist with weekly communication on change requests to varying levels of leadership 
 Schedule and attend change control meetings as needed 
 Able to work well solo and in a team member setting 
 Prioritize work appropriately 
 Create and manage project plans for integrated master schedules (including budget, structure, schedule, and staffing needs). 
 Monitor and report progress/changes against the plan. 
 Drive implementation by providing vision and direction. 


Qualifications

Disclaimer:

Certain US customer or client-facing roles may be required to comply with applicable requirements, such as immunization and occupational health mandates.

Range and benefit information provided in this posting are specific to the stated locations only

US: Hiring Range in USD from $27.07 - $54.13 per hour; from: $56,300 - $112,600 per year. May be eligible for bonus and equity.

Oracle maintains broad salary ranges for its roles in order to account for variations in knowledge, skills, experience, market conditions and locations, as well as reflect Oracle's differing products, industries and lines of business.

Candidates are typically placed into the range based on the preceding factors as well as internal peer equity.

Oracle US offers a comprehensive benefits package which includes the following:

 Medical, dental, and vision insurance, including expert medical opinion
 Short term disability and long term disability
 Life insurance and AD&D
 Supplemental life insurance (Employee/Spouse/Child)
 Health care and dependent care Flexible Spending Accounts
 Pre-tax commuter and parking benefits
 401(k) Savings and Investment Plan with company match
 Paid time off: Flexible Vacation is provided to all eligible employees assigned to a salaried (non-overtime eligible) position. Accrued Vacation is provided to all other employees eligible for vacation benefits. For employees working at least 35 hours per week, the vacation accrual rate is 13 days annually for the first three years of employment and 18 days annually for subsequent years of employment. Vacation accrual is prorated for employees working between 20 and 34 hours per week. Employees working fewer than 20 hours per week are not eligible for vacation.
 11 paid holidays
 Paid sick leave: 72 hours of paid sick leave upon date of hire. Refreshes each calendar year. Unused balance will carry over each year up to a maximum cap of 112 hours.
 Paid parental leave
 Adoption assistance
 Employee Stock Purchase Plan
 Financial planning and group legal
 Voluntary benefits including auto, homeowner and pet insurance


The role will generally accept applications for at least three calendar days from the posting date or as long as the job remains posted.

Career Level - IC2

About Us

Only Oracle brings together the data, infrastructure, applications, and expertise to power everything from industry innovations to life-saving care. And with AI embedded across our products and services, we help customers turn that promise into a better future for all. Discover your potential at a company leading the way in AI and cloud solutions that impact billions of lives.

True innovation starts when everyone is empowered to contribute. That’s why we’re committed to growing a workforce that promotes opportunities for all with competitive benefits that support our people with flexible medical, life insurance, and retirement options. We also encourage employees to give back to their communities through our volunteer programs.

We’re committed to including people with disabilities at all stages of the employment process. If you require accessibility assistance or accommodation for a disability at any point, let us know by emailing accommodation-request_mb@oracle.com or by calling 1-888-404-2494 in the United States.

Oracle is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability and protected veterans’ status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Oracle will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records pursuant to applicable law.

applications.role: Supporting FINRA registration and licensing processes for Commercial Broker Dealers, including processing regulatory forms, managing compliance documentation, facilitating associate onboarding, and ensuring adherence to FINRA regulatory requirements
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
The Associate Risk Specialists supporting Capital One’s Commercial Broker Dealers are highly motivated Risk Management professionals with excellent communication skills, strong judgment and decision-making skills with integrity and ownership. They have a high level of exposure across multiple lines of business as well as the opportunity to create and implement innovative solutions to identify and mitigate potential risks to the Company using various compliance tools and methods. The Associate Risk Specialist’s primary responsibilities will be to provide support to the FINRA registration and licensing process for associates and the Commercial Broker Dealers.

Key Responsibilities:

Facilitate onboarding processes for registered and non-registered fingerprinted associates
Utilize FINRA’s Gateway / CRD System to:
Process FINRA Form U4s, U4 Amendments, and Form U5s
Process State licensure requirements
Process Form BRs for OSJ and Branch Office locations and Form BD. 
Electronic recordkeeping and maintenance in accordance with FINRA regulatory requirements
Day-to-day monitoring of team mailbox with the ability to communicate outside of function and tailor messages to audience as appropriate

Basic Qualifications:

High School Diploma, GED or equivalent certification
At least 2 years of experience within a broker dealer registration and licensing function

Preferred Qualifications

3+ years of experience supporting, partnering, and interacting with internal business clients
3+ years of broker dealer compliance experience. 
Bachelor’s degree or military experience
Strong analytical writing and communication skills
Proficiency in Microsoft 365, Google Workspace and aptitude to learn various compliance systems
Excel when working both independently and as part of a team
Be flexible and capable with the scope of work (small, medium, large efforts)
Excellent organizational and time management skills
Ability to handle and deliver multiple priorities
FINRA Series 99 or Series 7 license

applications.role: This role focuses on providing administrative and operational support to the PMO, managing end-to-end personnel onboarding/offboarding processes, maintaining program documentation, tracking KPIs and deliverables, managing facility access and equipment inventory, and supporting multiple federal government programs with seamless execution and administrative excellence.
applications.jobDescription:
Business Analyst
Reston, VA
 
At Accenture Federal Services, nothing matters more than helping the US federal government make the nation stronger and safer and life better for people. Our 13,000+ people are united in a shared purpose to pursue the limitless potential of technology and ingenuity for clients across defense, national security, public safety, civilian, and military health organizations. 
 
Join Accenture Federal Services, a technology company within global Accenture. Recognized as a Glassdoor Top 100 Best Place to Work, we offer a collaborative and caring community where you feel like you belong and are empowered to grow, learn and thrive through hands-on experience, certifications, industry training and more. 
 
Join us to drive positive, lasting change that moves missions and the government forward!
 
We are seeing a detail-oriented, Business Analyst to manage end-to-end onboarding processes, support program management initiatives, handle personnel-related tasks, and execute administrative responsibilities efficiently.

applications.role: Supporting senior analysts in producing equity research on companies with exposure to digital assets, blockchain, and cryptocurrencies, including financial modeling, valuation analysis, and client-facing deliverables
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Title: Equity Research Associate - FinTech Coverage

Role Overview: We are currently partnered with a leading financial institution that is looking to add an Equity Research Associate to its Digital Assets and Blockchain team in Washington D.C. Due to strong client demand and rapid developments in the sector, the firm is seeking talent that can support senior analysts in producing high-quality research, manage data-driven projects, mentor junior analysts, and work directly with senior team members to deliver actionable investment insights.
Key Responsibilities:

Support senior analysts in the publication of equity research reports, thematic pieces, and industry primers.

Conduct financial modeling, valuation analysis, and sector research on companies with exposure to cryptocurrencies, blockchain, and digital finance.

Prepare client presentations, marketing materials, and investment recommendations.

Participate in due diligence processes, management calls, and industry conferences.

Build and maintain relationships with clients, investors, and internal stakeholders.

Qualifications:

1-3 years of experience in Equity Research, Investment Banking, or Asset Management.

Strong understanding of financial statements, valuation techniques, and equity research processes.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously.

Demonstrated interest in cryptocurrencies, blockchain technology, and digital finance is a plus.

applications.role: Supporting pre- and post-acquisition integration planning and execution for a PE-backed transportation and towing platform acquiring approximately one business per quarter, ensuring acquired brands integrate smoothly with Valor standards while maintaining operational stability
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
M&A Integration Associate

Valor Fleet Services

Valor Fleet Services is hiring an M&A Integration Associate to support the growth of our portfolio of integrated transportation and towing brands.
We are a PE-backed platform currently operating five integrated brands, with an acquisition pace of approximately one new partner business per quarter. Our focus is not just acquiring businesses but integrating them thoughtfully and effectively: preserving what makes each partner successful while building scalable standards across the platform.
This role is centered on pre- and post-acquisition integration, not M&A deal execution. Transaction execution is led by senior leadership and external partners; this role ensures integrations are planned and carried out on the right timeline without disrupting core operations.
What You’ll Do

You will work closely with the Director of Strategic Operations, acquired brand leadership, and cross-functional teams to plan and execute integrations.
Key responsibilities include:
Support pre- and post-acquisition integration planning and execution, including the development and tracking of detailed integration workplans across functional areas.
Act as a central point of coordination for integration activities, ensuring acquired brands are aligned with Valor standards, timelines, and priorities.
Partner with acquired brand leadership to identify integration priorities before close and support execution after close.
Monitor integration progress against defined milestones, escalating risks and issues and helping drive timely resolution.
Balance speed with stability, ensuring integrations move forward without breaking or disrupting the businesses Valor partners with.
Support broader strategic growth initiatives and ongoing acquisition activity as needed.
What We’re Looking For

2–5 years of experience in project management, integration, corporate development, consulting, operations, or a related field.
Strong organizational skills and comfort managing multiple, cross-functional workstreams.
Clear communicator who can work effectively with leadership and operating teams.
Proactive, detail-oriented, and comfortable operating in fast-moving environments.
High EQ, low ego, and a collaborative approach to change.
Nice to have: experience in M&A integration, PE-backed environments, or multi-location operating businesses.
Why Valor Fleet Services

Hands-on exposure to frequent acquisitions and real integration work.
Close partnership with senior leadership in a high-visibility role.
A platform that prioritizes thoughtful integration over speed for speed’s sake.
Opportunity to grow with a rapidly scaling organization.

Integration problems require rapid identification and structured escalation. No evidence of managing project risks or driving resolution across functions.

applications.role: Supporting investment operations for Fannie Mae's affordable housing and tax credit investments through data analysis, business intelligence tools, process automation, and operational coordination to ensure efficient oversight and deployment of housing finance investments
applications.jobDescription:
Playing an essential role in the U.S. economy, Fannie Mae is foundational to housing finance. Here, your expertise can help fuel purpose-driven innovation that expands access to homeownership and affordable rental housing across the country. Join Fannie Mae to grow your career and help people find a place to call home.

Job Description

As a valued colleague on our team, you will collaborate with your team and peers to solve business needs, use applications and BI tools to explore and understand data, execute on key processes, and collaborate with the team to prepare plans to find solutions.

THE IMPACT YOU WILL MAKE

The Investment Operations Associate role will offer you the flexibility to make each day your own, while working alongside people who care so that you can deliver on the following responsibilities: 

Assist in identifying and addressing internal and external customer needs and strive to do what's right for our customers.
Coordinate activities with team to design, develop, and maintain data analysis and business intelligence (BI) applications and tools.
Support developing, refining, and implementing automated business processes and mechanisms to help us deliver on Fannie Mae's vision and purpose in safely and soundly deploying and overseeing affordable housing investments.
Compile data and analyses to inform business decision-making capabilities. Assist with identifying, researching, and resolving technical problems.
Maintain a focus on operational efficiency, including both speed and quality, while overseeing the timely completion of key processes that support Fannie Mae’s housing tax credit investments.
THE EXPERIENCE YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

Desired Experience

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent, including coursework in accounting, finance, and/or operations
Interest in or exposure to commercial real estate concepts
Excellent attention to detail
High level of familiarity and comfort with technology, including the Microsoft Office suite of programs (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Outlook) and BI software like Tableau, Power BI, or similar
Multifamily Customer Engagement - Business Intelligence - Associate

Target Pay Range: $85,000 - $110,000 a year

Qualifications

Education:

Bachelor's Level Degree
The future is what you make it to be. Discover compelling opportunities at Fanniemae.com/careers.

For most roles, employees are expected to work onsite on a regular basis at their designated office location. In-office work cadence is determined by your manager. Proximity within a reasonable commute to your designated office location is preferred unless the job is noted as open to remote.


Fannie Mae is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity/gender expression, marital or parental status, or any other protected factor. Fannie Mae is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities who are employees or applicants for employment, unless to do so would cause undue hardship to the company. If you need assistance using our online system and/or you need a reasonable accommodation related to the hiring/application process, please complete this form.

The hiring range for this role is set forth below. Final salaries will generally vary within that range based on factors that include but are not limited to, skill set, depth of experience, certifications, and other relevant qualifications. This position is eligible to participate in a Fannie Mae incentive program (subject to the terms of the program). As part of our comprehensive benefits package, Fannie Mae offers a broad range of Health, Life, Voluntary Lifestyle, and other benefits and perks that enhance an employee's physical, mental, emotional, and financial well-being. See more here.

applications.role: Execute and manage portfolio trades using Orion and brokerage platforms while ensuring compliance with Islamic finance principles, managing cash flows, implementing tax-efficient strategies, and maintaining accurate portfolio operations for a faith-based asset management firm
applications.jobDescription:
Trading Operations Specialist
Location: Falls Church, Virginia (Onsite Required)
Employment Type: Full-time

About Us

Azzad Asset Management is a leader in faith-based, Islamic investing (or halal investing). It specializes in socially responsible strategies that align with Islamic finance principles.

Position Overview

We seek a Trading Operations Specialist to manage and execute portfolio trades and updates using Orion and various brokerage/custodian platforms. This role involves executing trades, implementing model adjustments, ensuring portfolio compliance, managing cash balances, and applying tax-efficient strategies.

Key Responsibilities

Trade Execution & Portfolio Updates: Execute trades, engage with brokers and custodians to ensure timely trade settlement, communicate daily trade data, rebalance accounts for tax-efficiently, and update portfolios based on model changes. Ensure compliance with investment policies.
Cash Management & Account Monitoring: Manage daily cash flow activity, maintain adequate cash balances for withdrawals, RMDs, and investments, and proactively address liquidity needs.
Trade Operations & Compliance: Perform post-trade account reviews to ensure trades align with internal policies and strategies and resolve deviations.
Technology & Platform Management: Use Orion and other brokerage platforms for trade execution, portfolio management, and account monitoring. Ensure accurate data and troubleshooting of system issues.
Additional Trading & Investment Operations: Perform other related duties as assigned in support of trading and investment operations.
Qualifications & Skills

Bachelor’s Degree in a related field
2-3 years of trading experience within a fast-paced, registered investment advisory firm
Proficiency in Salesforce, Microsoft Excel, and Orion trading platform
Strong multitasking abilities with the ability to prioritize, work under pressure, and meet deadlines
Excellent communication and organizational skills with a sense of urgency and responsiveness
Analytical and detail-oriented with a high degree of accuracy

missingSkills.reason: This is the PRIMARY platform for executing trades and managing portfolios. The entire job centers on Orion proficiency. Zero demonstrated experience.
The job requires 2-3 years of actual trading experience in an RIA firm. Candidate has zero trading experience whatsoever - no equity, fixed income, ETF, or any securities trading background.
Daily work involves engaging with Schwab, Fidelity, TD Ameritrade, Pershing, etc. No demonstrated experience with any custodial platforms or trade settlement processes.
Core responsibility includes rebalancing client accounts tax-efficiently. No evidence of portfolio management, asset allocation, or rebalancing experience.

applications.role: Providing analytical and operational support for Capital Markets structured finance initiatives, including securitization, warehouse lending, and whole loan sales to optimize the bank's balance sheet. The role involves financial modeling, risk assessment, deal evaluation, and supporting day-to-day warehouse lending operations.
applications.jobDescription:
Capital Markets Analyst I
Remote
We are a hybrid, remote-office company dedicated to growing our talent anywhere!

We have onsite locations in: Sioux Falls, SD,  Scottsdale, AZ,  Louisville, KY,  Troy, MI,  Franklin, TN,  Easton, PA.

At Pathward, we take tremendous pride in our purpose to create financial inclusion for all™. We are a financial empowerment company that works with innovators to increase financial availability, choice, and opportunity for all.  We strive to remove barriers that traditional institutions put in the way of financial access, and promote economic mobility by providing responsible, secure, high quality financial products.

We are a team of problem solvers and innovators who celebrate our differences and know that our unique perspectives make us stronger and well-positioned for success.  We celebrate, and embrace, our team members through our *HUMBLE*HUNGRY*SMART approach, and we believe that we are strongest when we embrace the voices of our employees, customers, partners, and the communities we serve. 

About the Role:

Contribute to efforts to identify, evaluate and achieve strategic business opportunities through new Capital Markets structured finance initiatives in order to optimize the bank’s balance sheet and improve the company’s financial performance. 

What You Will Do:

Provides analytical and operational support in managing the bank’s Capital Markets initiatives.
Assists in the evaluation, structuring and execution of potential new Capital Markets structured finance opportunities including securitization, whole loan sales and forward flow agreements.
Assists in reviewing Lender Finance / Warehouse term sheets and legal documentation, assessing deal risk and return metrics, preparation of deal approval write ups, and presentations to the bank’s Executive Credit Committee.
Assists with day-to-day warehouse lending operations including preparing advance / paydown requests and reviewing borrowing base and compliance reporting.
Develops and executes ongoing controls, mitigation plans, testing, and oversight of the warehouse lending business.
Identifies and implements new concepts, best practices and approaches related to Capital Markets reporting and operational & credit risk management.
Assists in overseeing all third-party Capital Markets financial and operational audits.
Assists in overseeing all Capital Markets due diligence, financial modeling, risk management and legal documentation.
Works in a collaborative environment to perform detailed financial analysis and assist in building complex transaction models for new and existing warehouse – structured finance transactions.
Supports the Chief Investment Officer and Head of Capital Markets in other assigned tasks related to the Bank’s Structured Finance Business.
Other duties as assigned.
What You Will Need:

Bachelor's degree in Finance or related field or equivalent education and work experience.
Up to 2+ years with bachelor's or equivalent.
Communication skills (verbal and written)
Critical thinking skills
The responsibilities listed above are not all inclusive and may be changed at any time.

Salary range:  $60,000 – $100,500

 
The salary range reflects the minimum and maximum target for a new hire in this role. Individual pay within the range will be determined by multiple factors which can include but are not limited to a candidate’s experience, qualifications, skills, and location. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary for your location during the hiring process. Ranges may be modified in the future.
This role is also eligible for an annual performance-based incentive opportunity.  Pathward offers a comprehensive benefits package for eligible employees, including health insurance, 401(k) retirement benefits, life insurance, disability benefits, paid time off, and more.

qaHistory.q: We are excited to learn more about you! Please share what initially sparked your interest in this role and how it aligns with your professional goals. Additionally, we’d love to hear your thoughts on Pathward as an organization—what drew you to us, and how do you see yourself contributing to our mission?*
qaHistory.a:
I'll be direct: I don't have traditional capital markets experience, but this role immediately caught my attention because it sits at the intersection of three things I've genuinely enjoyed in my work—financial analytics, building operational systems, and problem-solving with data. In my Flashconverter project, I built a financial intelligence platform that processes bank statements and categorizes transactions using machine learning, which taught me how to think about financial data structures, risk patterns, and automated analysis. While that's personal finance rather than structured finance, the analytical mindset of breaking down complex financial flows, assessing patterns, and building models to support decisions feels directly applicable here. My retail strategy project at Mason also gave me experience with predictive modeling and translating data insights into business recommendations, which seems relevant to the deal evaluation and financial modeling aspects of this role.

What drew me to Pathward specifically is your mission around financial inclusion and working with innovators to increase financial access. That resonates with me personally—I've seen how traditional banking systems create barriers, and the idea of optimizing balance sheets not just for profit but to enable more lending and financial opportunity feels meaningful. I'm also genuinely excited about the learning curve here. I know I'd need to get up to speed quickly on securitization mechanics, warehouse lending operations, and credit risk frameworks, but I'm confident in my ability to learn complex systems—I've done it before when I taught myself cloud architecture for my AWS/Azure project and when I built ML algorithms from scratch without formal training. I see this role as a chance to apply my analytical foundation to a specialized area of finance while contributing to a company that's actually trying to solve real problems in financial access rather than just moving money around.

applications.role: Execute and manage portfolio trades using Orion and brokerage platforms, ensuring compliance with investment policies, managing cash flows, implementing tax-efficient rebalancing strategies, and maintaining accurate portfolio data for a faith-based investment advisory firm
applications.jobDescription:
Trading Operations Specialist
Location: Falls Church, Virginia (Onsite Required)
Employment Type: Full-time

About Us

Azzad Asset Management is a leader in faith-based, Islamic investing (or halal investing). It specializes in socially responsible strategies that align with Islamic finance principles.

Position Overview

We seek a Trading Operations Specialist to manage and execute portfolio trades and updates using Orion and various brokerage/custodian platforms. This role involves executing trades, implementing model adjustments, ensuring portfolio compliance, managing cash balances, and applying tax-efficient strategies.

Key Responsibilities

Trade Execution & Portfolio Updates: Execute trades, engage with brokers and custodians to ensure timely trade settlement, communicate daily trade data, rebalance accounts for tax-efficiently, and update portfolios based on model changes. Ensure compliance with investment policies.
Cash Management & Account Monitoring: Manage daily cash flow activity, maintain adequate cash balances for withdrawals, RMDs, and investments, and proactively address liquidity needs.
Trade Operations & Compliance: Perform post-trade account reviews to ensure trades align with internal policies and strategies and resolve deviations.
Technology & Platform Management: Use Orion and other brokerage platforms for trade execution, portfolio management, and account monitoring. Ensure accurate data and troubleshooting of system issues.
Additional Trading & Investment Operations: Perform other related duties as assigned in support of trading and investment operations.
Qualifications & Skills

Bachelor’s Degree in a related field
2-3 years of trading experience within a fast-paced, registered investment advisory firm
Proficiency in Salesforce, Microsoft Excel, and Orion trading platform
Strong multitasking abilities with the ability to prioritize, work under pressure, and meet deadlines
Excellent communication and organizational skills with a sense of urgency and responsiveness
Analytical and detail-oriented with a high degree of accuracy

At Pathward, we take tremendous pride in our purpose to create financial inclusion for all™. We are a financial empowerment company that works with innovators to increase financial availability, choice, and opportunity for all.  We strive to remove barriers that traditional institutions put in the way of financial access, and promote economic mobility by providing responsible, secure, high quality financial products.

We are a team of problem solvers and innovators who celebrate our differences and know that our unique perspectives make us stronger and well-positioned for success.  We celebrate, and embrace, our team members through our *HUMBLE*HUNGRY*SMART approach, and we believe that we are strongest when we embrace the voices of our employees, customers, partners, and the communities we serve. 

About the Role:

Contribute to efforts to identify, evaluate and achieve strategic business opportunities through new Capital Markets structured finance initiatives in order to optimize the bank’s balance sheet and improve the company’s financial performance. 

What You Will Do:

Provides analytical and operational support in managing the bank’s Capital Markets initiatives.
Assists in the evaluation, structuring and execution of potential new Capital Markets structured finance opportunities including securitization, whole loan sales and forward flow agreements.
Assists in reviewing Lender Finance / Warehouse term sheets and legal documentation, assessing deal risk and return metrics, preparation of deal approval write ups, and presentations to the bank’s Executive Credit Committee.
Assists with day-to-day warehouse lending operations including preparing advance / paydown requests and reviewing borrowing base and compliance reporting.
Develops and executes ongoing controls, mitigation plans, testing, and oversight of the warehouse lending business.
Identifies and implements new concepts, best practices and approaches related to Capital Markets reporting and operational & credit risk management.
Assists in overseeing all third-party Capital Markets financial and operational audits.
Assists in overseeing all Capital Markets due diligence, financial modeling, risk management and legal documentation.
Works in a collaborative environment to perform detailed financial analysis and assist in building complex transaction models for new and existing warehouse – structured finance transactions.
Supports the Chief Investment Officer and Head of Capital Markets in other assigned tasks related to the Bank’s Structured Finance Business.
Other duties as assigned.
What You Will Need:

Bachelor's degree in Finance or related field or equivalent education and work experience.
Up to 2+ years with bachelor's or equivalent.
Communication skills (verbal and written)
Critical thinking skills
The responsibilities listed above are not all inclusive and may be changed at any time.

Salary range:  $60,000 – $100,500

 
The salary range reflects the minimum and maximum target for a new hire in this role. Individual pay within the range will be determined by multiple factors which can include but are not limited to a candidate’s experience, qualifications, skills, and location. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary for your location during the hiring process. Ranges may be modified in the future.
This role is also eligible for an annual performance-based incentive opportunity.  Pathward offers a comprehensive benefits package for eligible employees, including health insurance, 401(k) retirement benefits, life insurance, disability benefits, paid time off, and more.

applications.role: Managing and executing portfolio trades, implementing model adjustments, ensuring compliance, and maintaining cash balances using Orion and brokerage platforms within an Islamic/socially responsible investment advisory firm
applications.jobDescription:
Trading Operations Specialist
Location: Falls Church, Virginia (Onsite Required)
Employment Type: Full-time

About Us

Azzad Asset Management is a leader in faith-based, Islamic investing (or halal investing). It specializes in socially responsible strategies that align with Islamic finance principles.

Position Overview

We seek a Trading Operations Specialist to manage and execute portfolio trades and updates using Orion and various brokerage/custodian platforms. This role involves executing trades, implementing model adjustments, ensuring portfolio compliance, managing cash balances, and applying tax-efficient strategies.

Key Responsibilities

Trade Execution & Portfolio Updates: Execute trades, engage with brokers and custodians to ensure timely trade settlement, communicate daily trade data, rebalance accounts for tax-efficiently, and update portfolios based on model changes. Ensure compliance with investment policies.
Cash Management & Account Monitoring: Manage daily cash flow activity, maintain adequate cash balances for withdrawals, RMDs, and investments, and proactively address liquidity needs.
Trade Operations & Compliance: Perform post-trade account reviews to ensure trades align with internal policies and strategies and resolve deviations.
Technology & Platform Management: Use Orion and other brokerage platforms for trade execution, portfolio management, and account monitoring. Ensure accurate data and troubleshooting of system issues.
Additional Trading & Investment Operations: Perform other related duties as assigned in support of trading and investment operations.
Qualifications & Skills

Bachelor’s Degree in a related field
2-3 years of trading experience within a fast-paced, registered investment advisory firm
Proficiency in Salesforce, Microsoft Excel, and Orion trading platform
Strong multitasking abilities with the ability to prioritize, work under pressure, and meet deadlines
Excellent communication and organizational skills with a sense of urgency and responsiveness
Analytical and detail-oriented with a high degree of accuracy

Understanding of equities, fixed income, ETFs, mutual funds, and market mechanics is foundational. Candidate shows no finance industry knowledge.
Azzad specializes in halal investing. Understanding riba, gharar, and prohibited sectors would be highly valuable though potentially trainable.

applications.role: Supporting investment operations for affordable housing and multifamily housing tax credit investments through data analysis, business intelligence tools, process automation, and operational coordination to ensure efficient execution of key processes
applications.jobDescription:
Playing an essential role in the U.S. economy, Fannie Mae is foundational to housing finance. Here, your expertise can help fuel purpose-driven innovation that expands access to homeownership and affordable rental housing across the country. Join Fannie Mae to grow your career and help people find a place to call home.

Job Description

As a valued colleague on our team, you will collaborate with your team and peers to solve business needs, use applications and BI tools to explore and understand data, execute on key processes, and collaborate with the team to prepare plans to find solutions.

THE IMPACT YOU WILL MAKE

The Investment Operations Associate role will offer you the flexibility to make each day your own, while working alongside people who care so that you can deliver on the following responsibilities: 

Assist in identifying and addressing internal and external customer needs and strive to do what's right for our customers.
Coordinate activities with team to design, develop, and maintain data analysis and business intelligence (BI) applications and tools.
Support developing, refining, and implementing automated business processes and mechanisms to help us deliver on Fannie Mae's vision and purpose in safely and soundly deploying and overseeing affordable housing investments.
Compile data and analyses to inform business decision-making capabilities. Assist with identifying, researching, and resolving technical problems.
Maintain a focus on operational efficiency, including both speed and quality, while overseeing the timely completion of key processes that support Fannie Mae’s housing tax credit investments.
THE EXPERIENCE YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

Desired Experience

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent, including coursework in accounting, finance, and/or operations
Interest in or exposure to commercial real estate concepts
Excellent attention to detail
High level of familiarity and comfort with technology, including the Microsoft Office suite of programs (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Outlook) and BI software like Tableau, Power BI, or similar
Multifamily Customer Engagement - Business Intelligence - Associate

Target Pay Range: $85,000 - $110,000 a year

Education:

Bachelor's Level Degree
The future is what you make it to be. Discover compelling opportunities at Fanniemae.com/careers.

applications.role: The role primarily focuses on serving as the central systems administrator for ERP platforms (payroll, billing, HRIS, ATS) across a national security services organization, providing comprehensive system support, training, troubleshooting, and coordination between divisional offices and corporate departments to ensure operational efficiency and compliance.
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Job Summary

Admiral Security Services is a leading provider of professional security services across the United States. With a strong commitment to operational excellence, integrity, and service, we deliver customized security solutions that meet the unique needs of our clients across a wide range of industries.


The Business Systems Support Specialist serves as the primary systems administrator for Admiral Security Services and ADM Security Solutions, overseeing the management and optimization of our enterprise resource planning platforms, including payroll, billing, HRIS, and Applicant Tracking Systems. This position functions as the central conduit for systems support, guidance, and issue resolution for divisional and regional offices across the United States. In addition, the role collaborates closely with our parent company’s corporate HR, Accounting, and IT departments to ensure alignment, compliance, and seamless integration of organizational processes and system functions.


Salary & Benefits

Annual Salary: $85,000 - $90,000
Sign-On Bonus: $3,000
Comprehensive Benefits Package:
Medical, dental, and vision (individual and family options)
Free employee life insurance + supplemental coverage
Short-term and long-term disability plans
Accident and critical illness coverage
401(k) plan with annual discretionary match
Paid vacation, paid holidays, and sick leave
Generous tuition reimbursement




Key Responsibilities

Serve as the primary liaison and systems administrator for ERP and related platforms supporting all Admiral/ADM divisional and regional staff nationwide.
Train and support employees and managers on the ERP, payroll, billing platform, applicant tracking system, and other enterprise HRIS functions.
Provide administrative and helpdesk support for all system users, ensuring timely assistance with system access, functionality, and troubleshooting needs.
Investigate and resolve system issues by identifying root causes and coordinating resolution with vendors or the parent company’s IT department.
Coordinate, test, and validate new system features, enhancements, and process improvements across payroll, billing, HRIS, and ATS platforms.
Develop, maintain, and distribute training materials for all enterprise systems; deliver virtual training sessions, onboarding instruction, and refresher courses.
Monitor and ensure compliance with payroll and billing deadlines for all locations and users.
Regularly review systems-generated reports, identify discrepancies, and escalate issues to the senior leadership.
Prepare analytical reports for Division Vice Presidents and Senior Leadership, including operational efficiency metrics, recruitment statistics, and compliance data.
Coordinate billable charges (such as parking and other reimbursables) with the Accounts Payable department.
Process job modifications to ensure client information, pay rates, and billing configurations are accurately updated in BOETA.
Review and validate invoice drafts
Submit new job bids and temporary job setups in alignment with corporate procedures.
Review and process billable bonuses, including holiday bonuses, ensuring accuracy and documentation compliance.
Conduct periodic audits of pay rates and bill rates across all systems to ensure consistent accuracy.
Review and approve Personnel Action Forms (PAFs).
Analyze payroll variance reports to identify overages and escalate issues as needed.
Make approved purchases using the company purchasing card in accordance with policy.
Coordinate with Divisional Offices, Corporate HR and Accounting Department to support the resolution of payroll issues.
Provide Notary Public services to the organization as required.
Support special projects assigned by Corporate Operations or senior leadership.
Perform other related duties as assigned to support corporate operations and enterprise systems effectiveness.


Qualifications

Experience with Workday ERP
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Must be proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook).
Be detail oriented.
Possess strong time management skills and project management experience. 
3+ years’ experience with payroll, billing, ATS or other systems.
Prior HRIS experience a plus!
Extremely professional appearance and demeanor.
A college degree preferred but solid relevant experience with a proven history of excellence may be considered in lieu of a degree.
This job requires sitting in front of a computer 95% of the time. Some standing lifting, bending, twisting, and walking required. Some repetitive motion required when using a computer.
Work is performed inside in a climate-controlled environment.


Work Environment

This is a full-time, in-office position located at Admiral Security Services’ headquarters in Bethesda, Maryland.


Benefits

Comprehensive single and family medical coverage with generous employer cost-sharing
Dental and Vision insurance
Free employee life insurance
Supplemental life insurance for employees and family members
Short-term and long-term disability insurance
Voluntary accident and illness plans
401(k) with annual discretionary match
Generous tuition reimbursement program
Two or more weeks of annual paid vacation
Paid holidays.
Annual sick leave allowance


To learn more about Admiral Security Services visit www.admiralsecurity.com

applications.role: Evaluating and providing feedback on AI-generated financial content to improve AI understanding of finance concepts, using real-world financial analysis and modeling expertise in a flexible, contract-based capacity
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Overview

Handshake is seeking experienced Finance Professionals to support AI research through flexible, hourly contract work. This is not a traditional full-time corporate, banking, consulting, or investment role. You’ll use your real-world financial analysis, modeling, and advisory experience to evaluate AI-generated content and provide feedback that helps AI better understand financial concepts, quantitative reasoning, industry terminology, and professional communication.

This is an ongoing, project-based opportunity that can be done alongside research, teaching, postdoctoral work, or industry employment.

Details

This project involves using your professional experience as a Finance Professional to design job-related prompts and review AI-generated responses for accuracy, analytical rigor, clarity, and relevance to real-world financial tasks.

Status: Independent contractor (not a full-time employee role)
Location: Fully remote; work from anywhere with a reliable internet connection and access to a desktop or laptop computer
Schedule: Flexible and asynchronous, with no minimum hour requirement. Many contributors work approximately 5–20 hours per week when assigned to an active project
Duration: The Handshake AI program runs year-round, with projects opening periodically across different areas of expertise. Placement depends on current project needs, with opportunities to be considered for future projects as they become available

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Finance or a closely related field
Experience as a Finance Professional in corporate finance, investment banking, consulting, asset management, or related roles
Hands-on experience conducting financial analysis, building or reviewing financial models, and interpreting financial statements
Strong written communication skills and attention to detail
Comfort working independently and following written guidelines
Professional judgment, reliability, and a high standard of discretion and confidentiality, especially with sensitive or proprietary information

No Prior AI Or Technical Experience Is Required.

Work authorization information

applications.role: Developing new business opportunities and retaining existing business within Federal Markets Division by building relationships with federal agency benefit officers and employees, providing training and coaching, and utilizing consultative sales strategies to sell medical and ancillary products
applications.jobDescription:
Resp & Qualifications

Must live in the DC, MD, or Northern VA service area.

PURPOSE: 
This role is responsible for developing new business opportunities and/or retain existing business within the Federal Markets Division. This position represents the organization in acquisition strategy development, prospecting, network analytics, financial evaluation and negotiating with clients and brokers using consultative selling skills in order to grow new business and/or retain existing business and enhance profitability. 

ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS:

Under general guidance, aggressively grow new business and/or renew existing business for medical and ancillary products in the Federal Markets Division. Ensure appropriate update of Sales strategy/sales activities documentation on a monthly basis.
Develop strong relationships with Agency Benefit Officers, federal employees and retirees, postal employees and uniformed service members, utilizing strategic and consultative selling skills both in person and through virtual channels.
Provide documented formal training of assigned Agency Benefits Officers and Postmasters throughout the year including the use of virtual training technology where appropriate.  Must be able to create and effectively conduct training presentations on all medical and ancillary products, as well as regulatory changes.
Management and coaching of assigned Agency Benefits Officers and Postmasters to ensure paperwork and enrollment process is followed correctly. Take appropriate action where necessary to ensure that distribution partners are following guidelines.
QUALIFICATIONS:

Education Level: Bachelor's Degree in Business, Finance or related field OR in lieu of a Bachelor's degree, an additional 4 years of relevant work experience is required in addition to the required work experience.

Licenses/Certifications Upon Hire Required:

Current health and life license for the jurisdictions of Maryland, DC, and Virginia.
Experience: 3 years of federal sales/healthcare experience in a similar sales position.

Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in a similar Federal sales position with a competitor or another Blue Cross Blue Shield plan is preferred.
Knowledge, Skills and Abilities (KSAs)

Knowledge and understanding of CRM, Salesforce, and other client management tools.
Strong customer service orientation.
Ability to recognize, analyze, and solve a variety of problems.
Excellent communication skills both written and verbal.
Must be able to meet established deadlines and handle multiple customer service demands from internal and external customers, within set expectations for service excellence. Must be able to effectively communicate and provide positive customer service to every internal and external customer, including customers who may be demanding or otherwise challenging.

 
Salary Range: $60,800 - $98,800

 

Salary Range Disclaimer

The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the work is being performed. This compensation range is specific and considers factors such as (but not limited to) the scope and responsibilities of the position, the candidate's work experience, education/training, internal peer equity, and market and business consideration. It is not typical for an individual to be hired at the top of the range, as compensation decisions depend on each case's facts and circumstances, including but not limited to experience, internal equity, and location. In addition to your compensation, CareFirst offers a comprehensive benefits package, various incentive programs/plans, and 401k contribution programs/plans (all benefits/incentives are subject to eligibility requirements).

Service Charge

applications.role: Supporting investment operations for affordable housing and tax credit investments through data analysis, business intelligence tools, and process automation while maintaining operational efficiency and customer focus
applications.jobDescription:
Playing an essential role in the U.S. economy, Fannie Mae is foundational to housing finance. Here, your expertise can help fuel purpose-driven innovation that expands access to homeownership and affordable rental housing across the country. Join Fannie Mae to grow your career and help people find a place to call home.

Job Description

As a valued colleague on our team, you will collaborate with your team and peers to solve business needs, use applications and BI tools to explore and understand data, execute on key processes, and collaborate with the team to prepare plans to find solutions.

THE IMPACT YOU WILL MAKE

The Investment Operations Associate role will offer you the flexibility to make each day your own, while working alongside people who care so that you can deliver on the following responsibilities: 

Assist in identifying and addressing internal and external customer needs and strive to do what's right for our customers.
Coordinate activities with team to design, develop, and maintain data analysis and business intelligence (BI) applications and tools.
Support developing, refining, and implementing automated business processes and mechanisms to help us deliver on Fannie Mae's vision and purpose in safely and soundly deploying and overseeing affordable housing investments.
Compile data and analyses to inform business decision-making capabilities. Assist with identifying, researching, and resolving technical problems.
Maintain a focus on operational efficiency, including both speed and quality, while overseeing the timely completion of key processes that support Fannie Mae’s housing tax credit investments.
THE EXPERIENCE YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

Desired Experience

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent, including coursework in accounting, finance, and/or operations
Interest in or exposure to commercial real estate concepts
Excellent attention to detail
High level of familiarity and comfort with technology, including the Microsoft Office suite of programs (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Outlook) and BI software like Tableau, Power BI, or similar
Multifamily Customer Engagement - Business Intelligence - Associate

Target Pay Range: $85,000 - $110,000 a year

Qualifications

Education:

Bachelor's Level Degree
The future is what you make it to be. Discover compelling opportunities at Fanniemae.com/careers.

For most roles, employees are expected to work onsite on a regular basis at their designated office location. In-office work cadence is determined by your manager. Proximity within a reasonable commute to your designated office location is preferred unless the job is noted as open to remote.


Fannie Mae is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity/gender expression, marital or parental status, or any other protected factor. Fannie Mae is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities who are employees or applicants for employment, unless to do so would cause undue hardship to the company. If you need assistance using our online system and/or you need a reasonable accommodation related to the hiring/application process, please complete this form.

The hiring range for this role is set forth below. Final salaries will generally vary within that range based on factors that include but are not limited to, skill set, depth of experience, certifications, and other relevant qualifications. This position is eligible to participate in a Fannie Mae incentive program (subject to the terms of the program). As part of our comprehensive benefits package, Fannie Mae offers a broad range of Health, Life, Voluntary Lifestyle, and other benefits and perks that enhance an employee's physical, mental, emotional, and financial well-being. See more here.

Requisition compensation:

85000

applications.role: Supporting investment portfolio management through quantitative and qualitative analysis, conducting due diligence on investment opportunities across multiple asset classes, and preparing investment reports and materials for committees and stakeholders
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description
Overview

AARP is the nation's largest nonprofit, nonpartisan organization dedicated to empowering people 50 and older to choose how they live as they age. With a nationwide presence, AARP strengthens communities and advocates for what matters most to the more than 100 million Americans 50-plus and their families: health and financial security, and personal fulfillment. AARP also works for individuals in the marketplace by sparking new solutions and allowing carefully chosen, high-quality products and services to carry the AARP name. As a trusted source for news and information, AARP produces the nation's largest-circulation publications, AARP The Magazine and the AARP Bulletin.

 

Treasury is responsible for the cash and investment management activities of the AARP enterprise. This includes maintaining sufficient liquidity for operating needs, maximizing earnings on investments within policy guidelines and efficiently processing daily transactions.

 

The Investment Analyst assists with the management of a broadly diversified portfolio including investments in various asset classes such as public equity, fixed income, hedge funds, private equity, real assets and private credit. Compile data and prepare reports on portfolio valuations, holdings, fees, other investment information, for use by investment staff, committees, and boards. Conduct quantitative and qualitative due diligence on existing and prospective funds.


Responsibilities

Research, analyze, and evaluate investment opportunities in various asset classes leading to preparation of investment recommendations.
Assist in due diligence by coordinating manager meetings, preparing relevant reports in advance of investment manager meetings, attending annual meetings or onsite meetings, and documenting meeting notes.
Conduct quantitative performance/risk analysis on portfolio and investments.
Conduct research on trending topics by gathering and summarizing data, writing whitepapers.
Prepare investment materials for Investment Committee members, Board members, and internal/external stakeholders
Act as a liaison between the investment team and external consultants in order to prepare monthly and quarterly data feeds needed by our consultants.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Finance, Business Administration, or related field, with 2+ years of relevant experience.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Office Suite.
Strong communication skills with all levels of management and stakeholders.
 

Preferred:

CFA or CFE candidate
 

AARP will not sponsor an employment visa for this position at this time.

Additional Requirements 


Regular and reliable job attendance
Effective verbal and written communication skills
Exhibit respect and understanding of others to maintain professional relationships
Independent judgement in evaluation options to make sound decisions
In office/open office environment with the ability to work effectively surrounded by moderate noise
 

Hybrid Work Environment

AARP observes Mondays and Fridays as remote workdays, except for essential functions. Remote work can only be done within the United States and its territories.

 

Compensation and Benefits

AARP offers a competitive compensation and benefits package including a 401(k); 100% company-funded pension plan; health, dental, and vision plans; life insurance; paid time off to include company and individual holidays, vacation, sick, caregiving, and parental leave; performance-based and peer-based recognition and tuition reimbursement.


Equal Employment Opportunity

AARP is an equal opportunity employer committed to hiring a diverse workforce and sustaining an inclusive culture. AARP does not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, sex, color, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, mental or physical disability, genetic information, veteran status, or on any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

applications.role: Learn forex trading through personalized one-on-one mentorship, complete an evaluation process by trading a personal live account, and qualify for a company-funded trading account to earn commission-based income by trading forex markets remotely
applications.jobDescription:
Job description
Important Notes:

This is a performance-based opportunity with no fixed salary; earnings depend on trading success.

Trading involves significant financial risk; no guaranteed income or funding.

Location: Remote (US-Based Applicants only)

Salary: Commission-Based (Up to 80% Profit Split, No Fixed Salary)

About Us: We are a leading forex trading education platform dedicated to transforming beginners into successful traders. Our program offers personalized, one-on-one live training and a unique opportunity for aspiring traders to become funded, trading our capital and earning commissions, even with little to no prior experience.

Job Description: We are seeking motivated individuals with a passion for learning forex trading to join our Junior Forex Trader Program. No prior trading experience is required—just a willingness to learn and trade! During our training program, you’ll receive online one-on-one live lessons with expert mentors and trade your own live account with a regulated broker. If you meet our evaluation criteria, you’ll qualify for a fully funded account provided by our company, allowing you to trade remotely and keep up to 80% of the profits you generate.

Key Responsibilities:

Participate in online one-on-one live forex trading lessons with personalized mentorship.
Trade a live personal account during training to meet our evaluation criteria (e.g., consistent profitability, risk management).
Upon qualifying, trade forex markets using a company-provided fully funded account, adhering to risk management guidelines.
Apply feedback from mentors to refine your trading skills.
Requirements:

No prior forex trading experience required; beginners are welcome!
Basic understanding of financial markets or a strong desire to learn (training provided).
Ability to open and fund a live trading account with a CFTC/NFA regulated broker.
Access to a computer, reliable internet, and availability for scheduled one-on-one live lessons.
Commitment to completing the training and evaluation process.
US residents only.
Disciplined, coachable, and eager to succeed in forex trading.
Benefits:

Qualify for a fully funded trading account, eliminating personal financial risk after evaluation.
Earn up to 80% of trading profits as commissions.
Work remotely with flexible trading hours in the 24/5 forex market.
Receive personalized, one-on-one live training and ongoing mentorship tailored for beginners.
Trade with confidence through a regulated broker.
Application Process:

Submit your CV or a 150-word statement explaining why you want to learn forex trading and join our junior trader program.

No trading records required—tell us about your motivation and goals!

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to schedule an introductory call, enroll in our one-on-one live training program, open a live trading account, and begin the evaluation process.

Apply Now: Ready to launch your forex trading journey? Apply today to join our personalized training program and become a junior forex trader!

applications.role: Supporting investment management operations by conducting research, analysis, and due diligence on diverse asset classes, preparing reports and materials for investment committees and boards, and coordinating between investment teams and external consultants to manage a broadly diversified portfolio
applications.jobDescription:
Job Description
Overview

AARP is the nation's largest nonprofit, nonpartisan organization dedicated to empowering people 50 and older to choose how they live as they age. With a nationwide presence, AARP strengthens communities and advocates for what matters most to the more than 100 million Americans 50-plus and their families: health and financial security, and personal fulfillment. AARP also works for individuals in the marketplace by sparking new solutions and allowing carefully chosen, high-quality products and services to carry the AARP name. As a trusted source for news and information, AARP produces the nation's largest-circulation publications, AARP The Magazine and the AARP Bulletin.

 

Treasury is responsible for the cash and investment management activities of the AARP enterprise. This includes maintaining sufficient liquidity for operating needs, maximizing earnings on investments within policy guidelines and efficiently processing daily transactions.

 

The Investment Analyst assists with the management of a broadly diversified portfolio including investments in various asset classes such as public equity, fixed income, hedge funds, private equity, real assets and private credit. Compile data and prepare reports on portfolio valuations, holdings, fees, other investment information, for use by investment staff, committees, and boards. Conduct quantitative and qualitative due diligence on existing and prospective funds.


Responsibilities

Research, analyze, and evaluate investment opportunities in various asset classes leading to preparation of investment recommendations.
Assist in due diligence by coordinating manager meetings, preparing relevant reports in advance of investment manager meetings, attending annual meetings or onsite meetings, and documenting meeting notes.
Conduct quantitative performance/risk analysis on portfolio and investments.
Conduct research on trending topics by gathering and summarizing data, writing whitepapers.
Prepare investment materials for Investment Committee members, Board members, and internal/external stakeholders
Act as a liaison between the investment team and external consultants in order to prepare monthly and quarterly data feeds needed by our consultants.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Finance, Business Administration, or related field, with 2+ years of relevant experience.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Office Suite.
Strong communication skills with all levels of management and stakeholders.
 

Preferred:

CFA or CFE candidate
 

AARP will not sponsor an employment visa for this position at this time.

Additional Requirements 


Regular and reliable job attendance
Effective verbal and written communication skills
Exhibit respect and understanding of others to maintain professional relationships
Independent judgement in evaluation options to make sound decisions
In office/open office environment with the ability to work effectively surrounded by moderate noise
 

Hybrid Work Environment

AARP observes Mondays and Fridays as remote workdays, except for essential functions. Remote work can only be done within the United States and its territories.

 

Compensation and Benefits

AARP offers a competitive compensation and benefits package including a 401(k); 100% company-funded pension plan; health, dental, and vision plans; life insurance; paid time off to include company and individual holidays, vacation, sick, caregiving, and parental leave; performance-based and peer-based recognition and tuition reimbursement.


Equal Employment Opportunity

AARP is an equal opportunity employer committed to hiring a diverse workforce and sustaining an inclusive culture. AARP does not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, sex, color, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, mental or physical disability, genetic information, veteran status, or on any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

Location: Washington, DC

Job Description:



Duties and Responsibilities:



Perform complete Ethical and Business Conflicts process for client/matters and stock purchases. This includes, but not limited to, running conflicts, entering in systems, adding info to and running reports, correspondence to firm, reviewing billing guidelines and new matter compliance.
Review and enter billing rates for new client/matters (the Sr. Analyst will also manage annual updates)

analysis.criticalVerdict: This is a complete mismatch. You're a data scientist/analyst trying to fit into a specialized legal operations role that requires zero technical coding skills and 100% legal industry domain knowledge. Your Python, machine learning, and cloud architecture skills are completely irrelevant here - this role needs someone who understands legal ethics, can navigate conflicts software, and knows law firm operations. You have impressive technical abilities, but you're applying to the wrong industry entirely.
improvementPlan: Stop applying to legal operations roles - your skills don't transfer. Target data analyst, business intelligence, or technical analyst positions where your Python/SQL/ML skills are actually valued.

If genuinely interested in legal tech (not legal operations), pivot to legal technology companies building AI/ML products for law firms (e.g., Relativity, Casetext, Harvey AI) where your technical skills matter.

For legal operations specifically: You'd need to start as a legal secretary or administrative assistant in a law firm for 1-2 years to learn the industry, then transition to conflicts. This would be a significant step backward in your career trajectory.
Alternatively, explore compliance analyst or risk analyst roles in regulated industries (finance, healthcare) where your analytical skills could transfer with some domain training.

Be strategic - you're 1-2 years out of college with strong technical skills. Don't waste them on administrative legal roles. Target roles titled 'Data Analyst', 'Business Intelligence Analyst', or 'Operations Analyst' in tech companies.

applications.role: Supporting daily operations and reporting functions for a wealth management firm, including account reconciliation, performance reporting, trading assistance, and document management for high net worth clients
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
MASON INVESTMENT ADVISORY SERVICES, INC.

 

OPERATIONS AND REPORTING ANALYST



Based in Reston, Virginia, we are a national financial planning and investment advisory firm serving high net worth individuals, endowments, and Fortune 500 corporate sponsored financial planning clients.


This is a great opportunity to join a well-established and growing team. We have a very positive culture where we encourage and provide ample opportunities for growth and development.


We are seeking a strong Operations and Reporting Analyst who will work collaboratively with members of both the Reporting and Operations Departments on daily tasks to include the following:


Operations:


Daily digital document filing.
Assist with client requests for cash transactions.
Assist with daily trading and reconciliation.
Assist with special projects as necessary.
 
Reporting:


Assist in maintaining the day-to-day reconciliation of accounts in our portfolio aggregation system.
Assist in the production of accurate and timely monthly and quarterly performance reports for all Mason clients.
Able to handle multiple requests at once and still provide accurate and timely responses/deliverables.
Manual account data entry.


Skills and qualifications:


Attention to detail with strong analytical and technical skills.
Basic understanding of investment securities and terminology.
Some college preferred. 


We offer excellent benefits including a 401(k) plan, a competitive salary, and a great work environment. We are looking forward to finding the right person to join The Mason Companies team.


Please send resume and salary requirements to rpylypko@masoncompanies.com.

applications.role: General operations execution role focused on autonomous project ownership, rapid planning and delivery, cross-functional collaboration, and doing whatever is needed to bring value to users in a fast-paced healthcare startup environment
applications.jobDescription:
About Solace
Healthcare in the U.S. is fundamentally broken. The system is so complex that 88% of U.S. adults do not have the health literacy necessary to navigate it without help. Solace cuts through the red tape of healthcare by pairing patients with expert advocates and giving them the tools to make better decisions—and get better outcomes.

We're a Series C startup, founded in 2022 and backed by Inspired Capital, Craft Ventures, Torch Capital, Menlo Ventures, Signalfire, and IVP. Our U.S. based team is lean, mission-driven, and growing quickly.

Solace isn't a place to coast. We're here to redefine healthcare—and that demands urgency, precision, and heart. If you're looking to stretch yourself, sharpen your edge, and do the best work of your life alongside a team that cares deeply, you're in the right place. We’re intense, and we like it that way.

Read more in our Bloomberg funding announcement here.

About the Role
As the Operations Associate at Solace, you are responsible for bringing value to our users by doing whatever Solace needs. You will be expected to take general tasks and simply execute at the highest level.

This is a rare opportunity where you will report to our Head of Operations and work on projects with direct visibility to our Chief Executive Officer.

Who are you? You are a talented operator who consistently delivers the highest quality work on timelines that would be unrealistic for most others. You get a rush from seeing your plans live in the real world. You take pride in your vision, execution and communication skills, and you absorb feedback exceptionally well. You enjoy taking ownership and are stifled by large organizations.

What You’ll Do

Analyze task requirements and produce high-quality plans and output.

Be autonomous. You’ll take full ownership of your work, and you take responsibility for every last detail, every step of the way.

Work with small multi-disciplinary teams. You’ll partner closely with engineering, product, and business folks to find elegant solutions to challenges.

Rapidly produce multiple ideas. You're not attached to a particular tactic, only around solving the problem.

Actively contribute to testing and ensuring the quality of our product

Plan and Do. You create a plan and execute it. You're equally comfortable managing a large project team as well as executing entirely yourself.

Learn and become a guardian of our standards to bring Solace to life.

What You Bring to the Table

Experience working in a chaotic environment.

2+ years of relevant experience working in consumer startups, banking, consulting, or any demanding job in a high pressure environment that required organization and performance.

An intuitive understanding of what it takes to bring a plan to life.

Exceptional skills in planning skills and using Notion.

A bias toward action and execution. You get antsy if you’re not getting something done.

Great communication skills that help you work with executive teams to make visions come to life.

Bonus

You've worked in healthcare and understand credentialing or billing codes

Applicants must be based in the United States.

They have strong technical chops and academic strategic thinking, but zero startup operational experience. Solace needs someone who's already survived the chaos, shipped under pressure, and thrived with ambiguity - this person has worked in highly structured environments (Apple retail, academic projects, structured AI training). The skills mismatch is significant: they're bringing Python and SQL to a role that needs Notion mastery and operational grit. Hard pass unless desperate.
improvementPlan: Get 12-18 months of actual startup operations experience - join an early-stage company (Series A/B) in an ops role where you'll wear multiple hats and ship weekly
Build a portfolio of operational projects, not analytical ones: launch a side project that serves real users, manage it end-to-end, iterate based on feedback, and document the chaos
Master Notion immediately - build complex project management systems, document processes, create dashboards. Make it second nature since it's explicitly required
