analysis.summary: This candidate is fundamentally mismatched for an operations role at a Series C healthcare startup. They bring data analysis and technical skills to a position demanding operational execution, startup chaos navigation, and healthcare domain knowledge. With 3 years at Apple retail and AI training work, they lack the 2+ years of consumer startup/consulting/banking operations experience that's non-negotiable. The technical skills are irrelevant here - Solace needs someone who ships, not someone who analyzes.

qaHistory.q: Why are you the one to carry a message to Garcia?
qaHistory.a:
I'm the person who doesn't wait for perfect instructions—I figure out what needs to happen and I make it happen. At Apple, I wasn't supposed to be building B2B relationships from a retail floor, but I saw business owners struggling with their tech infrastructure and knew I could help them. I taught myself MDM implementation, created my own consultation framework, and converted over 50 retail interactions into long-term business partnerships. Nobody told me to do that—I just saw the opportunity and executed.

When I needed to understand my finances before buying a house, I didn't find a tool that worked the way I needed, so I built Flashconverter from scratch. I went from knowing basic JavaScript to shipping a full financial intelligence platform with machine learning categorization and complete client-side architecture in about three months. I taught myself IndexedDB, worked through API integration challenges at 2am, and kept iterating until it actually solved my problem. Now it processes my bank statements in three seconds and powers the financial backend for my consulting work.

The "message to Garcia" essay resonates with me because I've lived that mentality. When I ran Dispomode, I couldn't use traditional advertising because of regulations, so I learned technical SEO through trial and error, built a custom Shopify storefront, and scaled to 100,000 monthly visitors purely through organic search. I handled everything—coding the frontend, managing inventory in QuickBooks, staying compliant with constantly changing regulations, optimizing conversion funnels. There was no playbook, just problems that needed solving.

I get restless when I'm not making progress on something real. At my AI training role, I don't just complete tasks—I study why certain prompts work better, how models respond to different approaches, and I apply that learning immediately. I'm not attached to a specific method; I care about solving the actual problem in front of me. That's what Solace needs—someone who sees what users need, builds a plan, and ships it without waiting for permission.

qaHistory.q: Please share an example of project that you led that you're particularly proud of.  Discuss what happened and why you  think it's worth sharing.
qaHistory.a:
I'm most proud of building Flashconverter because it forced me to own every single piece of a project from concept to shipped product—which seems like exactly what this role requires.

The problem was personal: I was trying to save for a house and realized I had zero visibility into where my money was actually going. I'd download bank statements as PDFs, then waste hours manually copying transactions into spreadsheets. I thought "there has to be a better way," so I just started building one.

I taught myself the technical pieces I didn't know as I went. I figured out how to parse PDFs on the client side for privacy, built a machine learning algorithm that learns spending patterns over time, and created a dashboard that actually gives useful insights. The hardest part wasn't the code—it was making decisions when I had no one to ask. Should I prioritize better categorization accuracy or faster processing? Do users need recurring subscription detection or savings optimization first? I had to test, get feedback from friends, iterate, and just make calls.

What I'm genuinely proud of isn't that it works—it's that it started as a personal frustration and I turned it into something real that people use. I went from idea to functional product in about two months of nights and weekends, shipping updates based on what actually mattered. The privacy architecture was non-negotiable for me, so I rebuilt parts of the system twice to keep everything client-side. Now I use it to run finances for both my personal life and my old e-commerce business, and it's saved me probably 50+ hours of manual work.

I think it's worth sharing because it shows how I operate: I see a problem, I don't wait for permission, and I figure out what I need to learn to fix it. I care more about shipping something that works than making it perfect, and I'm comfortable being the only person responsible when something breaks.

qaHistory.q: Why does Solace's mission resonate with you?
qaHistory.a:
I've seen firsthand how broken systems prevent people from getting the help they actually need—and how powerful it is when someone cuts through that complexity for them.

At Apple, I worked with business owners who were completely overwhelmed by choosing the right technology for their teams. The options were endless, the jargon was confusing, and most people didn't have the expertise to navigate it alone. I realized that my real value wasn't just selling products—it was being their advocate, asking the right questions to understand their actual needs, and translating technical complexity into clear solutions. When I helped a client streamline their entire mobile device management system after they'd been struggling for months with different vendors, they told me it felt like someone finally spoke their language. That feeling of cutting through confusion to deliver real outcomes stuck with me.

Healthcare is infinitely more complex and higher stakes than buying business tech. When I read that 88% of adults lack the health literacy to navigate the system, that statistic hit hard because I've watched family members struggle with denied claims, confusing bills, and not knowing who to call for help. The idea that someone could have an expert advocate to fight through that red tape and actually get better outcomes—that's the kind of tangible impact I want my work to have.

What excites me most about Solace is that you're not just building another healthcare app—you're pairing real human advocates with the right tools to solve messy, individual problems. That combination of people and technology solving something that genuinely matters is exactly where I want to focus my energy.

applications.role: Supporting the coordination and tracking of external technology partnerships through project management, dashboard development, KPI tracking, and operational reporting to provide visibility into technology innovation portfolio performance
applications.jobDescription:
The Leidos Office of Technology (OOT) — Technology Integration, External Technology Partnerships team — is seeking an External Technology Analyst Intern to support project coordination, technology tracking, and operational execution across our external innovation portfolio.
 

This role is well-suited for a highly organized, analytically minded intern who is interested in emerging technology and enjoys bringing structure, clarity, and measurable progress to complex initiatives. The intern will help the team manage active external technology engagements, track key milestones and metrics, and build dashboards that provide visibility into portfolio performance.

The position offers exposure to commercial technology partners, internal engineering teams, and senior stakeholders while developing practical skills in project management, data organization, and technology analysis.
 

Primary Responsibilities.

Support coordination of active external technology projects, including tracking milestones, deliverables, risks, and dependencies.

Develop and maintain dashboards and reporting tools to provide visibility into portfolio status, metrics, and outcomes.

Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) related to technology evaluations, integrations, and transitions.

Assist in organizing partner engagements, meeting notes, action items, and follow-ups to ensure timely execution.

Conduct structured research on emerging technologies and summarize findings in concise briefing materials.

Assist in conducting structured due diligence on prospective external partners, including reviewing technical documentation, market positioning, maturity, and risk factors.

Prepare presentations, executive-ready summaries, and visual reports to communicate progress and insights.

Identify opportunities to improve process efficiency, reporting clarity, and operational rigor within the team.
 

Basic Qualifications.

Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Management, Economics, Public Policy, Information Systems, or a related discipline. 

US Citizenship is required.

Strong organizational and project management skills with attention to detail.

Experience creating dashboards, reports, or data visualizations (e.g., Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI, Tableau, or similar tools).

Ability to synthesize information clearly and communicate effectively in written and verbal formats.

Demonstrated ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a structured environment.

Interest in emerging technology and innovation ecosystems.
 

Preferred Qualifications.

Experience supporting project coordination, portfolio tracking, or operational reporting efforts.

Familiarity with project management tools (e.g., JIRA, Confluence, Smartsheet, Microsoft Project, or similar).

Exposure to technology strategy, venture ecosystems, or competitive analysis.

Demonstrated initiative in building structure, metrics, or process improvements.

If you're looking for comfort, keep scrolling. At Leidos, we outthink, outbuild, and outpace the status quo — because the mission demands it. We're not hiring followers. We're recruiting the ones who disrupt, provoke, and refuse to fail. Step 10 is ancient history. We're already at step 30 — and moving faster than anyone else dares.

Original Posting:
February 25, 2026
For U.S. Positions: While subject to change based on business needs, Leidos reasonably anticipates that this job requisition will remain open for at least 3 days with an anticipated close date of no earlier than 3 days after the original posting date as listed above.

Pay Range:
Pay Range $48,100.00 - $86,950.00
The Leidos pay range for this job level is a general guideline only and not a guarantee of compensation or salary. Additional factors considered in extending an offer include (but are not limited to) responsibilities of the job, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity, alignment with market data, applicable bargaining agreement (if any), or other law.

applications.role: Customer service and data operations support for municipal securities business, including data entry, quality assurance, customer inquiry resolution, license verification, and account registration processing
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Position Summary:

The Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) is seeking a Product Operations Associate. This customer service role will play a crucial role in supporting our business operations within the financial industry. The Product Operations Associate will function as a part of a team in a role that combines customer service, data entry, quality assurance and the opportunity learn about the municipal bond market, which powers many American communities and public projects. 



About Us:

The Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board was created by the U.S. Congress in 1975 with the purpose of creating regulations that would help prevent fraud and abuse in the securities industry.


Key Responsibilities:

Daily responsibilities will include: 
Reviewing financial data for consistency 
Data entry to ensure accuracy 
Interacting with customers via phone or email to resolve issues and/or route inquiries to appropriate team members
Conducting license verifications for data orders 
Maintaining an organized filing system 
Initiating routine outbound data quality calls to customers 
Processing data orders 
Reviewing and approving new account registrations


Qualifications:

All candidates must be legally authorized to work in the United States without the need for current or future visa sponsorship. 
Excellent customer service skills. Prior experience in a help desk, call center, or related customer service role is highly desirable. 
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written.
Strong organizational skills and keen attention to detail.
Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
Technical savvy with the ability to quickly adapt to new software
A proactive, adaptable, and willing-to-learn attitude.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Outlook, Excel).
Ability to work a flexible 5-day schedule, including 12-hour shifts as needed.
Familiarity with the financial services industry is a strong plus

applications.role: Provide technical product support for Anthropic's AI systems, handling API debugging and complex technical issues for users ranging from individuals to large enterprise customers, while building support processes and improving user satisfaction at scale
applications.jobDescription:
Product Support Specialist (Wed-Sun)
Remote-Friendly (Travel-Required) | San Francisco, CA | Seattle, WA | New York City, NY
About Anthropic
Anthropic’s mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. Our team is a quickly growing group of committed researchers, engineers, policy experts, and business leaders working together to build beneficial AI systems.

About the role:
As a Product Support Specialist, you’ll be at the front lines of safely delivering AI to the world by responding to, investigating, and tracking user needs in your day to day. Additionally, you’ll help us identify – and close – gaps in our team’s technical knowledge, provide high-touch support to strategic customers, and demonstrate deep care for how we systematically support customers at scale. 

Note: Specialists will work Wednesday - Sunday

Responsibilities:
Become an expert in all Anthropic products
Respond to user support cases with a variety of complexity, from questions for individuals to complex API debugging for large businesses 
Clearly and empathetically communicate with a wide range of user personas, context-switching between guiding executives in a high-touch model to assisting consumer users in a rapid pace
Manage on-call tasks for high-urgency user issues with extreme ownership
Prioritize critically and comfortably adapt to an ever-evolving product landscape
Operate in ambiguity, making informed decisions even in never-before-seen situations
Partner with engineers, teammates, and other internal stakeholders to diagnose and resolve user issues, both individually and at scale
Suggest and drive improvements to increase user satisfaction through support processes as well as own initiatives that increase efficiency and drive down contact rates
Uplevel our team’s technical knowledge by scoping gaps, working with cross-functional partners to deeply understand relevant nuances, and building resources that grow with our products
You may be a good fit if you:
Have experience in technical product support, including API debugging, preferably in a second tier, escalated, or priority support team
Are familiar with APIs and technical SaaS products and can deeply understand technical docs with ease
Have demonstrated an ability to thrive in fast-paced, reactive situations while meeting core support metrics targets (e.g. CSAT, SLA, etc.)
Possess strong user empathy and are expert in the lifecycle of a support case; you can read between the lines of a user’s question, put yourself in their shoes, and get at the heart of their needs for a speedy, satisfying resolution
Have crisp but kind written communication skills and a deep care for the details
Enjoy helping others learn about new features and complex concepts
Experience troubleshooting SSO, SAML, and OAuth authentication flows
Are persistent and curious; you delight in the hunt of tracking down a bug or issue, and are energized by fixing this for all similar users going forward
Have experience contributing to the foundations of a support team – this is essential, highly valuable, but often unglamorous work
Are proficient at working in a technical environment and are interested in Anthropic’s products
Possess a deep sense of ownership, and are excited to help us build our team!
Strong candidates may also have:
While not required, we're particularly excited about candidates with one or more of these specializations:

Comfort with command line interfaces and basic scripting (Bash, Python, JavaScript)
Understanding of LLM capabilities, practical applications, and current limitations
Familiarity with enterprise networking concepts and IT infrastructure
Familiarity with Git workflows and version control concepts
SQL proficiency for querying logs and investigating issues
Experience supporting government or public sector customers, including familiarity with compliance requirements and security frameworks
Background in team lead roles or managing contract/vendor support teams
We're hiring across a range of experience levels to build depth in both technical capabilities and enterprise support.

 

The annual compensation range for this role is listed below. 

For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings ("OTE") range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role.

Annual Salary:

$116,480 - $165,000 USD

Logistics
Education requirements: We require at least a Bachelor's degree in a related field or equivalent experience.

Location-based hybrid policy: Currently, we expect all staff to be in one of our offices at least 25% of the time. However, some roles may require more time in our offices.

Visa sponsorship: We do sponsor visas! However, we aren't able to successfully sponsor visas for every role and every candidate. But if we make you an offer, we will make every reasonable effort to get you a visa, and we retain an immigration lawyer to help with this.

We encourage you to apply even if you do not believe you meet every single qualification. Not all strong candidates will meet every single qualification as listed.  Research shows that people who identify as being from underrepresented groups are more prone to experiencing imposter syndrome and doubting the strength of their candidacy, so we urge you not to exclude yourself prematurely and to submit an application if you're interested in this work. We think AI systems like the ones we're building have enormous social and ethical implications. We think this makes representation even more important, and we strive to include a range of diverse perspectives on our team.

Your safety matters to us. To protect yourself from potential scams, remember that Anthropic recruiters only contact you from @anthropic.com email addresses. In some cases, we may partner with vetted recruiting agencies who will identify themselves as working on behalf of Anthropic. Be cautious of emails from other domains. Legitimate Anthropic recruiters will never ask for money, fees, or banking information before your first day. If you're ever unsure about a communication, don't click any links—visit anthropic.com/careers directly for confirmed position openings.

How we're different
We believe that the highest-impact AI research will be big science. At Anthropic we work as a single cohesive team on just a few large-scale research efforts. And we value impact — advancing our long-term goals of steerable, trustworthy AI — rather than work on smaller and more specific puzzles. We view AI research as an empirical science, which has as much in common with physics and biology as with traditional efforts in computer science. We're an extremely collaborative group, and we host frequent research discussions to ensure that we are pursuing the highest-impact work at any given time. As such, we greatly value communication skills.

The easiest way to understand our research directions is to read our recent research. This research continues many of the directions our team worked on prior to Anthropic, including: GPT-3, Circuit-Based Interpretability, Multimodal Neurons, Scaling Laws, AI & Compute, Concrete Problems in AI Safety, and Learning from Human Preferences.

Come work with us!
Anthropic is a public benefit corporation headquartered in San Francisco. We offer competitive compensation and benefits, optional equity donation matching, generous vacation and parental leave, flexible working hours, and a lovely office space in which to collaborate with colleagues. Guidance on Candidates' AI Usage: Learn about our policy for using AI in our application process

Customer success metrics and support operations (CSAT, NPS, SLA, FRT, resolution time)

Technical writing for support documentation and knowledge bases
SDK troubleshooting across multiple programming languages
analysis.summary: Candidate shows strong technical aptitude, relevant AI/LLM knowledge, and customer-facing skills from retail, but critically lacks actual technical product support experience. This is fundamentally a support operations role requiring API debugging expertise and support case management - not a technical sales or consulting position. The 35/100 score reflects that while the technical foundation exists, the core job requirements around support experience, authentication troubleshooting, and SLA-driven operations are completely absent.

qaHistory.q: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
qaHistory.a:
I want to work at Anthropic because I'm genuinely fascinated by what happens when powerful AI technology meets real users—and I believe that interface is where safety and usefulness actually get defined.

My current work as a Generative AI Fellow has shown me firsthand how much thoughtfulness goes into making AI systems behave reliably. When I'm evaluating voice AI outputs or testing computer vision models, I'm not just checking if they work—I'm thinking about edge cases, unexpected user inputs, and how someone who isn't technical would actually interact with these tools. That experience made me realize I don't just want to train models; I want to be part of the team that helps people use them successfully and safely.

What draws me specifically to Anthropic is your research-first approach to AI safety. I've read your work on Constitutional AI and interpretability, and it's clear you're not just building capable systems—you're trying to understand them deeply enough to make them steerable and trustworthy. That philosophy resonates with me because I've seen in my own projects how easy it is to build something technically impressive that doesn't actually solve the user's real problem. When I built Flashconverter, the hardest part wasn't the machine learning algorithm for transaction categorization—it was understanding what people actually needed from their financial data and designing the experience around that insight.

I also genuinely enjoy the investigative nature of technical problem-solving. Whether I was debugging cloud networking configurations in my AWS/Azure project or troubleshooting why a categorization confidence score dropped in Flashconverter, I get energized by the hunt to understand root causes. The idea of doing that work at scale—helping everyone from individual developers to enterprise teams get the most out of Claude—while also contributing to the knowledge base that makes future support better, is exactly the type of work I want to be doing.

The Wednesday-Sunday schedule honestly works perfectly for me too, and I'm excited about the prospect of helping build support foundations at a company that's still defining what best-in-class AI support looks like.

qaHistory.q: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
qaHistory.a:
I want to work at Anthropic because I'm genuinely fascinated by the challenge of making powerful AI systems actually safe and useful for everyone, and I think the support team is where that promise becomes real for users.

My interest in LLMs started when I joined Handshake's Generative AI Fellowship, where I've been training multimodal AI systems by evaluating voice and vision models. Working hands-on with these systems showed me both their incredible potential and their limitations—I've seen how a poorly-engineered prompt can completely derail a model's output, and how crucial quality feedback is for improvement. That experience made me realize I want to be involved in AI at a company that takes the "making it work correctly" part as seriously as the "making it powerful" part.

What draws me specifically to Anthropic is the research transparency. I've read your work on Constitutional AI and scaling laws, and it's clear you're not just building impressive models—you're trying to understand them. That interpretability focus matters to me because I've seen in my AI training work how easy it is to get outputs that look right but are subtly wrong. The idea of working somewhere that's genuinely trying to solve that problem, rather than just ship fast, is exactly what I'm looking for.

I'm also excited about the role itself because it sits at the intersection of technical problem-solving and user empathy. At Apple, I spent three years helping business owners understand complex technology ecosystems, translating between their actual needs and what the products could do. I got really good at reading between the lines—figuring out what someone was actually trying to accomplish when their initial question was pointing in the wrong direction. I know supporting API integrations is very different from explaining MDM setups, and I'm ready to build that specific technical expertise, but I think that same instinct for understanding the real problem translates.

Finally, I'm drawn to the Wednesday-Sunday schedule because it means supporting users when they're actively building. The work feels more urgent and real when you're helping someone debug their production issue, not just answering general questions.

qaHistory.q: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
qaHistory.a:
I want to work at Anthropic because I'm genuinely excited about helping people actually use AI safely and effectively, rather than just building it. Throughout my work training AI models at Handshake and my technical projects with LLMs, I've become fascinated by the gap between what these systems can do and how real users struggle to interact with them. That's exactly the problem this role solves—being the bridge between groundbreaking AI technology and the people trying to integrate it into their work.

What draws me specifically to Anthropic is your research-driven approach to AI safety and interpretability. I've read your work on Constitutional AI and scaling laws, and it's clear that you're not just chasing capabilities—you're thinking deeply about building systems that are genuinely steerable and beneficial. That resonates with me because during my AI training work, I've seen firsthand how important it is to have rigorous evaluation processes and clear feedback loops. The idea of supporting products built on that foundation is incredibly motivating.

The technical aspects of this role align perfectly with where I want to grow. I've built data pipelines in Python, worked extensively with APIs in my Flashconverter project, and configured cloud infrastructure across AWS and Azure. But I haven't done formal technical support work yet, and I'm genuinely excited to develop that expertise. The combination of API debugging, working directly with enterprise customers on complex integrations, and helping build support processes from the ground up is exactly the challenge I'm looking for. I learn quickly in technical environments—I taught myself JavaScript and built a full financial platform in a few months—and I know I'd bring that same persistence to mastering Claude's API and helping customers solve problems.

Finally, I care deeply about the details and genuinely enjoy helping people understand complex concepts—something I did constantly at Apple when explaining technical solutions to business owners. But at Anthropic, I'd be doing that work in service of technology that could fundamentally reshape how we work and think. That's the kind of mission that gets me out of bed.

qaHistory.q: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
qaHistory.a:
I've spent the last few years building things with AI – training models at Handshake, experimenting with prompt engineering for computer vision, and honestly just geeking out over what's possible with LLMs. But the more I've worked in this space, the more I've realized the technical capability means nothing if we're not thoughtful about how we deploy it. That's what draws me to Anthropic.

When I built Flashconverter, I processed people's bank statements entirely client-side specifically because financial privacy felt non-negotiable to me. I could have made the app simpler by sending data to a server, but that felt wrong. That small decision taught me that how you build something matters as much as what you build. Anthropic is the only AI company I've seen that puts that principle at the core of everything – not as marketing, but as actual research priorities like interpretability and constitutional AI.

I'm also genuinely excited about being at the front lines of how people actually use this technology. At Apple, my favorite part was sitting down with a small business owner who was overwhelmed by technology and translating complexity into something they could actually use. I got energized by that moment when someone's frustration turned into understanding. Product support at Anthropic feels like that same challenge, but with much higher stakes – I'd be helping people navigate tools that are fundamentally reshaping how they work.

What really sealed it was reading through your research on scaling laws and model behavior. The fact that you publish this work openly, even when it might reveal limitations, shows you're trying to build AI systems the right way rather than just the fast way. I want to be part of a team that's wrestling with the hard questions about safety and interpretability while also making sure the people using these tools day-to-day actually have good experiences. That combination of technical rigor and genuine care for users is rare, and it's exactly where I want to build my career.

qaHistory.q: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
qaHistory.a:
I want to work at Anthropic because it sits at the precise intersection of three forces that have defined my career trajectory: technical depth, user empathy, and building foundational infrastructure that scales responsibly.

My journey with AI began hands-on at Handshake, where I'm currently training multimodal AI systems across voice and vision domains. Recording dialogue scripts with 100% accuracy requirements and evaluating computer vision outputs taught me something critical—the gap between what AI can do and what users actually need is where the most important work happens. I've spent hours developing prompt engineering strategies to regenerate original images from AI-edited versions, which gave me direct insight into model behavior, edge cases, and the iterative troubleshooting process that mirrors what I'd do debugging API integrations for enterprise customers.

What draws me specifically to Anthropic is your commitment to building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. I've read your research on Constitutional AI and scaling laws, and it aligns with a conviction I developed while building Flashconverter—my financial intelligence platform that processes sensitive bank data 100% client-side. I architected that system around a core principle: powerful technology must be designed with user trust and safety as non-negotiable constraints, not afterthoughts. That same philosophy permeates everything Anthropic builds.

The "unglamorous but essential" work of building support team foundations genuinely excites me because I've lived it. At Apple, I was promoted from seasonal to Business Specialist by systematically improving processes—mastering the service methodology, maintaining 95%+ NPS, and transforming how we consulted with business owners on MDM implementations. I didn't just solve individual problems; I identified patterns that improved outcomes for all similar users going forward. That's exactly the ownership mindset this role requires.

I also deeply respect that you're tackling the hardest version of this problem—supporting a product that's constantly evolving, where "never-before-seen situations" aren't edge cases but the daily reality. My background building a custom ML categorization system that learns from user patterns, combined with my technical foundation in Python, SQL, and API integration, positions me to become an expert in Anthropic's products quickly while contributing technical knowledge resources that scale with your growth.

applications.role: Supporting commercial strategy through data analysis, visualization, and reporting. The role focuses on collecting and analyzing data from multiple sources, creating dashboards and visualizations in Power BI, automating reporting processes, managing CRM systems, and providing data-driven insights to leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Product teams to drive business growth and operational efficiency.
applications.jobDescription:
Company Description
ATPCO is the world's primary source for air fare content, holding over 200 million fares across 160 countries. Every day, the travel industry relies on ATPCO's technology and data solutions to help millions of travelers reach their destinations efficiently. Join us and contribute to the development of innovative applications that power global travel. ATPCO is everywhere people buy flights.

We’re not just shaping the future of air travel– we’re redefining how and where great work happens. At ATPCO, we believe in flexibility, trust, and a culture where your wellbeing comes first

Why Join Us?​

✅ Remote-First Culture – Flexibility to work from home in your country of hire​

✅ “Leave Your Way” PTO– Take the time you need, when you need it​

✅ 401(k) with Generous Employer Match– Invest in your future​

✅ Comprehensive Benefits– Medical, dental, vision, & mental health​

✅ Global Tuition and Gym Reimbursement– Learn and grow on us​

✅ Standby Flight Program– Explore the world​

✅ Inclusive, Collaborative Culture– Be seen, heard, and valued​

Our Culture:​

We’ve built a remote-first culture rooted in trust, transparency, and belonging. With open-door leadership, weekly 1:1s, and real-time recognition, we keep our people connected and valued—no matter where they work.​

Our cultural pillars of Collaboration, Empowerment, Innovation, Learning, Transparency, and Trust guide how we show up for one another and grow together.

Job Description
As a Data Strategist, you will play a vital role in supporting the development and implementation of ATPCO’s commercial strategy. The ideal candidate is a problem solver who leverages analytical thinking, technical skills, and business acumen to uncover key insights, findings, and recommendations that drive corporate objectives.

In this role, you will continuously improve data quality and collection, enhance reporting and visualization methods, and analyze data to generate strategic insights that influence financial performance and business growth. You will work closely with teams across Sales and Account Management, Marketing, Finance, Customer Service, Product, and Technology.             

  

You Will:

·         Collect and organize data from various sources (revenue, product usage, customer insights, etc.), analyze it, and develop data-driven recommendations for leadership.

·         Conduct research and provide analysis to support changes or improvements in business practices.

·         Recommend process improvements and operational efficiencies within the department and across divisions.

·         Support the automation of data reporting tools and create clear, user-friendly data visualizations.

·         Develop analytical frameworks to better understand customer behavior and trends.

·         Assist with the configuration, testing, and maintenance of enterprise Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems to enhance business operations and customer processes.

·         Contribute to the development, automation, and validation of KPI reports.

·         Help design, document, and maintain operational processes to ensure consistency and efficiency

 

The Ideal Candidate:

Advanced Excel skills, including data manipulation, automation, and visualization using formulas, pivot tables, and Power Query.
Proficient in Power BI and SOL, with experience designing interactive dashboards and delivering data-driven insights.
Experience with Microsoft Power Apps or Power Automate to streamline workflows, improve efficiency, and support process automation.
Knowledgeable in data modeling techniques and data management best practices.
Skilled in developing clear, compelling presentations, primarily using PowerPoint.
Strong collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across multiple divisions and teams, including Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Product.
Highly organized, with excellent time management, planning, and attention to detail.
Able to translate complex data and technical concepts into clear, actionable insights.
Demonstrates leadership and initiative in managing projects and delivering results.
Comfortable working independently as well as collaboratively in a team environment.
Holds a Bachelor’s degree in Industrial and Systems Engineering, Business Analysis, Business Administration, Data Analytics, or a related field.

More importantly, they have zero professional experience as a data analyst or strategist - their work history is retail sales and AI training, with analytics experience limited to academic projects. The role demands someone who can immediately support commercial teams with revenue analysis, CRM administration, and operational reporting, which requires 2-3 years of hands-on business analytics experience this candidate simply doesn't have.

This role requires someone who can immediately configure CRMs, automate reporting with Power Apps/Automate, perform advanced Excel work, and deliver revenue insights to leadership, requiring 2-3 years of hands-on business analytics experience the candidate doesn't possess.

qaHistory.q:
Message to the Hiring Team
Let the company know about your interest working there
qaHistory.a:
I'm genuinely excited about this Data Strategist opportunity at ATPCO because it sits perfectly at the intersection of my technical skills and the commercial impact I want to drive in my career. The travel technology space fascinates me—the idea that ATPCO's data solutions power millions of travelers' journeys daily gives the analytical work real-world meaning beyond just dashboards and reports.

What draws me most to this role is the emphasis on supporting cross-functional strategy across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Product teams. In my Retail Strategy project, I built predictive models using R and tidymodels to solve a business problem for PrimeStop Convenience—identifying which customers would convert from low-margin fuel purchases to higher-margin convenience shopping. I didn't just deliver technical metrics; I translated Random Forest model outputs and Variable Importance Plots into an executive-level strategic recommendation that directly addressed revenue growth. That experience taught me that the most valuable data work isn't just technically sound—it's strategically actionable for decision-makers.

I've been building my technical foundation specifically around the tools this role requires. I'm proficient in SQL and Power BI for data analysis and visualization, with hands-on Python experience using Pandas and NumPy for data manipulation across multiple academic and personal projects. My Flashconverter financial intelligence platform demonstrates my ability to build end-to-end data solutions—I engineered a full data pipeline that processes unstructured financial data, applies machine learning for categorization with 70-95% confidence scores, and delivers interactive Chart.js visualizations that drive real financial decisions. While I'm still developing advanced Excel automation skills like Power Query, I have solid foundations in formulas, pivot tables, and data manipulation that I'm actively strengthening.

I'm transparent that I'm transitioning from retail and AI training work into a dedicated analytics role, so I haven't yet configured CRM systems professionally or automated KPI reporting in a corporate environment. However, my Apple Business Specialist experience gave me substantial exposure to consultative problem-solving with business clients, understanding operational workflows, and translating technical capabilities into business value. I consistently maintained a 95%+ NPS by diagnosing client needs and architecting technology solutions—skills that translate directly to understanding stakeholder requirements and delivering data insights that drive action.

ATPCO's remote-first culture and emphasis on transparency, collaboration, and learning particularly resonates with me. I thrive in environments where I can work independently while contributing to cross-functional goals, and your cultural pillars align perfectly with how I approach problem-solving. I'm ready to bring my analytical rigor, technical foundation, and genuine enthusiasm for turning data into strategic insights to help ATPCO's commercial teams drive business growth.

applications.role: Analyzing complex business problems and key performance metrics for GEICO's Commercial Auto Insurance product line, developing statistical models for underwriting and pricing practices, and providing data-driven insights to support strategic business decisions and company goals
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
At GEICO, we offer a rewarding career where your ambitions are met with endless possibilities. 

Every day we honor our iconic brand by offering quality coverage to millions of customers and being there when they need us most. We thrive through relentless innovation to exceed our customers’ expectations while making a real impact for our company through our shared purpose. 

When you join our company, we want you to feel valued, supported and proud to work here. That’s why we offer The GEICO Pledge: Great Company, Great Culture, Great Rewards and Great Careers.

Seeking highly motivated individuals looking for an exciting career in Insurance Product Analytics! 

Do you love to solve problems? Do challenging analytical questions keep you up at night? Do you want to have a career with a focus on development and intellectual growth on a growing team that provides ample opportunity for upward mobility? We are looking for bright and driven individuals to join our Commercial Auto Insurance Product Analytics team at GEICO!

Insurance Product Analytics is essential to GEICO’s continued success. The duties consist of analyzing complex business problems and key performance measures, understanding the drivers of performance, helping develop solutions to problems, and much more! Senior Insurance Product Analysts are motivated, self-driven, and excellent analytical thinkers. As a Senior Insurance Product Analyst, in addition to working with peers and management, you can expect to work closely with our partners in other areas of the business unit. You will do more than crunch numbers – you will be active in determining business strategies, educating management on our results, and communicating across departments to ensure company success. Your work as a Senior Insurance Product Analyst will inform a wide range of critical business decisions.

As a Senior Insurance Product Analyst, You Will

Develop, prepare, and interpret statistical analyses of key business metrics to help support strategic decisions within the business unit
Develop, interpret, and present analysis and proposals to various stakeholder groups to help solve mid to high level business problems in pursuit of company goals
Employ statistical techniques to model various underwriting and pricing business practices

Candidate Qualifications And Skills

Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Business, Math, Statistics, Data/Business Analytics, or other relevant quantitative field of study OR Bachelor’s degree in any field of study plus prior experience
Proven analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities
Highly effective communication skills
Intermediate to advanced skills in Excel
Intermediate to advanced proficiency in SQL or other similar languages to extract data
Basic to intermediate proficiency with data analysis software like R, SAS, Python, or Power BI
Ability to work independently given general direction and to thrive in a team environment that is constantly changing
Ability to handle multiple concurrent priorities, providing accurate and timely results
Intellectual curiosity to learn and ability to ask insightful questions
Effective time management, attention to detail, communication, and organizational skills
1 - 2 years of analytical experience in insurance or a similar field preferred
Previous experience in auto insurance is a plus

Benefits

At GEICO, we make sure you have the support and resources to leverage and develop your skills, secure your financial future, and take care of your health and well-being. GEICO continually seeks to provide a workplace where everyone can be their authentic self. To help achieve this goal, we support associate-led Employee Resource Groups that foster a true sense of community. Through GEICO’s competitive benefits offerings and various training and development opportunities, we have you covered with our Total Rewards Program* that includes:

Premier Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance with no waiting period**
Paid Vacation, Sick and Parental Leave
401(k) Plan
Tuition Assistance including Direct Billing and Reimbursement payment plan options
Paid Training, Licensures, and Certificates
Benefits may be different by location. Benefit eligibility requirements vary and may include length of service.
Coverage begins on the date of hire. Must enroll in New Hire Benefits within 30 days of the date of hire for coverage to take effect.

Annual Salary

$73,800.00 - $157,850.00

The above annual salary range is a general guideline. Multiple factors are taken into consideration to arrive at the final hourly rate/ annual salary to be offered to the selected candidate. Factors include, but are not limited to, the scope and responsibilities of the role, the selected candidate’s work experience, education and training, the work location as well as market and business considerations.

At this time, GEICO will not sponsor a new applicant for employment authorization for this position.

The GEICO Pledge

Great Company: At GEICO, we help our customers through life’s twists and turns. Our mission is to protect people when they need it most and we’re constantly evolving to stay ahead of their needs.

We’re an iconic brand that thrives on innovation, exceeding our customers’ expectations and enabling our collective success. From day one, you’ll take on exciting challenges that help you grow and collaborate with dynamic teams who want to make a positive impact on people’s lives.

Great Careers: We offer a career where you can learn, grow, and thrive through personalized development programs, created with your career – and your potential – in mind. You’ll have access to industry leading training, certification assistance, career mentorship and coaching with supportive leaders at all levels.

Great Culture: We foster an inclusive culture of shared success, rooted in integrity, a bias for action and a winning mindset. Grounded by our core values, we have an an established culture of caring, inclusion, and belonging, that values different perspectives. Our teams are led by dynamic, multi-faceted teams led by supportive leaders, driven by performance excellence and unified under a shared purpose.

As part of our culture, we also offer employee engagement and recognition programs that reward the positive impact our work makes on the lives of our customers.

Great Rewards: We offer compensation and benefits built to enhance your physical well-being, mental and emotional health and financial future.

Comprehensive Total Rewards program that offers personalized coverage tailor-made for you and your family’s overall well-being.
Financial benefits including market-competitive compensation; a 401K savings plan vested from day one that offers a 6% match; performance and recognition-based incentives; and tuition assistance.
Access to additional benefits like mental healthcare as well as fertility and adoption assistance.
Supports flexibility- We provide workplace flexibility as well as our GEICO Flex program, which offers the ability to work from anywhere in the US for up to four weeks per year.

The equal employment opportunity policy of the GEICO Companies provides for a fair and equal employment opportunity for all associates and job applicants regardless of race, color, religious creed, national origin, ancestry, age, gender, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, familial status, disability or genetic information, in compliance with applicable federal, state and local law. GEICO hires and promotes individuals solely on the basis of their qualifications for the job to be filled.

GEICO reasonably accommodates qualified individuals with disabilities to enable them to receive equal employment opportunity and/or perform the essential functions of the job, unless the accommodation would impose an undue hardship to the Company. This applies to all applicants and associates. GEICO also provides a work environment in which each associate is able to be productive and work to the best of their ability. We do not condone or tolerate an atmosphere of intimidation or harassment. We expect and require the cooperation of all associates in maintaining an atmosphere free from discrimination and harassment with mutual respect by and for all associates and applicants.

Technical skills alone cannot compensate for the critical domain expertise gap in commercial auto insurance underwriting and pricing analytics that this position requires. Should target entry-level insurance analyst positions to build necessary industry foundation.

qaHistory.q: List your reasons for leaving your last three positions.*
qaHistory.a:
**Position 1 - Apple Business Specialist (Sep 2021 — Jun 2024):**
After three years of exceptional performance at Apple—including promotion from seasonal to permanent and then to Business Specialist—I made the strategic decision to transition into a dedicated analytics career. While I excelled at architecting enterprise technology solutions and consistently maintained a 95%+ Net Promoter Score, my role was fundamentally client-facing rather than analytical. I was spending my time conducting consultative sales and technical troubleshooting when what truly energized me was analyzing the business data behind our performance metrics—understanding conversion patterns, identifying operational inefficiencies, and optimizing processes. I realized I wanted to leverage my analytical mindset full-time rather than applying it peripherally within a sales environment. The role provided invaluable experience in translating complex technical concepts to business stakeholders, but I was ready to pivot toward building statistical models and conducting the deep quantitative analysis that drives strategic decisions.

**Position 2 - Handshake Generative AI Fellow (Dec 2025 — Present):**
I'm actively seeking this opportunity at GEICO while continuing my current work at Handshake, which is project-based and flexible. My AI training work involves evaluating multimodal models and developing prompt engineering strategies, which has sharpened my analytical rigor and attention to detail—skills directly applicable to insurance product analytics. However, this role is fundamentally task-oriented gig work rather than strategic analytical problem-solving within a business context. I'm eager to transition into a position where I can apply my technical skills in Python, R, and SQL to analyze complex business problems, develop statistical models, and directly influence strategic decisions. The GEICO Senior Insurance Product Analyst role represents exactly the type of analytical challenge I'm seeking—combining my technical proficiency with meaningful business impact in a growth-oriented environment that offers clear opportunities for development and upward mobility.

applications.role: Building predictive models and conducting data analysis to provide actionable insights for NCAA basketball, football, and baseball programs regarding transfer portal player evaluation and decision-making
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
The company operates in the sports data science and analytics space, focusing on long-term partnerships with emerging professional talent. Its core model provides financial support and career resources to individuals early in their professional journey, in exchange for a share of future earnings over a defined period. They also work with many well-known NCAA programs utilizing data science and analytics to evaluate player value and fit in the new and evolving transfer portal.


Employee testimonials:

"Casual yet collaborative environment with a great work/life balance."
"Company is revolutionizing the use of data science in the sports industry!"
"Culture that truly values and supports its employees."


Title: Sports Data Scientist

Location: Remote

Salary: $90,000 - $120,000



Our client is seeking a Sports Data Scientist to help us add value to their NCAA basketball clients.
This position is an exciting opportunity to leverage data analysis to provide actionable insights, support decision-making within the NCAA collegiate football, baseball and basketball transfer portal. The ideal candidate will have a passion for sports analytics, specifically in football, baseball or basketball, a strong foundation in statistical data analysis and data visualization, along with deep sports knowledge.


Key Responsibilities

Build predictive models utilizing insights from client and sport knowledge
Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify data-driven insights on the transfer portal to collegiate programs
Answer ad-hoc questions regarding transfer targeting
Ensure data integrity and accuracy across several data sources
Build predictive models utilizing insights from client and sport knowledge


Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field
Programming experience
Proficiency with Python (pandas, polars, numpy) and SQL
Experience generating data visualizations programmatically
Deep Understanding of sports
Strong communication skills and ability to convey complex data concepts to nontechnical audiences
Strong self-motivation and curiosity to challenge traditional sports beliefs •
Attention to detail for accurate and timely report delivery

applications.jobDescription:
Why Clipboard Exists:
We exist to lift as many people up the socioeconomic ladder as possible. We dramatically improve lives by letting professionals turn extra time and ambition into career growth and financial opportunity. We achieve this with our app-based marketplace that connects workplaces and professionals, allowing professionals to book on-demand shifts and workplaces to access on-demand talent.

About Clipboard:
Clipboard is a fast-growing Series C marketplace. We are a leader in our Long Term Care vertical and are expanding into several others (Dental Offices, Schools, etc).
We are a YC Top Company with a global, remote team of 700+ people. We have been profitable since 2022, and fill millions of shifts annually at partner workplaces across the US, where tens of thousands of professionals work with us every year.
We are looking for your help to keep growing so we can serve more professionals and workplaces.
To learn more about us, take a look at our website here.

The Revenue Strategy and Operations role is a generalist position at Clipboard, suited for the “all-around athlete” who can do it all and wants exposure to a range of problems and opportunities.

You’re likely someone with an analytical mind who’s interested in and capable of solving different problem types – you’re a critical thinker, resourceful, adaptable, and confident that you can figure it out if you can sink your teeth in deep enough. These are the ingredients for a problem-solver who is eager to flex into multiple types of projects and responsibilities, depending on what’s needed and most impactful.

Examples of What You'll Do

Run marketing experiments to increase inbound leads

Build a system for targeting competitors

Create an algorithm that identifies churn in high utilization customers

Develop an outbound email strategy

Who You Are

0–2 years of professional experience in sales, account management, customer-facing roles, or B2B sales

A fast learner who thrives in fast-paced and dynamic environments

Eager to uncover insights to inform a country-wide sales strategy

Data-driven and strategic, able to shape plans and potentially pivot your thinking based on insights

Comfortable owning results end to end with minimal oversight

Experience in analytical roles, such as Management, Consulting, Revenue Operations, and Sales Operations, is a bonus but not mandatory

Having worked on Marketplaces and/or Startups is a bonus, but not mandatory

What You Bring

Strong analytical thinking skills

A foundation in data manipulation tools is beneficial, but not mandatory (E.g., Excel, SQL)

Curiosity, persistence, and a bias for action

Organization and autonomy in managing your own schedule

What We Offer

Competitive base salary

Insurance benefits and 401k options

Unlimited PTO and flexible holidays

Remote-first flexibility

missingSkills.skill: B2B sales or account management experience
missingSkills.reason: This role requires 0-2 years in sales, account management, or B2B sales. Candidate has B2B solutions experience at Apple but not true quota-carrying sales or managing accounts through a sales cycle. Their experience was retail-based consultative selling, not outbound B2B sales.

The role explicitly involves running marketing experiments to increase inbound leads and developing outbound email strategies. Candidate has zero demonstrated marketing experience - no campaign management, A/B testing, email marketing, or lead generation work.

missingSkills.skill: Revenue Operations or Sales Operations experience
missingSkills.reason: Role is titled Revenue Strategy and Operations. Candidate has no RevOps, SalesOps, or CRM pipeline management experience. Their Salesforce skill is listed but not demonstrated in actual revenue operations context.

missingSkills.skill: Customer success or churn analysis experience
missingSkills.reason: Role requires creating algorithms to identify churn in high utilization customers. While candidate has ML skills, they have zero experience in customer lifecycle management, retention, or churn prediction in a real business context.

Building systems for targeting competitors requires competitive analysis experience. Candidate has strategy consulting academic projects but no real-world competitive intelligence gathering or go-to-market strategy execution.

Clipboard is a two-sided marketplace. Understanding supply-demand dynamics, marketplace metrics (take rate, GMV, liquidity), and platform economics is crucial. Candidate has no marketplace experience.

experienceGaps: No professional sales, account management, or customer-facing quota-carrying role experience
Zero marketing operations experience - no lead generation, campaign management, or growth experiments
No Revenue Operations or Sales Operations experience - missing CRM management, pipeline analytics, sales enablement
Lack of startup or high-growth company experience - only worked at Apple (mature enterprise) and Handshake (contractor role)
No demonstrated experience managing end-to-end revenue-impacting projects with measurable business outcomes
Missing customer lifecycle management experience - no churn analysis, retention strategies, or account health scoring
No outbound sales strategy development - lacks experience with prospecting, email sequences, or sales cadences
Absence of cross-functional collaboration with sales and marketing teams in a revenue-generating capacity
No marketplace or platform business experience - unfamiliar with two-sided marketplace dynamics
Technical skills are overweight toward data science/ML but underweight toward practical business application in revenue context
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is fundamentally a data analyst/data scientist with retail consulting experience, not a revenue operations professional. While they have strong analytical and technical capabilities that exceed the role's requirements, they're missing the critical business foundation: actual sales, marketing operations, or revenue strategy experience. The role needs someone who can run marketing experiments, develop outbound strategies, and understand B2B sales dynamics - none of which this candidate has done professionally. Their Apple experience is retail B2B solutions, not true account management or sales operations.
improvementPlan: Get real sales or account management experience - take a BDR, SDR, or Account Executive role for 6-12 months to understand the revenue generation process firsthand
Learn marketing operations fundamentals - take courses on email marketing, A/B testing, lead scoring, and use tools like HubSpot, Marketo, or Salesforce Marketing Cloud
Build RevOps/SalesOps specific projects - create a portfolio project analyzing sales pipeline data, building lead scoring models, or creating churn prediction systems using real or simulated CRM data
Study marketplace business models - read case studies on Uber, Airbnb, DoorDash economics; understand key metrics like take rate, network effects, liquidity
Gain customer success experience - volunteer to help startups with customer health scoring, retention analysis, or onboarding optimization to build churn prevention expertise
Execute actual marketing experiments - help a small business or startup run growth experiments, track conversion rates, and optimize campaigns to demonstrate marketing acumen
Learn sales enablement and competitive intelligence - shadow sales teams, create battlecards, analyze competitor positioning to understand go-to-market execution
marketSkillsToLearn: Salesforce CRM administration and reporting
HubSpot or similar marketing automation platforms
Sales pipeline management and forecasting
Customer health scoring and churn prediction
A/B testing and growth experimentation frameworks
Outbound email sequence design and copywriting
Lead scoring and qualification (BANT, MEDDIC)
Competitive intelligence and win/loss analysis
Customer segmentation and ICP development
Marketing attribution modeling
Sales compensation and quota planning
Marketplace-specific metrics (GMV, take rate, liquidity, CAC by side)
Revenue forecasting and capacity planning
Sales enablement content creation
analysis.summary: Score 42/100. This candidate has impressive technical and analytical skills that actually exceed the role's requirements, but is missing the fundamental revenue operations foundation. They lack professional sales, marketing operations, or customer success experience - the core competencies needed for this role. Their background is data science/analytics-heavy with retail consulting experience, not the sales-facing, marketing-executing, revenue-driving profile Clipboard needs. They'd need 12-18 months in actual sales or marketing ops roles to be competitive.

applications.role: Providing technical and operational support to sales teams by managing Salesforce and revenue operations systems, resolving Jira tickets while meeting SLAs, identifying process improvements, and serving as a subject matter expert on sales technology stack to enable growth team efficiency
applications.jobDescription:
Revenue Operations Associate
Remote
Headway’s mission is a big one – to build a new mental health care system everyone can access. We’ve built technology that helps people find great therapists with the first software-enabled national network of providers accepting insurance. 

1 in 4 people in the US have a treatable mental health condition, but the majority of providers don’t accept insurance, making therapy too expensive for most people. Headway is building a new mental healthcare system that everyone can access by making it easy for therapists to accept insurance and scale their practice. 

Headway was founded in 2019. Since then, we’ve grown into a diverse, national network of over 60,000 mental healthcare providers across all 50 states who run their practice on our software and have served over 1 million patients. We’re a Series D company with over $325m in funding from a16z (Andreessen Horowitz), Accel, GV (formerly Google Ventures), Spark Capital, Thrive Capital, Forerunner Ventures and Health Care Service Corporation.

We want your time here to be the most meaningful experience of your career. Join us, and help change mental healthcare for the better.

As Headway grows, so too does the volume and complexity of the challenges we face. As a Revenue Support Associate, you will ensure we are the best possible partners to our sales team along with several other cross functional teams. Trust will be built on your ability to provide timely responses, take ownership of requests, and become a subject matter expert on the systems and processes being supported.

You will directly own the resolution of Jira tickets and ensure SLAs are met. You will be one of two team members who support Jira tickets and systems access requests. You will work cross-functionally to reduce our monthly contact rate and engage with the senior administrators on enhancements and feature requests. This role sits on the systems pillar of the Revenue Operations team and you will report into the Manager of Revenue Systems. You will be essential in supporting requests that are sent from sales associates on the Growth team.

To be successful, you will identify efficiencies or improvements that will reduce the case volume and improve the user experience. Strong written and verbal communication skills are required to support both synchronous and asynchronous updates. While this is an entry level position, increasing your skill-set and integrating into the Revenue Operations Systems team will be important as the teams we support grow and become more complex.

You Will

Support a world class sales team by solving Salesforce cases and meeting our monthly SLA
Identify key opportunities for efficiencies within our systems
Learn supported systems including Salesforce, Leandata, Chili Piper, Revenue.io, Attention, among others
Collaborate with cross-functional partners (Enablement, Data, Engineering, Marketing) and RevOps team members to reduce contact rate
Learn automations, access controls, reporting, and routing logic in various systems used by the Growth team
Investigate operational, technical, or process failures to identify underlying causes rather than just treating symptoms

You’d be a great fit if…

You have 1+ years of experience supporting Salesforce or a similar enterprise-level application
You have experience supporting stakeholders in a large-scale sales or service organization (50 - 200 users)
You have experience managing a ticking system with a high-volume and diversity of requests (1,000 / month).
You are interested in learning from an experienced team how to support Salesforce and best-in-class sales applications.
You are dedicated to expanding your skill-set to manage projects and system changes.
You want to learn what world-class growth looks like.
You like collaborating with teams from technical and non-technical backgrounds and get energy from solving issues.
You are a self-starter, with the ability to distill complex problems into simple solutions.
You work hard, are a team player, and expect the best from yourself and team members.
You are inherently curious and seek to understand the “why” of a request or issue.
You are resourceful and lean on your past experiences to be successful, even if not directly related.
You are knowledgeable and passionate about adhering to systems best practices and are not afraid to challenge others when 
Clearly and concisely communicate project requirements, goals, and results that elicit action
Compensation and Benefits:

The expected base pay range for this position is $60,520 - $89,000, based on a variety of factors including qualifications, experience, and geographic location. In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for an equity grant, depending on the position and level.

We are committed to offering a comprehensive and competitive total rewards package, including robust health and wellness benefits, retirement savings, and meaningful ownership opportunities through equity. Compensation decisions are made holistically, ensuring fairness and alignment with market benchmarks while recognizing individual contributions and potential.

Benefits offered include:
Equity compensation
Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage
HSA / FSA
401K
Work-from-Home Stipend
Therapy Reimbursement
16-week parental leave for eligible employees
Carrot Fertility annual reimbursement and membership
13 paid holidays each year as well as a Holiday Break during the week between December 25th and December 31st
Flexible PTO
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Training and professional development
 

We believe a team's strength is in its people, and we cannot achieve this mission without a team that reflects the diversity of this problem – across race, ethnicity, gender, sexuality, age, national origin, religion, family status, disability, military status, and experience. Headway is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, Headway will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided with reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please inform the recruiter when they contact you to schedule your interview.

Headway participates in E-Verify. To learn more, click here.

A notice to Headway applicants: To protect yourself against phishing and recruitment fraud, please note that Headway only accepts applications through our official careers page at https://headway.co/careers. Headway will never refer you to external websites, ask for payment or personal information, or conduct interviews via messaging apps. All official communication will come from a @findheadway.com email address. If you are contacted by someone claiming to be from Headway via an unofficial channel, please do not share any information and report it as spam.

Revenue operations tools ecosystem (Outreach, Gong, LeanData, Chili Piper)
