analysis.summary: Significant mismatch. Candidate has strong data science and analytical capabilities but zero demonstrated experience in the core requirements: Salesforce administration, high-volume ticket support, or supporting internal sales teams. Listing 'CRM & Salesforce' as a skill without any evidence of hands-on admin work is a red flag. This person needs 1-2 years of actual support/admin experience before being competitive for this role.

applications.role: Supporting Genesys contact center platform enhancements, implementations, and migrations by gathering requirements, documenting technical specifications, and coordinating between business operations and technical teams to deliver voice, IVR, routing, and integration solutions
applications.jobDescription:
Genesys Business Analyst
Post Date

Feb 23, 2026
Location

New York,
New York
ZIP/Postal Code

10112
Job Type

Contract
Category

Business Analyst (BA)
Req #

HOU-bca8346d-0444-4d9b-a58f-5a9c0ff59de4
Pay Rate

$40 - $50 (hourly estimate)
Job Description
The Genesys Business Analyst will support enhancements to the existing Genesys platform as well as implementations for de novo clinics and newly acquired entities transitioning onto Genesys. This role will bridge business, operations, and technical teams to translate contact center requirements into clear, actionable specifications that enable predictable delivery across voice, IVR, routing, CTI, and API integrations.

Key Responsibilities:

• Gather and document business and operational requirements for Genesys enhancements and new implementations
• Support discovery and current state assessments for de novo launches and telephony migrations
• Translate requirements into detailed user stories, process flows, and acceptance criteria
• Document IVR call flows, routing strategies, queue configurations, CTI behavior, and integration touchpoints
• Partner with Product Owners, architects, and developers across onshore and offshore teams
• Support system integration testing and user acceptance testing by clarifying requirements and validating outcomes
• Assist in cutover planning, deployment readiness, and hypercare support
Maintain traceability across requirements, design, and delivered functionality

Pay Range: $40-$50/hr

We are a company committed to creating diverse and inclusive environments where people can bring their full, authentic selves to work every day. We are an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that believes everyone matters. Qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment regardless of their race, color, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, marital status, national origin, ancestry, genetic factors, age, disability, protected veteran status, military or uniformed service member status, or any other status or characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please send a request to HR@insightglobal.com.To learn more about how we collect, keep, and process your private information, please review Insight Global's Workforce Privacy Policy: https://insightglobal.com/workforce-privacy-policy/.
Required Skills & Experience
Required Skills & Experience:
• 5 to 8 years of Business Analyst experience supporting contact center platforms
• Working knowledge of Genesys Cloud or Genesys Engage
• Strong understanding of IVR design, routing logic, CTI, SIP, APIs, and telephony workflows
• Experience documenting process flows and integration requirements
• Experience supporting implementations and platform migrations
Strong communication and stakeholder coordination skills
Nice to Have Skills & Experience
Healthcare, payer, or provider environment

Experience working in regulated environments including HIPAA

Familiarity with patient access workflows and contact center KPIs
Benefit packages for this role will start on the 1st day of employment and include medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as HSA, FSA, and DCFSA account options, and 401k retirement account access with employer matching. Employees in this role are also entitled to paid sick leave and/or other paid time off as provided by applicable law.

missingSkills.skill: Genesys Cloud or Genesys Engage platform experience
missingSkills.reason: This is the core platform the role supports. Without hands-on Genesys experience, the candidate cannot document IVR flows, routing strategies, queue configurations, or CTI behavior specific to this platform. This is non-negotiable.

missingSkills.skill: Contact Center Platform BA experience (5-8 years)
missingSkills.reason: Role explicitly requires 5-8 years of BA experience supporting contact center platforms. Candidate has ~3 years total work experience, none in contact centers or telephony systems. This is a fundamental seniority and domain mismatch.

missingSkills.skill: IVR design and call flow documentation
missingSkills.reason: Core responsibility includes documenting IVR call flows and routing strategies. Candidate has zero experience with IVR systems, voice channels, or telephony workflows.
Must document CTI behavior and integration touchpoints. Candidate shows no exposure to CTI systems or how they integrate with CRM/business applications.

missingSkills.skill: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) and telephony workflows
missingSkills.reason: Required to understand and document telephony infrastructure. Candidate has no telecommunications or voice protocol experience.

missingSkills.skill: Contact center routing logic and queue configuration
missingSkills.reason: Must translate business requirements into routing strategies and queue configurations. Candidate lacks any contact center operational knowledge.

missingSkills.skill: User story creation with acceptance criteria for technical teams
missingSkills.reason: Role requires translating requirements into detailed user stories for developers. Candidate has no Agile/Scrum experience or history working with product owners and development teams in structured delivery environments.

missingSkills.skill: System integration testing and UAT support
missingSkills.reason: Must support SIT and UAT cycles by clarifying requirements and validating outcomes. Candidate has no formal testing experience in enterprise implementations.

missingSkills.skill: Platform migration and cutover planning experience
missingSkills.reason: Role involves supporting telephony migrations and deployment readiness. Candidate has never participated in enterprise system migrations or cutover activities.
Must document API integration touchpoints between Genesys and other systems. While candidate has general API knowledge, they lack experience documenting enterprise integration requirements.

missingSkills.skill: HIPAA and healthcare regulatory environment experience
missingSkills.reason: Nice-to-have but important for healthcare context. Candidate has zero healthcare or regulated environment experience.

experienceGaps: Zero contact center or call center experience in any capacity
No enterprise telephony or voice channel experience
No experience supporting Genesys or any competing contact center platform (Five9, Avaya, Cisco UCCX, Amazon Connect, etc.)
Lacks 5-8 years of BA experience requirement (has roughly 3 years total, none in relevant domain)
No experience with de novo implementations or platform migrations
No healthcare, payer, or provider environment exposure
No experience working with offshore development teams or structured delivery methodologies
No traceability matrix or requirements management tool experience (JIRA, Azure DevOps, etc.)
Never worked as BA in Agile/Scrum environments with product owners and technical architects
Lacks understanding of contact center KPIs (AHT, ASA, FCR, abandonment rates, service levels)
analysis.criticalVerdict: This is a complete mismatch. The candidate is a data analyst/AI enthusiast with retail sales background, not a contact center BA. They lack every critical requirement: no Genesys platform knowledge, zero contact center experience, no IVR/CTI/telephony expertise, and fall 2-5 years short on required BA experience. Their skills are heavily weighted toward data science, machine learning, and analytics—none of which are relevant here. The hiring manager would reject this resume in 30 seconds.
improvementPlan: Gain actual contact center experience: Work as a contact center agent, supervisor, or quality analyst for 6-12 months to understand operations, IVR flows, routing, and telephony basics from the inside

Get hands-on Genesys platform exposure: Pursue Genesys Cloud certifications (Genesys Cloud CX Professional, Architect certifications) and set up a free Genesys Cloud trial to build demo IVR flows and routing strategies
Learn contact center fundamentals: Study IVR design principles, ACD routing strategies, CTI integrations, SIP trunking, and contact center KPIs through vendor documentation and industry resources

Transition into BA role at current contact center: If working in contact center operations, volunteer for technology upgrade projects, shadow existing BAs, and document process flows to build portfolio

Build relevant project portfolio: Document mock requirements for contact center scenarios—create sample IVR call flows, routing logic diagrams, user stories for omnichannel implementations, and integration specifications
Learn enterprise BA tools: Get proficient in JIRA, Confluence, Lucidchart/Visio for process flows, and understand Agile user story writing with acceptance criteria
Target junior/associate BA roles in contact centers first: Apply for entry-level BA positions supporting contact center technologies to build the required 5-8 years of domain-specific experience
marketSkillsToLearn: Genesys Cloud CX (platform architecture, routing, IVR design)
Genesys Engage (on-premise platform knowledge)
Contact center operations and workforce management principles
IVR/Voice application design and call flow diagramming
CTI integrations (screen pop, click-to-dial, API-based integrations)
SIP, VoIP, and telephony fundamentals
Omnichannel routing strategies (voice, chat, email, SMS)
Contact center analytics and KPI frameworks
CRM integrations (Salesforce Service Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics)
Agile/Scrum methodology for BA role
User story writing with technical acceptance criteria
Requirements traceability and management tools (JIRA, Azure DevOps)
System integration testing and UAT execution
HIPAA compliance basics for healthcare contact centers
Workforce management systems (Verint, NICE IEX, Genesys WFM)
analysis.summary: Candidate is fundamentally unqualified for this Genesys Business Analyst role. They lack the required 5-8 years of contact center BA experience, have zero exposure to Genesys platforms, and possess none of the critical telephony skills (IVR, CTI, SIP, routing logic). Their background is in data analytics and retail sales—completely different domains. This would require a 3-5 year career pivot to become remotely competitive.

Job Description
A client of Insight Global is looking for a DevOps Engineer to support their deployment pipelines within their Firco Insight product. The ideal candidate will have a background in Octopus Deploy, Github Actions, JFrog Artifactory, and AWS. It would be a bonus if the candidate had SRE experience. The pay rate for this role ranges based off years of experience between $45-55/hour.

We are a company committed to creating diverse and inclusive environments where people can bring their full, authentic selves to work every day. • Develop and implement software build tools and processes in line with SDLC pipelines using GitHub Actions, ProGet Artifact Repository, and OctopusDeploy • Develop Infrastructure Deployment processes using AWS, Terraform, Ansible, and GitHub Actions • Manage infrastructure in AWS with over 200 EC2 instances (Windows Based virtual machines), and deployment processes for those machines in multiple single-tenant environments, with a focus on Security, data protection, and reliability. • Other AWS Services used: S3, SNS, SQS, Lambda (with nodejs), RDS, Cloudwatch, and more • Developed local test stack and processes using LocalStack

applications.role: Managing and maintaining Astra DB Vector database environments in production to support GenAI and semantic search applications, ensuring high availability, performance, scalability, and security while transitioning the PEPVIGIL initiative from proof-of-concept to production
applications.jobDescription:
Production DBA – Astra Vector Database
Post Date

Nov 12, 2025
Location

Plano,
Texas
ZIP/Postal Code

75024
Job Type

Contract
Category

Database Administrator (DBA)
Req #

DAL-f998ea31-dbe6-49db-9fe8-57b2922b6c38
Pay Rate

$16 - $20 (hourly estimate)
Job Description
PepsiCo is seeking a skilled Production Database Administrator (DBA) with expertise in Astra DB (Vector) to support their PEPVIGIL initiative as it transitions from proof-of-concept to production. This role is critical to ensuring high availability, performance, and scalability of our vector database infrastructure supporting GenAI and semantic search applications.

Key Responsibilities:
Database Administration:
Manage and maintain Astra DB Serverless (Vector) environments in production.
Monitor database health, performance, and availability using built-in tools and custom alerts.
Implement backup, restore, and cloning strategies for disaster recovery and operational continuity.
Data Operations:
Support ingestion and indexing of vector data for Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) applications.
Optimize storage and query performance using Storage Attached Indexing and vector search capabilities.
Collaborate with data engineering teams to design and maintain schemas and collections.
Security & Compliance:
Ensure database security through role-based access control, encryption, and audit logging.
Maintain compliance with internal and external data governance policies.
Automation & Integration:
Automate operational tasks using Astra APIs and CI/CD pipelines.
Integrate Astra DB with AI platforms (e.g., OpenAI, Vertex AI) and developer tools (e.g., GitHub, Vercel).
Support & Troubleshooting:
Provide production support, including incident response and root cause analysis.
Collaborate with DataStax support and internal stakeholders to resolve issues.

We are a company committed to creating diverse and inclusive environments where people can bring their full, authentic selves to work every day. We are an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that believes everyone matters. Qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment regardless of their race, color, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, marital status, national origin, ancestry, genetic factors, age, disability, protected veteran status, military or uniformed service member status, or any other status or characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please send a request to HR@insightglobal.com.To learn more about how we collect, keep, and process your private information, please review Insight Global's Workforce Privacy Policy: https://insightglobal.com/workforce-privacy-policy/.
Required Skills & Experience
Proven experience as a DBA in production environments, preferably with NoSQL or distributed databases.
Hands-on experience with DataStax Astra DB, especially vector database features.
Familiarity with GenAI use cases, semantic search, and vector embeddings.
Proficiency in Python or similar scripting languages for automation and data operations.
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and multi-region deployments.
Strong understanding of database security, performance tuning, and monitoring.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
Benefit packages for this role will start on the 1st day of employment and include medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as HSA, FSA, and DCFSA account options, and 401k retirement account access with employer matching. Employees in this role are also entitled to paid sick leave and/or other paid time off as provided by applicable law.

Job Description
One of the largest logistics and transportation employers in the US is seeking a Jr. Data Engineer to join their expanding team. The Jr. Data Engineer will play a crucial role in understanding business problems and assisting in designing solutions that transform complex ideas into sustainable processes. This individual will focus on supporting the onboarding of new lines of business into the data analytics team. Additionally, the Jr. Data Engineer will support escalation tasks, contribute to system development, help automate processes, maintain internal systems, and assist in data modeling. They will use their growing expertise in data structures specific to the company's operating systems to help turn data into valuable business insights.

applications.role: Partner with cross-functional teams to define and drive product roadmap for Navy Federal Credit Union members by identifying opportunities, refining business outcomes, building new capabilities, managing product backlog, and serving as liaison between business stakeholders and technology teams throughout the end-to-end product development lifecycle in an agile environment.
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Job Title: Product Manager Associate

 Experience Level Desired: Level 1 (1-5yrs)

 Locations: Hybrid in Vienna, VA, Pensacola, FL or Winchester, VA

 Shift (Time): 8:30am - 4:30pm (local time)

 Pay Rate: Open to Both C2C and W2 options 

 Position Type: Multiyear Contract 

Description

 To partner with cross-functional teams across Navy Federal Credit Union to identify opportunities, 


 refine business outcomes, build new capabilities, and drive product roadmap for our members. 


 Defines what the product features should be and explains why they are needed. Serve as a liaison 


 between key business stakeholders and technology teams to define, develop, communicate, and execute 


 strategic plans for introducing new product initiatives. Own and drive recommendations for the 


 roadmap and refine and prioritize product backlog based on business objectives, member research, 


 competitive dynamics, and data analytics.


Responsibilities

 • Responsible for defining recommendations for and supporting product initiatives that help build 


 desirable, feasible, and sustainable products/services that meet member and internal customer needs


 • Develops an understanding of the division’s business objectives, risk tolerance, and member 


 needs in order to proactively identify needs, problems, and solutions based on analyses


 • Support the end-to-end product development life cycle including ideation, product definition, 


 build/prototyping, design, validation, and delivery


 • Advances assigned product features (new or existing) from ideation to enhancements to 


 post-delivery maintenance


 • Contributes to ensure the Portfolio, Program, & Team layers are aligned on outcomes that deliver 


 tangible business value and bridges big picture understanding and tactical needs


 • Content authority of the program backlog, accountable for feature creation, feature acceptance, 


 prioritization, delivery plans, and overall management


 • Explores the market, conducting market research on product features to understand end-to-end the 


 current and future member needs


 • Defines, owns, and communicates recommendations for the product functions, vision, and roadmap 


 while supporting the architectural runway


 • Identifies, communicates, escalates, and resolves project issues and risks to direct management


 • Contributes to a focus on the agile methodology of delivery by scoping iterative releases, 


 collaborating with and influencing stakeholders to meet business goals


 • Acts as the voice of the internal team member and advocate for the needs of those stakeholders 


 on their behalf


 • Builds partnerships and alliances, and manage relationships with partners to drive forward the 


 implementation on all fronts to develop innovative products and services


 • Enables decision making for leaders around product features through clear and concise 


 communication


 • Develops recommendations for and implements change management strategies and plans for product 


 features


 • Develops new ideas, initiatives, and recommendations that drive continuous improvement


Qualifications

 • Collaborative attitude and able to work with technology and business partners to ensure efficient 


 and effective delivery, independently or in a team environment


 • Quick learner with an analytical and flexible thinking style


 • Effective at multi-tasking and the ability to meet deadlines


 • Experience in communicating effectively (written and oral) and working well on cross-functional 


 teams


 • Comfortability with big, undefined problems and the capability to break down problems to find 


 solutions


 • Exposure to working in an agile environment


 • Exposure to data, combined with analytical awareness to help make informed recommendations


 • Aptitude and curiosity for learning how technical process flows work


 • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Management, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related 


 field, or the equivalent combination of experience, education, and training


 • 1+ years of experience in product management or related roles • Knowledge of the financial 


 services environment and Navy Federal's operations, policies, and procedures


 




 System One, and its subsidiaries including Joulé, ALTA IT Services, and Mountain Ltd., are leaders in delivering outsourced services and workforce solutions across North America. We help clients get work done more efficiently and economically, without compromising quality. System One not only serves as a valued partner for our clients, but we offer eligible employees health and welfare benefits coverage options including medical, dental, vision, spending accounts, life insurance, voluntary plans, as well as participation in a 401(k) plan.



 System One is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, family care or medical leave status, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate is a data analyst/engineer trying to pivot into product management without the requisite experience. While they have strong analytical and technical skills that could support a PM role, they lack all core PM competencies: no product backlog ownership, no roadmap development, no feature definition, and zero professional product management experience. The Business Specialist role at Apple is sales-focused, not product work. For a role requiring 1-5 years of PM experience at a regulated financial institution, this candidate is 1-2 years away from being competitive.

Get Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO) certification to demonstrate understanding of agile product development
Network with current PMs at financial services companies and seek informational interviews or internal transfers if currently employed
Contribute to open-source projects as a product contributor to demonstrate backlog management and feature prioritization skills
Take a product-adjacent role (Business Analyst, Technical PM, or Product Operations) at a fintech company to gain domain knowledge and transition pathway
Create case studies showing how you would approach Navy Federal product problems - demonstrate product thinking through spec writing
Learn product management tools hands-on: JIRA for backlog management, Aha! or ProductPlan for roadmapping, Mixpanel/Amplitude for analytics

Agile/Scrum methodologies as Product Owner

Product discovery and user research techniques
Prioritization frameworks (RICE, MoSCoW, Kano)

Stakeholder management and influence without authority
Go-to-market strategy and product launches
Technical feasibility assessment with engineering teams
Product-market fit evaluation
Competitive analysis for product positioning
Product requirement documentation (PRDs)
Wireframing and product design collaboration
A/B testing and experimentation design
Product lifecycle management
API and technical architecture basics for PM roles
Financial services regulations and compliance (for fintech PM roles)
Customer journey mapping
OKR and KPI framework development
analysis.summary: This candidate has strong analytical and technical capabilities but lacks the core product management experience required for this role. With zero professional PM experience, no product backlog ownership, and no agile product development background, they fall significantly short of the 1-5 year requirement. The retail sales experience doesn't substitute for product work, and the technical skills, while valuable, don't compensate for missing PM fundamentals. They need 1-2 years in an entry-level PM or product-adjacent role before being competitive for this position.

applications.role: Business Systems Analysis and Quality Assurance Testing with emphasis on UAT, test case development, defect management, and translating business requirements into functional specifications using Azure DevOps
applications.jobDescription:
Job#: 3013281

Job Description:

BSA/ Tester

Qualifications for Candidates

• Proven ability to apply critical thinking to resolve complex business and technical

problems, including root cause analysis and impact assessment.

• Demonstrated capacity for rapid comprehension of new systems, business

processes, and technical environments.

• Extensive experience collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver business

and technology solutions.

• Strong background in managing and contributing to large-scale initiatives with

significant business risk and visibility.

• Deep expertise in system testing and user acceptance testing (UAT), including:

o Test planning and strategy development

o Writing and executing detailed test cases

o Defect tracking and resolution

o Coordinating with stakeholders to validate business requirements

• Skills in eliciting, analyzing, and translating business requirements into functional

specifications and actionable solutions.

• Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver results independently or within a

team setting.

• Analytical and research skills, with a focus on identifying trends, patterns, and

actionable insights.

• Excellent time management and organizational skills, with a proactive approach to

solution delivery.

• Skilled in interpreting and applying complex data, procedures, and regulatory

guidelines to business scenarios.

• Ability to synthesize large volumes of information quickly and accurately to support

decision-making.

• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to convey

technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

• Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), database tools, and

presentation software.

• Hands-on experience with Azure DevOps, including test case management, work

item tracking, and release coordination.

• Familiarity with change management principles and their application in systems

implementation and process improvement.

• Bachelor’s Degree in a related field, or equivalent combination of education,

training, and experience.

Apex Systems is a world-class IT services company that serves thousands of clients across the globe. When you join Apex, you become part of a team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning. We offer quality career resources, training, certifications, development opportunities, and a comprehensive benefits package. Our commitment to excellence is reflected in many awards, including ClearlyRated's Best of Staffing® in Talent Satisfaction in the United States and Great Place to Work® in the United Kingdom and Mexico. Apex uses a virtual recruiter as part of the application process. Click here for more details.

Apex Benefits Overview: Apex offers a range of supplemental benefits, including medical, dental, vision, life, disability, and other insurance plans that offer an optional layer of financial protection. We offer an ESPP (employee stock purchase program) and a 401K program which allows you to contribute typically within 30 days of starting, with a company match after 12 months of tenure. Apex also offers a HSA (Health Savings Account on the HDHP plan), a SupportLinc Employee Assistance Program (EAP) with up to 8 free counseling sessions, a corporate discount savings program and other discounts. In terms of professional development, Apex hosts an on-demand training program, provides access to certification prep and a library of technical and leadership courses/books/seminars once you have 6+ months of tenure, and certification discounts and other perks to associations that include CompTIA and IIBA. Apex has a dedicated customer service team for our Consultants that can address questions around benefits and other resources, as well as a certified Career Coach. You can access a full list of our benefits, programs, support teams and resources within our ‘Welcome Packet’ as well, which an Apex team member can provide.

applications.role: Sales Development Representative
applications.jobDescription:
About Ramp
At Ramp, we’re rethinking how modern finance teams function in the age of AI. We believe AI isn’t just the next big wave. It’s the new foundation for how business gets done. We’re investing in that future — and in the people bold enough to build it.

Ramp is a financial operations platform designed to save companies time and money. Our all-in-one solution combines payments, corporate cards, vendor management, procurement, travel booking, and automated bookkeeping with built-in intelligence to maximize the impact of every dollar and hour spent. More than 50,000 businesses, from family-owned farms to e-commerce giants to space startups, have saved $10B and 27.5M hours with Ramp. Founded in 2019, Ramp powers the fastest-growing corporate card and bill payment platform in America, and enables over $100 billion in purchases each year.

Ramp’s investors include Lightspeed Venture Partners, Thrive Capital, Sands Capital, General Catalyst, Founders Fund, Khosla Ventures, Sequoia Capital, Greylock, Redpoint, and ICONIQ, as well as over 100 angel investors who were founders or executives of leading companies. The Ramp team comprises talented leaders from leading financial services and fintech companies—Stripe, Affirm, Goldman Sachs, American Express, Mastercard, Visa, Capital One—as well as technology companies such as Meta, Uber, Netflix, Twitter, Dropbox, and Instacart.

Ramp has been named to Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies list and LinkedIn’s Top U.S. Startups for more than 3 years, as well as the Forbes Cloud 100, CNBC Disruptor 50, and TIME Magazine’s 100 Most Influential Companies.

About the Role
As a founding Sales Development Representative in the Public Sector, you will be on our first SDR team focused exclusively on building Ramp’s presence in the Public Sector market. This is an individual contributor role with a clear and primary focus: sourcing high-value deals. Our world-class sales organization is looking for self-motivated teammates with high social intelligence and a natural inclination for selling to accelerate our growth as we scale rapidly! Individuals who are eager to learn, embrace challenges, and passionate about succeeding will thrive at Ramp. Our SDRs act as the initial point of contact and are responsible for hunting and qualifying leads to build sales opportunities for the team while working closely with leadership to refine our approach to this new market. You’ll work closely with C-level executives, finance leaders, and decision-makers to bring Ramp’s value proposition to life, while partnering internally with leadership, product, marketing, and operations to ensure success in the Public Sector.

What You’ll Do

Source new Public Sector opportunities through outbound outreach

Qualify prospects through consultative selling while leveraging BANT criteria

Grow top of sales funnel through warm and cold outbound campaigns

Research companies and add prospects to our outbound list

Maintain accurate client relationship data within Salesforce

What You Need

Experience working directly in the Public Sector/SLED space

Strong written and verbal communication skills; Excellent listening skills and energetic phone presence

High level of comfort with sales, prospecting and marketing tools & processes

Dedication to tracking and improving performance and efficiency on a daily basis

Deep interest in understanding business challenges

Sense of entrepreneurship: a self-starter with a high sense of urgency and ability to work within undefined processes

Nice to Haves

Prior experience with customer-facing and/or sales roles

Prior CRM experience (preferably Salesforce)

Experience with financial services sales

Experience at a high-growth startup

Ability to understand financial services

Bachelor’s degree from a four-year university

missingSkills.skill: Public Sector/SLED (State, Local, Education) Sales Experience
missingSkills.reason: This is explicitly listed as a required qualification and the role is specifically for a founding SDR team in Public Sector. The candidate has zero public sector experience - only B2B retail/enterprise at Apple. Public sector sales has entirely different procurement processes, budget cycles, compliance requirements, and stakeholder dynamics.

SDR roles are fundamentally about cold outreach, prospecting, and generating pipeline through high-volume outbound activity. The candidate's Apple experience was inbound retail/enterprise sales where customers came to them. No evidence of sourcing leads, cold calling, email sequences, or building outbound campaigns.

The job explicitly requires qualifying prospects using BANT (Budget, Authority, Need, Timeline) criteria. No evidence the candidate has used this or any formal sales qualification framework in their background.

Ramp is a financial operations platform involving corporate cards, payments, and expense management. The candidate has no financial services background and their fintech exposure is limited to building a personal finance tool, not selling financial products.

missingSkills.reason: Modern SDR work requires proficiency with sales engagement platforms, lead enrichment tools, and outbound automation. Candidate lists Salesforce CRM but no actual prospecting/outbound tools.

Public sector sales requires understanding RFPs, government procurement processes, compliance requirements (FedRAMP, security clearances), and public sector buying cycles. Complete absence of this knowledge.
SDRs typically make 50-100+ calls per day. The candidate's Apple role involved in-person consultations with walk-in customers, not cold calling or phone-based prospecting.

experienceGaps: Zero direct sales development or SDR experience - no track record of sourcing opportunities, building pipeline, or hitting activity metrics (calls, emails, meetings booked)
No public sector, government, or SLED market experience whatsoever - completely unfamiliar with this specialized sales vertical
No experience in a startup environment or high-growth tech company sales culture (Apple is mature enterprise retail)
No demonstrated experience with outbound prospecting campaigns, cold outreach, or top-of-funnel lead generation
Limited true sales experience - Apple role was more consultative retail/account management with existing customers rather than hunting new business
No financial services, payments, or fintech domain experience beyond personal projects
No evidence of working in undefined processes or founding team environments - Apple has highly structured playbooks
analysis.criticalVerdict: This is a complete mismatch. The candidate is a data analyst/technical consultant trying to pivot into sales, but lacks the foundational SDR experience that would make them competitive. More critically, they have ZERO public sector experience, which is the non-negotiable requirement for this founding team role. Their Apple experience was inbound retail consultation, not outbound prospecting or lead generation. They're 2-3 career moves away from being qualified for this specific position.
improvementPlan: Get actual SDR experience first: Apply for entry-level SDR roles at tech companies (NOT public sector focused initially) to learn fundamentals of cold calling, email prospecting, BANT qualification, and pipeline generation. Target 6-12 months of proven SDR performance.

Build public sector knowledge immediately: Take courses on government sales (NIGP, NCMA certifications), study public procurement processes, learn FedRAMP/compliance basics, and understand state/local/education buying cycles. Consider roles that sell to government even in non-SDR capacities.

Develop outbound prospecting skills: Master tools like Outreach.io, SalesLoft, Apollo, ZoomInfo. Practice cold calling scripts, A/B test email sequences, learn cadence optimization. Document specific metrics (meetings booked, conversion rates).

Gain fintech/financial services exposure: Take courses on corporate payments, expense management, and B2B fintech. Follow Ramp's content, understand their value proposition deeply, and learn how CFOs/finance teams evaluate financial operations platforms.

Network into public sector sales: Connect with government sales professionals on LinkedIn, join SLED sales communities, attend public sector tech conferences, and seek informational interviews with people in government-focused sales roles.

Reposition resume for sales roles: Completely reframe experience to highlight consultative selling, relationship building, and quota attainment rather than technical/analytical skills. Quantify business development results from Apple role.

Consider alternative entry point: Apply for Ramp's commercial/mid-market SDR roles (non-public sector) where lack of government experience won't disqualify you, then transition internally after proving yourself.

analysis.summary: This candidate is fundamentally misaligned with the role requirements. They're a data-focused analyst/technical professional with retail consultation experience, not a sales development professional. Most critically, they completely lack the required Public Sector/SLED experience that is non-negotiable for this founding team position. They need 1-2 years of SDR experience in any vertical first, then specifically build government sales expertise before being remotely qualified for this role.

qaHistory.q: We believe exceptional performance in one area is a good indication of performance in other areas. Do you have any examples of exceptional performance you want to highlight?
qaHistory.a:
During my three years at Apple, I was promoted from seasonal hire to permanent Business Specialist within 3 months—a conversion that typically takes 6-12 months—by consistently maintaining a 95%+ Net Promoter Score while mastering Apple's entire product ecosystem and service methodology. What made this exceptional wasn't just the speed, but that I simultaneously transformed my role from reactive customer service into proactive business development, architecting enterprise technology solutions for over 50 B2B clients and leading our store's Apple Vision Pro early adoption program. This required teaching myself Mobile Device Management systems, enterprise security protocols, and business financing structures that weren't part of my initial training, demonstrating the same self-directed learning approach I'd need as a founding SDR entering the Public Sector.

Outside of work, I've demonstrated exceptional performance through Flashconverter, a financial intelligence platform I built from scratch that now processes my personal finances and powers financial operations for my consulting business. I engineered a full-stack solution with zero formal software engineering background—teaching myself JavaScript ES6, IndexedDB architecture, and machine learning algorithms that achieve 70-95% confidence scores in transaction categorization. The platform actively drives financial decisions that are preparing me for homeownership, proving I can master complex technical domains through urgency and self-direction.

Most relevant to Ramp's mission, I scaled Dispomode from zero to 100,000 monthly visitors and $1,000 in daily revenue without traditional advertising channels, which mirrors the challenge of building Ramp's Public Sector presence from the ground up. I executed this by identifying market inefficiencies through data analysis, building technical infrastructure I had to learn on the fly, and converting cold traffic into sustained revenue—the same consultative, research-driven approach I'd apply to sourcing high-value Public Sector deals in an undefined market.

qaHistory.q: Please explain why you would like to work at Ramp.
qaHistory.a:
I'm drawn to Ramp because you've engineered exactly what I wish existed when I was running my e-commerce business—an AI-powered financial operations platform that treats every dollar and hour as optimizable data points. Managing Dispomode's finances through QuickBooks while manually categorizing transactions and tracking vendor payments showed me firsthand how fragmented financial tools create massive inefficiency, which is precisely the $10B problem Ramp has already solved for 50,000+ businesses.

What excites me most is the opportunity to be a founding SDR in the Public Sector vertical. At Apple, I built my reputation by identifying process improvement opportunities—I didn't just sell devices to 50+ B2B clients, I architected solutions around MDM implementations and workflow optimization that transformed how they operated. That same consultative mindset of "how can technology eliminate friction?" is exactly what Public Sector organizations desperately need, and I want to be the person translating Ramp's value proposition to finance leaders who are stuck in outdated procurement cycles.

The AI-first approach resonates deeply with my current work training multimodal AI systems at Handshake and my technical background building ML-powered financial tools. I built Flashconverter specifically to automate the tedious work of financial categorization using pattern recognition—the same intelligence Ramp has embedded at scale. Understanding how AI can transform backend operations from a technical perspective will make me a far more credible voice when explaining Ramp's automated bookkeeping and spend intelligence to CFOs.

I'm energized by the challenge of building something from the ground up. Founding my own business taught me the urgency and adaptability required when processes are undefined, and being on Ramp's first Public Sector SDR team means I'd be directly shaping how we approach this market. The combination of working with a world-class sales organization backed by Sequoia and Founders Fund, while having the autonomy to refine our approach alongside leadership, product, and marketing teams, is exactly the high-growth environment where I thrive. I want to help Ramp become as indispensable to government agencies and educational institutions as it already is to space startups and e-commerce giants.

qaHistory.q: Please outline your prior experience with CRM software.
qaHistory.a:
I've worked extensively with Salesforce during my time at Apple as a Business Specialist, where it was central to managing our B2B client relationships and tracking the enterprise sales pipeline. I used it daily to log client interactions, document technical consultations, and maintain detailed records of where each business client was in their buying journey—from initial needs assessment through MDM implementation and post-sale follow-up. The system was critical for tracking which decision-makers I'd engaged with at each organization, what their specific pain points were around device management or workflow optimization, and what solutions we'd proposed. I became proficient at creating detailed opportunity records that captured not just transaction data, but the consultative context—things like budget constraints, timeline requirements, and technical infrastructure needs—which helped ensure seamless handoffs when involving technical specialists or business financing teams.

Beyond Salesforce, I've also worked with CRM principles through my e-commerce venture, where I integrated customer data management systems to track purchase patterns, segment audiences for targeted email campaigns through Klaviyo, and analyze customer lifetime value to optimize retention strategies. While that wasn't Salesforce specifically, it gave me hands-on experience with the core CRM discipline of turning customer data into actionable insights that drive revenue.

I'm aware that as an SDR at Ramp, the Salesforce usage will be more focused on pipeline generation activities—logging outbound touches, tracking qualification notes using BANT criteria, and ensuring data hygiene for the Account Executives who'll inherit my qualified opportunities. My Apple experience gave me the foundation of working within a structured CRM environment where accuracy and detail matter, and I'm excited to deepen that expertise in a high-velocity outbound sales context.

qaHistory.q: Please tell us about a time you handled a complicated customer-facing sales situation.
qaHistory.a:
During my time at Apple, I worked with a mid-sized construction company owner who was initially resistant to our enterprise solutions because he'd been burned by a previous technology vendor who oversold capabilities and underdelivered on support. He came in frustrated, saying he just needed "a couple iPads" but clearly had deeper operational pain points he wasn't sharing.

Rather than pushing products, I shifted into discovery mode—I asked about his daily workflow challenges, how his field teams communicated with the office, and where he was losing time. He eventually opened up about crews using personal devices with no security, project managers manually entering data twice, and zero visibility into equipment tracking. The trust wasn't there yet, so I didn't pitch anything that day. Instead, I scheduled a follow-up consultation where I came prepared with a customized workflow demonstration using our Mobile Device Management system, showing exactly how his specific pain points—duplicate data entry, device security, real-time job site communication—could be solved.

The complication intensified when he revealed his budget was significantly lower than what the solution required. Instead of walking away or discounting arbitrarily, I collaborated with our business team to structure a phased implementation plan—starting with securing 10 devices for his project managers with MDM, then scaling to field crews over two quarters. I also connected him with Apple Financial Services to spread costs and showed him the ROI calculation based on hours saved from eliminating duplicate data entry alone.

The deal closed at $18,000, and he became one of my strongest referral sources, bringing in three other contractors from his network. What I learned was that complicated sales situations are rarely about the product—they're about rebuilding trust, deeply understanding the business problem, and creatively structuring solutions that align with both operational needs and financial reality.

qaHistory.q: Do you have direct experience working in the Public Sector/SLED space? If so, please describe
qaHistory.a: I don't have direct experience working in the Public Sector/SLED space. However, I want to be transparent about why I'm pursuing this founding team opportunity despite that gap. During my time as a Business Specialist at Apple, I developed consultative selling skills working with 50+ B2B clients, where I had to quickly learn diverse operational contexts—from healthcare practices to construction firms—and translate complex technology solutions into business value. While these weren't government entities, the approach of understanding procurement processes, compliance requirements, and longer sales cycles mirrors the consultative methodology needed for Public Sector selling. Additionally, my background building data analytics solutions and working with enterprise systems like Salesforce has given me the technical fluency to understand how Ramp's financial operations platform could solve the specific pain points government agencies face with budget transparency and spend management. I'm specifically drawn to this role because as a founding SDR, I'd be building the playbook rather than executing an established one—which plays to my entrepreneurial background founding and scaling an e-commerce business to $1K daily revenue through self-taught market research and process optimization. I recognize I'd need to rapidly develop expertise in government procurement cycles, compliance frameworks, and the unique decision-making structures in SLED organizations, but my track record shows I excel at steep learning curves when given the autonomy to define new approaches in ambiguous environments.

applications.role: Supporting digital strategy and SEO initiatives to enhance Freddie Mac's web presence through technical optimization, content optimization, analytics, and AI-powered search strategies while providing data-driven insights to align with business objectives and Go-To-Market campaigns
applications.jobDescription:
At Freddie Mac, our mission of Making Home Possible is what motivates us, and it’s at the core of everything we do. Since our charter in 1970, we have made home possible for more than 90 million families across the country. Join an organization where your work contributes to a greater purpose.

Position Overview:
Are you a strategic and detail-oriented professional passionate about digital strategy and search engine optimization? Do you excel at leveraging data, technology, and AI to drive digital performance? Freddie Mac’s Seller Engagement Digital strategy, Insights and Sales Enablement team is seeking a Digital & SEO Associate II who will play a pivotal role in supporting digital strategy, enhancing our digital footprint, optimizing content for search engines, and providing actionable insights through analytics. This role is ideal for a professional with a strong foundation in SEO, technical web optimization, analytics, and digital strategy.

Our Impact:
Freddie Mac’s “We Make Home Possible” tagline establishes the mission we work toward every day. Single-Family supports that mission, serving Sellers, Servicers, and a variety of other stakeholders to provide liquidity, stability, and affordability to the U.S. housing market in all economic conditions.

Single-Family is a strategic ally in housing finance, offering a more responsive way for Seller/Servicers to advance homeownership through technology-forward offerings, risk management excellence, and a deeply collaborative service model.

Your Impact:
In this role, you will leverage your expertise in digital optimization and SEO to enhance Freddie Mac’s web presence, drive strategic digital initiatives, and provide data-driven insights to align with broader business objectives. You will:

Support keyword research and web content optimization for SEO best practices.
Support initiatives to improve and maintain technical SEO elements, including structured data (schema), meta descriptions, site architecture, site speed, mobile optimization, broken links, and redirect chains.
Support strategies to optimize digital content for AI Search.
Collaborate with Go-To-Market team to maximize organic visibility and reach opportunities for GTM campaigns.
Analyze digital channels using website analytics (GA4) and behavioral analysis (heatmaps and session recordings) to translate quantitative information into relevant audience narratives.
Support campaign performance analysis to derive actionable insights and inform optimization strategies.
Support development and execution of digital marketing campaigns when needed, including email and web.
Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Digital Media, or related field (or equivalent experience) preferred.
1–3 years of experience in digital marketing, SEO, analytics, or related roles (mortgage industry experience a plus).
Proven experience optimizing content for SEO, including keyword research and on-page optimization.
Strong understanding of technical SEO (structured data, meta descriptions, site architecture, site speed, mobile optimization, broken links, and redirect chains).
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Understanding of website analytics platforms (GA4 preferred) and behavioral analysis tools (heatmaps, session recordings).
Familiarity with AI tools, prompt engineering, and AI-powered search strategies.
Strong strategic thinking and ability to organize and prioritize multiple initiatives based on impact to broader business goals.
Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.
Experience with project management tools and CRMs a plus (Wrike and Salesforce preferred).
Keys to Success in This Role:
Highly communicative, collaborative, and proactive.
Strong strategic and analytical mindset.
Ability to manage multiple ongoing projects and competing priorities.
Relationship builder inside and outside of the immediate team.
Comfortable learning and adapting to new digital technologies and industry trends.
Current Freddie Mac employees please apply through the internal career site.

We consider all applicants for all positions without regard to gender, race, color, religion, national origin, age, marital status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, physical and mental disability, pregnancy, ethnicity, genetic information or any other protected categories under applicable federal, state or local laws. We will ensure that individuals are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

A safe and secure environment is critical to Freddie Mac’s business. This includes employee commitment to our acceptable use policy, applying a vigilance-first approach to work, supporting regulatory mandates, and using best practices to protect Freddie Mac from potential threats and risk. Employees exercise this responsibility by executing against policies and procedures and adhering to privacy & security obligations as required via training programs.

CA Applicants:  Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

Notice to External Search Firms: Freddie Mac partners with BountyJobs for contingency search business through outside firms. Resumes received outside the BountyJobs system will be considered unsolicited and Freddie Mac will not be obligated to pay a placement fee. If interested in learning more, please visit www.BountyJobs.com and register with our referral code: MAC.

Time-type:Full time
FLSA Status:Non-Exempt
Freddie Mac offers a comprehensive total rewards package to include competitive compensation and market-leading benefit programs. Information on these benefit programs is available on our Careers site.

This position has an annualized market-based salary range of $65,000 - $97,000 and is eligible to participate in the annual incentive program. The final salary offered will generally fall within this range and is dependent on various factors including but not limited to the responsibilities of the position, experience, skill set, internal pay equity and other relevant qualifications of the applicant.

applications.role: Supporting business development operations through CRM data management, pipeline analysis, and reporting for strategic opportunities in government contracting. The role involves ensuring data integrity, creating dashboards and reports, and providing analytical insights to executive leadership to support capture and pursuit decisions.
applications.jobDescription:
Job ID
322650
Job Title: Business Development Operations Analyst
Job Category: Business Development and Marketing
Time Type: Full time
Minimum Clearance Required to Start: None
Employee Type: Regular
Percentage of Travel Required: None
Type of Travel: None
* * *
CACI is seeking a Business Development Analyst to support the collection, analysis, and reporting on our strategic opportunities and pursuits.  In this role you will work closely with our Business Development and Senior Leadership Team to manage our customer relationship management (CRM) and pipeline management system including ensuring the integrity and accuracy of our data, generate analytic reports, and respond quickly to various ad hoc requests for information on our strategic pursuits. Working in this high-paced environment takes an individual with knowledge of the government contracting sales/acquisition lifecycle alongside a problem-solving attitude to assist the business development, capture, and operations teams on securing new business.

Note: Position will be require 4 days onsite in Reston, VA.

Responsibilities:

-Ensure the integrity and accuracy and quality of opportunity related data in the system
-Assist users with entering, managing, and reporting system data, including ad hoc training sessions for basic and advanced system related tasks.
-Provide oversight and insight to the corporate system Admin team on behalf of the lines of business, including possible improvements to the system processes.
-Establish relationships with cross-company support teams to complete larger data visualization projects, as needed.
-Participate in requirements gathering and dashboard creations in internal systems, Excel, and PowerBI, as needed.
-Generate and distribute reports and analysis to support New Business Reviews, Pipeline Reviews, Management Operations Review, Bid and Proposal Reviews, etc.
-Establish and maintain relationships and collaborate with Executive Managers and other stakeholders across multiple lines of internal business
-Provide ad hoc analysis of business data to support Executive understanding of their pipeline data
-Maintain data dictionary of system data usage for views, dashboards, and user roles.
-Conduct research on specific opportunities, federal agencies, and competitors.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree with minimum 3+ years of experience
Understanding of Federal business development life cycle and pipeline management/development
Demonstrated ability to communicate succinctly and directly (both verbal and written) with Executive Leadership
Highly detail oriented; data-driven, and experienced in data analytics
Expert in all aspects of Microsoft Office Suite (Excel and PowerPoint especially)
High levels of professionalism        

Desired Qualifications:

Interest in the defense and intelligence sectors.
Experience working with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR), Request for Proposal (RFP), and Request for Information (RFI).
-
What You Can Expect:

 A culture of integrity.

At CACI, we place character and innovation at the center of everything we do. As a valued team member, you’ll be part of a high-performing group dedicated to our customer’s missions and driven by a higher purpose – to ensure the safety of our nation.

An environment of trust.

CACI values the unique contributions that every employee brings to our company and our customers - every day. You’ll have the autonomy to take the time you need through a unique flexible time off benefit and have access to robust learning resources to make your ambitions a reality.

A focus on continuous growth.

Together, we will advance our nation's most critical missions, build on our lengthy track record of business success, and find opportunities to break new ground — in your career and in our legacy.



Pay Range:

There are a host of factors that can influence final salary including, but not limited to, geographic location, Federal Government contract labor categories and contract wage rates, relevant prior work experience, specific skills and competencies, education, and certifications. Our employees value the flexibility at CACI that allows them to balance quality work and their personal lives. We offer competitive compensation, benefits and learning and development opportunities. Our broad and competitive mix of benefits options is designed to support and protect employees and their families. At CACI, you will receive comprehensive benefits such as; healthcare, wellness, financial, retirement, family support, continuing education, and time off benefits.

The proposed salary range for this position is:

$72,700 - $149,200
CACI is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, age, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other protected characteristic.

missingSkills.reason: This is explicitly required and fundamental to the role. The candidate has zero experience with government contracting sales cycles, procurement processes, or how opportunities flow from identification through capture to contract award. This isn't something you pick up in a few weeks.

missingSkills.skill: Pipeline management in government contracting context
missingSkills.reason: Government BD pipeline management is completely different from retail or e-commerce. Understanding opportunity stages, gate reviews, color team processes, PWIN assessments, and contract vehicles is essential. Candidate has no demonstrated experience here.

While listed as 'desired,' understanding FAR is practically essential for conducting meaningful research on opportunities and competitors in this space. The regulatory framework governs everything in federal contracting.
Supporting Bid & Proposal Reviews requires understanding what an RFP actually is, how to read them, and how they drive the capture process. Candidate shows no exposure to this.

missingSkills.skill: CRM systems specific to government BD (likely Deltek, GovWin, or similar)
missingSkills.reason: While candidate has generic CRM/Salesforce experience, government BD uses specialized platforms like Deltek CostPoint, GovWin IQ, or customized Salesforce instances with BD-specific workflows. The data structures, fields, and processes are completely different from consumer CRM.

CACI operates in classified and sensitive spaces. Understanding customer agencies (DoD, IC, DHS), security clearances, contract types (IDIQ, GWAC, BPA), and the competitive landscape is important for meaningful analysis and executive reporting.

missingSkills.skill: Experience supporting executive leadership in high-stakes BD environments
missingSkills.reason: Supporting New Business Reviews and Management Operations Reviews at a $6B+ defense contractor requires political savvy, understanding of corporate governance, and ability to present pipeline data that directly impacts revenue forecasts. Retail sales doesn't translate directly.

experienceGaps: No government contracting experience whatsoever - candidate's background is entirely consumer retail (Apple) and e-commerce/personal projects
No demonstrated experience managing or analyzing BD pipelines - has analytics skills but hasn't applied them to opportunity tracking, capture strategy, or bid/no-bid decisions
No experience working with federal agencies, understanding their organizational structures, budget cycles (PPBE), or procurement officers
No exposure to the defense industrial base, prime/sub relationships, teaming agreements, or competitive intelligence in the federal space
Limited professional experience period - only 3 years, mostly in retail, with recent AI fellowship that's tangential at best
No evidence of working in high-compliance, process-driven environments that characterize government contracting
Zero exposure to the language and acronyms of federal contracting (NAICS codes, PSC codes, contract vehicles, LPTA vs best value, etc.)
No experience conducting competitive analysis in B2B or government markets - e-commerce SEO is fundamentally different from tracking competitors on GSA schedules or in re-compete battles
analysis.criticalVerdict: This candidate has strong technical analytics skills and some BI tool proficiency, but is fundamentally mismatched for a government BD operations role. They have zero experience in federal contracting, which is the entire context of this job. The role requires someone who understands the government sales lifecycle, can speak the language with BD and capture teams, and knows what data matters for pipeline reviews at a defense contractor. This person would be learning the industry, the customer base, the regulatory environment, AND the job responsibilities simultaneously - a recipe for failure in a fast-paced environment supporting senior executives on multi-million dollar pursuits.
improvementPlan: Gain foundational federal contracting knowledge: Complete NCMA (National Contract Management Association) courses on government contracting fundamentals, take FAR basics training, and study the federal acquisition lifecycle

Get inside a government contractor: Target entry-level proposal coordinator, capture analyst, or contracts administrator roles at smaller contractors to learn the industry before attempting a BD operations role
Learn the tools of the trade: Get hands-on with Deltek, GovWin IQ, or similar government BD platforms through trials or informational interviews with BD professionals

Understand the customer: Study federal agency organizational structures, research DoD and Intelligence Community missions, and follow federal IT spending trends through sources like GovConWire, Washington Technology, and FedScoop
Network in GovCon: Attend AFCEA, NDIA, or local government contracting events to understand the ecosystem and terminology
Bridge the gap with adjacent roles: Consider business analyst or data analyst roles at government contractors first, then transition to BD operations once you understand the domain
Demonstrate relevant research: Conduct independent analysis of a federal agency's spending patterns using USASpending.gov or FPDS data and write it up to show capability and interest

Capture management methodology (Shipley, APMP standards)
Competitive intelligence gathering in government markets
Understanding of contract vehicles (IDIQ, GWAC, BPA, GSA Schedules)
NAICS and PSC code classification systems
Proposal development process and color team reviews
PWIN (Probability of Win) analysis methodologies
Federal budget cycles and appropriations process
Security clearance levels and requirements
SAM.gov, FPDS, and USASpending.gov database navigation
Teaming agreement structures and prime/sub dynamics
analysis.summary: Strong technical analytics foundation with Power BI, Excel, SQL, and data visualization skills, but completely lacks the government contracting domain expertise this role requires. The candidate has zero federal business development experience, no understanding of the acquisition lifecycle, and no exposure to defense/intelligence sectors. While the technical skills are transferable, the 3+ years of required experience was meant to be in the government BD space, not retail sales.

applications.role: Supporting technology modernization initiatives within financial regulatory systems by assisting with business analysis activities including requirements gathering, documentation, stakeholder engagement, user acceptance testing, and agile project support for enterprise applications such as NMLS, SES, and CRM platforms
applications.jobDescription:
About the job
Unlock Your Potential with the CSBS Summer Internship Program

For more than 110 years, the Conference of State Bank Supervisors (CSBS) has been uniquely positioned as the only national organization dedicated to advancing the nation’s dual-banking system, strengthening consumer protection, and promoting safe and sound financial supervision. Every day, we work alongside state regulators to oversee the financial services industry, foster economic growth, and drive innovative, responsive regulation. Our goal? To be the recognized leader in enhancing the quality and effectiveness of state financial oversight.

Are you ready to kickstart your career in the vibrant heart of the nation’s capital? The CSBS Summer Internship Program offers undergraduate, graduate, and law school students an unparalleled opportunity to gain hands-on experience, expand their professional network, and make a tangible impact in the world of financial policy and regulation.

This dynamic 12-week paid internship immerses you at the intersection of policymaking, technology, business, and regulation. You’ll work on meaningful, high-impact projects aligned with your background and interests, gaining exposure to industry leaders, policy experts, and fellow emerging professionals. In addition to your individual assignments, you’ll collaborate with a cohort of interns on a strategic enterprise-wide project that will leave a lasting impact on CSBS. Commuter benefits are also provided.

If you’re passionate about government, law, technology, public policy, business, or non-profit work, this internship is your gateway to professional growth and real-world impact.

Are you ready to explore, learn, and lead? Join us this summer at CSBS in Washington, DC, and take the next step in your career!

Values Instilled Behaviors for Excellence (VIBE)

At CSBS, work-life balance isn't just a policy; it's our VIBE! We recognize that our team members have lives that deserve attention and care. That’s why we create strong, supportive relationships that help everyone grow both professionally and personally. We honor each other's expertise and speak the truth, even when it’s a bit awkward. And guess what? This honesty creates a vibe of respect and trust that powers our efficiency and our excellence. It lets us chase those career goals while also nurturing our personal pursuits. At CSBS, you can thrive at work and at home—it's the best of both worlds!

Just like a healthy work-life, collaboration is an essential part of CSBS’s mission. In fact, it is the heartbeat of everything we do! We're all about pitching in, giving props to our colleagues, and having each other’s backs. This allows us to push ourselves to our maximum potential and embrace those bold risks and innovative solutions. No matter what comes our way, our commitment to communication and teamwork strengthens us. We at CSBS are on mission and on the move, tackling all challenges together!

Intern Position Summary

The Business Analyst internship at the Conference of State Bank Supervisors (CSBS) offers hands-on experience supporting technology modernization initiatives across the financial regulatory landscape. The Business Analyst intern will work closely with Business Analysts, Product Owners, Developers, and Quality Assurance teams to support requirements elicitation, process analysis, documentation, and user acceptance testing (UAT) for high-impact systems such as NMLS, SES, CRM platforms, and other enterprise applications.

The Business Analyst internship provides an excellent opportunity to gain exposure to agile delivery, financial regulation, state supervisory workflows, and enterprise product development.

Essential Functions

Assist in gathering, analyzing, and documenting business requirements for ongoing projects and enhancements.
Participate in discovery sessions, stakeholder interviews, and sprint ceremonies (standups, refinements, planning).
Draft user stories, acceptance criteria, process flows, and use cases under BA guidance.
Support creation and maintenance of Business Requirements Documents (BRDs) and High-Level Requirements (HLRs).
Support planning and execution of UAT cycles by creating test scenarios, test cases, and capturing results.
Assist with triaging UAT findings, clarifying expected behaviors, and verifying defect fixes.

Education And Experience

Currently pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, Public Policy, Finance, or related field.
Familiarity with Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
Experience with Jira, Azure DevOps, or similar requirement-tracking tools.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

Knowledge of Agile or Scrum methodologies.
Basic understanding of workflow diagrams (Visio, Lucidchart, or similar tools).
Knowledge of financial regulation, banking supervision, or government systems.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Detail-oriented with the ability to organize complex information.
Ability to work collaboratively within cross-functional teams.

Requirements

Due to the nature of CSBS’s business in support of state financial services supervision, all CSBS employees have the potential of interacting with confidential information related to the supervision of financial services companies (“Confidential Supervisory Information”). As a result, in addition to general business conflicts of interest, all CSBS employees are expected to disclose conflicts of interest in financial services companies on at least an annual basis and to proactively avoid such conflicts. 
Protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of CSBS information and information systems in accordance with CSBS policies and procedures. 
Candidate must be in the DC area and able to work in the CSBS office as needed. 

Compensation at CSBS

At CSBS, salary offers are determined within the established range based on a candidate's experience, education, and the market demand for the role. Where appropriate, we also carefully benchmark against similar internal positions to ensure equity and consistency.

In addition to competitive salaries, CSBS offers a comprehensive benefits package, a flexible hybrid work environment, and an inclusive culture. Learn more about our benefits, culture, and work environment by visiting CSBS Careers.

Sponsorship

Please note that CSBS is not able to provide employment sponsorship for this position.

Zero exposure to financial services, banking, government systems, or regulatory environments - CSBS works with NMLS and state banking supervisors
